Now that our family has reached a state of moderate prosperity, there is no need to think about traveling again." Dong then sold a hundred acres of land to build a refined pavilion surrounded by bamboo and trees, remaining there for years, living in leisure and joy. He had one wife and three concubines, loving them as if they were siblings. They wore clothes easily, ate from the same dishes, had no jealousy among the spouses, and expected mutual support. Within the family, the Sheng family oversaw the household, Nuanyun managed sewing, Lanxiao assisted in cooking, and Ah Jin handled accounting and writing, each taking on their respective duties. All excelled in their respective roles, even when tasks were complex, nothing was neglected. Any woman entering Dong's household was influenced by his virtue and became good, and at that time, there were no complaints or disputes in the neighborhood of Ziyu.

    Whenever couples were intimate, even a small star could cause discord; even a roar of a lion in the forest or a cry of a cuckoo on the hill could cause unrest. How much more so, then, with repeated and intertwined relationships. Even if three virtues were praised, if one person caused trouble, how could one maintain such ordinary happiness? Some say Dong is good at managing his household, but I say Dong has the way of self-cultivation. When he was covered by a fan, his demeanor was already different. If Dong were to enter public service, transforming his family into a model for the nation, it would indeed be impressive. Ah! Dong does not trade the honor of high office for this domestic happiness.

    234

    盛氏，胶东人，号淑娟。修眉方面，性恬静，好佛。尝独坐一室，瀹茗诵经，焚香缕缕。逮下多喜颜承奉者，不敢有惰事，非不敢也，实不忍违其意旨耳。
    暖云，苏人，幼鬻于董。身瘦怯如春月柳，微有白麻。足缠似锥，爱着通绣红履。善针工，一家衣着袜鞋出其手。盛氏所供髮绣观音像，暖云之制也。
    兰绡，扬州人。目秀准直，心灵警，口滑稽，能令人善听。精于庖厨，每饭一蔬，多出奇想。如以紫玉光熯肉、荷叶粉鱼炸、醋浸山兰蕊、木瓜花作酱，皆味外味云。
    阿进，钱唐人。体丰而软，好洁，喜穿青蓝，以显其白。古人谓丰若有余、柔若无骨是也。能书算，一家度支悉付之。暇时洗桐拭竹，扫径浇花，绝无一毫俗韵。或于灯前月下，一家环坐，听阿进弄阮弦唱平湖调，文词数折，其音袅袅，如流莺云。

    Explanation

    234

    The Sheng family, from Jiaodong, is known by the nickname Shujuan. She has well-groomed eyebrows and a beautiful face, a calm and quiet nature, and is fond of Buddhism. She often sits alone in a room, brewing tea, reciting scriptures, and burning incense, with the smoke rising in thin lines. Her subordinates are often pleased and attentive to her, and they dare not be lazy, not because they are unwilling, but because they cannot bear to go against her wishes.

    Warm Cloud, from Suzhou, was sold into servitude at a young age by someone named Dong. She is thin and delicate like a willow branch in spring, with a slight white complexion. Her feet are tightly bound, and she loves to wear red shoes embroidered with intricate patterns. She is skilled in needlework, and all the clothing and footwear of the family are made by her. The hair embroidery statue of Guanyin that the Sheng family has was made by Warm Cloud.

    Lanxiao, from Yangzhou, has beautiful and direct eyes, an intelligent and alert mind, a witty and humorous mouth, and can make others listen attentively. She is skilled in cooking, and each meal often features creative and surprising dishes. For example, dishes like "Purple Jade Light Grilled Meat," "Lotus Leaf Flour Fish Fritter," "Pickled Mountain Orchid Petals," and "Papaya Flower Sauce" all have flavors beyond the ordinary.

    Ajin, from Qiantang, has a plump and soft body, is clean and hygienic, likes to wear blue and green clothes to highlight her fair skin. Ancient people described her as having an abundance of flesh and a softness that seems boneless. She is skilled in writing and arithmetic, and all the family's financial matters are managed by her. During her free time, she cleans the lutes and flutes, sweeps the paths, and waters the flowers, with no trace of vulgarity. Sometimes under the lamp or moonlight, the whole family sits in a circle, listening to Ajin playing the lute and singing the "Pinghu Tune." The lyrics have several parts, and the melody is soft and melodious, like the song of a skylark.

    235

    (妙事、妙人、妙文，令观者叹赏不置。)

    郑延
    郑延，淇人。幼失怙，十六岁入泮，韶秀无比，人美其名曰“郑大姐”。聘陈氏，未娶夭殂。郑母爱之，慎择所配，恐拂其意。而郑又高自位置，故二十五而求凰未就也。
    会入郡，过府桥下一小绫绢铺，柜前有女子白洁，瓜仁面，腰细刚一捻。心爱好之，趋入铺买绫。女呼其兄，兄出，非郑意，乃故为低昂而去。明日郑见女，又至。女欲呼兄，郑曰：“无庸。”指架上包：“即此绫。昨已言明若干镪。”女初利其价，遂与郑。郑脱贯如价。女沉吟，返其半曰：“毋须太多。”郑德之，由此日往觑焉。既而女亦目逆而送之。郑乃属媒妪通其意。其兄返命曰：“弱妹得秀才耦，大佳，但只好作画中人，恐不任井臼事。”郑曰：“吾家颇有薄产，断不至使新妇入厨下。”归告其母，遂委禽焉。

    Explanation

    235

    Exquisite Things, Exquisite People, Exquisite Writings, All Evoke Unending Admiration.

    Zheng Yan
    Zheng Yan was a person from Qi. As a young child, he lost his father and entered school at the age of sixteen. He was extraordinarily graceful and handsome, and people praised him with the nickname "Big Sister Zheng." He was engaged to a Chen family but died before marriage. Zheng's mother loved him dearly and carefully chose his partner, fearing to go against his wishes. Zheng also held himself in high esteem, so at twenty-five, he had not yet found a match.
    One day, he entered the prefecture and passed by a small silk and satin shop under a bridge. A girl stood by the counter, white and clean, with a round face like a melon seed, and a waist as thin as a pinch. Zheng liked her very much and hurried into the shop to buy silk. The girl called her brother, who came out, but it was not what Zheng wanted, so he deliberately lowered his head and left. The next day, Zheng saw the girl again and went back. The girl wanted to call her brother, but Zheng said, "No need." He pointed to a package on the shelf: "This is the silk. I already told you the price yesterday." The girl initially liked the price and agreed to Zheng. Zheng paid the price. The girl hesitated and returned half of the money, saying, "Not too much is needed." Zheng felt grateful and started visiting her daily. Soon, the girl also looked after him and saw him off. Zheng then asked a matchmaker to convey his intentions. Her brother replied: "A young sister getting a scholar as a partner is great, but she is only fit to be a figure in a painting, and may not be able to handle household chores." Zheng said, "Our family has some modest property, so I won't let the new wife enter the kitchen." He returned home and told his mother, and they finally arranged the marriage.

    236

    逾月，其兄送之淇上，即返卫。合卺之夕，女哭泣不自持。郑抱入帏，极温款，并道眷恋。女呜咽，郑怜之，三日不敢问鼎。后郑强之，女不得已任郑，牢不可破。郑固伟男，遂驰骤焉，亦不可。于是降格以求，女竟开门以纳。噫，何前倨而后恭耶？郑问女以故，女曰：“奴小字改姑，盖石女子。自幼阴道绝，前曾适人，见弃。今蒙爱而娶焉，倘不为嫌，当屈体以奉，无所后悔。君必注念前好，妾诚逊谢不敏矣。”郑雅爱好，伉俪殊笃。
    弥月，其兄来淇，见妹有喜色，郑亦无他词，乃幸甚。女事母最孝，尝于母前欲为郑娶姬，母曰：“新媳妇老耶？几月不伏雌，便望儿子若眼穿。倘房中添一牝货，酸梅子入口，便要作切齿痛。”郑亦不肯，女隐为后嗣忧。

    Explanation

    236

    After more than a month, his brother sent him to the Qi River area, and then returned to Wei. On their wedding night, the woman cried uncontrollably. Zheng comforted her, showing great warmth and affection, and spoke of his fondness for her. She sobbed, and Zheng pitied her, refraining from intimacy for three days. Later, Zheng insisted, and the woman, unable to refuse, yielded to him, and they became inseparably bonded. Zheng, a strong and vigorous man, tried to rush things, but it was not possible. So, he compromised, and the woman eventually opened the door to him. Alas, why was she so arrogant before and so respectful now? Zheng asked her the reason, and she replied, "My nickname is Gai Gu, and I am a woman of stone. Since childhood, I have no vaginal passage. I was once married but was abandoned. Now, I am honored by your love and marriage. If you do not object, I will submit and serve you without regret. You must remember our past affection, and I humbly acknowledge my lack of skill." Zheng had a refined taste, and the couple was very affectionate.

    After more than a month, his brother came to the Qi River and saw his sister with a happy expression. Zheng also had no other words, and they were both very fortunate. The woman was filial to her mother and once wanted to help Zheng by suggesting he take a concubine. Her mother said, "Is a new wife too old? If she has not been satisfied for a few months, she will pine for a son. If you add a concubine to the household, it will be like putting a sour plum in your mouth, causing you great pain." Zheng also refused, and the woman secretly worried about the continuation of the family line.

    237

    女常供观音像，朝夕礼拜甚虔。日者有老尼至，女敬礼之，郑亦喜与女冠子谈，遂留斋供。尼曰：“有几公子？”女曰：“不育。”尼曰：“大娘子何以美而无子？”郑应之曰：“此卫人为之赋《硕人》也。”尼曰：“若然，我治之。”请间，女与尼入他室，出曰：“无伤也。石外也，非内也，尚可以疗。”诘旦携药来，令女入帏，以翎点药敷之，继以刀圭，曰：“觉痛楚否？”女曰：“不也。愿吾师施大法力，广为洞开。毋使一线蚕丛，致郎君又叹蜀道崎岖耳。”尼笑曰：“适可而止，想此中无并辔行者。覆以膏皮，留一小孔可以便溲。百日之后，客将入门。”而老尼不受谢，竟去。
    女谨奉教，郑移外寝。无何而瓜期及，郑为之揭门封。阖辟之间，已若有稚子候门也。女先固瘦怯不胜衣，自此而丰颐美颊，肌肤有余脂。两乳膨膨，如合覆二建磁钟。惟有双钩三寸，依然故我耳。二年生一子，名晚生。

    Explanation

    237

    The woman often worshipped the image of Guanyin, practicing diligently every morning and evening. One day, an old nun arrived, and the woman showed her great respect. Mr. Zheng was also pleased to talk with the nun and invited her to stay and be treated. The nun asked, "Do you have any sons?" The woman replied, "I have none." The nun then asked, "Why is the lady so beautiful but childless?" Mr. Zheng responded, "This is the Shuo Ren (硕人) that the people of Wei sang about." The nun said, "If so, I will treat it." She requested privacy, and the woman entered another room with the nun. When they came out, the nun said, "There is no harm. It is external, not internal, and can still be treated." The next day, she brought medicine and instructed the woman to enter a curtain. She applied the medicine with a feather, followed by a small amount of liquid, and asked, "Do you feel any pain?" The woman replied, "No. I hope my teacher can use great power to fully open it. Do not leave even a thread, so that my husband will not lament the difficult path to Shu." The nun smiled and said, "Stop at the right time, for there is no one who can ride side by side in this." She covered the area with a layer of skin, leaving a small hole for urination. After a hundred days, the guest will enter. The old nun did not accept thanks and left.

    The woman followed her instructions carefully, and Mr. Zheng moved to a separate room. Soon after, the time for the treatment was reached, and Mr. Zheng opened the door. As the door opened and closed, it seemed as if a young child was waiting at the door. The woman had previously been thin and weak, but now she had a full face and beautiful cheeks, with ample flesh. Her breasts were large, like two covered magnetic bells. Only her legs, three inches long, remained as they were. Two years later, she gave birth to a son named Wan Sheng.

    238

    郑尝与枕上谓女曰：“人生得意之遭，即伏于失意之内。忆吾两人桥头肆上，一盼留情。及至于归，入帐之夕，倘以卿故，一旦翻然弃置，卿复何辞，而吾亦未为失德。第使再续其弦，安知不仍脱其輹。如今日者，卿怜我，我复怜卿，是今之视昔，更甚于昔之视今。纵前后判若两途，彼此皆同一致。区区之情，恒有所固结而不可解，然后叹诚通变化，而心坚者之石与俱穿也。”郑终身不二色，夫妻偕焉。

    孙筠
    孙筠，掖县人。父宗南，住城北，业农。先以东村宋姓之女为筠定婚焉。宗南无行，好博，不数年家日落。宋家见孙贫，欲退婚，风示于孙。孙愤，将与较，复思鸣于官。孙妻讪其夫曰：“当自惭，何尤人？他家女岂肯来汝家受饿耶？”孙筠乃谏其父曰：“父莫较，儿自立成名后，何患无妻子耶？”父从此顿改前非，悉心正业。父力田，筠又力学。逾岁，筠十六，入邑庠。家复稍裕，遂于东村之东王姓女结褵。择日，筠冠服，行亲迎礼，鼓吹往过东村。

    Explanation

    238

    Zheng once said to his wife in bed, “The moments of success in life are often hidden within moments of failure. I remember the time we exchanged glances at the shop by the bridge. When we were married, on the night we entered the bridal chamber, if it were because of you that I suddenly turned away, what could you say, and I would not have acted improperly. Even if we were to continue our marriage, how can we be sure it wouldn’t still end in failure. But today, you love me, and I love you. The way we see each other now is even more intense than how we saw each other then. Even though our circumstances may seem different, our feelings remain the same. These deep emotions are always firmly rooted and unresolvable, and only then do we realize that true feelings can withstand change and even penetrate stone.” Zheng remained faithful to his wife throughout his life, and they lived together harmoniously.

    Sun Jun
    Sun Jun was from Yexian. His father, Zongnan, lived in the northern part of the city and worked as a farmer. Before, he had arranged for Sun Jun to marry a girl from the Cao family in Dongcun. Zongnan was not a man of good character; he loved gambling, and within a few years, the family’s fortunes declined. The Cao family saw Sun Jun’s poverty and wanted to break off the engagement, hinting at this to Sun Jun. Sun Jun was furious and wanted to confront them, but then he thought about taking the matter to court. His wife mocked him, saying, “You should feel ashamed of yourself, why blame others? Would a girl from another family come to your house to starve?” Sun Jun then advised his father, “Father, don’t be so upset. Once I establish myself and gain fame, what need will we have for a wife and children?” From then on, his father completely changed his ways and focused on his work. His father worked hard in the fields, and Sun Jun studied diligently. After a year, when Sun Jun was sixteen, he entered the local school. The family’s situation improved slightly, and so they arranged for Sun Jun to marry a girl from the Wang family in Dongcun. On the chosen day, Sun Jun put on his formal attire and performed the wedding ceremony, with music and drumming as he went to Dongcun to welcome his bride.

    239

    宋女固未许人，闻乐声，与其婢小曼出视。小曼识之，曰：“此新秀才筠孙郎也。向使主人翁不以孙郎一时贫穷，食言渝盟，姑姑今日岂不居然娘子耶？甚矣，善择者择高郎，不善择者择高房！”宋女怏怏归，向隅，泪荧荧如珠串，乱落襟袖。小曼复笑曰：“姑姑最是无用者。寡是哭，饶尔再哭一夜，人家女儿睡熟孙家炕矣。”宋女乃挽小曼手曰：“奈何？能为我划一谋乎？”小曼曰：“何难？三十六计，走为上策。姑趁此时，直抵孙家。要知咸阳先到者即是君。一臀坐下，他用十八金刚，也抬尔不出来。”宋女曰：“我羞，且不识路。”小曼曰：“姑敢行，我便保得将军去。”

    Explanation

    239

    The Song girl, who had not yet been betrothed, heard the music and went out with her maid Xiao Man to see. Xiao Man recognized him and said, "This is the new scholar Jun Sun Lang. Had the master not rejected Sun Lang due to his temporary poverty and broken his word, wouldn't you be a wife today? How great it is! Those who choose well choose a good husband, those who choose poorly choose a good house!" The Song girl returned home in a sullen mood, facing a corner, her tears like strings of pearls, falling haphazardly onto her sleeves. Xiao Man laughed and said, "You are the most useless person. If you cry, even if you cry all night, the daughter of another family will already be sleeping in Sun's bed." The Song girl then took Xiao Man's hand and said, "What shall we do? Can you help me with a plan?" Xiao Man replied, "What's difficult? Of the Thirty-Six Stratagems, fleeing is the best strategy. You should take this opportunity to go directly to Sun's house. You should know that the first to arrive in Xianyang will be you. Once you sit down, even if they use eighteen Iron Men, they won't be able to move you." The Song girl said, "I am ashamed, and I don't know the way." Xiao Man said, "If you dare to go, I will escort you there."

    240

    于是两人梳洗结束，蒙以青盖，内袭华衣，扎履结袜，悄步出村，竟投孙舍。遥见结彩于门，近之，则宾客列满。小曼携女昂然入，皆问谁家姑，小曼答曰：“看新妇者。”进内，孙父母见之。小曼扶女跪堂前，女乃泪盈花晕，羞泛春红，不作一语。小曼曰：“我姑宋女也，原许作孙郎妇。自主人退悔，我姑誓死不二。今闻孙郎另娶，我姑情极来归，望二老怜而收之。否则请死于孙氏之庭，以明我姑之心。”其言侃侃。女闻之大哭，伏不能起。孙父母喜，掖女曰：“此我佳妇也，毋庸悲。”

    Explanation

    240

    After the two women finished dressing, they were covered with a green canopy, wore fine clothing inside, tied up their shoes and socks, and stealthily stepped out of the village, finally arriving at Sun's house. From afar, they saw decorations at the door, and upon approaching, they found the hall filled with guests. Xiao Man led the girl in with a composed demeanor, and everyone asked who the aunt was. Xiao Man replied, "She is here to see the new bride." Upon entering, the parents of Sun Lang saw them. Xiao Man supported the girl and had her kneel in front of the hall. The girl's tears overflowed the flower-like blush on her cheeks, and shyness spread like spring red; she did not say a word. Xiao Man said, "I am Cui's daughter, originally promised to be Sun Lang's wife. After the host withdrew, my aunt swore never to change her mind. Now hearing that Sun Lang has remarried, my aunt comes back out of extreme emotion, hoping the two elders will pity and accept her. Otherwise, I will die in the courtyard of the Sun family, to show my aunt's heart." Her words were firm and resolute. The girl, upon hearing this, burst into tears and collapsed, unable to get up. The parents of Sun Lang were pleased and supported the girl, saying, "This is my good wife, there is no need to be sad."

    241

    俄而孙筠偕王女亦至，见已一女坐青庐中。父母告筠以故，筠亦喜。堂上贺客亲友，哄然皆喜，遂令二女皆成礼，称两大焉，但惟恐其不相能。三朝后，宋与王相敬爱，如亲姊若妹。于是小曼谓女曰：“东吴招赘已完，赵子龙当用不着。俟明年我姑诞儿，再来长坂坡抱阿斗耳。”欲去。宋女留之，商于王女，禀明父母，愿分一席以酬其劳。许之，纳为姬。宋父母陪送妆奁，登门赎罪。

    Explanation

    241

    Soon after, Sun Yun arrived together with the princess, and they saw another woman sitting in a simple tent. The parents explained the situation to Sun Yun, who was pleased. The guests and relatives in the hall were all delighted, and they proceeded to have the two women complete the marriage ceremony, celebrating their union. However, they were only concerned that the two might not get along. Three days later, Song and Wang treated each other with mutual respect and affection, like sisters. Then, Xiao Man said to the woman, "The match-making in Eastern Wu is complete, and Zhao Yun will no longer be needed. Wait until next year when my aunt gives birth to a child, and I will come back to Changban Slope to hold up little A Dou." She then wished to leave. The Song woman tried to keep her, and they discussed the matter with the princess, informing their parents, and wished to share a seat with her to repay her efforts. They agreed, and she was taken as a concubine. The Song parents provided a dowry and went to the door to make amends.

    242

    王姓父母以其女亚于宋，有怼意，而王女欲之，无间言。次年，二妻一妾皆孪生，得六子。会学师某生子，门生为汤饼酒。序坐，学师曰：“今日以子众多者首屈。”佥曰：“若然，孙生居上。年虽十七，有子六人。”师问故，相与缅述其事。
    (济宁许殿生，贾人也。娶妻，逾年春孪生二子，及冬又孪二子。盖一岁而得四男，亦奇也。)

    陈万言
    陈万言，清丰人。清虽下邑，交于直、豫之间，通衢大道，商贾往来不绝。万言居城，聘妻宁氏，居乡。陈有中户产，是年冬尽将婚。某日之夜，炬而亲迎。北俗婚期取岁尽者何？曰无忌禁也，又农商之隙也。夜而往者何？曰恐示人以朴，故多卜夜。亲迎者何？曰古礼也。贫者不能备彩舆，或驾牛车，蒙以猩毡，郎则马而前导。

    Explanation

    242

    A family with the surname Wang had their daughter marry into the C family, but the parents felt resentful about it. However, the Wang daughter wanted to marry into the C family and had no objections. The following year, their two wives and one concubine all gave birth to twins, resulting in six sons. Coincidentally, a schoolmaster's son was born, and his disciples prepared a feast of soup, noodles, and wine. Seated in order, the schoolmaster said, "Today, the one with the most children is the most prominent." They all agreed, "If so, then Sun's son should be ranked first. Although he is only seventeen, he has six sons." The teacher asked for the reason, and they recounted the story together.

    In Jining, there was a merchant named Xu Diansheng. He married a woman named Ning, who lived in the countryside. Xu had a middle-class household and was about to get married at the end of winter that year. On a certain night, he personally came to fetch his bride with a torch. Why did northern customs choose the end of the year for weddings? It was said that there were no taboos and it was a gap in farming and commerce. Why go at night? It was said that they wanted to avoid showing simplicity, so they often chose the night. Why personally fetch the bride? It was said that it was an ancient custom. Poor people could not afford a decorated carriage, so they drove an ox cart, covered with red felt, and the groom rode a horse and led the way.

    243

    陈至宁舍。如婚礼，出载其妻归。大雪，车中伴娘，先自陈家来者，俗呼之取女客，盖贱而非婢仆等——是日饮宁酒而醉，车行欲呕，不顾而唾。新妇恐其渍新衣，退诸后箱。车固无式楺木，时超乘度舆梁，辕仰新妇坠。前行者拥而奔，不知也。
    有豫人布客卞丰者，乘骡冒雪，遄归度除，遭女哭于途。卞下视之，新妇也，询以故。卞思欲送归追婿，则有北门之管；将归其女家，又无前路之征夫。弃之不可，送之何往？斯时为卞计者，惟有停骖待旦，相与株守，义也。而卞一转念则不然。乃诳之，掖妇上骑，卞随行。少而雪甚，遂欲与女并辔。女羞，不能却。卞喜，纵鞭七十里，抵家启户，曰：“得偕一新妇归。”家人固以为卞之新妇，而卞即亦居为己之新妇。彼新妇者，早已含颦于走马时矣，遂不贰焉。

    Explanation

    243

    Tchen Zhi Ning left his home. The event was conducted like a wedding, where he took his wife back. It was snowing heavily, and the women in the carriage, who had come from Tchen's home, were called "取女客" (qǔ nǚ kè), which means they were considered low-status but not servants. On that day, they drank Tchen's wine and got drunk, causing the carriage to lurch as they tried to vomit, and they spat without regard for the new bride. The new bride was afraid that the spittle would stain her new clothes, so she moved them to the back of the carriage. The carriage had no safety bars, and when the groom leaped onto the carriage, the front of the carriage lifted, causing the new bride to fall off. The people in front of the carriage rushed forward, not knowing what had happened.

    There was a man from Yu named Bian Feng, who was riding a donkey through the snow, hurrying home, and encountered a woman crying on the road. Bian Feng looked down and saw that it was the new bride, and he asked her why she was crying. Bian Feng thought about sending her back to find her husband, but he realized he had control over the situation; if he returned her to her family, there was no one to guide her. He could not abandon her, but he did not know where to send her. At that time, the only thing Bian Feng could do was to stop his carriage and wait until dawn, staying together with her, which was the right thing to do. But Bian Feng had a different idea. He deceived her, helped her onto his donkey, and they started walking together. After a while, the snow became heavier, and he wanted to ride side by side with the woman. The woman blushed and could not refuse. Bian Feng was happy, and he whipped the donkey for seventy miles, arriving at his home and opening the door, saying, "I have brought a new bride home with me." His family naturally assumed that the woman was his new bride, and Bian Feng also treated her as his new bride. The new bride, who had already frowned when they were riding together, remained steadfast in her decision.

    244

    卞无妻，有母多病，一妹十岁。宁氏能作家，事母抚妹颇任劳，夫妻笃爱。一日，宁氏至后园种豆苗，铲浮土，得二罂，皆白镪，可数千金。乃以其一告卞，家遂裕。
    当麦秋，卞贸归，辰出收获，见一人持镰卧地上，卞曰：“若何不为刈？”其人曰：“人皆外我，将不我佣。”询其里，曰：“清人。”卞曰：“清去我不远，何外之，盍为我佣？”其人随卞往。问其姓，曰陈。陈勤恳，人登一陇，而陈秀两歧。卞喜，厚而佣之。

    Explanation

    244

    Bi had no wife, but he had a sick mother and a ten-year-old sister. Ning, his wife, was capable of managing the household and served her mother and took care of her sister with great diligence. The couple had deep love for each other. One day, Ning went to the back garden to plant bean seedlings and while digging the soil, she found two jars, both filled with white silver, worth several thousand gold coins. She told Bi about it, and their family became prosperous.

    During the time of wheat harvest, Bi went out in the morning to harvest. He saw a man lying on the ground with a sickle. Bi asked, "Why don't you cut the wheat?" The man replied, "Everyone treats me as an outsider, and they won't hire me." When Bi asked where he was from, the man said, "I am from Qing." Bi said, "Qing is not far from here. Why do they treat you as an outsider? Why don't you work for me?" The man followed Bi home. When asked his name, he said Chen. Chen was diligent, and when others climbed one ridge, Chen showed two ears of grain. Bi was pleased and hired him at a good wage.

    245

    卞思茨屋，欲致墁师，陈曰：“无庸，我能之。”是日亭午，宁氏黹于窗前，陈则茅于阶下。宁听其言，如清之声，问曰：“尔何许人？”陈曰：“清人也。”宁曰：“有陈万言，识之否？”陈笑曰：“佣也。何知佣名？”宁曰：“我宁氏之瓜葛也。闻尔娶妻而失妻，有诸？”陈叹曰：“惟其然，而佣之所以有今日也。当时娶妇，归失妇，我以为宁之匿；而宁之女归无女，又以为我之害之也。我仇宁，宁复冤我；鸣之于官，两姓被絷，终不能结，遂悬其案。迄今事隔五年，官经四任，与其疑而不解，何如疑而释之。乃告宁家，情甘罢讼。而我家落，宁氏亦贫。”宁曰：“尔今犹与宁氏仇否？”陈曰：“今两无欺隐，固耦俱而无猜矣。”宁曰：“我久不通宁氏母，欲假尔作寄书邮，曷往焉？”陈曰：“惟命是听。”宁即封布函，有物累累付之。给川资，并具糗粮往。卞归问，宁曰：“伊连日欲归，酬之不受而去。”

    Explanation

    245

    卞 was planning to build or repair his house and intended to hire a plasterer. Chen said, "There's no need; I can do it myself." That day at noon, Ning was working at her loom in front of a window, while Chen was working with straw at the base of the stairs. Hearing her words, Ning asked, "Who are you?" Chen replied, "I am from Qing." Ning said, "Do you know Teng Wanyan?" Chen laughed and said, "I am just a commoner. How could I know the name of the servant?" Ning said, "I am related to the Ning family. I heard that you lost your wife after marrying her, is that true?" Chen sighed and said, "Indeed, that is why I am in this situation today. At the time, when I married a woman, she disappeared, and I thought the Ning family had hidden her; but when the Ning family's daughter returned without a wife, I suspected that they had harmed her. I harbored resentment against the Ning family, and they, in turn, felt wronged by me. We took our dispute to the authorities, and both families were detained, but we could not resolve the matter, and it remained unresolved. By now, five years have passed, and four officials have taken office. It would be better to doubt and resolve the matter rather than doubt and not resolve it. I informed the Ning family, and we both willingly agreed to drop the lawsuit. As a result, my family fell into poverty, and the Ning family also became poor." Ning asked, "Are you still harboring resentment against the Ning family?" Chen said, "Now, there is no deceit or concealment between us, and we both trust each other." Ning said, "I have not communicated with the Ning family's mother for a long time. I want to entrust you to deliver a letter to her. Where should I go?" Chen said, "I will do as you command." Ning immediately sealed a cloth envelope, filled it with various items, and gave it to Chen. She provided him with travel expenses and provisions. When Bian returned, Ning said, "He has been wanting to return for several days, but he refused payment and left."

    246

    陈归途饥，掰糗以啖，中馅一金，三掰如是，不之解。抵宁氏，呈书而告。宁母疑，拆其函，金之外，则其女当时受陈氏钗也。宁父母乃往卞，见女抱头痛哭，寻卞争詈，卞不敢出。而陈复诣卞，汹汹四起，讼将兴矣。卞惴惴无所计出。

    Explanation

    246

    Chen, on his way back, was hungry and broke some dried food to eat, finding a piece of gold inside three times but not understanding its significance. Upon arriving at the Ning family, he presented a letter and reported the matter. The Ning mother became suspicious, opened the letter, and found out that the gold was part of a hairpin that her daughter had received from the Chen family. The Ning parents then went to the Bian family, where they saw the daughter crying and hugging her head. They searched for the Bian family and argued, cursing them, but the Bian family was too afraid to respond. Chen then went to the Bian family again, causing a chaotic situation, and a lawsuit was about to be initiated. The Bian family felt anxious and unable to find a solution.

    247

    宁氏于是乃请父母及卞母、卞丰、陈万言咸集于庭而言曰：“我为宁氏女，今为卞氏妇。既为卞氏妇，则不得复为陈氏妻。当女之适陈也，陈实弃女；女之归卞也，天实与卞。至若乘危于昏暮之间，要之而去，则卞之咎所难辞。然而以尔车来，胡为乎泥中？是陈之自失也实甚。今即鼠牙雀角，官断前归，而女守从一之义，虽速讼，而必不汝从陈，将奈何？为今之计，父母以女故家落，女愿以金为父母赎产。陈万言亦以女故遭家不造，迄今未娶。卞丰有妹，我姑也，今迨吉，将以适陈而偿，我更以五百金为之奁。由是姻娅相通，嫌疑尽释。虽曰人为，岂非天道。不然者，讼者终凶也。请以质之三老。”卞惧及祸，宁利其金，陈乐得偶，遂皆从之。于是卞拜陈，陈复拜卞。女乃出其半藏之罂，分宁及陈。后其妹归陈之日，其兄从之。宁谓卞曰：“往送之家，毋使人马上得之也。”

    Explanation

    247

    The Ning family then asked her parents, her mother-in-law, her brother-in-law, and her brother-in-law's wife to gather in the courtyard and said, "I was a daughter of the Ning family, now I am a wife of the Bian family. Since I am now a wife of the Bian family, I cannot be a wife of the Chen family again. When I married into the Chen family, they truly abandoned me; when I returned to the Bian family, Heaven truly favored the Bian family. If they took advantage of the danger during the twilight hours and forcibly took me away, then the Bian family would bear great blame. However, 'Your carriage comes, why are you stuck in the mud?' This shows that the Chen family truly lost themselves. Now, if there is a minor dispute, the official will rule in favor of the Bian family, and I will adhere to the principle of fidelity. Even if you quickly sue, I will certainly not follow you to the Chen family. What can you do? For the present situation, my parents' family has fallen because of me. I wish to use gold to redeem their property. Chen Wan Yan also suffered because of me and has not yet married. My brother-in-law has a sister, who is my aunt. On the auspicious day, she will marry Chen Wan Yan as compensation, and I will provide a dowry of five hundred gold. Thus, the families will be connected through marriage, and all suspicions will be resolved. If it is done by human effort, it is not contrary to the will of Heaven. Otherwise, those who sue will ultimately suffer. Please let the elders decide." The Bian family feared the consequences, the Ning family benefited from the gold, and the Chen family was happy to find a match. They all agreed. Then the Bian family bowed to the Chen family, and the Chen family bowed back to the Bian family. The woman then took half of her dowry and divided it between the Ning and Chen families. Later, on the day her sister married into the Chen family, her brother accompanied her. The Ning family said to the Bian family, "Go to send her home, but do not let anyone take her away on horseback."

    248

    幽宫诗
    粤东惠来滨海，渔盐辐辏之区。城南有岳庙，最壮丽。两廊塑像，作十殿阎罗天子，狞恶骇人。夜叉急脚，以及刀锯鼎镬，无不咄咄逼人，虽图画传神，不能至此。每至春夏赛会，乡城男女，愿献楮帛，焚积如山。殿旁有石兽，角端土人皆割牲滴血其上，石中猩红，经岁不干。
    邑中翁姓，富甲一城。长女名如珠，初生时，其母梦黑龙绕其身。年十三，白皙娉婷，性慧识字，即为吟咏声。后工诗，其《送春》诗有云：“彩笔堪题肠断句，柳丝难系落花魂。”其序四六云：“缅飞絮之随风，仿佛真魂飘荡；妒落花之时雨，依稀血泪缤纷。”其兄贸易湘潭，伊书促归，中有一行云：“愿化衡山之石，雁使回归；因呼粤岭之禽，哥行不得。”皆佳。

    Explanation

    248

    Poem of the Secluded Palace

    In the eastern part of Guangdong, Hui Lai by the sea, a place where fishing and salt production converge. To the south of the city stands a magnificent temple of Mount Tai. The side halls are adorned with statues of the Ten Kings of Hell, each one terrifying and awe-inspiring. Night demons with swift feet, along with swords, saws, cauldrons, and tripods, all confront the viewer with their intensity. Even if the paintings were lifelike, they could not match the realism of these statues. During the spring and summer festivals, both rural and urban men and women offer paper money and silk, burning piles as high as mountains. Beside the temple, there is a stone statue; local people cut animals and drip blood on it, leaving a deep red stain that does not dry out for years.

    In the city, the wealthy Wang family is the richest. Their eldest daughter, named Ru Zhu, was born when her mother dreamed of a black dragon coiling around her body. At thirteen, she was fair-skinned and graceful, intelligent and literate, already reciting poetry. Later, she became skilled in poetry. Her poem "Sending Off Spring" includes the lines: "Her colorful brush can write heart-wrenching lines, willow branches cannot bind the soul of fallen flowers." In her four-six prose, she writes: "Remembering the flying catkins following the wind, it seems like true spirits are drifting; envious of the rain falling on falling flowers, it looks like blood and tears are scattered." When her brother was trading in Xiangtan, she wrote to him urging him to return, with one line saying: "Wishing to transform into a stone on Mount Heng, the goose messenger returns; calling out to the birds of Yue Mountains, brother cannot go." All of her poetry is excellent.

    249

    年十五，随母诣岳庙，游两廊间。至转轮王殿，诸娣姊皆游观，如珠手指王像曰：“如此狰狞王，夫人朝夕对之，不栗生畏怖耶？”其娣云：“汝畏之，夜间即来娶汝。”如珠答云：“王如欲之，我何畏彼哉！”及归家，病，既乃大渐，百药罔效。忽自语云：“越三日癸丑，我当入宫，拜受采纳矣。”众以为谵。至期，如珠令人为之薰沐，着新衣，辞父母曰：“儿已为冥府王妃。外舆从久驾，儿不敢稽。从此侯门似海，膝下长辞。”父母始惊骇哀泣。如珠曰：“无过伤痛。今儿作王侯妃，充六宫，班九嫔，岂比作田舍郎媳妇，尚烦二老体恤耶？”乃自吟曰：“大邦有子，遵彼海滨。窈窕淑女，曰嫔九京。”言讫而逝。

    Explanation

    249

    At the age of fifteen, Luzhu accompanied her mother to her husband's ancestral temple, wandering between the two corridors. Upon reaching the Hall of the Turning Wheel King, all her sisters-in-law were also sightseeing. Luzhu pointed at the image of the king and said, "Such a ferocious king, does one not tremble with fear when facing him every day and night?" One of her sisters-in-law replied, "You are afraid of him, he will come to marry you at night." Luzhu responded, "If the king desires it, why should I fear him?" When she returned home, she fell ill, and then her condition worsened greatly, with all medicines being ineffective. Suddenly, she spoke to herself and said, "In three days, on the day of Gui Chou, I will enter the palace, receive the embrace and acceptance." Everyone thought she was delirious. On the appointed day, Luzhu ordered someone to bathe and perfume her, and she wore new clothes, bidding farewell to her parents and saying, "I have become the queen of the underworld. The outer carriage has been waiting for a long time, I dare not delay. From now on, the gate of the noble family is like the sea, farewell to my parents' knees." Her parents were startled and wept. Luzhu said, "Do not grieve too much. Now I am the queen of the nobles, filling the six palaces, ranking among the nine consorts. How could it compare to being the wife of a country bumpkin, still troubling my parents with their concerns?" She then recited, "A great state has a daughter, who follows to the seaside. A graceful and virtuous lady, who becomes a consort in the nine graves." After finishing her words, she passed away.

    250

    三年，其父病危，复苏，告其妻云：“我夫妻皆增寿一纪。儿果为转轮王妃，群下左右，请位中宫。一年之间，实辅转成君德，燮理阴政。幽囚犯科严重，传其脱簪侯门，进谏不已，今以摩利才人充嫔。一人见嫉，预政挠权，如楚王郑褎故事。乃幽儿于别宫，抑郁以死。后宫中魙告祟，王悔之，乃知其冤。乃下摩利而封儿，号金轮阿耨夫人。祀以壤田，建庙于酆山之阴，凡后之族，赐寿一纪。”犹忆其传诵幽宫诗八绝云：
    阴霾穗帐旧时容，禁阁重重马鬣封。听彻森罗宫殿外，更无人撞景阳钟。
    伤心遥拜九泉恩，永诀双亲一缕魂。河满曲终肠寸断，谁知地府有长门。
    城开枉死能容罪，殿少长生不种缘。安得成都人作赋，也应输与纸衡钱。
    飒飒凄风入苑来，歌声乱逐鬼声哀。冰寒彻骨桃笙冷，知是君王宴夜台。
    白玉楼头望碧潺，黄泉水绕奈河湾。弃捐秋草埋幽径，采卷如登嵩里山。

    Explanation

    250

    Three years later, his father fell gravely ill and then revived, telling his wife: "Both my wife and I have had our lifespans extended by a decade. If my son becomes the queen of the Wheel King, the officials and attendants will request that she be installed in the central palace. Within a year, she will truly assist in cultivating the king's virtues and managing the internal affairs of the state. Those who commit serious crimes will be imprisoned in dark dungeons, and it is said that she removed her hairpin and entered the noble gate to offer repeated advice. Now she is being made a concubine as the Moli Talent. One person envied her, sought to interfere in politics, and disrupt power, just as in the story of King Chu and Zheng You. She was then imprisoned in a separate palace, where she died of depression. Ghosts in the harem began to cause disturbances, and the king regretted it, realizing her injustice. He demoted Moli and enfeoffed my son, giving him the title of Golden Wheel Anu Lady. He offered sacrifices with fertile land and built a temple on the north side of Feng Mountain, and all descendants of the queen were granted an additional decade of life." I still remember the eight quatrains about the dark palace that were passed down:

    Dark, gloomy curtains of the old palace, the heavily guarded palace gates, sealed like a horse's mane. The sound of the palace gates being heard outside the palace, no one strikes the Jingyang bell anymore.

    Grieving, she pays respects from afar to the kindness of the underworld, farewell to her parents, her soul as a single thread. The river of sorrow ends, but the heart is broken, who knows if there is a long gate in the underworld?

    The city opens, but wrongful deaths can still be forgiven, the palace is short of long life, but it does not lack the seeds of virtue. How can the people of Chengdu write an ode, they should also offer paper money in exchange.

    The cold, sad wind enters the garden, the songs are confused and follow the sad sounds of ghosts. The ice is cold and penetrates the bones, the peach wood is cold, knowing that the king is feasting in the night hall.

    From the top of the jade pavilion, looking out at the green stream, the yellow spring water winds around the Naihe River. Abandoned in the autumn grass, buried in a secluded path, the scrolls are like ascending Mount Songli.

    251

    百结云鬟内样妆，茜红衫子带鹅黄。轻盈舞罢旋风阵，羞比昭阳掌上狂。
    溶溶默默惨无神，点点幽情诉未真。一片琉璃帘外影，姗然自认李夫人。
    曾无月色到深宫，燐火光微辇路空。回首木棉花下住，沙塘箫鼓画桥东。
    (七如氏曰：如珠之事，固属荒诞。但寺庙为僧道所居，众目所睹，且轻儇子弟更于此处窥探调笑，讥刺品题，如蜮如狂，凌犯拥挤，无所不至。此时隐忍受辱，惟有落牙自咽而已。今浙省之游天竺云林，每至春月，无不如云逐队；虽夫不能止其妻，母亦不能禁其女。更有吾乡无知妇女，相聚结社朝山，或金鼎、或东岳、或南海普陀，跋涉数千里，杂沓数十昼夜。其中恣性越礼，又岂笔墨所能罄。作者记转轮王一段，盖犹有忠厚之微旨也夫！
    按律载：官及军官之家，纵令妇女入庙烧香者，笞四十；无夫、男者，罪坐本妇；住持不禁止者，与同坐罪。而妇女不知犯法，反以为祈福。吾曾见一秀才妻登峄山，其夫亲扶掖之，恬不为怪云。)

    Explanation

    251

    A woman with a complex, knotted hairstyle and makeup, dressed in a bright red shirt and pale yellow skirt, gracefully danced in a whirlwind formation, modestly refusing to compare herself to the extravagant dancers of the palace. She was silent and lacked vitality, her deep, unspoken feelings not fully expressed. Her reflection in the crystal curtain was graceful, and she recognized herself as a courtesan. There was no moonlight in the deep palace, only a faint glow of phosphorescence on the empty road. She looked back at the place where she once lived, near the sandy pond and the painted bridge where flutes and drums were played.

    (Commentary by SevenLike: The story of Lady Zhu is indeed fantastical. However, temples are places where monks and Taoists reside, and they are open to public view. Moreover, young and playful individuals often gather there to spy, joke, and criticize, causing chaos and congestion. At such times, one must endure humiliation silently, swallowing one's pride. Nowadays, in Zhejiang Province, pilgrimages to Tianzhu and Yunlin temples are common, especially during the spring months, when people travel in large groups. Even husbands cannot stop their wives, and mothers cannot prevent their daughters. There are also many ignorant women in my hometown who form associations to climb mountains, visiting places like the Golden Cauldron, Mount Tai, or the Southern Sea (Potala), traveling thousands of miles and spending dozens of days and nights. Among them, there are many who act recklessly and violate social norms, which cannot be fully described in writing. The author's account of the Wheel-Turning King has a subtle and sincere message.

    According to the law: If officials or military officers allow their wives to enter temples to burn incense, they will be lashed forty times. If the woman is unmarried or widowed, the punishment falls on her. If the abbot of the temple does not prevent it, he will be punished along with the woman. Women do not know they are breaking the law and instead believe it brings good fortune. I once saw a scholar's wife climb Mount Yi, and her husband personally helped her, showing no surprise.)

    252

    郑让
    郑让，字耐村，利津人。无兄弟，父母钟爱之。美丰仪，又慧，十五应童子试，郡中游。过平康，见妓心荡，晚潜往妓家宿。招覆，师觅之不得。将曙，让始至，考棚已封门。师以书贻其父，让母曰：“儿大矣，当婚。”聘马氏女。是年，让入学，遂婚矣。
    先是女在闺中，尝蓄一婢，将出阁，力遣去。凡见庭花新摘色艳者，必手揉碎之以为快意。合卺后，夫妻若胶漆。一日，马见让之溺器乌啄而长项，恶之，熔化成饼。夜，让求之弗得，乃用女器，自此让并不敢与妻异溺器。让斋中挂一仇实父美人图，马见之辄痛心，裂之如糜，疾遂瘳。让后不得就外寝，渐至出必告、反必面焉。每有所事出，马以如意簪点胭脂印其要处，如守宫砂，归而验。稍不符，便穷诘研问，至再至三。不数年，妻之焰日以张，让之气日以馁。让愈防检，而过愈丛积，几不可支。为翁姑者劝之，马怒曰：“汝养子不教，我为汝约束，不德我，反仇我耶？”

    Explanation

    252

    Vang Rang
    Vang Rang, courtesy name Nai Cun, was from Lijin. He had no siblings, and his parents doted on him. He was handsome and intelligent, and at the age of fifteen, he took the examination for young scholars, traveling around the prefecture. Passing through the pleasure district, he was captivated by the sight of prostitutes, and at night, he secretly went to their homes to stay. The teacher searched for him but could not find him. Just before dawn, Vang Rang returned, but the examination hall had already closed. The teacher wrote a letter to his father, and Vang Rang's mother said, "Your son is grown, it is time for him to marry." She arranged for him to marry a girl from the Ma family. That year, Vang Rang was admitted to school and subsequently married.

    Before the wedding, the bride had a maid whom she insisted on dismissing before leaving home. Whenever she saw newly picked, brightly colored flowers in the courtyard, she would crush them by hand to find pleasure in doing so. After their wedding night, the couple were inseparable, like glue and lacquer. One day, the bride saw Vang Rang's urinal, which she found repulsive due to its black, elongated shape, and melted it into a cake. At night, when Vang Rang sought it, he could not find it and had to use hers, and from then on, he did not use a separate one. In Vang Rang's study, there was a painting of a beautiful woman by Qiu Ying, and whenever the bride saw it, she would tear it up in distress, and her illness would subside. After that, Vang Rang was not allowed to sleep in the outer chamber, and gradually, he had to inform her before leaving and returning. Whenever he went out, the bride would mark his body with a red mark using a hairpin, like a vermilion mark, and check upon his return. If there was any discrepancy, she would interrogate him repeatedly, sometimes multiple times. Within a few years, the bride's dominance grew stronger, while Vang Rang's confidence waned. The more Vang Rang tried to be cautious, the more mistakes he made, and he could barely cope. His in-laws tried to mediate, but the bride angrily said, "You did not teach your son well, and I am the one who is disciplining him. Do you not appreciate my efforts, and instead blame me?"

    253

    一日，马忽持剪入翁室，欲阉其翁，盖以翁与姑犹有童心，恐其生子析产也。后翁姑夜寝，必严锢其户。让由是狼狈滋甚。父母亲戚，咸为之忧，让固恬然安之。让周身之针孔、爪痕、烙斑、齿伤，多人时令脱以相示，凡百余处，未尝不为之指瘢太息。而让反似三国吴大帝奖周泰军功，以为得意，恨不诸公满浮大白也。尝于妻前读《石崇传》，至绿珠坠楼一节，拍案曰：“妇人能如是，一斛珠不足多也。”马曰：“绿珠何以独有千古？”让不敢对。马氏遂登楼，一跃及地，救之起，左腿已折。让是科中乡榜。马闻捷，哭之七日。人问之，马曰：“吾闻贵易交，富易妻。田舍翁得十斛麦，尚欲易妇；今郎君贵，必多金，能保其不置姬妾乎？”

    Explanation

    253

    One day, Ma suddenly entered the old man's room with scissors, intending to castrate him. This was because the old man and his wife still had a childlike heart, and they feared that if they had a son, the property would be divided. Afterward, when the old man and his wife slept at night, they always locked their door tightly. Thus, Let became increasingly embarrassed and distressed. His parents and relatives were all worried about him, but Let remained calm and content. Let showed his body, which was full of needle holes, claw marks, burn scars, and tooth marks. Whenever there were many people, he would take off his clothes to show them, and he had over a hundred such marks. They never failed to point out his scars and sigh. But Let was like Emperor Wu of Wu in the Three Kingdoms period, who praised Zhou Tai's military achievements and thought it was a great honor, wishing that all the guests would drink a full cup together. Once, in front of his wife, he read the biography of Shi Chong, and when he came to the part where Lu Zhu fell from the tower, he pounded the table and said, "If a woman could do that, even a hundred measures of pearls wouldn't be enough." Ma said, "Why does Lu Zhu have a place in history alone?" Let dared not answer. Ma then climbed up the tower and jumped down to the ground, saving her, but her left leg was broken. Let passed the provincial examination in this year. Ma cried for seven days upon hearing the news. People asked her, and she said, "I heard that when someone becomes wealthy, they change friends, and when they become rich, they change wives. Even a farmer who gets ten shi of wheat would still want to change his wife. Now that my husband is wealthy, he must have a lot of money, can you guarantee that he won't take concubines?"

    254

    当北上之日，送诸南浦，要以盟誓而还。让乃发轫。抵都，寓旅邸。邻有闽人伊某，身小而须微。与之谈，蔼如也，渐来往密。让每过伊舍，闻其后有女子声，让问伊曰：“宝眷亦在京耶？”伊曰：“非也。客中寂寞，新购得一裹头奴耳。”遂令其出拜让，奉茗。郑伊两人颇称相得，谈及郑尚无子，伊曰：“吾观君须眉表表，未必即龙眠居士。况燕赵颇有佳丽，何不置一小星为后嗣计？”其妾亦耸郑曰：“两家由此同住，朝夕相聚甚好。倘郑公旅囊羞涩，妾愿拔钗以助。”让踌躇曰：“兄爱我，弟非忘情。但家室悍毒甚，恐不相容。

    Explanation

    254

    On the day of his northward journey, he was sent off at the southern bank of a river, and they made a solemn oath to return. Then, he set out. Upon arriving in the capital, he stayed in a temporary lodging. His neighbor was a person from Fujian named Yi, who was short and had sparse beard. Conversations with him were pleasant and cordial, and their relationship gradually became closer. Whenever the narrator visited Yi's residence, he heard the sound of a woman's voice coming from behind the house. The narrator asked Yi, "Are your family members also in the capital?" Yi replied, "No. I am lonely while away from home, and I recently bought a head-wrapping servant." He then had the servant come out to greet the narrator and serve tea. The two (presumably the narrator and Yi) got along quite well. When they discussed the fact that the narrator still had no children, Yi said, "I observe that you have a distinguished appearance. You might be someone of similar distinction to the famous calligrapher and painter. Besides, there are many beautiful women in the regions of Yan and Zhao. Why not take a concubine or mistress to ensure the continuation of the family line?" His wife also encouraged the narrator, saying, "If the two households live together, spending time together every day would be very good. If your travel funds are tight, I am willing to remove my hairpin to help financially." The narrator hesitated and said, "You love me, but I am not being unfeeling. However, my home is very harsh and unforgiving, and there is fear that it would not be tolerated."

    255

    ”伊曰：“千里之外，嫂夫人鞭长莫及也。”郑素困于阃闼，不敢纵。今如离鞲之鹰，脱网之鱼，加之伊又预成其事，宁复计及褰裳捉跪时哉！遂买一姬，王姓。无何试毕。榜落，让故迟迟吾行。伊曰：“归计可决，长安居不易。”让不得已，泣告背盟之故，欲久客以避其锋。伊曰：“是谋非我所敢许也。夫父母桑梓之地，祖宗依恋之邦，一旦轻弃其乡，以糊其口于四方，安见其可以图存？即尊阃有刻眉之行，亦且尚无其事，又何必未来逆料，先以不肖待人哉！兄请偕丽人归，余不日摒挡，便道造访。万一果有别故，到时我自有安排法。”让始允，复谆属伊速来，遂握别。

    Explanation

    255

    伊说：“千里之外，嫂夫人鞭长莫及也。”郑素被困于闺房之中，不敢放纵。如今如同离鞲之鹰，脱网之鱼，再加上伊已经预谋此事，又何必计较褰裳捉跪之时呢！于是买了一位姬妾，姓王。不久考试结束。榜上无名，让因此迟迟不肯动身。伊说：“归计已定，长安居住不易。”让不得已，哭泣着告诉伊背叛盟约的原因，打算长期客居以避开锋芒。伊说：“这样的计谋我不敢答应。父母桑梓之地，祖宗依恋之邦，一旦轻易放弃家乡，四处流浪谋生，怎能保证可以生存下去？即使尊阃有刻眉之行，也尚未发生，又何必预先料想，先以不肖待人呢！兄请偕同丽人回去，我不久就会准备妥当，顺便拜访。万一果真有其他缘故，到时候我自己有安排的办法。”让这才同意，再次叮嘱伊尽快前来，于是握手告别。

    256

    让抵家，尚十余里，谕其仆勿泄，先自独归。妻瞥见，诈之曰：“汝在京中干得好事！”让失色，莫知措词。妻乃拷问，让以实对。妻大怒，挞让无完肤。继以带系让手项，幽于帐后净所，曰：“汝作此大孽，当永堕恶道地狱，再无见天日之期！”父母以其自都返，欲见之，问其妇，马曰：“若犯罪，在狴牢中，不必探视。”无如之何，父母惟有长叹数声而已。马欲刺其新买之姬，家人乃匿诸邻屋。时有至戚某，知其事，为之记曰：秋七月，郑子偕王姬归自京。君子曰：“不度德，不量力，其以桎梏死也固宜。”

    Explanation

    256

    Liang arrived home, but was still more than ten miles away. He instructed his servant not to reveal, and first returned alone. His wife caught sight of him and pretended to say, "What good deeds did you do in the capital?" Liang turned pale, not knowing what to say. The wife then questioned him, and Liang answered truthfully. The wife became greatly angry, and beat Liang until he had no unblemished skin. She then tied Liang's hands and neck with a rope, and confined him in the clean area behind the curtain, saying, "You have committed such a great sin, you should fall forever into the evil path and hell, never to see the light of day again!" His parents, thinking he had returned from the capital, wanted to see him. They asked his wife, who said, "If he has committed a crime, he is in prison, there is no need to visit." There was nothing they could do, so his parents could only sigh a few times. Ma wanted to kill her newly bought concubine, and the family hid her in a neighbor's house. At that time, a close relative named someone, who knew the matter, wrote a record saying: In the seventh month of autumn, Zheng Zi accompanied the princess back from the capital. A gentleman said, "Not measuring virtue, not considering strength, it is indeed fitting that he should die in shackles."

    257

    十日，伊至，叩扉，郑父母见之。伊欲见让，马闻而出，即詈伊。伊于袖中出一木杵击妇，仆地，跛而奔。伊入郑室，褰帏见让，如楚囚不敢仰视。伊呼出，让曰：“君祸我矣！奈何劫之，以加吾罪？”伊曳其衣曰：“有我在。”郑出书舍，父母始得见之，环而泣。伊问：“王姬安在？”家人不敢言。伊曰：“速令之来。”姬至，见郑及父母，以礼。晚，郑不敢与姬私语。伊又壮之，乃择别室而居。郑以为其妻必于是夕枕戈而待也。三日而妻无诟詈声。郑不安，入室视马。见而泣，既而絮絮，故态复作，郑复长跪床前。家人飞告伊，伊曰：“吾以牧马者将不敢南下矣，竟复猖獗乃尔！”伊持杵入，将及门，妇股栗缩榻间。伊曰：“泼悍尚不悛改，当挞杀汝！”乃以目视郑，郑起，随伊出，举家德伊。

    Explanation

    257

    Ten days later, Yi arrived, knocking on the door, and Zheng's parents saw him. Yi wanted to see Rang, but Ma heard and came out, immediately cursing Yi. Yi took a wooden club from his sleeve and hit the woman, causing her to fall to the ground, limp, and run away. Yi entered Zheng's room, lifted the curtain to see Rang, who looked like a prisoner from Chu and dared not look up. Yi called out, and Rang said, "You have brought me trouble! Why did you take advantage of me, adding to my guilt?" Yi pulled his clothes and said, "I am here." Zheng left the study, and his parents finally saw him, crying all around. Yi asked, "Where is Wang Ji?" The family dared not speak. Yi said, "Quickly bring her." Wang Ji arrived, saw Zheng and his parents, and greeted them respectfully. At night, Zheng dared not speak privately with Wang Ji. Yi encouraged her and chose a separate room for her to live in. Zheng thought his wife would certainly be waiting with a sword under her pillow that night. After three days, the wife had no more curses. Zheng felt uneasy, went into the room to see Ma. He saw her and cried, then she began to talk incessantly, returning to her old ways, and Zheng knelt at the foot of the bed again. The family quickly informed Yi, who said, "I thought the shepherd would not dare to go south anymore, but he has become even more outrageous!" Yi entered with the club, almost at the door, and the woman trembled and cowered on the bed. Yi said, "You are still not repentant, and I will beat you to death!" He looked at Zheng with his eyes, and Zheng got up, following Yi out, and the whole family praised Yi.

    258

    一日伊欲去，郑与父母恐其复发，苦留之，伊曰：“一击后永断妒根。”众不之信。伊乃取杵付郑，曰：“君其宝之！倘河东复吼，持之可当金锁。”伊遂去。让谨受而藏之椟。后马果异从前，相安载余，而此杵庋之高阁，未尝复用。忽一日伊至，仓皇失措，如有急难。郑延入甫坐，伊曰：“无暇他说，速还我杵。”郑即付伊。问何以匆遽若此，伊曰：“君有不知。我非人，本狐侠也，尝为人间报不平。因君困于妒妇，恐斩君嗣，于神库中窃得周文王后妃娘娘浣衣杵，又名化妒捶，为君制奇祸。今库中失此镇物，入宫见嫉，憎及蛾眉。缙绅显宦，以至首善之区，其风大振，几有不可扑灭之势。上帝震怒，访缉窃杵之盗，急不可待，故来取以归还耳。”言讫不见。而郑一妻一妾，终身无复间言，俱产一子。问马前事，每颜厚云。

    Explanation

    258

    One day, he wanted to leave. Zheng and his parents were afraid that the situation would recur, so they tried hard to keep him. He said, "After one strike, the root of jealousy will be permanently severed." The crowd did not believe him. He then took the pestle and gave it to Zheng, saying, "You should treasure it! If the Eastern River roars again, holding it can serve as a golden lock." He then left. Zheng carefully accepted it and stored it in a box. Later, the horse indeed became different from before, and they lived in harmony and carried on. This pestle was placed on a high shelf and was never used again. Suddenly, one day, he arrived, in a state of panic and confusion, as if in urgent difficulty. Zheng invited him in and he just sat down. He said, "No time for other things, quickly return my pestle." Zheng immediately gave it to him. He asked why he was in such a hurry, and he said, "You don't know. I am not a human, I am originally a fox hero. I have often reported injustices in the human world. Because you are trapped by a jealous wife, I fear that your descendants will be cut off, so I stole the washing pestle of the empress of King Wen of Zhou from the divine treasury. It is also known as the jealousy-transforming pestle, and I created a strange disaster for you. Now the divine treasury has lost this stabilizing object, and entering the palace, it is envied, even the beautiful women are hated. High-ranking officials, even the most virtuous regions, the trend is greatly prevalent, almost to the point of being impossible to extinguish. The Heavenly Emperor is furious, investigating the thief who stole the pestle, impatiently waiting, so I came to take it back." After speaking, he disappeared. And Zheng had one wife and one concubine, for the rest of their lives, there were no more disputes, both gave birth to one son. They asked about the previous events, each time, they said with a thick face.

    259

    (近日狮吼大盛，安得此杵遍及人间？[七如])

    少霞
    晋沁水孝廉张本义，年七十，妻早死。为临安太守，精神强干，有班伯黄霸之目。一子，名成，粗鄙近利。公颇无舐犊爱，盖知其弗克负荷也。
    公有婢少霞，年十七，简静而文，常侍公左右。一日春阑无事，雨过阶除，公忽忆去年有人赠碧凤仙子，命少霞出之。公启缄见其稗落，恐不能萌蘖。少霞曰：“但趁此一块好润土，可毋论其隔年种也。”公感其言，遂纳之。逾年生一子，命名复。郡僚绅士，皆为公作汤饼，送洗儿钱。独其子揶揄之，以为老蚌生珠，恐未必然。

    Explanation

    259

    A recent lion's roar has grown very strong, how can this mallet spread throughout the world? [Seven Like]

    Shao Xia
    Zhang Benyi, a scholar-official from Jin Province's Qinzhou, was seventy years old, and his wife had died early. He served as the governor of Lin'an, with strong spirit and capable governance, akin to Ban Bo and Huang Ba. He had one son named Cheng, who was crude and close to profit. The official had little affection for his son, knowing he could not bear the burden.
    He had a servant named Shao Xia, seventeen years old, quiet and refined, always attending to him. One day, as spring drew to an end and there was nothing to do, after the rain passed the steps, the official suddenly remembered that last year someone had given him a green phoenix fairy seed. He ordered Shao Xia to bring it out. Upon opening the package, he saw the seed was damaged, fearing it could not sprout. Shao Xia said, "Just take advantage of this good, moist soil, and there's no need to consider whether it is a seed from last year." The official was moved by her words and planted it. The following year, a son was born, named Fu. Officials and gentry all prepared noodles for the official, sending gifts of newborn money. Only his son mocked him, thinking it was like an old clam producing a pearl, fearing it may not necessarily be so.

    260

    公致仕归林下，少霞尝私语公曰：“妾以蒲柳托根桃叶，不敢以老去诗人，遂忘情于半臂。幸而征兰有梦，但呱呱者在怀，正恐先生风烛，长公子非爱惜紫荆花者。则一斗粟竟不相舂，我母子当不知死所矣！”言罢，呜咽欲绝。公乃作书一卷贻少霞曰：“我死后，尔母子料不见容。我今即厚尔，伊夺之，无益也。我筹之审，俟复儿年长大，吾邑有贤令尹至，可令赴诉焉。尔母子之恒产出于斯，善宝之。”少霞乃密藏诸椟。公又命长子成至，稽田籍，点什物及骡马婢仆，悉归焉。成请曰：“少霞母子，何以置之？”公曰：“嬖人之子也，西山有石田二十亩，屋一区，足矣。”呼少霞告之，泣而去。初不以为复之薄也。逾岁公卒，成主殡事，多不循礼。欲为其父柩前导龙凤节，如法驾仪。又令画士于父影上加珊瑚顶，邑人讪之，乃止。

    Explanation

    260

    The husband retired to the countryside, and one day, the concubine, named Shaoxia, privately spoke to him, saying, "I compare myself to a weak plant relying on a strong one, and I dare not expect to be cherished as a poet in your old age, so I have neglected my duties. Fortunately, I have a dream of hope, but the baby in my arms makes me fear that your life is as fragile as a candle in the wind, and the eldest son does not cherish me like a precious purple plum blossom. If so, we will have no resources to live on, and my mother and I will not know where to find our end!" After speaking, she wept uncontrollably. The husband then wrote a scroll and gave it to Shaoxia, saying, "After my death, your mother and son will likely not be accepted. I will treat you well now, but he will take it away, and it will be of no use. I have carefully considered this: wait until the child grows up, and when a virtuous magistrate comes to our county, you can appeal to him. Your mother and son's stable property comes from here; treasure it well." Shaoxia then secretly hid the scroll in a box. The husband also called his eldest son, Cheng, and had him check the land registry, inventory the household items, livestock, and servants, and take them all. Cheng asked, "What should be done with Mother and the child?" The husband replied, "They are the children of a concubine; there are twenty acres of stone fields and a house in the West Mountain, which is enough." He called Shaoxia and told her, and she wept as she left. Initially, he did not think the inheritance was too small. After a year, the husband died, and Cheng oversaw the funeral arrangements, doing many things that were not in accordance with ritual. He wanted to lead a dragon and phoenix procession in front of his father's coffin, like an imperial ceremonial procession. He also ordered a painter to add a coral crown to his father's portrait. The townspeople mocked him, and he stopped.

    261

    有名娼女，成艳之，买为妾，未小祥，岳家责之。成殴长妇，遂自缢。多方请托，事乃寝。所交结皆豪富棍徒。人有借贷者，必重息盘剥，以充其欲。日者告少霞曰：“西山之阳，有先人之田庐在。尔母子盍往焉？父命也，不可违。”少霞悽惶携子往，一切井灶瓢杓，皆无所为谋。少霞纺绩自给。十年间，复知有母而不知父，问母曰：“人莫不有父，我独无？”少霞凄然曰：“尔父死矣。”后复从塾师读，归问母曰：“城中大郎，皆说是儿兄。何以兄锦绣而弟蓝缕？”少霞曰：“儿但读，俟长大便有好衣着。”又数年，复已成人。当十月朔，少霞携复展墓。成方令妾着紫貂裘，跨少骊驹，随从仆妇皆戎装，猎于郊。便道过墓，见少霞母子单寒，傲视之。少霞命复拜兄，成拱手曰：“小客贵姓？我不敢弟汝也。”即其婢仆也不与齿。其妾取钱二百与复。复掷于地曰：“我不屑尔臭镪也！”妾曰：“小乞儿不识好歹！”遂各匆匆车骑去。

    Explanation

    261

    A famous courtesan was bought by Cheng as a concubine. Before the one-year anniversary of the death of her former husband, her family blamed Cheng. Cheng beat the eldest wife, and she hanged herself. After much pleading and intercession, the matter was finally dropped. The courtesan associated with wealthy and unscrupulous people. Those who borrowed money from her charged high interest rates and exploited them to satisfy her desires. A fortune-teller told Xiao Xia that on the south side of the West Mountain, there were ancestral fields and houses. You and your mother should go there; it is my father's command, and cannot be disobeyed. Xiao Xia, filled with sorrow and anxiety, took her son there. All the necessities of life, such as wells, stoves, ladles, and spoons, had to be provided for. Xiao Xia spun and wove to support herself. For ten years, she knew only of her mother and not of her father. She asked her mother, "Everyone has a father, but I don't have one." Xiao Xia said sadly, "Your father is dead." Later, she studied again with a schoolmaster. She returned home and asked her mother, "The young men in the city all say that they are my brothers. Why are my brothers dressed in fine clothes while I am in rags?" Xiao Xia said, "Just study, and when you grow up, you will have good clothes to wear." A few more years passed, and she became an adult. On the first day of the tenth month, Xiao Xia took her son to visit the graves. Cheng had his concubine wearing a purple sable fur coat, riding a young black horse, and the servants following her were all dressed in military attire, hunting in the suburbs. On the way, they passed by the graves and saw Xiao Xia and her son, who were poor and cold, looked at them with disdain. Xiao Xia told her son to bow to his "brothers." Cheng bowed his hands and said, "What is your distinguished name? I dare not call you my younger brother." Even his servants did not consider themselves equal to them. His concubine gave two hundred coins to the son. The son threw the coins on the ground and said, "I do not care for your stinking money." The concubine said, "You little beggar don't know what's good for you." They all hurriedly left in their carriages.

    262

    斯时少霞触景伤心，抚膺垂泪，九原已杳，遗子堪怜，不觉失色大恸，响振林木。复撤馔，掖母归，犹呜呜不辍。复乃长跽而请曰：“母毋伤也。母为父妾，抚子受困，分所当然。况剥极必回，天之常道。儿读书何事？或得捧毛生檄，以慰吾母十余年冰霜节操，亦未可定。何必以当境迍邅，用是悲泪为耶？”少霞闻之，乃收泪而为喜，忖曰：“儿子长矣。”
    时当童子试。少霞缅述遗嘱，出字一卷。复盥手展视，上有诗一首曰：
    七十年来又一春，此春度后更无春。只因风木秋凋后，恐有同根釜泣人。

    Explanation

    262

    At this time, Xue Xia was deeply saddened by the scenery, put his hand to his chest and shed tears. The realm of the dead was far away, and his orphaned children were pitiable. He couldn't help but lose his composure and grieve deeply, the sound of his weeping reverberating through the trees. He then removed the food from the table, supported his mother back home, and still wept continuously. He knelt for a long time and pleaded, "Mother, do not be sad. As the concubine of the father, you raised children in hardship; it is as it should be. When things reach their extreme, they will return to normal, which is the constant way of heaven. What is the purpose of my studying? Perhaps I will hold a document written by Mao Sheng to comfort my mother's unwavering integrity over the past ten years. It is not yet certain. Why should you be sad because of the current difficulties and shed tears out of sorrow?" Xue Xia heard this and collected his tears, feeling a sense of relief, thinking, "My son has grown up."

    At the time, it was during the examination for the status of scholar. Xue Xia recalled his father's last words and took out a scroll. He washed his hands and unrolled it, and on it was a poem:
    Seventy years have passed, and now it is another spring. This spring will pass, and there will be no more spring. Only because the wind and rain have caused the autumn leaves to fall, I fear that there will be someone at the root who will weep in a pot.

    263

    读罢涕泗而受，入城赴考。令见其垂髫韶秀，衣服破绽。及阅清贯，为故张宦子，曰：“汝缙绅郎，何一贫至此？”复曰：“但富于文，贫何病？”令异之，乃捻数页书曰：“自《学而》第一起，至《八佾》第三止，面试汝一破题。”复应声曰：“学而优则仕，乐其可知也。”令大赏识。试毕擢第一。后入署谢令，乃告曰：“复，故临安守侧室之子也。因兄成不相能，逐我母子于外，衣单食缺，十有五年矣。父在时，曾有遗诗一卷，嘱谓死后如兄果相凌，有贤邑侯至，呈之，当为我母子地也。”袖卷出。侯接视其诗，并有钤印年月日，且犹在官时，生复之年。侯曰：“贤契暂归，诗卷留阅数日，当缓图之。”复谢出。一日，令忽拘成至，问曰：“汝父有几子？”成曰：“居长，有父妾生一弟复。”令曰：“安在？”成曰：“居乡业儒，现蒙擢首者是也。”令拍案曰：“父死未寒，逐庶母，弃稚子，乃坐拥多资，奇赢陇断。恶迹款款，不可指数。

    Explanation

    263

    After reading the scroll, he was moved to tears and accepted it, then entered the city to take the exam. The magistrate saw that he was a young man, graceful and handsome, but his clothes were tattered. Upon checking his background, he learned that he was the son of a former official named Zhang, and said, "You are a scholar-official, how can you be so poor?" The young man replied, "I am rich in literature, what harm does poverty do?" The magistrate was impressed and took out a few pages of the Analects of Confucius, saying, "From Chapter One of The Analects, 'Learning and practicing it' to Chapter Three of The Eight Rows, I will test you with a composition." The young man responded immediately, "Those who excel in learning should become officials, the joy of learning can be understood." The magistrate greatly admired him and ranked him first after the exam. Later, he entered the magistrate's office to thank him, saying, "I am the son of a concubine of the former governor of Lin'an. Because my older brother Cheng did not get along with me, he expelled my mother and me from the household. We have been living in poverty for fifteen years. My father, when he was alive, left behind a scroll of poems and instructed that if my brother treated us unjustly after his death, a virtuous county magistrate should be presented with the scroll, and you should provide us with a place." He took out the scroll. The magistrate read the poems and saw that they were stamped with the date of the year when his father was still in office, the year he was born. The magistrate said, "My dear student, please return temporarily. I will keep the scroll and read it for a few days before making a decision." The young man thanked him and left. One day, the magistrate suddenly summoned Cheng, asking, "How many children did your father have?" Cheng replied, "He had one son, and a younger brother born to a concubine." The magistrate asked, "Where is he?" Cheng said, "He lives in the countryside, studying literature, and is now the one who was ranked first in the exam." The magistrate slammed the table and said, "Your father had not even been buried, and you expelled your stepmother and abandoned your young brother, while you sit on a pile of wealth, engaging in unfair business practices. Your evil deeds are numerous, they cannot be counted."

    264

    弟兄手足，分虽有长次之序，而产自无嫡庶之分，奈何令其子母单寒不给？汝尚有人心乎！”成闻言，汗流浃背，龃龉曰：“母弟乡居，父命所在。”令大怒，掷父诗于地，曰：“汝不以兄弟应分之恒产是与，乃借口于汝父临终之乱命是遵。试观此诗，尔父亦逆料尔有今日之丧心也。”令乃着其族长计产均分。成亦不能致辩，遂遵其判。析产后，复顿富。因感令德，令去沁时，复以千金赆之。令不受，曰：“我不欲多金，恐将来不能安我二子也。

    Explanation

    264

    Brothers and sisters, though there is a natural order of seniority, property should be divided equally, without distinction between legitimate and illegitimate children. How can you allow your mother and younger brother to suffer poverty and hardship? Do you still have a human heart?" Upon hearing these words, Cheng broke out in a cold sweat and stammered, "My younger brother lives in the countryside, and I am following my father's last wishes." The magistrate became very angry and threw his father's poem to the ground, saying, "You are not giving your younger brother his rightful share of the family property, but instead, you are claiming to follow your father's last wishes. Look at this poem; your father also foresaw that you would act this way." The magistrate then ordered the clan head to divide the property equally. Cheng could not argue against it and thus followed the magistrate's judgment. After the division of the property, he became suddenly wealthy. Because he was grateful for the magistrate's virtue, when the magistrate left the county, Cheng gave him a thousand gold pieces as a farewell gift. The magistrate refused, saying, "I do not want a lot of money, for fear that I will not be able to ensure the well-being of my two sons."

    265

    曷修孔子庙堂，为一邑光，且为尔先人德。”令临歧，谓复曰：“士人怀才抱道，拥琴书，卧空山，萧然啸傲，斯已耳。一旦与人家国事，一官一邑，上何以不负朝庭，下何以子我百姓。即琴鹤相随，效赵清献往来蜀郡，未为不可。又何必竭小民脂膏，充我囊橐乎？子孙贤，或谨守吾业；不贤，将灾害及身。如贤契者，鹏博鸿举，正未有艾。得志后，尤当痛心疾首，引以为戒。其毋忘西山藜藿也。”复谨受教而书绅焉。兄虽嫉复，亦无如何。
    兄以刻薄，弟以宽仁。刻则寡恩，仁皆慕德，旧时婢仆，皆归于复，而少霞又有贤母风。成生二子，皆淫荡，家遂败。复成进士，为刑曹五年。出守临安，成且来任，复恭事之，郡人称之小张太守。复固廉介，不见喜于当途，以终养告。去临安，泊如也，人以为不若老太守满载归。复慨然曰：“我有所受之矣。”

    Explanation

    265

    Why should the Confucius temple be repaired? To bring glory to the entire county and to honor the virtue of your ancestors." As the magistrate was about to leave, he said to Fu, "Scholars with talent and virtue carry their musical instruments and books, live in seclusion in the mountains, and live a reclusive and carefree life. This is enough. When they get involved in the affairs of the state and the people, as an official of a county, how can one not serve the court above and the people below? Even to travel with a qin (a Chinese zither) and a crane, following the example of Zhao Qingxian's travels to Shu Prefecture, is not impossible. Why drain the people's resources to fill my own pockets? If their descendants are virtuous, they will carefully follow my profession; if not, they will bring disaster upon themselves. For those who achieve great and noble deeds, with no end in sight, they should take it as a warning after achieving success. Do not forget the humble life in the West Mountain." Fu respectfully accepted the advice and wrote it on his sash. Although the brother envied Fu, he could not do anything about it.

    The brother was harsh and cruel, while the younger brother was benevolent. Harshness leads to a lack of kindness, while benevolence leads to admiration for virtue. The former maids and servants all returned to Fu, and Xiao Xia also had the virtue of a good mother. Cheng had two sons, both of whom were licentious, and the family thus declined. Fu became a Jinshi (imperial scholar) and served as a judge for five years. He was appointed to govern Lin'an, and Cheng came to take up his position, and Fu treated him with respect, and the people of the county called him the Young Governor Zhang. Fu was firmly廉洁 and upright, and was not favored by those in power, so he requested to retire to care for his parents. Fu left Lin'an calmly, and people thought he was not as successful as the old governor who returned with a full treasury. Fu said with a sigh, "I have received something from someone."

    266

    喜娘
    闻人垿，海州人。家温饱，乡居，优贡生。娶妻黄氏，年三十，贤而无嗣。妻欲为闻纳妾，海固僻壤，无当意者。其中表单伯言，以吏考得仪封丞，将之任，谓闻曰：“兄闲居无所事，家中计不劳布置，盍随弟之官？衙廨纵冷落，未必不如村落。升斗禄，亦可分供作游资。徒老牖下，使眼界狭窄，岂非憾事？”闻妻亦曰：“同叔叔往，大好事。汝兄年四旬，膝前尚空空。弟在官，一呼唤皆百应，觅得一善养子者，备防老计。我固非吼吼虔乞婆，终日抱醋瓶的，想叔叔亦深悉也。”闻初意懒，因妻言，忻然。妻为之办装，盘费外，又以百金置行橐，谓闻曰：“千里跋涉，囊中物是丈夫胆。或有所遇，一时叔叔处未便凑手，求人何如求己也？”

    Explanation

    266

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    Wen Ren Xù, a man from Haizhou, lived a comfortable life in the countryside. He was an excellent student recommended for further education. He married Huang Shi, who was thirty years old, virtuous but childless. His wife wanted him to take a concubine, but since Haizhou was a remote area, there were no suitable candidates. Among his relatives, Dan Ber Yan, who had been appointed as the magistrate of Yifu through a civil service examination, was about to take up his new position. He told Wen, "Brother, you are idle and have nothing to do. There's no need to worry about household affairs. Why not follow me to the post? The office may be cold and deserted, but it may not be any worse than living in a village. A small salary, modest as it is, can also be shared as travel expenses. To grow old sitting by the window, with narrow horizons, isn't that a regrettable thing?" Wen's wife also said, "To go with Uncle is a great thing. Your brother is forty years old, and still empty in front of the knees. While he is in office, a call will bring a hundred responses. If we can find someone good at raising a child, we can prepare for old age. I am not a nagging or persistent woman, nor am I jealous or suspicious. I think Uncle knows this too." Initially reluctant, Wen was delighted because of his wife's words. His wife prepared his outfit, in addition to travel expenses, she also placed a hundred gold pieces in a travel bag and told Wen, "A thousand-mile journey, the contents of the bag are the courage of a man. If there is something encountered, if it is inconvenient for Uncle to help at the moment, isn't it better to rely on oneself than to ask others?"

    267

    遂行，与单同车。仪封滨大河，人物繁盛。丞虽佐僚，而防河守险，鱼雁堤岸，正不得以闲曹目。哦松拄笏，非为仪丞言也。闻性疏旷，不受拘束，尝恣情游览。古刹荒原，信步则往。或临水而低徊，或登山而凭眺，皆足以畅叙幽情，更饶胜具，即使沽酒买鱼，亦复探囊可得。
    日者闻甫出，见皂衣人絷白发翁，翁垂首泣。闻伫立，见皂怒勒索，批翁颊曰：“老绝物，欠皇帝老子债，恃不得肉头相！”闻解之曰：“门中友，何不稍怜恤老年人？”皂素知闻为二尹戚，乃释手，曰：“玩户也。”翁跪而泣，闻掖起。翁曰：“我岂敢累积年银米？因此一条河沙压后，至今不一毛。家贫又无儿，又髦迈，嗷嗷数口，不能逃亡。今催科急，死而已。”闻恻然曰：“所欠几何？”翁曰：“新旧二十余金。”闻谓皂者：“与翁少待。”闻去移时返，出金，如数完纳，释翁。翁感谢，遂志闻姓名以归。闻返署，亦不告单。

    Explanation

    267

    He then proceeded and traveled with Dan in the same carriage. The city of Yi, located near the Yellow River, was populous and prosperous. Although the deputy official was a subordinate, his responsibilities included guarding the river and protecting against dangers, managing fish and birds along the riverbank, and ensuring the safety of the area. The phrase "哦松拄笏" does not specifically refer to the official in Yi, as it describes a leisurely and scholarly lifestyle. The subject, whose name is Wen, was known for his free-spirited and unrestrained nature. He often traveled freely and went wherever he pleased, sometimes lingering by the water or gazing up at mountains, always able to express deep feelings and enjoy the beauty of the surroundings. Even simple pleasures like buying wine and fish were easily obtained.

    One day, Wen was about to leave when he saw a person in black robes restraining an elderly man with white hair, who was weeping. Wen stood still and saw the official angrily demanding payment, slapping the old man's cheek and saying, "Old man, you owe the emperor's debt, and you can't afford to be soft-hearted!" Wen intervened and said, "Friend, why don't you show some compassion to the elderly man?" The official, knowing that Wen was related to a high-ranking official, released the old man and said, "It's just a joke." The old man knelt down and wept, and Wen helped him up. The old man explained, "I dare not accumulate silver and rice over the years. Because of one riverbank that collapsed, I have not paid a single coin since then. My family is poor and has no children, and I am old and frail, with several mouths to feed. We cannot escape. Now the tax collection is urgent, and I will die if I don't pay." Wen felt compassion and asked, "How much do you owe?" The old man replied, "About twenty gold pieces, new and old debts combined." Wen told the official, "Let the old man wait a moment." Wen left and returned after some time, bringing out the gold and paying off the debt in full. He then released the old man. The old man thanked him, and Wen recorded his name before returning home. Wen returned to his office without informing Dan.

    268

    明年，会乡有赛会。闻观返，雨载途，奔村落避之。适见茅屋环墙，门楼草茨，有垂杨一树卧路口，闻趋立门中，雨直潇潇下。西来一翁，破笠荷锄，提茄子数头，见闻忻喜曰：“恩人至矣！”闻审之，则去岁之欠粮翁也。翁告其妻，延之入内。闻谢曰：“雨住即行。”翁固挽之，闻不得已，入草舍。翁无他所，惟左右复室，一翁自居，一女居焉。翁夫妇拜跪曰：“去腊蒙恩，俾得一家团聚。再造深仁，铭肌浃髓。”遂指案上炉几：“我两口清贫，惟日烧一炷香，祝恩人福寿增耳。”闻逊谢不当。翁呼曰：“喜妮子，出来拜见。”

    Explanation

    268

    The following year, there happened to be a village festival. The narrator returned, and the rain was heavy, so he ran to the village to avoid it. Just then, he saw a thatched house surrounded by a wall, with the gate and roof covered with thatch, and a willow tree leaning over the road corner. He hurried to stand in the doorway, and the rain was falling heavily and continuously. An old man came from the west, wearing a tattered straw hat and carrying a hoe, with several eggplants in hand. Seeing the narrator, the old man was very happy and said, "The benefactor has arrived!" The narrator asked about it, and it turned out to be the old man who owed grain last year. The old man told his wife, and they invited the narrator inside. The narrator thanked and said, "As soon as the rain stops, I'll leave." The old man insisted on keeping him, and the narrator, unwillingly, entered the thatched cottage. The old man had nothing else but two rooms, one for himself and one for his daughter. The old man and his wife knelt and said, "Last winter we were favored, allowing us to reunite as a family. Your deep kindness again is engraved in our bones and marrow." Then he pointed to the stove and table and said, "We two are very poor, only burning one incense stick a day, to wish the benefactor more blessings and longevity." The narrator humbly declined, saying it was not appropriate. The old man called out, "Happy girl, come out and pay respects."

    269

    女出，年十五，挽大辫，分头，小水缵，面白泛红，弯眉，两颊微涡，着新翠布衫，双靸假套鞋，鞋半截红如狗牙椒。女插烛拜，闻不敢受。翁挟闻令其拜毕，起立。翁曰：“莫得闲，厨下与汝母具馔去。”女微哂，出随其母。闻辞，翁必不可，曰：“恩人太矫矣，即不获千金报，讵不容我作一饭主人耶？”闻又见檐前淅沥，遂留。少而村醪雏鸡、山蔬麦饭罗列于前。虽市远绝少佳肴，而田家况味，即饼圆葱寸，何莫非洁治以延宾也。

    Explanation

    269

    The girl emerged, aged fifteen, with a long braid and parted hair, wearing a small water band. Her face was pale with a hint of red, her eyebrows curved, and she had slight dimples on her cheeks. She wore a new green cloth shirt and a pair of traditional cloth shoes, the upper part of which was red like dog-tooth pepper. The girl lit a candle and bowed, but the guest declined the offer. The old man insisted that the guest bow first before standing up. The old man said, "Don't be idle, go to the kitchen with your mother to prepare the meal." The girl smiled slightly and went out with her mother. The guest declined again, but the old man would not allow it, saying, "The benefactor is too polite. Even if I cannot repay with a thousand gold, how can I not be allowed to host a meal?" The guest saw the rain dripping from the eaves and decided to stay. Soon, a simple but hearty meal was laid out, including village wine, young chickens, mountain vegetables, and wheat rice. Although the market was far and there were few delicacies, the simple flavors of the countryside, even if they were just round buns and inch-long scallions, were all clean and prepared to entertain guests.

    270

    座间问闻，闻亦询翁，两人家世，无不悉述。翁曰：“恩人当早为后嗣计。无似老朽今日，如门前挂无儿肉，茕茕苦，诮让更复难堪。”语次，女携茶具入，闻视女，翁辞顿辍。女立翁后，翁指女告闻曰：“村姑儿颇不拙，终日语刺刺，只能要针线。怎不作一男子，替汝父撑门户？”女拔鬓边小搔头，低首剔履上泥。继烛，雨更盆注，翁即设榻女舍。女欣欣持彗襆被，闻不自安。天甫白，翁出，母女入厨，作桃花糁、荷叶面啖闻。闻起，翁劝箸。翁恐泥泞，已备蹇于门。闻谢归，遂告单。

    Explanation

    270

    During the conversation, someone asked about 闻, and 闻 also inquired about the old man. They exchanged details about their family backgrounds, leaving nothing unsaid. The old man said, "Your benefactor should think about securing an heir soon. Don't let me be like an old man today, hanging meat in front of the door with no son to eat it, feeling lonely and miserable, and being ridiculed and rebuked even more." While talking, the daughter brought in the tea set. 闻 looked at the daughter, and the old man abruptly stopped speaking. The daughter stood behind the old man, and the old man pointed to her and told 闻, "This village girl is not clumsy, but she talks incessantly all day, and can only do needlework. Why can't she be a man to support the family?" The daughter pulled out a small hairpin from her hair and lowered her head to pick dirt off her shoes. After the lamp, the rain poured down, and the old man immediately set up a bed in the daughter's room. The daughter happily brought a broom and bedding, but 闻 felt uneasy. When the sky just turned white, the old man went out, and the mother and daughter entered the kitchen to prepare peach blossom paste and lotus leaf noodles for 闻. 闻 got up, and the old man encouraged him to eat. The old man was afraid of the mud and had already prepared a donkey at the door. 闻 thanked and returned, then reported to his master.

    271

    自此以往，翁尝入城探闻。如春野一蔬，秋田一黍，翁必致送。闻与单宦况萧条，固乐得此田舍翁相往来也。女时制香囊袜履，以为闻寿。单见之，颇称其巧，单乃阴为闻往媒其女，而议聘焉。翁夫妇首肯曰：“我受闻君大德，又知夫人不育，久欲以弱息赠，但恐闻君不受，固未敢启口。今尹侯一言，足重九鼎。谨请择吉，聘则不必言也。”闻知之，咎单曰：“拯人急而利人之女，恐非君子之行。”单曰：“不然，救人当俄顷之间，解囊于行李之际，时当饥溺，惠施行路，宁复计及翁有女而后出此？翁之云报，表君德也。兄何辞焉？固当坦腹东床耳。”闻遂娶女，阖署称为喜娘。

    Explanation

    271

    From this point onward, the old man would often go into the city to visit the official. He would bring gifts as simple and sincere as spring vegetables and autumn grains, which he would always present to the official. The official and the other official were in a state of poverty, so they were indeed happy to have this rural elder as a companion. The daughter would sometimes make fragrant sachets and socks for the official's birthday. The other official saw this and praised her skill. Secretly, he arranged for the official to marry the daughter and began to discuss the marriage. The old man and his wife nodded in agreement and said, "We have greatly benefited from the official's kindness. We also know that your wife does not have children. We have long wanted to give our daughter to you, but we were afraid that you might not accept her, so we did not dare to speak up. Now that the official has spoken, his word carries great weight, like the nine tripods of ancient China. Please respectfully choose a favorable day for the wedding, and we will not need to discuss the marriage further." The official heard this and reproached the other official, saying, "To help someone in need and then benefit from their daughter, I fear this is not the behavior of a gentleman." The other official replied, "No, true charity is spontaneous and does not consider the recipient's family circumstances. When helping someone, one should act quickly, opening one's purse in a moment of need. In times of hunger and drowning, one should offer kindness on the road without considering whether the person has a daughter or not. The old man's offer of his daughter is a way to repay your kindness and demonstrate his virtue. Why refuse? You should accept the marriage proposal." The official then married the daughter, and the entire office celebrated the match, calling her the "matchmaker."

    272

    喜娘慧而能，善体闻意。阅月，闻与俱东归。闻先至家告黄氏。喜娘至，展拜黄，举止恪顺，早息不辍，代黄氏劳，黄亦爱之。告闻，迎其父母来海。一日，黄偶忘一件事，喜娘曰：“妾已办之。”黄素多病，今审喜娘可托，乃尽委以家务。喜娘董率家人，亲操井臼，课纺绩，功倍往昔。又通悉田事，祈晴问雨，播种助苗，罔不井井有条。喜娘多种麦则麦收，多种黍则黍收，虽老农圃不如也。五六年中，家益裕，广治沃田四百余亩。生一子，黄氏待之如己出。黄氏尝对人曰：“自喜娘到吾家，皆不知穿食事，但个个饭来张口，衣来动手而已。”

    Explanation

    272

    The Maid, Hui, was both wise and capable, skilled at understanding Wen's intentions. After a month, Wen and Hui traveled east together. Wen arrived home first and informed Huang. When Hui arrived, she paid respects to Huang, showing respectful and obedient behavior, resting early but not stopping, and taking over Huang's work. Huang also loved her. She informed Wen and arranged for her parents to come to the sea. One day, Huang forgot something, and Hui said, "I have already taken care of it." Huang, who was often ill, now trusted Hui and entrusted her with all household affairs. Hui oversaw the family, personally handling household chores, teaching spinning and weaving, and achieving twice the results of the past. She also thoroughly understood agricultural matters, performed rituals related to weather, planted seeds, and helped the crops grow, all in a well-organized manner. Hui planted more wheat and harvested more wheat, and planted more millet and harvested more millet, even though experienced farmers could not match her results. Over five or six years, the family became increasingly prosperous, and they cultivated over four hundred acres of fertile land. They had a son, and Huang treated him as if he were her own.
