    是时刘已成名，酒肆已收。明春公车，女亦与偕。榜发被黜，刘亦不以为意。后至两踬南宫。刘问女将来科分，女不答，谆问之，女乃就其书筴上写八字云：“进士二字，恐怕不成。”刘曰：“然则可废书矣。”女曰：“恐怕不成，才要读书，何可废与？”

    Explanation

    315

    "Where is your brother going?" the woman asked. "He has returned home," she replied, "I have entrusted the maid to come and keep you company." She then laughed and took a pair of men's shoes from outside the curtain, handing them to Liu and saying, "These are your brother's remaining possessions." Liu took them and saw that they were stuffed with cotton inside, and tears streamed down his face. The woman laughed and said, "Do not grieve, I am indeed your brother, not your sister." Liu cried, "I also know that you are not my sister, but my brother. It is because he is my brother that I am grieving. Why didn't you tell me earlier?" The woman then took a flowered handkerchief from her sleeve and wiped Liu's face, saying, "You were born too late; it was not that I did not want to tell you earlier. While the sheep has not yet died, the pen can be repaired, and we two are still like a little husband and wife." Liu then wiped away his tears and smiled, and they began to be intimate. The woman said, "Do not be hasty, three years ago, the light shadows came in pairs. If it cannot be achieved, tonight we can still share a bed." Liu responded, "Many times of hard advice, just labor to point out and help the lamp." The woman nodded, and they became husband and wife. The next day, the woman also did not leave.

    At this time, Liu had already gained fame, and the wine shop had closed. In the spring of the following year, he would take the imperial examination, and the woman accompanied him. When the results were announced, Liu failed, but he did not take it seriously. Later, he failed the examination twice. Liu asked the woman about her future prospects in the examination, but she did not answer. After repeated questioning, she wrote eight characters on his book: "The two characters of jinshi, perhaps it will not succeed." Liu said, "Then should we give up studying?" The woman replied, "If it is not going to succeed, then we should study even harder, how can we give up?"

    316

    一日，女忽堕泪曰：“奴与郎缘分尽在今夕。”刘惊泣不知所措，欲筹所以代之者。女曰：“此定数，不可逃。”刘不得已，满设良酝，与女尽醉。且斟且哭，两饮两伤。六载离情，难消此夕；二人别绪，更尽一杯。刘问女何往，女曰：“上清承值。”刘曰：“岂无瓜代？”女曰：“一班可避一劫，盖五百年也。”又自问终身官禄，女曰：“天机安敢泄漏人间。”乃举杯灌地曰：“君其鉴此。”既而鸡筹三唱，东有启明，女大哭而杳。刘已昏绝复苏，从此踪迹渺茫。刘至今悉除杯杓，不事涓滴，恐对酒怀人，不克终日也。
    后至戊戌科成进士，方知二字不成之判。由词垣至国子祭酒，又悟一杯灌地之验。予告归林下，年已八十矣。

    Explanation

    316

    One day, the woman suddenly wept and said, "My fate with you is exhausted tonight." Liu was shocked and cried, not knowing what to do, and sought a way to replace her. The woman said, "This is a fixed destiny, it cannot be escaped." Liu, having no choice, fully prepared fine wine and drank with the woman until they were both drunk. As they poured and drank, they wept together, both drinking and both feeling sorrow. Six years of separation, hard to dispel in this night; the two of them, parting, drank one more cup. Liu asked where the woman was going, and she said, "I am serving in the celestial realm." Liu asked, "Is there no renewal?" The woman replied, "One shift can avoid one calamity, it takes about five hundred years." She then asked about Liu's official career, and he said, "Heavenly secrets, how dare I reveal them to the human world." She then raised the cup and poured it onto the ground, saying, "You should take this as a sign." Soon after, the rooster crowed three times, the morning star appeared in the east, and the woman wept loudly before disappearing. Liu fainted and then regained consciousness, and from then on, her traces were lost. Liu has since completely removed all cups and ladles, no longer drinking even a drop, fearing that drinking would remind him of her and make him unable to bear the day.

    Later, during the year of Wu Xu, Liu passed the imperial examination and became a Jinshi, realizing the significance of the judgment of "two characters not becoming." He rose from the literary realm to the position of Guozijiu, and once again understood the omen of pouring the cup onto the ground. He then retired to the countryside, having reached the age of eighty.

    317

    (近日《红楼梦》中小儿女情景，有此等别致否？七如)

    拜书
    豫章之永丰木塘源最僻，去城七十里，皆山箐。一村之人，不识毛锥，老幼嬉嬉，有上古结绳风。一樵者为段云岩，孑立一身，翘然自异。尝入城市，见邑令舆盖甚都，慨然曰：“大丈夫不当如是耶？”偶得残本四子书，每置之几上，以为黄金屋当在此中。奈十室间无可问途者，惟有焚香百拜，稽首而已。如是出必拜，反必拜。当雨雪，不出户庭，则默默对书，恨我不见古人。
    一日樵归，见室中饮食盈案，段异之。诘朝，键户出伺之。见有女子坐几侧，持书反覆展视，继又燃火具馔。段启闼入，女子亦无所怖避，曰：“妾乃天汉素女离珠也，天帝悯君孤苦，有上进志，故遣妾来主中馈，以佐灯膏。”段喜，遂与合。女艳如桃李，而冷若冰霜，节之以礼，不敢与狎，所谓坤道而有师道焉。女遂出镪资办饔飧，不令其执柯出樵，杜门闭户。

    Explanation

    317

    A Woman from the Remote Village of Mu Tang Yuan

    In recent times, have the scenes of young people in Dream of the Red Chamber been as unique as this? (Seven Like)

    I bow to you,

    The village of Mu Tang Yuan in Yongfeng, Yuzhang, is the most remote, seventy miles from the city, all surrounded by dense forests. The villagers are unfamiliar with writing tools, and both the old and the young live carefree lives, reminiscent of ancient times when people used knots for record-keeping. A woodcutter named Duan Yunyan stands alone, distinguished and exceptional. Once he entered the city, he saw the magistrate's carriage and canopy, which were splendid, and he sighed, saying, "Shouldn't a true man be like this?" He accidentally found a damaged copy of the Four Books and placed it on his table, thinking that the golden house of knowledge must be found within them. However, there was no one among the ten households to ask for guidance, so he could only burn incense, bow a hundred times, and prostrate himself. He would bow upon leaving and upon returning. When it rained or snowed, he did not leave his home, but instead sat silently with his books, regretting that he had never met ancient scholars.

    One day, when the woodcutter returned from his work, he saw a table full of food in his room and was surprised. The next morning, he locked the door and waited outside to see who was there. He saw a woman sitting beside the table, holding a book and flipping through its pages. She then lit a fire and prepared the meal. When Duan opened the door, the woman showed no fear and said, "I am the celestial maiden Li Zhu from the Milky Way. The Heavenly Emperor, moved by your loneliness and your desire to improve, has sent me to manage your household affairs and assist you with your studies." Duan was delighted and agreed to marry her. The woman was as beautiful as peach and plum blossoms, yet as cold as ice and frost. She adhered to propriety and did not allow herself to be overly familiar, embodying both the qualities of a woman and a teacher. She then used her wealth to prepare meals and did not allow him to go out to chop wood, leading him to close the door and stay inside.

    318

    初则妆台诘屈，床笫咿唔，口讲指画，循循善诱，春风座上，俨列巫山，而段亦备极瞻望仰钻之妙。女子尝曰：“读书有三到：心到，眼到、口到。书意不醒，曷问我心？书读不熟，曷视我目？书旨不剖，曷观我口？日变焉，月化焉，循其序不躐其等，庶几竿头日进，庸玉汝于成乎！”段亦谨受教，能殚诸心，研诸虑。抑或废书三叹，顿转于秋水之流波；又或把卷沉吟，忽悟于樱桃之启齿。甚至触色闻声，罔不惬心而莫逆。一时之交相酬对，正静不佻，觉美而益增其艳，正妙而莫可名言。于是十易寒暑，女子呼段而进之曰：“吾人于载籍极博之中酝酿焉，果克尝其旨乎？夫不尝之不得其旨。尝之也未必尽得其旨。可知机缄所在，本无易辟之区；阅历所经，正有难弛之担。善学者所为，不留其隙也。”

    Explanation

    318

    In the beginning, the study table was cluttered, and the bed was a place of quiet recitation. The teacher spoke and gestured, guiding patiently and gently, like a spring breeze in a room, with the majestic scenery of Mount Wushan in sight. And the student, too, experienced the wonderful feeling of looking up and striving to understand. The girl once said, "There are three aspects to reading: the heart, the eyes, and the mouth. If the meaning of the book is not clear, why should I ask my heart? If the book is not read well, why should I look at my eyes? If the purpose of the book is not understood, why should I look at my mouth? Changing daily and transforming monthly, following the order without skipping levels, perhaps one can make daily progress and eventually achieve success!" The student also carefully followed the teachings, able to devote his heart and mind to the task. Sometimes, he would sigh three times after putting down a book, suddenly turning in the flowing waves of autumn water; or hold the book and ponder deeply, suddenly realizing when the cherry blossom opens its mouth. Even touching colors and hearing sounds, all pleasing the heart and finding no opposition. The mutual response at the time, properly calm and not frivolous, feeling beautiful and increasing its beauty, truly wonderful and beyond words. After ten changes of cold and heat, the girl called out to the student and said, "We have been cultivating ourselves in the vast sea of books. Have we truly tasted its essence? If not tasted, one cannot know its essence. Even if tasted, one may not fully understand its essence. The mechanism lies in the place where there is no easy path to open; the experiences one has gone through are truly difficult burdens to bear. What a good learner does is to leave no gaps."

    319

    女乃劝段入童子试，隽。次年举孝廉。后成进士，出宰河阳。夫人佐之理政，卓卓有声。
    女一日饮而倦卧，段入搴帷，见白狐伏焉，转睫而夫人起曰：“缘尽矣！”振衣欲去。段泣曰：“卿饮食教诲，成我之身，感恩佩德，实同再造。即为异物，安敢见猜？”女慰曰：“非此之谓也。妾本狐也，因怜君拜书之诚悫，故假素女之名，冒天帝之诏，以耸君听而励君志。实亦君自为之，妾何功之有？今君学明道立，妾亦当功成而退，理所宜然。至若恋恋作儿女态，此蚩蚩者之所为，岂出自达人君子也哉！二十年后，再图佳会。”言讫不见。
    段抚膺痛切，若失师保。由此仕进之心悉淡，告归田里。妾生一子，名景贤，十三入邑庠。段年七十，辰起徘徊于亭，忽见狐女艳服立云端，如画屏仙子，炊时而杳。段乃具衣冠，备棺椁，理后事。浃旬，无疾溘逝。今犹称乡先生焉。

    Explanation

    319

    The girl then encouraged her husband, Duan, to take the child examination, and he performed exceptionally well. The following year, he was recommended for a government position based on moral integrity and filial piety. Later, he became a Jinshi and was appointed as the magistrate of Heyang. His wife assisted him in governing, and they achieved great success and renown.

    One day, the wife drank and fell asleep. Duan entered the room and lifted the curtain, seeing a white fox lying there. Instantly, the wife stood up and said, "Our connection has ended!" She straightened her clothes and was about to leave. Duan wept and said, "You nourished and educated me, shaping my character, and I am deeply grateful and indebted to you, as if you had given me a second life. Even if you are an unusual being, how could I dare to doubt you?" The woman consoled him, saying, "This is not what I mean. I am originally a fox, and because of your sincere and honest dedication to your studies, I took the name of the White Lady, claimed an imperial decree, to inspire and encourage you. It is truly due to your own efforts, and what merit do I have? Now that you have mastered the Dao and established yourself, I should also retire after achieving my purpose, which is only natural. As for clinging to a romantic attachment and behaving like a child, this is what the ignorant do, not what a wise and virtuous person would do! In twenty years, we will plan another beautiful meeting." After speaking, she disappeared.

    Duan grieved deeply, as if he had lost a teacher and guardian. Thus, his ambition for official advancement faded, and he retired to his hometown. His wife gave birth to a son, named Jingxian, who entered the local school at the age of thirteen. When Duan was seventy years old, he would rise early and wander around the pavilion. Suddenly, he saw the fox woman dressed in splendid attire standing on a cloud, like a fairy in a painted screen. She disappeared at the time of cooking. Duan then prepared his attire, arranged for his coffin, and handled his funeral arrangements. Ten days later, he died suddenly without illness. He is still referred to as a respected scholar in the village.

    320

    醋姑娘
    王梅，鱼台人。美丰格，读书目过辄不忘，廿年来困于青衿。后读书济上萧寺中，尝拾薪数粒为炊，鹑衣百结，望之咸若浼也。
    一日，鬻书以易食。时当春初，草桥上风如刀刺，至日昃无问者。适一老翁见而异之，王呈书以进，翁曰：“君家书几何？”王曰：“只此一策。”翁曰：“是戋戋者，何足与畀哉！君请纳袖中，盍从我而餐焉。”生随翁至一处，去市较远，柴门掩映，颇不俗。入门，一女子笑迎翁曰：“爹爹购得芙蓉粉未？”翁曰：“有客戾至。”女趋而入。生登堂拜翁，翁让生坐，备问旅况。翁入内出，无何，女捧馔至檐下，翁接进曰：“家止此女，应门更无三尺童。足下努力加餐。”生曰：“一饭之恩，百日之泽，盖不敢不饱。”翁曰：“自今以始，但来就食。一饭主人，我力能办。”生起谢。翁呼女曰：“醋儿，出来见客。”

    Explanation

    320

    Acid Girl
    Wang Mei was from Yutai. She was beautiful and well-proportioned, and when reading, she could remember everything she saw. For twenty years, she remained stuck in the status of a scholar. Later, she studied at Xiao Temple in Ji, where she often gathered a few twigs to cook with, her clothes tattered and patched, and everyone looked at her as if she were dirty.
    One day, she sold books to buy food. It was early spring, and the wind on the grass bridge cut like a knife. By noon, no one had asked her to buy anything. Just then, an old man saw her and was surprised. Wang presented the book to him, and the old man asked, "How many books does your family have?" Wang replied, "Only this one." The old man said, "This is a small amount, not worth giving anything for! Please put it in your sleeve and come with me to eat." Wang followed the old man to a place a bit away from the market, where a simple but dignified thatched door could be seen. Entering, a girl greeted the old man with a smile and asked, "Daddy, did you buy the lotus powder?" The old man replied, "There is a guest here." The girl hurried inside. Wang entered the hall and bowed to the old man, who asked her to sit and inquired about her travels. The old man went inside and came out shortly, and the girl brought food to the eaves. The old man took it and said, "Our family only has this girl; there is no child under three feet old to answer the door. Please eat well." Wang said, "The kindness of a single meal is a blessing for a hundred days; I dare not be unsatisfied." The old man said, "From now on, just come to eat whenever you need to. I can provide a meal for you." Wang stood up to thank him. The old man called out, "Acid Girl, come out to meet the guest."

    321

    女出，丰容白晢，目长而角，眉细而弯，年约十八。翁指生谓女曰：“此王郎，有才无命，倘我不家，来时当款留之。”女笑曰：“穷措大一日不过八勺米，儿何恤馀炊以待？”生归。越三日，馁甚，又往。至门，呼无人，径入，见女坐室中捏水角子。女见生，起曰：“来趁阇黎饭后钟耶？”生曰：“长者命，故不敢辞。”女延之坐，乃以手捏馅，问生所自。生见女有慢士风，略吐生平，颇形肮脏。女曰：“未免自负。人不患有司不明，当患吾学不成耳。”生请女面试，女曰：“且出一对何如？鸟惜春归，噙住落花啼不得。”生构思良久不就，生曰：“卿固作此以相厄。”女笑曰：“足下何不以此厄人？”生亦出一对曰：“芍药花开，红粉佳人做春梦。”女知其谤己也，应声曰：“梧桐叶落，青皮光棍打秋风。”女起，拍掌胡卢，面簌簌应手如烟。

    Explanation

    321

    The woman emerged, her face full and fair, her eyes long and slightly angular, her eyebrows fine and curved, and she was about eighteen years old. The father pointed to the male protagonist and said to the woman, "This is Wang Lang, talented but unfortunate. If I do not marry him, I will treat him kindly when he comes." The woman smiled and said, "A poor scholar can only afford eight scoops of rice a day; how can I worry about having extra food to wait for him?" The male protagonist returned home. After three days, he was very hungry and went again. Upon arriving at the door, he called out but no one answered, so he entered directly. He saw the woman sitting in the room making water dumplings. The woman saw the male protagonist, stood up, and said, "Are you here to catch the monk's bell after the meal?" The male protagonist replied, "I am commanded by the elder, so I dare not decline." The woman invited him to sit, then used her hand to make the filling and asked where he came from. Seeing that the woman had a disdainful attitude toward scholars, the male protagonist briefly recounted his life, revealing his disreputable past. The woman said, "You cannot help but be arrogant. One should not worry that the authorities do not understand, but rather that one's learning is incomplete." The male protagonist asked the woman to test his abilities with a couplet. The woman said, "Let's see how you fare with this one: 'The bird laments the spring's departure, holding onto the fallen flower but unable to weep.'" The male protagonist thought for a long time but could not come up with a response. He said, "You must have created this to trouble me." The woman laughed and said, "Why don't you use this to trouble others?" The male protagonist also composed a couplet: "The peony flowers bloom, and the beautiful lady has a spring dream." The woman realized he was insulting her and responded immediately, "The osmanthus leaves fall, and the young man in his prime seeks to take advantage of the autumn wind." The woman stood up, clapped her hands, laughed heartily, and her face shook like smoke in response to his actions.

    322

    生方惭怍，翁忽自外至，见生，谓女曰，“王郎尚未辰餐。”令女速具馔。女入厨下，翁曰：“老夫有一言奉告，未审尊意允否？”生曰：“尊丈所谕，何敢违。”翁曰：“弱息年已及笄，尚未委禽。知足下现在求凰，倘不相弃，愿谛良姻。”生曰：“三生何幸，得附鸾鸣！惟自愧蒹葭，不堪倚玉。”翁曰：“女幼时，有相者谓必配一穷儒，此固前定数也。但彼此客中，繁文胥简，为老夫计，且为足下地，今日即当成就。”生唯唯。翁入，携女出，令生合拜，既而拜翁。女着一红衲袄，馀无修饰。女复入，炊水角为饷。夜合卺焉。生将书箧携至女居，不作老僧伴矣。

    Explanation

    322

    The man was just feeling ashamed when the old man suddenly came from outside. Seeing the man, the old man said to his daughter, "Wang Lang has not yet had his lunch." He ordered the daughter to quickly prepare the meal. The daughter went into the kitchen, and the old man said, "I have something to say to you, and I wonder if you will agree." The man replied, "Your esteemed father's words, how dare I disobey?" The old man said, "My daughter has already reached the age of marriage, but she has not yet received a proposal. Knowing that you are now seeking a wife, if you do not reject me, I wish to arrange a good marriage for you." The man said, "How fortunate I am, to join in the singing of the phoenix! But I feel ashamed, like a reed, and not worthy to lean against jade." The old man said, "When my daughter was young, a fortune-teller said she would marry a poor scholar, which is indeed a predestined fate. But since we are both guests here, and elaborate formalities are simplified, for the sake of the old man and for your sake, we will make it happen today." The man agreed. The old man went inside and brought the daughter out, ordering the man to bow together, and then the man bowed to the old man. The daughter was wearing a red quilted coat, with no other adornments. The daughter went back inside and cooked dumplings for the meal. They consummated their marriage at night. The man brought his book chest to the daughter's residence, no longer acting as a monk's companion.

    323

    是年省试，翁备行资。至期生就道。未几试毕，至济访故居，惟见荒原蔓草，野冢累累而已。询之土人，云此地素无人居，为狐兔出没所。生怅惘，号痛失声。彼王子贫者也，当友朋畏避、亲戚惧匿之时，独翁能识之，翁之恩义可谓厚已。宜乎其感恩，而知己之，又何论狐兔哉！生仍寓萧寺，屡次侦访，杳无踪迹。

    Explanation

    323

    In that year, when the provincial examination took place, the old man prepared his travel expenses. When the time came, the scholar set out on his journey. Soon after the exam was finished, he returned to his former home in the city of Ji. What he saw was only a desolate plain covered with wild grass and numerous wild graves. He asked the local people, who told him that this place had never been inhabited and was a place where foxes and rabbits roamed. The scholar was filled with sorrow and lamented, crying out in pain. He was a poor scholar, and during the time when his friends and relatives avoided him, only the old man recognized him. The old man's kindness and righteousness were truly great. It was appropriate for him to be grateful and to recognize the old man as a true friend. What difference does it make that the place is now inhabited by wild animals? The scholar still stayed in the temple, searching repeatedly, but there was no trace of the old man.

    324

    榜发，王中第二。入都，僦住果子巷。一日，生偶步窑台，归途见翁来，趋拜于道，泣诉想慕。翁曰：“我以匆匆去济，故未留信于坦。后欲相访，又恐坦不在济，遂不果。固料礼闱之必来都也。坦盍随老夫一叙离悰？”生随往。至一园亭，极幽敞，书策几榻，莫不精良。翁曰：“舍女今番未入都，在曲阜依外母家。有侄女今随侍在侧。”遂呼：“佾儿，出见姊夫。”女哝哝不肯出，翁曰：“自家人，毋相避也。”出见生。生揖，视女，约十五六，低首含颦，妙丽无双，流动处微逊其姊。立顷遂入。翁曰：“坦客中想无人，何不携行李来？此间亦可读书。”

    Explanation

    324

    The results of the examination were announced, and Wang came in second place. Upon arriving in the capital, he rented accommodation in Fruit Alley. One day, while strolling on the kiln terrace, he happened to see his father-in-law coming and hurriedly bowed to him on the road, weeping and expressing his longing. The father-in-law said, "I left hurriedly for Jizhou, so I did not leave a letter for Tan. Later, I wanted to visit, but I feared Tan might not be in Jizhou, so I did not go. I was sure that the imperial examination would bring Tan to the capital. Why don't you follow me to have a talk about our parting sorrow?" Wang followed him. They arrived at a secluded and spacious garden pavilion, where the books, scrolls, tables, and chairs were all of the finest quality. The father-in-law said, "My daughter did not come to the capital this time; she is staying with her maternal family in Qufu. I have a niece who is currently serving me." He then called out, "Yi'er, come out to see your brother-in-law." The girl hesitated and did not come out. The father-in-law said, "It's just family, don't be shy." The girl then came out and saw Wang. Wang bowed, and looked at the girl, who was about fifteen or sixteen years old, with a lowered head and a frown, exquisite beauty unmatched, but slightly inferior to her sister in terms of grace. After a short while, she went inside. The father-in-law said, "Tan, you probably have no one with you while traveling, why don't you bring your luggage here? You can also study here."

    325

    饭毕，生遂移来。翁舍无婢仆，只佾姑一人董司饮食。翁在舍，生则与翁谈；翁出，生则与佾姑两人嬉笑终日。佾姑又善得人意，尝持绣匣来黹，窗前相与闲话。翁归猝遇，亦不之怪。一日，女偶持一卷诗曰：“姊夫，你看这是谁家帖子？”生视之，乃回文诗三首。其一曰：
    泉水新煎香味寒，薄罗轻试小冰纨。翩翩弄影花飞蝶，点点垂丝雨上坛。怜爱若扶今后醉，只单频忆旧时欢。缘因问据为谁语，弦尾焦馀空欲弹。

    Explanation

    325

    After the meal, the young man moved over. The old man's house had no servants, only Yi Gu, who managed the household affairs. When the old man was at home, the young man would converse with him; when the old man left, the young man would spend the day laughing and joking with Yi Gu. Yi Gu was also good at understanding people's intentions, often carrying a box of embroidery to sew, chatting idly by the window. When the old man returned unexpectedly, they were not surprised. One day, a woman happened to hold a scroll of poetry and said, "Brother-in-law, look at this poem. Whose is it?" The young man looked at it and saw that it was a three-part palindrome poem. The first part read:

    Freshly brewed spring water, fragrant and cold,
    Thin silk lightly tested, small ice brocade.
    Graceful shadows play, flowers and butterflies fly,
    Drops of rain fall, threads hang, dampening the altar.
    With love, I support you, intoxicated by the future,
    Alone, I often recall past joys.
    Out of curiosity, I ask, whose words are these?
    The strings at the end are scorched, yet still want to be played.

    326

    其二曰：
    东窗小坐夜深凉，默默清寒透薄裳。风片片秋三径水，月钩钩处一亭霜。红灯独照孤衾冷，翠袂双凝别路伤。同梦客时行道远，空空意绪别愁长。
    其三曰：
    长路关心悲道难，妾应愁叹客衣单。黄花菊老秋风厉，赤叶枫飘晚照残。行断雁迷云黯黯，梦多人阻水漫漫。伤神吊影空思忆，凉月晶悬映彻看。
    生读罢，知为妻所作，遂什袭珍藏之。女笑曰：“姊夫将醋姐物视同白玉，恐人以为砆也。今日无事，与姊夫击蒙小叶子格戏，负则打掌心。”先是生负，女批之。忽生击得双叶，生狂喜，遂欲批女掌。女笑以手缩袖中不出，生固捉之。女曰：“必欲打耶？”乃挽袖，舒臂生前，曰：“请打。”生见指葱如而腕藕若，遂承之以口，曰：“吾欲食西子臂耳。”女急缩手，生抱求欢。女不得已，遂与之合。生亦备极温存，十分亲爱。既而浃席流丹，娇红似染。

    Explanation

    326

    Translation

    Second Poem:
    Seated by the eastern window in the deep cool of night, silently, the clear chill penetrates my thin clothes. The autumn winds rustle over the paths by the water, and the moon, like a hook, hangs above a pavilion covered in frost. The red lamp illuminates my solitary bed, cold and empty. My green sleeves, holding back tears, reflect the pain of separation. In dreams, we travel together, but the path is long. My empty thoughts and long-lasting sorrow of parting stretch endlessly.

    Third Poem:
    On the long road, I worry about the difficulty of the journey. I should feel sorrow and worry about the traveler's thin clothes. The withered yellow chrysanthemums and fierce autumn winds create a bleak scene. The red maple leaves fall, and the setting sun casts a fading light. The journey ends, and the geese are lost in dark clouds. Many dreams are obstructed by the vast waters. Sorrowful and lonely, I think of the past, and the cool moon hangs, clearly visible.

    The man read the poems and realized they were written by his wife, so he carefully preserved them. The woman laughed and said, "My sister-in-law treats her things like white jade, but people might think it's just a stone." Today, there's nothing to do, so let's play a game of tiles. If I lose, I'll slap my palm. At first, the man lost, so the woman slapped him. Suddenly, the man won two tiles, he was extremely happy, and wanted to slap the woman's palm. The woman laughed and hid her hand in her sleeve, but the man insisted on catching it. "If you really want to hit," she said, "then pull up your sleeve, extend your arm in front of me, and say, 'Please hit.'" The man saw her fingers like green onions and her wrists like lotus roots, so he kissed her hand and said, "I want to eat Xi Shi's arm." The woman quickly pulled her hand back, and the man hugged her to make love. The woman, unable to refuse, finally agreed to join him. The man was also very gentle and loving. Afterward, they made love, and her cheeks were flushed red, like they had been dyed.

    327

    女自此往往不自检点。生时悚惕，惟恐翁之知也。女告生曰：“我早孤，叔抚我，最所钟爱，谋之当无不从。”生曰：“我既姊也，而又妹之，是两坦也，恐事不谐。”女于是病而不起。翁忧之，问女，不答。复问生，生跽自首，翁怒曰：“得陇又望蜀也！”愤愤入内。见女呻吟床笫，又出，生复跽，翁挽之曰：“非坦之罪也。始我揖盗开门，今已成舟刻木。将罪坦则小女忧，小女忧则大女辱。使一坦获戾，两女失所，我必不忍。今迫我以不得不从之势也。”生谢。翁曰：“但我家女无与人为妾者。”生曰：“如事齐楚。”翁曰：“请为质。”生即书曰：

    Explanation

    327

    The woman from then on often failed to be cautious or self-disciplined. At the time of birth, she was trembling with fear, only fearing that the father-in-law would know. The woman told the man, "I was orphaned at a young age, and my uncle raised me, loving me most dearly. If we plan it, there will be no refusal." The man replied, "I am already your sister-in-law, and now I am also your younger sister, which makes us both in a difficult position. I fear that the matter will not work out." The woman then fell ill and did not get up. The father-in-law was worried about her, asked the woman, but she did not answer. He then asked the man, who knelt down and confessed. The father-in-law angrily said, "Having obtained one thing, you desire another!" He angrily went inside. Seeing the woman moaning in bed, he came out again, and the man knelt down once more. The father-in-law held him back and said, "It is not your fault. At first, I welcomed a thief into my house, and now it is like a boat carved from wood. If I blame you, the younger daughter will be worried, and if she is worried, the older daughter will be disgraced. If one of you is punished, both daughters will lose their place. I would never do that. You are forcing me into a situation where I have no choice but to comply." The man apologized. The father-in-law said, "But my daughters will not become concubines for others." The man replied, "If we treat them as we treat Qi and Chu." The father-in-law said, "Please serve as a guarantee." The man immediately wrote,

    328

    《典》称釐降，《风》咏饯郊。洵两美以同妍，自双葩以并秀。兹者再结麟文之彩，重联凤喙之胶。二薛联姻，竟是今朝永叔；小乔初嫁，应知昔日周郎。旧女婿为新女婿，半子之分当兼；小姨夫是大姨夫，两大之间并重。当年鹊驾，宁先入者称尊；此际鸾栖，岂后来者居上。本是同心树，弟不先兄；原为并蒂花，姐犹似娣。更信人行暮雨，看镜里之双栖；何妨婢唤春风，拟溪边之三笑。将左宜者自符右有，无后轻者愈少前轩。爰赋联芳，永偕合璧。映彩车于户外，雅照三星；挹绣羽于堂前，巧逢双燕矣。

    Explanation

    328

    In the Shangshu (Book of Documents), it is said that the qilin descended, and in the Shijing (Book of Songs), it is sung that a farewell ceremony was held at the outskirts of the city. Indeed, two beauties share their beauty, and two flowers flourish together. Now, we reconnect the colorful patterns of the qilin and the adhesive of the phoenix's beak. The marriage of the two Xue families, which is none other than the famous Ouyang Xiu of today, and the marriage of Xiaojiao, who should recognize the once-renowned Zhou Yu. The old son-in-law becomes the new son-in-law, and the relationship of a half-son should be combined; the younger brother-in-law is the older brother-in-law, and both are equally important. In the past, when the magpie was riding, the one who entered first was the master; now, when the phoenix is perched, the later comer does not take the lead. Originally a tree of the same heart, the younger brother does not precede the elder brother; originally a twin-flowered plant, the elder sister is like the younger sister. More so, people walk in the evening rain, looking at the double nest in the mirror; why not call the spring wind, imitating the three laughs by the stream. Those who are suitable on the left naturally correspond to those who have on the right, and those who do not follow are lighter and fewer than those who lead. Bestowing the linked fragrance, forever united as a pair of jade disks. Reflecting the colorful carriage outside, elegantly illuminated by the three stars; greeting the embroidered feathers in front of the hall, skillfully meeting the two swallows.

    329

    翁览毕喜，遂令佾娘与生成婚。生捷南宫，入词垣。后一年，翁已去都。生假归省墓，与女偕程。至里，营旧居数处，家人亲串如蚁。生遣人至曲迎翁并醋娘，不知其处。生问佾娘，亦复含糊应之。
    一夜将半，生闻叩户声，凝听，一女子与小儿语。佾娘曰：“似醋姊来。”生急起，披衣启扉，果醋。入便问床前女子为谁，佾娘前拜问曰：“大姊别来无恙耶？”醋娘怒曰：“贱婢！谁不是一个汉，汝何竟坐我床耶？”生亦前为陪礼。女愤坐，挽儿膝间，曰：“当日无升斗粟，孤影对四壁，谁复问你一杯水？今贵矣，床上接踵，都不知从何处得信来！”女呜呜泣，曰：“姐无怨妹，此叔父陷人也。姐如必不相容，下令逐客，妹亦不敢强自逗留，以自取戾，盍返我外母家。”乃咽声，振衣欲去。

    Explanation

    329

    The father, upon finishing his reading, was delighted and promptly arranged for the maiden Yi to marry his son Cheng. The son passed the imperial examination and entered the Hanlin Academy. A year later, the father had already departed for the capital. The son took leave to return home and visit the ancestral graves, traveling with the daughter. Upon arriving in the village, they set up several old residences, and family members and relatives gathered like ants. The son sent someone to the alley to welcome the father and Su-maiden, but they did not know where they were. When the son asked the maiden Yi, she responded vaguely.

    Halfway through the night, the son heard a knock at the door. He listened intently and heard a woman speaking to a child. The maiden Yi said, "It seems like Su-sister is coming." The son quickly got up, put on his clothes, and opened the door. Indeed, it was Su-maiden. She entered and asked the woman on the bed, "Who is this woman?" The maiden Yi bowed and asked, "Big sister, have you been well since we last met?" Su-maiden angrily replied, "Wretched maid! Who isn't a man? Why did you sit on my bed?" The son also went forward to apologize. Su-maiden sat down in anger, holding the child on her knee, and said, "When I had nothing but a handful of grain, I was alone with my shadow facing four walls. Who would have asked you for a cup of water? Now that you are wealthy, people are crowding onto the bed, and I don't even know where you got the news from!" She sobbed, saying, "Sister, do not resent me. It was Uncle who entrapped me. If you absolutely cannot tolerate me, then order me to leave, and I will not stay against my will to invite trouble. Why don't you send me back to my mother's family?" She then sobbed, shook her clothes, and was about to leave.

    330

    生惶恐，两处拜揖哀恳。女乃挽佾姑而笑曰：“前言戏耳！但不如是，恐天下后世议我徒负有醋之名，而无醋之实，故忍而为此态耳。”生与佾姑破涕为笑曰：“愿夫人有虚名而无实践也。”醋娘令其子认父。佾娘问外母安。生问岳翁近履，女告以入晋。后翁自晋来，常至生家探二女。二女亦常去省外母云。
    生得房中之乐，不愿利达，适意林泉，闭门谢客，日与两妇诙谐诗酒，瀹茗敲棋，唱和颇多。有《漉酿集》诗，惜未梓。尝见其四绝云：

    Explanation

    330

    The protagonist was filled with fear and anxiety, bowing and pleading earnestly in two places. The woman then pulled her sister-in-law and laughed, saying, "What I said before was just a joke! But if I didn't act this way, I fear that future generations would criticize me for having only the reputation of jealousy without the substance of it, so I pretended to be this way." The protagonist and his sister-in-law stopped crying and started laughing, saying, "We wish that you have only the reputation of jealousy without actually being jealous." The mother-in-law's sister-in-law ordered her son to acknowledge his father. The sister-in-law asked after the mother-in-law's well-being. The protagonist inquired about his father-in-law's recent activities, and the woman told him that he had gone to Shanxi. Later, the father-in-law came from Shanxi and often visited the protagonist's home to see the two daughters. The two daughters also frequently went to visit their mother-in-law.

    The protagonist enjoyed the pleasures of the marital bed, did not wish for wealth and success, found pleasure in the mountains and waters, closed the door and refused visitors, and spent his days with the two women in a lighthearted and playful atmosphere involving poetry and wine, brewing tea and playing chess, and exchanging many poems. He had a collection of poems titled Lü Nang Ji, but unfortunately, it had not been printed. He had seen four of his best works, which were particularly outstanding.

    331

    一双金菊对芙蓉，取次风流在个中。恰似鱼游莲叶底，刚从西去又还东。
    亚字栏中花两枝，娇含嫩蕊未开时。东君着意和香摘，不使无端蜂蝶知。
    一边送暖一边寒，二女同居志也安。自是联辉兰蕙好，不教左右做人难。
    川字烟儿品字茶，鼎称恩爱总无差。乘鸾合在三株树，化雪还同六出花。
    生每问二女命名之义。醋字，以女生之日时；佾字，以女生之月也。后生寿八十，无疾终。生终身未尝问二女为何物也。二女亦同是日死。合葬日，女柩皆空。其子孙皆科第相望。

    李维敬
    河南商邱李维敬，父子皆邑庠。学无师承，专用揣摩。方家前辈之文，从不入目，惟剽窃一二时墨，仿其声调。正如优孟衣冠，皆无实际。又加盲眼试官，目少全牛，胸无成竹，挟骑墙之见，当赝鼎之加，往往针芥投而水乳合。故李氏乔梓，尝列案首，且饩廪焉。用是自负，又以为渊源独得之秘。

    Explanation

    331

    A pair of golden chrysanthemums face a lotus, and the elegance naturally arises in this setting. Just like a fish swimming under a lotus leaf, it goes west and then returns east.

    Two branches of flowers in a lattice, delicate buds that are still closed. The spring god carefully picks flowers with fragrance, not allowing the bees and butterflies to know.

    One side brings warmth while the other brings cold, and the two women living together have peaceful hearts. Naturally, the brilliance of orchids and sweet-scented grasses is good, not making it difficult for those around to live their lives.

    Tea arranged in a "川" pattern and served in a "品" pattern, love and affection are always in perfect balance. Riding a phoenix is in harmony with three trees, and melting snow is like six-petaled flowers.

    The person often asks about the meanings of the two women's names. The name "醋" is based on the day and time of her birth, and the name "佾" is based on the month of her birth. The person who was born later lived to eighty and died without illness. Throughout his life, he never asked what the two women were. The two women also died on the same day. On the day of their joint burial, the coffins of the women were empty. Their descendants all passed the imperial examinations in succession.

    Li Weijing
    Li Weijing from Shangqiu, Henan, and his father both attended a local school. They had no teacher or tradition, relying solely on guesswork and imitation. They never read the writings of famous predecessors, only plagiarizing a few recent writings and imitating their style. Just like the historical figure who imitated others' clothing and mannerisms, all without substance. Furthermore, they had blind examiners who saw only the whole cow and had no plan, holding a fence-sitting view in the presence of fake bronze vessels, often like a needle hitting a magnet or water and milk blending. Therefore, Li Weijing and his son often ranked first and received stipends. They were proud of this, also thinking it was a secret unique source.

    332

    会当省试，父子来汴。闱考尚早，偶游郊外萧寺。二人入廊后，见数椽轩敞，修竹掩映，堆石垒垒，有门如圜，内窗格皆纱縠。俄一人背手吟而出，丰致不凡，拱李入室。书籍满架，位置精洁。问李父子，答以商邱人，应试。李问其人，曰：“山东即墨白姓，侨于此。”坐谈间，一奚童携一丫髻小儿，戏喧阶前。李问为谁，曰：“豚犬也。恐家居无教，故令其随侍。客中岑寂，课子排遣耳。”李视其案头，有时艺一本，篇面书“时文针砭”四字。李曰：“旅中尚不废此，想沉浸有日矣。”白曰：“仆素鄙时艺。因见风气不古，文尚浮靡，小儿辈不知取裁，恐堕恶道。闲窗无事，特为釐正。狂瞽删削，恐不足以当大观。”

    Explanation

    332

    At the time of the provincial examination, the father and son arrived in Bian. The examination had not yet begun, so they casually visited a secluded temple outside the city. After entering the hall, they saw a spacious room with several rafters, surrounded by tall bamboo that partially obscured the view, with a pile of stones arranged neatly, and a round door inside with gauze-like screens on the windows. Soon, a man emerged from the room, holding his hands behind his back and reciting poetry, with an extraordinary and graceful appearance. He bowed to Li and entered the room. The room was filled with neatly arranged books. When asked about themselves, the father and son replied that they were from Shangqiu and were taking the examination. Li asked about the man, who said, "I am Bai, from Jimo in Shandong, living temporarily here." During their conversation, a young boy carried a child with a topknot and played noisily at the steps. Li asked who the child was, and the man replied, "It's my pet. I was worried that the child might not receive proper education at home, so I brought him along to keep me company. It's quiet here, and I teach him to pass the time." Li saw a book on the desk titled "Critical Essays on Contemporary Literature." Li said, "Even in your travels, you don't neglect this; I imagine you have been deeply immersed in it." Bai replied, "I generally disdain contemporary literary works. I have seen that the customs have declined, and the writing is overly ornate. Young people do not know how to choose or apply proper standards, and I fear they might fall into bad habits. With nothing to do in my spare time, I specifically revised them. My edits may be flawed, and they may not be worthy of great attention."

    333

    李父子翻阅，无篇不批抹殆甚，其尤甚者，皆李所熟习之文。李曰：“先生过矣！当代名公卿以此得邀声誉，岂无所本？先生一味雌黄。使先生为之，未必臻此，无乃蹈眼高手低之诮乎？”白曰：“是卑卑者又乌足道？虽日试万言，倚马可待。”李即欲面试一题。白曰：“何用书题。”
    忽小儿在旁偶遗一屁，白笑曰：“我即作一放屁文字何如？”乃口占二比云：“人当迫不及待之顷，则情发于不自禁，而气以郁而思伸。遂不觉于稠人广众之中，如抒其无聊之喟。事以猝然相接之馀，则情急于无可奈，而声以砰然遽出。乃不顾夫掩鼻恶恶之臭，忍为此不平之鸣。”言罢鼓掌大笑。李是年即仿此文调，作“晨门曰”二句题补廪者，闻之失色。

    Explanation

    333

    Li Zi was browsing through the texts, and almost every page was annotated to a great extent, especially the ones he was very familiar with. Li said, "Sir, you are mistaken! Contemporary famous officials and high-ranking government officials have achieved their reputation through this, surely there must be some basis for it? You are always nitpicking. If you were to do it, you might not achieve this level, wouldn't it be seen as overestimating one's abilities?" Bai replied, "These trivial matters are not worth discussing? Even if one writes thousands of words daily and can write on the spur of the moment." Li immediately wanted to take a test. Bai said, "Why do we need a written topic?"

    Suddenly, a child beside them let out a fart. Bai laughed and said, "How about I compose a piece about farting?" He then orally composed two couplets: "When one is in a moment of urgency, emotions cannot be restrained, and the feeling of frustration seeks release. One does not realize in a crowd of people, expressing a sigh of boredom. After an unexpected event, emotions cannot be contained, and the sound suddenly bursts out. Without regard for the bad smell that makes people cover their noses, enduring and making this unjust sound." After finishing, he clapped and laughed loudly. That year, Li imitated this style, composing a piece titled "Morning Gate Says" for the stipend recipients, who turned pale upon hearing it.

    334

    李父子起身欲出，白固留设馔，肴品丰美。白高谈阔论，诋排时辈，更复诙谐笑骂，举世皆空。二人持杯倾耳，不能置喙。至论成宏先正之法，皆所未之前闻。饮酣，白又说一时文笑话云：“有父子二人私一娼。一日，其父谓其子云：‘罔极之深恩未报，而又徒留不肖支体，贻父母以半生莫殚之忧。’其子即应曰：‘百年之岁月几何，而忍吾亲以有限之精神，更消磨于生我劬劳之后。’”李父子素有此事，闻白言，惭沮不敢下箸，强为轩渠而已。俄而灯上，李父子辞归。心窃慕之，又畏其谩骂，数日不通访问。

    Explanation

    334

    The Li father and son rose to leave, but Bai firmly invited them to a banquet, setting out a sumptuous feast. Bai spoke at great length and in a grand manner, criticizing and belittling his contemporaries, and further engaged in humorous and mocking remarks, declaring that the entire world was empty and meaningless. The Li father and son held their cups and listened intently, unable to interject. When Bai discussed the methods of the eminent scholars of the Cheng and Zhu schools, they had never heard of such things before. After drinking deeply, Bai told them a literary joke: "There was a father and son who were involved with a prostitute. One day, the father said to his son, 'The profound and boundless kindness of my parents has not been repaid, and yet I have left behind only unworthy limbs, leaving my parents with endless worries for half their lives.' The son immediately replied, 'How many years are there in a hundred years? How can I bear to let my parents expend their limited energy, further wearing them out after the hard labor of giving birth to me?'" The Li father and son had indeed been involved in such a matter, and upon hearing Bai's words, they felt ashamed and embarrassed, unable to bring themselves to eat, and could only pretend to laugh. Soon after, as the lamps were lit, the Li father and son took their leave. They secretly admired Bai, but also feared his harsh words, and so they avoided visiting him for several days.

    335

    一日，白携其子来叩门相访。李父子最啬吝，僦居蜗陋，不堪住足。顷谈间，忽学斗来索年貌册费，李父子不与，致相争哄。白巧为排解。学斗曰：“相公不知，彼父子皆钱眼中翻筋斗者。伊父子入学来，我等未曾沾得伊一文钱。”白力劝而去。李父子感德白。白起身辞归，李取身畔囊中青蚨数文与白之子买果啖。白子持钱，向孔视曰：“此眼如何翻得筋斗？”白即曰：“可作一讲，谢长者赐。”白子应声曰：“有钱安身，无所不可矣。夫钱眼小人眼大，不可翻也。极拟之为爱钱者喻。甚矣，利途之狭窄也！其间几无可转圜之法矣。乃有心能生境，境即幻身，遂不禁于无可位置之中，作一无所不至之想，则有如翻筋斗于钱眼中者。”作完，李父子奇其慧。白遂归。李老忽忆其入学时所作文，亦是此调，诧异不已。

    Explanation

    335

    One day, Bai brought his son to visit by knocking on the door. The Li family, known for their frugality, lived in a cramped and humble dwelling that was unbearable to stay in. During the conversation, Xue Dou suddenly arrived to demand fees related to age and appearance records. The Li family refused to pay, leading to a heated argument. Bai skillfully mediated the dispute. Xue Dou said, "Sir, you don't know that these father and son are those who do somersaults in the eye of a coin. Since they entered school, we have never received a single coin from them." Bai tried his best to persuade them to leave. The Li family felt grateful to Bai. Bai then stood up to take his leave, and Li took a few coins from his pouch and gave them to Bai's son to buy fruits to eat. Bai's son held the coins, looked through a hole, and said, "How can one do somersaults with this eye?" Bai replied, "You can make a speech, thanking the elder for the gift." Bai's son responded, "With money, one can secure a place, and nothing is impossible. The eye of a coin is small, while the eye of a person is large, and it cannot be flipped. It is a metaphor for those who love money. Indeed, the path of profit is narrow! There is little room for maneuver. Yet, the mind can create situations, and these situations are illusory. Thus, one cannot help but have thoughts that go to all places, like doing somersaults in the eye of a coin." After finishing, the Li family were amazed by his wisdom. Bai then left. Li suddenly remembered the essay he wrote when he entered school, which was in the same style, and was astonished repeatedly.

    336

    浃辰，李父子来寺，荒芜榛荆，素无人居。前日之雕甍美园，倏忽颓垣败井，惟见壁上墨直数十馀条，如新书者。李怪而数之，得九十一条，不解其故。是年秋，父子俱落孙山。又有功令饬衡文者釐正体裁，革去腐词滥套，务取清真雅正，李由是皆三等。数年后，李老以误解书旨褫巾，愤而死。又二十年，李子因用典错误，亦列下等。痛哭归里，尽焚其所读秘本。乃忆其父子自出考以至今次试罢，恰合九十一等。噫！窃取侥幸之不可也，不惟不容于世，抑且不容于鬼。使李父子受白生之揶揄，力改前辙，犹未为晚。奈何至死不变，终取大辱，始叹白生之见早耶！

    Explanation

    336

    Twelve days later, Li the father and son came to the temple, finding it overgrown with brambles and thorns, and long abandoned. The beautiful garden with its finely crafted eaves and walls, which had once been so splendid, was now suddenly reduced to ruins with broken wells and crumbling walls. They saw only several dozen vertical lines of ink on the walls, as if newly written. Li found this strange and counted them, discovering ninety-one lines, but he could not understand their meaning. That same autumn, both father and son failed to pass the examination, ranking among the lowest. Later, official decrees were issued to regulate the style of exam essays, abolishing stale and clichéd phrases, and emphasizing clarity, sincerity, elegance, and correctness. As a result, Li and his son were both classified as third-rate. Several years later, the father was dismissed from his position due to misinterpreting a text, and he died in anger. Twenty years later, the son also failed due to errors in using classical allusions. He returned home in tears, burning all the secret books he had read. Reflecting on the total number of failures, he realized that it matched the number of ink lines. Alas! The dangers of taking shortcuts and relying on luck are clear; not only are such actions unacceptable in the world, but they are also unforgivable in the afterlife. If the Li family had taken Bai's criticism seriously and changed their ways, it would not have been too late. But how could they remain unchanged until their deaths, ultimately bringing great shame upon themselves, only then to lament that Bai had seen the truth early on!

    337

    (世俗读书多走捷径。有谓四书不必读，可怀挟；有谓诗书可从删，徒误时。类皆目为不急之务，亦只属意时艺，袭其声调，即可博科第、称雄伯矣。岂独李维敬父子足为白生揶揄哉？)

    神童
    山西安邑有景姓者，为邑庠生，豪放不羁，好诋诃前人。尝云：“老庄诋尧舜而成其书，沮溺訾孔子而传其人；人亦何必随波上下，拾人牙慧？岂今独异于古所云哉！”间有著作，皆怪诞不经。以四子艺谬旨，褫其巾，益肆嫚。
    中年举二子一女。其次子在母襁褓中，生呼其长子出对云：“鸡鸣。”长未及就，而次子即应云：“虎拜。”于是奇之。名芝荣，小字泰来，颇有谢李之目。三岁，其姊嫁归宁，父命其作诗，云：
    前日于归去，今朝反面来。愁容何易改，顿觉笑颜开。
    芝荣爱鸡，其叔抱一鸡云：“尔吟一诗，即赠尔。”芝荣应声云：
    堪笑当年王右军，漫将书画换鹅群。今因叔命题诗句，不是犹儿贱卖文。

    Explanation

    337

    People in the world often take shortcuts when reading. Some say that the Four Books don't need to be read and can be memorized; others say that the Shijing and Shujing can be cut and that doing so wastes time. All of these are considered non-urgent matters, and they only focus on contemporary arts, imitating their style, and can thus gain titles and fame. Is it only the father and son Li Weijing who deserve ridicule from Bai Sheng?

    A Child Prodigy
    In Shanxi's An County, there was a man surnamed Jing, who was a student at the local school, bold and unrestrained, and liked to criticize his predecessors. He once said, "Laozi and Zhuangzi criticized Yao and Shun to write their books, and Ju and Nie criticized Confucius to spread their ideas; why must people follow the waves and pick up other people's scraps? Is it only different from what was said in ancient times?" Occasionally he had writings, all of which were strange and baseless. Because his writings on the Four Books were wrong, he was stripped of his hat and became even more disrespectful.

    In middle age, he had two sons and a daughter. His younger son, still in his mother's arms, called out to his older brother, "Cock crows." The older brother didn't respond in time, but the younger son immediately replied, "Tiger bows." He was then considered extraordinary. He was named Zhi Rong, with the nickname Tai Lai, and was often compared to Xie and Li. At three years old, his sister married and returned home for a visit. His father asked him to write a poem, which said, "Yesterday I went to my husband's home, today I return with a smile. How easily can a frown be changed, suddenly my face brightens."

    Zhi Rong loved chickens. His uncle held a chicken and said, "If you recite a poem, I'll give it to you." Zhi Rong immediately replied, "It's laughable that Wang Xizhi once exchanged his paintings for a flock of geese. Now, because my uncle has set a topic for a poem, it's not like selling my writing cheaply."

    338

    其父行尝难之曰：“如‘烟锁池塘柳’，尔亦能对否？”芝荣曰：“浪暖锦堤桃。”亦强对焉。社中请乩，有对云：“水中星月鱼吞吐。”对云：“天半风霜雁往还。”如壮缪庙一联云：“未了一生事，已完万古人。”皆浑成大雅。等身书无不记诵，即字典、通书，皆如夙构，朗朗登答，无一字讹。让梨之年，名噪晋阳。往来好事者，莫不迂道往见。西原搢绅，悉以软鞯蒲轮，道途相望。晋藩尤爱之，呼之小老先生。
    其貌癯，二目炯炯，有不可犯之容。会当公宴，芝荣隅坐。吴优觞至某出，荣微晒，客问，荣曰：“此曲走一拍，顾其误耶！”询之场上，果然。闻有《梅花百咏》精绝，惜未之见。尝又自认为王文简再生云。晋藩云：“此子慧由天悟，秀彻丰神，古媲圣童，今称国瑞也。”近岁有客自晋来，云此子至十岁，忽云其颛，嗒然而偶，不惟指鹿，并亦失马。其父哀之，眦血肠断而死。今芝荣尚在，客见之，盖不及田家牧竖儿，且家益落，或曰狐祟使然。

    Explanation

    338

    His father once challenged him by saying, "For example, 'Smoke locks the pond willow,' can you also respond?" Zhi Rong replied, "Waves warm the brocade dyke peach." He also forced himself to respond. In the community, they invited a medium, and there was a response that said, "In the water, stars and moons are swallowed and spat out by fish." Another response said, "In the sky, winds and frost see geese coming and going." For example, at the Zhumu Temple, one couplet says, "Not yet finished with one's life, already complete with ten thousand ancients." All are grand and elegant. All books equal to his body he has memorized, even dictionaries and encyclopedias, all as if premeditated. He answered clearly, without a single error. At the age of yielding the pear, his name resounded in JinYang. Those who came and went, all took detours to see him. The scholars of the west all came with soft saddles and rush wheels, meeting each other on the road. The Prince of Jin especially loved him, calling him "Little Mr. Scholar."

    His appearance was emaciated, his eyes were bright, with an air of inviolability. At a public banquet, Zhi Rong sat in a corner. When the Wu performer came to drink, something happened; Zhi Rong slightly smiled, and the guest asked, "This tune misses a beat, is it wrong?" When asked on the field, it was indeed so. He heard that the Hundred Odes to Plum Blossoms was exquisite, but he had not seen it. He once said he was the reincarnation of Wang Wenjian. The Prince of Jin said, "This child's wisdom comes from divine insight, his beauty and spirit are clear and abundant, anciently compared to a sage child, today called a national treasure."

    Recently, a guest came from Jin, saying this child at ten years old suddenly said he was stupid, and became silent, not only losing his way but also losing his horse. His father mourned him, his eyes bleeding and his intestines breaking, and died. Now Zhi Rong is still alive, but when the guest saw him, he was no better than a village shepherd boy, and his family was even more destitute, or perhaps it was the fox spirit's doing.

    339

    (七如氏曰：非狐之为祟，盖景生之为厉也。其诋诽先哲，天故生是一人，以惊其才；复动其情，而终愚之、败之以死之而示惩。吁，可畏也！)

    金丹
    诸城人刘姓，奴于臧姓，性耽杯酌，醉时随卧街市中，里人不与齿列也。一日，与学斗饮，酩酊大醉，跛蹙不动，遂倒卧大成门侧。门故倾坏，与殿院相通，刘又移身入两庑神桌下，以畅其盹。
    夜半酒渴，起视秋空月白，照彻台趾。见古柏树下，有少年十数辈，丫髻双双，如戏蹴踘。抛掷小球，皆闪闪如灯，上下随身，旋舞不坠，以手承弄。刘视良久，踉跄突出，攘臂一呼，声振檐瓦。群鬼奔散，独剩一丸，跃跃地上。刘拾而吞之，顿觉神爽，而酒气拂拂从顶际出，遍身骨节皆鸣，固知为狐之丹也。

    Explanation

    339

    (Seven Like Master said: It is not the fox that is causing the disturbance, but rather the spirit of Jing Sheng. He defames and slanders the wise men of the past, and Heaven deliberately produced this person to shock his talent; then it stirs his emotions, ultimately making him foolish, leading to his downfall, and even causing his death to serve as a warning. Alas, it is truly terrifying!)

    Gold Elixir
    A man surnamed Liu from Zhucheng, who was a slave to a man surnamed Zang, was addicted to drinking. When drunk, he would lie down wherever he was in the streets, and the locals did not consider him worthy of being listed among them. One day, he drank with a scholar, getting completely drunk, staggering and unable to move, and eventually fell asleep leaning against the side of the Great Completion Gate. The gate was old and leaning, connecting to the temple courtyard, so Liu moved himself under the altars in the side wings to rest comfortably.
    At midnight, thirsty from the alcohol, he got up to look at the clear autumn sky and bright moon, which illuminated the base of the platform. He saw several dozen young boys with buns on top of their heads, playing a game of kicking a ball. They were throwing small balls that sparkled like lamps, moving up and down with their bodies, spinning and dancing without falling, and catching and playing with them in their hands. Liu watched for a long time, then stumbled out, raised his arms and shouted, his voice shaking the eaves and tiles. The ghosts scattered, leaving only one ball, which was jumping on the ground. Liu picked it up and swallowed it, suddenly feeling refreshed, and a stream of alcohol fumes rose from the top of his head, causing his bones and joints to make a sound. He knew it was the fox's elixir.

    340

    忽举念返，便至其家。刘大喜曰：“此真如意珠矣。”其妻正鼾鼾土炕，一茎灯方明灭，待刘归。刘蹑其妻，妻惊起曰：“汝何入不由户也？”刘曰：“吾得隐形五遁法。”妻訾其醉，曰：“夜过半，盍就寝？”刘忽思：“吾获此宝，何所不可？合邑好女子未遍阅历。如此良夜，盍快吾目？”于是举意一往，墙壁门径，一无障碍，鸡鸣始返。其妻涎其术，曰：“汝为仙，如吾累赘何？”刘曰：“是不难，彼处多于胡核，今夜当为汝致一粒。”次晚，刘复至县学东庑伏候之。二鼓下，闻有人互语曰：“昨马二水金丹被人拾去，不知所向。”众曰：“其人自庑间出，试搜之。”得刘，刘不能敌众，为众所缚，倒悬梁间索珠。刘告以吞入腹中。众以秫秸自其口贯腹，往来探取，如匙之投锁，珠出，血渍阶石，狐始散去。刘痛楚不能声。日晡，其饮友学斗来扫殿宇，见而解之，备述其苦。众掖之归，病三月始瘥，而经年嗽血，格格不休。

    Explanation

    340

    He suddenly changed his mind and returned home. Liu was overjoyed and exclaimed, "This is truly a wish-fulfilling jewel!" His wife was snoring on the earthen bed, with a single wick lamp flickering, waiting for Liu to return. Liu tiptoed to his wife, who woke up in surprise and asked, "Why did you enter without going through the door?" Liu replied, "I have learned the five ways of invisibility." His wife accused him of being drunk and said, "It's past midnight; why don't you go to sleep?" Liu suddenly thought, "With this treasure, what can I not do? I have not yet seen all the beautiful women in the town. On such a good night, why not satisfy my eyes?" So he decided to go out, and walls, doors, and paths offered no hindrance. He returned at dawn. His wife was envious of his skill and said, "You are like a deity; how can you bear the burden of me?" Liu replied, "It's not difficult. There are many hulunuts there; tonight I will bring you one." The next evening, Liu returned to the east wing of the county school to wait. At the second drumbeat, he heard people talking, "Last night, Ma Ershui's golden pill was taken by someone, and we don't know where it went." Someone said, "The person must have come out from the wing; let's search there." They found Liu, who could not withstand the crowd and was tied up by them, hanging upside down from the beam to search for the pearl. Liu told them that he had swallowed it. They used a stalk of millet to pass through his mouth and abdomen, probing back and forth like a key in a lock. The pearl was found, and blood stained the stone steps, and the foxes finally scattered. Liu was in pain and could not make a sound. At dusk, his drinking friend Xuedou came to sweep the hall and saw him, releasing him and detailing his suffering. They helped him home, and he was sick for three months before recovering, but coughed up blood for years, incessantly.

    341

    (儇薄子弟，好于暗中伺人亵事，安得遇二水诸人，一一悬之梁上，刺以梃哉！七如)

    小莲
    滕县之沙沟营李姓，有旧楼为狐所凭，人遂绝迹。楼上窗常自开合，往往见有老翁少妇依槛嬉眺。会当夏月，老翁正立窗前，忽窗格为风所刮，訇硼倒坠，老翁亦遂不见，至晚，闻哭声自楼中出。
    李姓有子名裕，新庠生，夜起见男女二三人哭而过，皆白衣衰絰，最后一女若回脸见生者。李视之，姣美无比，乃频频转顾而去。李曰：“此楼上狐也，岂老昧死耶？曷往吊焉？”乃取楮锞一串，摘缨，着素衣至楼下，亚霎方相，长旛悬于门，吊客往来几满。有候门者拱李入，行奠礼，觉孝帏有揭觑之者。李偷看，则昨日之顾盼女郎也，不禁心旌摇曳。遂故为鞠躬，使帽落地上，匍匐以首前顶之，如犬套柳圈状。但闻诸女眷哄笑不止。李乃徐徐戴帽而出，众挽之坐。忽二三女郎与一小儿约八九岁，皆斩缞杖出，跽谢阶前，见最后低首以目视李者，即女郎也。

    Explanation

    341

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    Translation of the Source Text

    Frivolous young men enjoy secretly watching others' intimate or shameful affairs. How could they have encountered the people of the Two Waters, one by one hanging them from the rafters and stabbing them with sticks! (Seven like)

    Xiao Lian
    In the village of Shagou, Teng County, there was an old house haunted by fox spirits, and people avoided it. The windows on the upper floor often opened and closed by themselves, and one could often see an elderly man and a young woman leaning on the railing and looking around. One summer night, the elderly man was standing in front of the window, when suddenly the window frame was blown down by the wind, crashing to the ground, and the elderly man also disappeared. By evening, crying sounds were heard coming from the building.

    The family of Li, who had a son named Yu, a recent graduate of a local school, woke up one night and saw two or three men and women crying as they passed by, all wearing white mourning clothes. The last woman turned her face to look at Yu. Yu looked at her and found her incredibly beautiful, and she frequently turned her head and then left. Yu said, "This is a fox spirit on the upper floor. Is the old one dead? Why not go to mourn them?" So he took a string of paper money, removed his hat, and wore plain clothes to the foot of the building, setting up a small flag and a long banner on the door. The mourners came and went, almost filling the space. Someone at the door bowed Yu in, and he performed the memorial offering. He felt that someone was peeking at him behind the mourning curtain. Yu secretly looked and saw the woman from the previous day. He could not help but feel his heart flutter. He deliberately bowed, causing his hat to fall to the ground, and crawled forward with his head bowed, like a dog being tied with a willow ring. But he heard the women laughing uncontrollably. Yu slowly put his hat back on and left. The others pulled him to sit. Suddenly, two or three young women and a child about eight or nine years old appeared, all in mourning attire, and they knelt to thank Yu at the steps. The last person to lower her head and look at Yu was the young woman.

    342

    无何设馔。李首屈，一人陪，询之，其大婿也，颇通款洽，既而大婿入复出，曰：“李相公宠临，真使泉下生辉。第窀穸在即，丧家男女皆幼稚无知，今欲借重衣冠，并一切指示成礼。不揣冒昧，托为转达。”李以女故，正欲联属，遂满应之。
    无何，一婢即来请李入庐，见孝男一人正嬉戏，孝女四人皆长跽，泣而谢。长女曰：“弟稚，不能当大事，百凡倚托鸿才。”李曰：“通家之谊，当效奔走。”睨视女郎，以袖掩口，正不辨其咷与笑也。李出，即为摒挡内外事，渐渐入室取什物。初大女应，渐至二女三女，李终不释然，必至女郎亦亲授受，而后已。

    Explanation

    342

    Shortly after, a meal was prepared. Li bowed slightly, and one person accompanied him. Upon inquiry, it was revealed that this person was the eldest son-in-law, who was very friendly and open. Soon after, the eldest son-in-law entered and then exited, saying, "The presence of Master Li is a great honor, truly bringing light to the afterlife. However, the burial is imminent, and the family members are all young and inexperienced. We now seek your assistance in preparing for the burial and ensuring that all rituals are performed correctly. We dare to ask you to convey this request." Li, due to his relationship with the family, was eager to help and agreed to assist fully.

    Shortly after, a servant came to invite Li into the house, where he saw one son in mourning playing and four daughters in mourning, all in a formal posture, weeping and thanking him. The eldest daughter said, "My younger siblings are too young to handle important matters; we rely on your great talent." Li replied, "As family friends, I will assist you." He glanced at the young ladies, covering his mouth, and was unsure whether they were crying or laughing. Li then left to arrange the internal and external matters, gradually entering the room to take household items. Initially, the eldest daughter responded, followed by the second and third daughters, but Li remained unsettled until the young ladies personally handed over the items to him.

    343

    至暮，设榻东厢，被襥温软。人散后，内只二三女郎。裕闭户不能成寝，起步中庭。月将西走，四无人声。入内，门犹半掩。李踅而进，视其灵帏，内皆寂静。旁有小屋，灯耀窗间，影闪烁，似妇人足，庋而动。疑之，就近谛视，则一少年与一妇人相狎，其声情颇觉动人，伏息伺之。既而少年谓妇曰：“小四姨今年绰约较甚去年，其胸次膨鼓鼓，想春心正窣窣痒。今岳翁又死，嫁婿当不知何日，真好难熬！”女嗤曰：“黄花女亦似汝猴急像耶？我今七未除，即被汝拦入勾当。谁家郎如这好房事？”李乃知为大婿，遂推户入。女惊起，絜裙顿逸，少年惭沮相对，不作一语。李曰：“夜未央，乃行露瀼瀼，而犯期功之丧！”少年谢过曰：“愿相公勿哗，我将为觅一良姻以赎罪。”李问为谁，曰：“少姨也。”李恐其诳，矢之乃散。

    Explanation

    343

    By evening, a bed was set up in the eastern wing, with warm and soft bedding. After everyone had dispersed, only a few young women remained inside. Ruy closed the door but could not fall asleep, so he started walking in the courtyard. The moon was moving westward, and there was no sound anywhere. He entered the inner chamber, where the door was still half open. Li turned and entered, looking at the curtain, and found the room silent. There was a small house with a light shining through the window, casting shadows that flickered and moved like a woman's feet. Suspecting something, Li approached closely to look carefully. He saw a young man and a woman being intimate, their sounds and expressions quite touching. He lay down quietly and waited. After a while, the young man said to the woman, "Little Auntie, you are more graceful this year than last. Your chest feels full, and I imagine your spring desires are itching. Now that your father-in-law has died, your husband won't know when he will come back. It's truly hard to endure!" The woman laughed and said, "A young unmarried woman is not like you, so impatient! I am still in mourning, and if you force me into this, who would be a good match for you?" Li realized the young man was the eldest son-in-law and pushed the door open. The woman was startled and got up, pulling her skirt and running away. The young man was ashamed and dejected, not saying a word. Li said, "The night is not yet over, and you are walking in the dewy night, disrespecting the mourning period!" The young man apologized and said, "Please do not make noise, I will find a good match to atone for my sins." Li asked who she was, and the young man replied, "She is my younger sister-in-law." Li feared he was lying and swore, then they dispersed.

    344

    次早，少年至，邀李起，碌碌丧务。又女郎捧椟请李题主，捧椟者，即夜来逸走女也，面辄红。李笑应之。至晚，少年谓李曰：“昨日之盟，荆妇已允。但须过百日，方可行。”李曰：“礼岂为我辈设哉？畴昔之夜，君两人所事何事，而乃律人明、自问疏耶？”少年又去。
    李将寝，闻窗前窃语曰：“姊姊大不是，何陷人至此？”但见女郎启管陡入，如后有人拥之者。女以扇障面，李急起抱持。女以扇指门，生阖户，并坐床隅。视女浑身素白，灯光之下，愈增妩艳。李乃极道垂青之意。女曰：“我固好觑人，未必于君独厚。”李求欢，女曰：“堂殡未七，不敢遵命。”李强之，女不能支，遂合就焉。李问女名，曰：“小莲，行四。”
    比晓，闻帏中号哭声甚厉，女惊听之。忽窗外低唤曰：“四姑，大爷爷自山西来。”李问女为谁，女曰：“我伯父也，最凶悍者。吾两人事恐中变，姑且去，俟有信息当告汝。”遂去。

    Explanation

    344

    Early the next morning, the young man arrived, invited Li to get up, and engaged in trivial matters. Then, the young lady carried a box and asked Li to write the inscription. The young lady who carried the box was the same girl who had run away last night, and her face turned red. Li smiled and agreed. By evening, the young man told Li, "My wife has already agreed to the previous agreement, but it must wait a hundred days." Li replied, "How could rites be made for us? What did you two do last night? Yet you are strict with others but lenient with yourselves." The young man then left.

    Li was about to sleep when he heard whispers in front of the window, saying, "Sister, you are very wrong. Why have you put me in this situation?" Then he saw the young lady quickly enter the room, as if someone was pushing her from behind. The young lady covered her face with a fan, and Li quickly got up and held her. She pointed to the door with her fan, and Li closed the door, and they sat together at the edge of the bed. Under the lamp, the young lady dressed entirely in white, her beauty even more striking. Li then expressed his deep admiration. The young lady said, "I always look favorably on people, I may not favor you more than others." Li asked for intimacy, and the young lady said, "The funeral has not yet reached the seventh day, I dare not comply." Li forced her, and she could not resist, so they came together. Li asked the young lady her name, and she replied, "My name is Xiao Lian, the fourth in my family."

    By morning, Li heard loud crying from inside the tent, and the young lady was surprised. Suddenly, someone called out from outside the window, "Auntie, my grandfather from Shanxi has come." Li asked who the caller was, and the young lady responded, "It is my uncle, who is very fierce. Our matter might change, let's leave for now, I will inform you when there is news." Then she left.

    345

    李起，则身卧楼下，一无所有。出亦不告人。家中疑其就馆舍，两日不归。至晚，李又来，则屋舍如昨。方踯躅间，见小莲仓皇至，谓李曰：“我伯父来，知大姊以我许汝，恚怒非类，今计欲害汝家，宜速归，举家避之，否将不利焉。”李不舍女，女泣曰：“我既以身许郎矣，当谋珠还也。”李曰：“汝伯何太不情？将亦思所以制之。”女曰：“难！难！”李尚欲言，忽闻声似驴鸣，奔飞而前。李出。
    是日，李家火，扑灭，其内室衣筮中又火。一日数次，所有衣服器皿荡然灰烬，食物中杂以秽恶，扰乱不堪。自此家无宁贴，遂僦他居。李每怀念小莲，魂梦皆杳。即时一至旧居，楼空人去，洒泣频呼，亦无应声而出者。

    卷十 神道类
    张睢阳
    黄州南门外安国寺，旧有睢阳张公祠。太守某，遍毁神祠，误以公像暴烈日中。太守一舆台隶，素来目不识丁，忽而发狂，瞋目怒骂，指太守曰：“尔以我为何人？敢来作践耶！”命笔札至，隶走书曰：

    Explanation

    345

    Li rose, and he lay on the floor below, having nothing. He left without informing anyone. His family suspected he had gone to a guesthouse and did not return for two days. By evening, Li came back, and the house was as it was yesterday. Just as he was hesitating, he saw Xiao Lian arrive in a panic and said to Li, "My uncle has come, knowing that my elder sister has promised me to you, he is angry at the mismatch, and now plans to harm your family. You should return quickly, and the whole family should avoid them, otherwise, it will be harmful." Li did not want to leave the girl, and she wept, saying, "I have already given my body to you, we should plan to return the pearl." Li said, "Why is your uncle so unreasonable? We should also think of how to deal with him." The girl replied, "Difficult! Difficult!" Li was still about to speak when he suddenly heard a sound like a donkey braying and rushed forward. Li left.

    On that day, Li's house caught fire, and it was extinguished, but another fire broke out in the inner room where clothes and divination tools were stored. Several times a day, all clothes and utensils were reduced to ashes, and food was mixed with filth, causing chaos and disorder. From then on, the family had no peace and rented another place. Li often missed Xiao Lian, and in dreams and in spirit, she was nowhere to be found. Whenever he visited the old house, the building was empty, and people had left. He wept and called out repeatedly, but there was no response.

    In Volume Ten, the category of divine ways:
    Zhang Suiyang
    There was an old temple of Zhang Gong from Suiyang in An Guo Temple outside the southern gate of Huangzhou. The governor, in his zeal to destroy all the shrines, mistakenly exposed Zhang Gong's statue to the scorching sun. One of the governor's servants, who was illiterate, suddenly went mad, glared at the governor, and shouted, "Who do you think I am? How dare you disgrace me!" He then ordered a writing instrument and paper, and the servant wrote:

    346

    皇天生我兮男儿，君王用我兮熊罴。力拔山兮风雷，气贯日兮虹霓。月正明兮拔枪捋剑，星未落兮击鼓掀旗。捣贼阵兮焚寨，脔贼肉兮充饥。食马革兮几尽，杀妻妾兮心悲。誓与死战兮身披铁甲，愿为厉鬼兮手执金锤。亦莫指我为张仪，亦莫指我为张飞，是张巡兮在世，与许远而同时。在东岳兮押案，都统事兮阴司。任蓬莱兮直殿，任酆都兮狱推。景佑真君兮人间封爵，忠烈大夫兮天上官资。漫濡毫而染翰，俾人世兮皆知。
    书罢投笔而仆。苏时问之，茫乎若迷。太守睹此灵异，悚惧无已。具牲醴，陈鼓乐，拜而舁归神座。至今春秋祀焉。

    判官须
    宁波樊道济，家贫乏资，不能谋省试之费。七月望，犹在牖下，未办行李。或劝之，以贫告。或曰：“此机何可失也！”赠以三金，乃行。

    Explanation

    346

    Heaven has born me, a man, and the king uses me like a bear or a tiger. With strength to move mountains, like wind and thunder, my spirit pierces the sun and the rainbow. At the full moon, I draw my sword and ready my spear; before the stars have set, I beat the drum and raise the flag. I attack the enemy's formation and burn their camp, cutting the enemy's flesh into pieces to satisfy my hunger. I have eaten almost all the horsehide, killing my wife and concubines, my heart filled with sorrow. I swear to fight to the death, clad in armor, and I wish to become a vengeful ghost, wielding a golden mace. Do not compare me to Zhang Yi or Zhang Fei; I am Zhang Xun, living in this world, contemporaneous with Xu Yuan. In the East Peak, I preside over cases, overseeing affairs in the underworld. In Penglai, I serve as a palace attendant; in Fengdu, I oversee the prison. I am the True Lord of Jingyou, with an earthly title; I am the Righteous and Loyal Doctor, with a heavenly rank. I write with a brush, staining the ink, so that all in the world may know. After finishing writing, I throw down my pen and fall. Su asked me, and I was lost and confused. The magistrate, seeing this marvel, was filled with fear and could not stop. He prepared offerings, set up drums and music, and carried the tablet back to its place of worship. To this day, they worship me in the spring and autumn.

    Judge's Mustache
    Fan Daoji of Ningbo, poor and lacking resources, could not afford the expenses for the provincial examination. On the seventh day of the seventh month, he was still at home, not having prepared his travel gear. Someone advised him, and he explained his poverty. Someone said, "This opportunity must not be missed!" He was given three gold pieces and then set out.

    347

    时岁歉，路有弃婴，人皆莫肯收养，且啼且饥，命将垂毙。道济见之恻然，即以所有三金，托道旁磨腐者夫妇善抚之。
    至杭，同考诸人皆厌其苦且贫，拒而不纳。独一僧与之相识，勉强留之。是夜，僧梦各府城隍，以乡试册汇进文帝，内有被黜者，尚欲查补。宁波城隍进曰：“樊某救人心切，是可中。”帝命召至。见其寒陋，曰：“此子貌寝，将奈之何？”城隍曰：“易尔须眉，可表丈夫。”樊之陋，无须之故，乃指一紫须判官曰：“尔其贷之。”判乃自颔下摘须，为之戴焉，如俳优所假者。僧醒，不胜诧异。次早披衣起，正欲告道济以梦。及相晤，见其向本无须，一夕之间，忽满腮萌动，若有飘飘之势，相与笑，不能止。道济不知其故，僧始言之。是科果中式。后归里，人异之曰：“昔之小人樊须也，今其君子多乎哉！”樊官至司李。

    Explanation

    347

    During a year of famine, there were abandoned infants on the road, and no one was willing to adopt or care for them; they were crying and hungry, on the verge of death. Dong Zhi, upon seeing them, felt compassion and immediately used all three of his gold coins to entrust the couple grinding soybeans by the roadside to take good care of them.

    When he arrived in Hangzhou, the other examiners all disliked him because of his poverty and hardships, and refused to admit him. Only one monk knew him and reluctantly kept him. That night, the monk dreamed that the city gods from various prefectures presented the provincial examination records to Emperor Wen. Among those who had been rejected, there were still those who wanted to be reviewed. The city god of Ningbo said, "Fan's heart to save people is so sincere, he should be allowed to pass." The emperor ordered him to be summoned. Upon seeing his poverty and shabbiness, the emperor said, "This man has an ugly appearance, what can be done about it?" The city god replied, "Change his eyebrows and mustache, to represent a true man." Fan's ugliness was due to his lack of facial hair. The city god then pointed to a purple-bearded judge and said, "You should grant him leniency." The judge then plucked a mustache from his chin and put it on Fan, like a mustache worn by an actor. The monk woke up, greatly surprised. The next morning, he got up and put on his clothes, and was about to tell Dong Zhi about the dream. When they met, he saw that Dong Zhi originally had no mustache, but in one night, it suddenly sprouted, as if it were floating in the air. They laughed together, unable to stop. Dong Zhi did not know the reason, and the monk then told him. That year, he indeed passed the examination. Later, he returned to his hometown, and people were amazed and said, "The former commoner Fan Ku was now a gentleman, how many more gentlemen are there now?" Fan eventually became a magistrate.

    348

    (七如曰：余俭于须，安得老判分惠半部耶？)

    折腰土地
    鉅野有张文翰者，屡赴童子试，不售，老于训蒙。尝偕其徒应考，弟子多获隽，而文翰辄被放，乡人号为“童生解子”。馆于某村口庙中。日夕课毕，诸童蒙皆鸟兽散，惟张一人而已。

    Explanation

    348

    Master Qi said: "I am frugal with my beard; how can I get half a part of the old judge's favor?"

    In Juye, there was a man named Zhang Wenhan who repeatedly took the children's exam but failed to pass. He spent a long time teaching young children. Once, he accompanied his disciples to take the exam, and most of his disciples passed, but Wenhan was always rejected. The villagers called him the "child scholar's interpreter." He taught in a temple at the entrance of a certain village. At dusk after the lessons were over, all the children scattered like birds and beasts, only Zhang remained.

    349

    偶当月望之夕，见门外有人蹀躞。张视之，一五十翁坐石上。庙前有积水一池，与月相映，须眉可鉴。张见其非本村人，问之，曰：“前村许姓，因爱此一泓水，故步月来游耳。”张延入，燃膏相对，瀹茗倾谈，颇称快。每夜必至，夜分而返。张固岑寂寡侣，得许甚契，促膝谈心，无有少间。甚至风雨过从，尝携杯酌就教也。
    日间曾不一至，张偶问及，许曰：“向不敢告，今交深矣，言无不尽。余前村之许茂修，五年前拖官谷无算，赴此水死。”张亦以久契，不为异，曰：“如君沉沦，将终于不返，遂郁郁久居此哉？”许曰：“不然。冥司如缢鬼、溺鬼以及虎噬、蛇伤，不比善终，皆有定额，五载为限。满之日，自觅替身，方准脱生。今期将届，别有日矣。”张曰：“百死不如一生，愿君早脱此厄为幸。”后许至，有喜色，谓张曰：“明日午，有男子来汲，索断桶沉，觅桶而溺，是我替身也。幸勿泄！”张贺之，夜深方散。

    Explanation

    349

    On the night of the full moon, Zhang saw someone pacing outside the door. He looked up and saw an old man, about fifty years old, sitting on a stone. In front of the temple was a pool of water, reflecting the moonlight so clearly that one could see the man's eyebrows and beard. Zhang noticed that the man was not from his village and asked him, "You are from the village of Xu, right? You came here to enjoy the moonlight and this clear pool of water." Zhang invited him in, lit a lamp, and they sat together, drinking tea and chatting, which was quite enjoyable. Every night, the old man would come, and he would return at midnight. Zhang, who was always solitary and had few companions, found Xu very compatible. They talked heart-to-heart, with no interruptions. Even during storms, they would sometimes share a cup of wine and talk.

    During the day, Xu never came. Zhang once asked him about it, and Xu replied, "I was hesitant to tell you before, but now that we are close friends, I can tell you everything. I am Xu Maoshou from the previous village. Five years ago, I owed countless officials and drowned myself in this water." Zhang, having known him for a long time, did not think it strange and said, "If you are doomed to stay here, will you remain here forever, feeling depressed?" Xu said, "Not at all. In the underworld, there are ghosts hanged, drowned, bitten by tigers, or wounded by snakes. All have a quota, with a five-year limit. On the day it is full, I will find a replacement, and then I can be reborn. This period is almost over, and there will be another day." Zhang said, "A hundred deaths are not as good as one life. I hope you can soon escape this misfortune." Later, when Xu came again, he had a happy expression and told Zhang, "Tomorrow at noon, a man will come to draw water. The rope will break and the bucket will sink, and he will drown while searching for the bucket. That will be my replacement. Please do not reveal it!" Zhang congratulated him, and they parted ways as the night deepened.

    350

    张次日于庙中窥之，果有人来汲，索果断，桶果沉，人果觅桶，则起而不溺，且汲以去。张以为许妄，及夜许来，曰：“我不忍此孤孽子也。有母八旬，瞽而待养，溺其子，是杀其母矣。亡羊补牢，犹未晚也。”二人相与太息。越日，许又谓张曰：“晨有少妇自东南来，以蒲扇蔽朝阳，为风吹堕入水，妇拾扇而溺。”张属曰：“如果得替身，尚须言别。”许应之。次早，张又伺之，果有妇来，果如许言。但拾扇洋洋而去，又毫不见异。候许来，张问及故，许曰：“又不谐矣。吾见此妇腹膨膨孕，将临蓐。溺之是二命也，如前善何？”张赞其德。自是二人订交聚首，此甘训诂，彼乐沉沦，曾不作一解馆脱厄想。

    Explanation

    350

    The next day, Zhang peered into the temple and indeed saw someone come to draw water. The rope broke decisively, and the bucket sank. The person searched for the bucket and rose up without drowning, then drew water away. Zhang thought Xu was lying. That night, Xu came and said, "I could not bear to see this orphaned child. She has an eighty-year-old mother who is blind and needs care. Drowning her son is like killing her mother. It's not too late to mend the fence after losing the sheep." The two sighed together. The following day, Xu told Zhang, "In the morning, a young woman came from the southeast. She used a palm-leaf fan to shield herself from the morning sun, but was blown off by the wind and fell into the water. The woman picked up the fan and drowned." Zhang said, "If she finds a replacement, she must say goodbye." Xu agreed. The next morning, Zhang watched again and indeed saw a woman come, just as Xu had said. But the woman picked up her fan and left calmly, showing no signs of distress. When Xu came, Zhang asked about the reason. Xu said, "It did not work out again. I saw this woman with a pregnant belly, about to give birth. Drowning her would mean two lives. How can it compare to the previous good deed?" Zhang praised Xu's virtue. From then on, the two became friends and met frequently. This one enjoyed interpreting classics, while the other enjoyed indulging in obscurity, never once thinking of seeking a solution to escape hardship.

    351

    许忽数夕不至，张悬望綦切。一夕许来，着新氅冠帻，后随一人，如厮役。张惊愕。许谢曰：“今真远别足下矣。冥曹以我前二事闻于帝，嘉之，授我河南滑邑李疃土地之神。刻当就道。今夜与君欲尽所言。”遂呼伻罗酒果，各相于悒。张曰：“君今脱离苦海，行见飞腾，莺迁指顾。如我轗轲一世，莫测荣枯，将来正不知作何底止也。”言罢，欷歔欲绝。许亦悲曰：“君无福相，虽一芹犹难撷也。功名富贵，自不可强。此地去滑只三百里，明春花暖，君可一游，我当为君不负囊橐。”张亦应之。鸡鸣，两人握手，洒泪而别。嗣后终夜寂然，张亦辞馆而归。

    Explanation

    351

    Xu did not arrive for several nights, and Zhang anxiously awaited his return. One night, Xu came, wearing a new cloak, cap, and headband, followed by a person who seemed like a servant. Zhang was startled. Xu apologized, saying, "Now I am truly leaving you forever. The underworld has reported my two good deeds to the Jade Emperor, who praised me and appointed me as the land god of Li Tuan in Huayi, Henan. I am about to depart immediately. Tonight, I want to say everything I need to say to you." He then called for a servant to bring out wine and fruits, and they both felt deeply moved. Zhang said, "You are now free from the suffering of the mortal world and will soon soar to great heights. As for me, I have had a lifetime of setbacks and misfortunes, and I cannot predict my success or failure. I don't know where my future will lead me." He sobbed almost to the point of breaking down. Xu also felt sad and said, "You have no auspicious appearance, even picking a single watercress is difficult. Achieving fame, wealth, and power cannot be forced. Next spring when flowers bloom, you can come for a visit, and I will not disappoint you." Zhang agreed. At dawn, the two clasped hands and wept as they parted. The entire night was silent, and Zhang left the lodging and returned home.

    352

    次年，张如其言，裹粮而往，不数日抵滑。至一村，村前有数人遮道而问曰：“先生吾神之故人张文翰乎？”张惊曰：“何以知之？”乡人曰：“前月村中家家得梦，梦神告我，今日有乡里来访，为神至交。我里中穆卜于明日为神开光首会。今先生果来，真奇验也。”张晨起盥漱，整衣入庙，见庙中神新塑，因祝曰：“故友张文翰如约来访，许君有灵，尚其鉴诸。”祝毕，张伛偻拜，而座上神亦如鞠躬状。众乡人乃扶张云：“毋过谦抑，神不安矣。”张乃止。于是张在村盘桓月馀，比户鸡豚。去之日，乡人于会中取二百金赆焉。张返里置田舍，称小康。至今滑村之中，犹有折腰土地云。

    Explanation

    352

    The following year, Zhang, as promised, packed food and set out on his journey. Within a few days, he arrived at Huá. Upon reaching a village, several people blocked the path and asked, "Are you Mr. Zhang Wenhán, the old friend of our deity?" Zhang was surprised and asked, "How do you know?" The villagers replied, "Last month, every household in the village had a dream. In the dream, the deity told us that today a fellow villager would visit, being a close friend of the deity. We are holding a divination ritual tomorrow for the deity's consecration ceremony. Now that you have come, it is truly a miraculous event." Zhang got up early, washed his hands and face, dressed, and entered the temple. He saw that the deity had been newly sculpted and prayed, "The old friend Zhang Wenhán has come as promised. If you are indeed divine, please bear witness." After finishing his prayer, Zhang bowed with a bent back, and the deity on the seat also bowed in a similar manner. The villagers then helped Zhang, saying, "Do not be too humble; the deity will feel uneasy." Zhang stopped. Thus, Zhang stayed in the village for over a month, visiting every household and collecting chickens and pigs. On the day of his departure, the villagers took two hundred gold pieces as a gift. Zhang returned home, bought land and a house, and lived in relative comfort. To this day, in the village of Huá, there is still a deity known as the "bent-over land."

    353

    (七如氏曰：友道消沉已久，如张、许，可谓死生一契。彼许之二善足称，固知张之生平，自有不异于许，声气应求，吾知其必有合也。
    这亦往往有雷同记之者，独折腰伛偻最新。)

    深深
    汉阳孝廉鲁柬，读书自好，性恬雅，寡交游。居家，茗碗香炉、草堂木榻，无不楚楚明洁。住滠口，瓦屋数椽，起小阁，颜曰“畹香阁”。生笃于伉俪，妻乙娘最幽娴。夫妻爱植花木，二人无事，相与分香弄色，挹翠摇红，顾而乐之。人谓闺房清福，鲁生占尽矣。
    阁房广可一亩，所种群芳外，更有西府海棠十树。芳时迷望，所谓胭脂欲滴，而爱护灌溉，靡不尽心。听其自开自落，从不令人拗取，示以可玩而不可狎。鲁曰：“弱质终年一花，犹人半生，只此几时好运，转瞬即过。其自爱当复何如？我辈忍而残藉，是诚何心哉！”夫妇相对芳菲，未尝不泫然欲绝。至蝶使蜂媒，莺俦燕侣，一入鲁园，栩栩自得，娇声尽态，机心为之胥化。

    Explanation

    353

    The way of friendship has been declining for a long time, but Zhang and Xu can be said to have a bond that transcends life and death. Xu's two virtues are worthy of praise, and I know that Zhang's life was no different from Xu's. If their spirits and aspirations align, I know they will inevitably find common ground.

    There are often those who record things in the same way, but the recent trend of bending the waist and hunching the back is new.

    Lu Jian, a scholar-official from Hanyang, is a man of letters who values his own studies, is gentle and refined, and has few social interactions. At home, his teacups, incense burners, thatched cottages, and wooden beds are all neat and clean. He lives in Shekou, where he built a small pavilion named "Wanxiang Pavilion." He is devoted to his wife, and his wife, Yibang, is exceptionally virtuous and elegant.

    The couple loves planting flowers and trees. When there is nothing else to do, they share in the fragrance and beauty, admiring the green leaves and red flowers, and are always delighted. People say that Lu Jian enjoys all the blessings of a harmonious home.

    The pavilion is about an acre in size, and besides the many flowers, there are ten trees of the Western Capital crabapple.
