During the flowering season, the blossoms are so beautiful that they seem to be about to drip with rouge. They take great care in watering and tending to the plants. They let the flowers bloom and fall on their own, never forcing them to be picked, showing that they can be admired but not taken advantage of.

    Lu Jian said, "A delicate flower blooms for only a year, just as a person's best years are brief. This fleeting period of good fortune passes in an instant. How should we cherish it? To be cruel and destructive is truly a heartless act!"

    When the couple admires the flowers together, they are often moved to tears. When butterflies, bees, oros, and swallows enter Lu's garden, they are so content and joyful that they display all their beauty, and even the most calculating hearts are transformed by the natural harmony.

    354

    一日生外出，乙娘坐阁中，觉墙头有人探视。乙娘觑之，乃十六七岁女子，盘百子结，丰姿韵绝，着松黄衫，向园中凝睇。乙娘惊异，起，出阁问：“是谁家姑娘窥我园中卉？”女曰：“奴前巷鞠姓。知娘子园中金钱花盛开，偶一探视。”乙娘曰：“盍入坐瀹一瓯茶？”女首肯。乙娘执花架代梯，女冉冉而下。登小阁，恰值生归，瞥见不及避，女子趋乙娘身后，俯身自弄衣带。乙娘曰：“有客。”生趋出，问乙娘，知为鞠姓女，颇动心。女辞归，仍自小墙出。从此花晨亭午，时来时去，生夫妻艳之，而不敢启，恐其恚而不来。然生访里中，并无鞠姓。

    Explanation

    354

    One day, the husband went out, and Yi娘 sat in the pavilion. She felt someone peeking over the wall. Yi娘 looked closely and saw a girl of sixteen or seventeen, wearing a hundred-children knot, with extraordinary grace and charm, dressed in a light yellow robe, gazing intently at the garden. Yi娘 was surprised, got up, came out of the pavilion, and asked, "Who is the girl who peeks at the flowers in my garden?" The girl replied, "I am from the Qu family in the front alley. I know that the money flowers in your garden are blooming, so I happened to take a look." Yi娘 said, "Why don't you come in and have a cup of tea?" The girl nodded in agreement. Yi娘 held a flower stand as a ladder, and the girl slowly descended, reaching the small pavilion. Just as the husband returned, he saw her in a glance and couldn't avoid her. The girl hurried behind Yi娘, bent over, and played with her sash. Yi娘 said, "There is a guest." The husband hurried out, asked Yi娘, and learned it was the Qu family girl. He was quite taken by her. The girl bid farewell and left, still exiting through the small wall. From then on, in the morning and at noon, she came and went, and the husband and wife admired her, but dared not invite her, afraid that she would be angry and not come. However, the husband visited the neighborhood, and there was no one named Qu.

    355

    会大比，生束装北上。临行，女子隔墙呼乙娘，赠以百花糖饼百枚，为新贵人壮行色。乙娘持以告生，生益爱之，因谓乙娘曰：“连日匆促，未遑访问。此女柔婉多情，见之令人忘味。注意在卿，曷为我图之？”乙娘曰：“郎君当勉图光大，努力青云。承君托，无不竭心。”丁宁而别。
    自此女子知生北上，与乙娘往来愈密，知女名深深。乙娘谓女曰：“尔态度堪怜，虽名闺淑媛无以过此。吾郎才品亦颇不凡。吾欲俟伊京旋，以赤绳系尔两人足尔。何如？”女曰：“妾久有言，但恐骇异。奴非人非鬼，非仙非狐，感君夫妇怜惜，愿托宇下。今蒙大娘相契，敢不唯命，第恐以非类见猜，忝君清华耳。”乙娘曰：“尔犹人，尚不可得，况神之来降耶？”遂不令其去，居阁中。晨女起，出园整理花丛，为乙娘分劳；夜与乙娘共枕，呼吸之间，香溢肌肤。

    Explanation

    355

    Just as the grand examinations were about to begin, the scholar packed his belongings and headed north to the capital. On the eve of his departure, a girl called out to Lady Yi from across the wall and gave her a hundred pieces of flower-scented sugar cakes to boost the scholar's morale. Lady Yi delivered this news to the scholar, who grew even more fond of her. He then said to Lady Yi, "These past few days have been so busy that I haven't had time to visit or inquire. This girl is gentle, tender, and affectionate. Seeing her makes one forget everything else. She is the focus, why don't you help me with her?"

    Lady Yi replied, "My lord should strive for greatness and rise to prominence. I will do my utmost to fulfill your trust." They parted with earnest instructions.

    From then on, the girl knew the scholar was heading north and interacted more closely with Lady Yi. She learned that the girl's name was Shen Shen. Lady Yi told the girl, "Your demeanor is pitiable, even if you are a virtuous lady, there is no one who surpasses you. My lord is also quite talented. I want to wait until he returns from the capital and tie the red thread that binds your feet together. What do you think?"

    The girl said, "I have had something to say for a long time, but I was afraid it would be shocking. I am neither human nor ghost, nor a god nor a fox. I am grateful for your kindness and wish to stay under your roof. Now that I have the favor of the great lady, how could I not obey? I am only afraid that you might suspect me of being an unworthy being and disgrace your noble household."

    Lady Yi said, "You are still a human, and you cannot be obtained, let alone a divine being." So she did not let her leave and she stayed in the hall.

    In the morning, the girl would wake up and go out to the garden to tidy up the flower beds, sharing the burden of work with Lady Yi. At night, she would share a bed with Lady Yi, and the fragrance would permeate their skin.

    356

    逾岁，得都中书，知生被放，大病旅邸。乙娘闻信呜咽，罔知所措。女曰：“无伤，妾往视之。”乙娘曰：“几千百里，岂裙钗所易至？”女曰：“不难，夜当发。”乙娘问归期，女曰：“三昼夜，当偕郎君返也。”夜分女至园中，袖中出五色帕铺地上，与乙娘作别，疾若飘风。乙娘举首北望，惟银汉之间一点黑子，如豆而尽。
    生在京病剧，延医罔治，幸逆旅主人颇贤，视汤药。午间，有人叩门曰：“南中鲁家人至。”延之入，则翩翩少年也。入视生，而生惘惘，瞪视而已。主人告以病，女谢主人。主人入内。女坐生床间，生执女手曰：“卿不似男，将毋是鞠？”女曰：“然。”生泣下。女诊生脉，云：“此病抑郁伤脏，犹可刀圭。”乃出药一丸，令生咽之，生觉周身温暖，竟体舒泰，顷刻之间而愈。

    Explanation

    356

    After a year, a letter from the capital arrived, informing that the protagonist had been exiled and was seriously ill in a temporary lodging. Upon hearing the news, the character wept and was at a loss, not knowing what to do. The female character said, "Don't worry, I will go and see him." The character replied, "Thousands of miles away, how could a woman easily reach there?" The female character responded, "It's not difficult, I will leave tonight." The character asked about the return date, and the female character said, "In three days and nights, we will return together with the husband." At midnight, the female character arrived in the garden, took out a colorful cloth from her sleeve and laid it on the ground, said goodbye to the character, and left swiftly as a whirlwind. The character looked northward and saw only a tiny black dot between the Milky Way, like a bean, which soon disappeared.

    The protagonist was seriously ill in the capital, invited doctors but they could not cure him. Fortunately, the innkeeper was very kind and looked after the medicine and soup. At noon, someone knocked on the door and said, "A messenger from the south has arrived." The protagonist invited him in, and he was a graceful young man. He entered and saw the protagonist, who was dazed and just stared blankly. The innkeeper told him about the protagonist's illness, and the young man thanked the innkeeper. The innkeeper went inside. The young man sat by the protagonist's bed, and the protagonist took his hand and said, "You don't look like a man, are you perhaps a spirit?" The young man replied, "Yes." The protagonist wept. The young man examined the protagonist's pulse and said, "This illness is due to depression and injury to the internal organs, but it can still be treated with a small amount of medicine." He then took out a pill and had the protagonist swallow it. The protagonist felt completely relaxed and at ease, and was cured in a short time.

    357

    曰：“梦耶？非耶？真耶？赝也？尔来何暮，几不相见！”晚饭毕，生命女襆被于生卧侧。生曰：“卿为鞠家女，何以改妆，数千里一人至？”女曰：“家中大娘得书，惶惑无地，妾固星夜来视。”生曰：“计程甚远，来日无多，何也？”女曰：“俟君返里，便悉其详。明日可束装也。”生挽之同枕。灯下摘去衣冠袜履，宛然前日粉面凤尖，毫不差错，而气息如百花竞馥，沁人心脾。生荡思求合，女曰：“愿少安而勿躁也，妾已许郎，固远接郎归去。今病少愈，不可以苟。”生怜之絮絮。

    Explanation

    357

    The student said, "Is it a dream? Is it not a dream? Is it real? Is it fake? Why have you been away for so long, almost not seen each other!" After dinner, the life-giving woman placed a quilt and pillow next to the student's side. The student asked, "You are from the family of Ju, why did you change your appearance and come here alone?" The woman replied, "My mother received a letter and was in a state of confusion, so I came here in a hurry." The student asked, "The journey is very long, and there is little time left, why is that?" The woman said, "Once you return home, everything will be explained. You can pack your belongings tomorrow." The student pulled her to share the same bed. In the dim light, they removed their clothes and shoes. Her appearance was exactly as it was the previous day, with a delicate face and graceful features, not a bit different, and her breath was fragrant like competing flowers, refreshing the heart and lungs. The student had a desire for intimacy, and the woman said, "Please be patient and do not act rashly. I have already promised you and am recovering from illness, so it is not appropriate." The student showed compassion and was persistent.

    358

    晓起，生霍然。谢主人辞归。觅车马出彰仪门，女曰：“可令舆返。我已备长行，前途不远来迎也。”遂卸行李，坐道旁，来人俱去。女取帕，置行李，携生一蹈，倏然而起。生骇欲坠，女搂之。时北风习习，女又掷一帕，直竖云表，如江上晴帆高挂满饱。生觉身在舫中，行云际，则冲絮而过。生见下方一道黄流，曲折不断，问曰：“此何处？”女曰：“郎不闻天上来者？即此水也。”既而风微帕卷，指顾之间，已在故园阁下矣。

    Explanation

    358

    Dawn rose, and the protagonist suddenly felt a change. He thanked the host and bid farewell to return home. Seeking a carriage and horses to leave through the Zhiyi Gate, the girl said, "You can have the carriage return. I have already prepared for the long journey, and I will come to meet you shortly ahead." So he unloaded his luggage and sat by the roadside, and all the people left. The girl took a handkerchief, placed it on the luggage, and carried the protagonist in one step, suddenly rising. The protagonist was frightened as if about to fall, and the girl embraced him. At that time, the north wind was gentle, and the girl threw another handkerchief, which stood upright in the clouds, like a full sail of a sunny day on the river. The protagonist felt as if he were in a boat, traveling through the clouds, and passed through the cotton-like clouds. He saw a yellow stream below, bent and continuous, and asked, "Where is this place?" The girl replied, "Have you not heard of those who come from heaven? This is the water." Soon, the wind gently rolled up the handkerchief, and in the blink of an eye, they were already at the pavilion of the old garden.

    359

    乙娘惊起来迎，夫妻各相慰。由是生闭门高尚，无意腾达。遂纳女为妾，坐卧不离。鱼水之欢，虽南面王不易也。乙娘嬖女如亲娣，衣履与易，更无间言。而女之事乙娘也备挚。后女生一子，皙肥如瓠，长慧，身轻举，十二入庠，称神童。生年八十馀，夫妻相继无疾而终。女措办后事，竭尽诚敬。逾月馀亦卒，香满一室。入殓后，举棺皆空。其子知为解去。是年里中见天际一物，蔽月如篷，望南而飞。至今鲁园花卉，犹啧啧称盛焉。

    泗州城隍
    有司马崧者，字松山。宜章人，知名之士。赴省试，泊舟耒阳。夜静，闻岸上有书声，慕之，舍舟而徙。见一破屋，四围败堵，棂间灯火闪烁。崧窥视，一人岸帻危坐，呕呕苦读，相间似哭。崧心动，叩门请见。生延入，通姓氏。自言陈十洲，邑庠生，偃蹇场屋。崧曰：“长沙之行何时？”生对以乏资。崧慨然自任，促其同往。陈即卷书整箧，阖户反关，与崧登舟。

    Explanation

    359

    乙娘惊起来迎，夫妻各相慰。由是生闭门高尚，无意腾达。遂纳女为妾，坐卧不离。鱼水之欢，虽南面王不易也。乙娘嬖女如亲娣，衣履与易，更无间言。而女之事乙娘也备挚。后女生一子，皙肥如瓠，长慧，身轻举，十二入庠，称神童。生年八十馀，夫妻相继无疾而终。女措办后事，竭尽诚敬。逾月馀亦卒，香满一室。入殓后，举棺皆空。其子知为解去。是年里中见天际一物，蔽月如篷，望南而飞。至今鲁园花卉，犹啧啧称盛焉。

    The City God of Sizhou
    There was a man named Sima Song, with the courtesy name Songshan. He was a renowned scholar from Yizhang. On his way to the provincial examination, he docked his boat in Laiyang. One quiet night, he heard the sound of reading on the shore and was moved by it. He left his boat and moved to a nearby dilapidated house, where he saw through a window a man wearing a hat and sitting upright, reading with great effort, as if crying. Moved by this, Song knocked on the door and asked to be admitted. The man invited him in and introduced himself as Chen Shizhou, a student of the local school, who had repeatedly failed the examinations. Song asked, "When are you going to Changsha?" Chen replied that he lacked the means. Song generously offered to help and urged him to go with him. Chen immediately packed his books and boxes, closed the door, and boarded the boat with Song.

    360

    辰发，三日抵省，共栖止焉。崧匆匆场事。生则终日寝，夜起向隅而泣。所读多非时艺，皆古人哀怨之作，如《招魂》、《山鬼》、《古战场》、《祭十二郎文》，尤为三复不辍。崧谓陈曰：“将届试期，人皆殷然，子独漠然，何也？”陈曰：“我疾作，不可以战。”崧不能强。三场毕，陈谓崧曰：“松山高第矣。”崧曰：“何以言之？”陈曰：“首艺绝佳，用‘高辛才子’八人，对以‘宁王世弟’八人，可谓工力悉敌，前茅必拔。”崧愕然。陈曰：“我观主文衡者，皆无根行实际。视其顶上，常出秽气熏人，恐不能不颠倒误人耳。”揭晓，竟落孙山，陈为之大痛。崧曰：“我之被放，诚不如兄之抱病也。文固不足凭，而命竟何如乎？”相与慰藉。崧乃治任，属陈同行，陈诺。舟中二人茶铛酒碗，颇不寂寞。至耒阳，陈告归，崧订后期。陈曰：“小春当造庐耳。”生握别，依依灞岸，如有所失。

    Explanation

    360

    At dawn, they set out and arrived at the province in three days, staying together at an inn. Song was busy with the examination affairs. The other person slept all day and, at night, got up and wept facing a corner. What he read mostly consisted of ancient lamentations and grievances, such as "The Lament," "The Mountain Spirit," "The Ancient Battlefield," and "The Memorial to My Nephew," which he repeatedly pondered over without stopping. Song said to Chen, "As the exam approaches, everyone is eager, but you alone seem indifferent. Why is that?" Chen replied, "I am ill and cannot compete." Song could not force him. After the three exams, Chen told Song, "You will surely rank high." Song asked, "How do you know?" Chen said, "Your first essay is excellent. You used 'the talented sons of Emperor Ku' eight times and matched it with 'the younger brothers of Prince Ning' eight times. This can be considered a perfect balance of skill, and your essay will surely be selected as the best." Song was astonished. Chen added, "I have observed the examiners; they lack genuine virtue and practical ability. Looking at their heads, they often emit noxious fumes that affect others. I fear they may not fail to misjudge the candidates." When the results were announced, Song failed, and Chen was greatly distressed. Song said, "My failure is indeed not as bad as your illness. Writing may not be reliable, but what of fate?" They consoled each other. Song then prepared his belongings and asked Chen to accompany him, to which Chen agreed. On the boat, the two of them, with their tea pots and wine cups, were not at all lonely. Upon reaching Leiyang, Chen returned home, and Song scheduled a later meeting. Chen said, "I will visit your home in the next spring." They clasped hands in farewell, reluctantly parting at the Bashi Riverbank, as if something were lost.

    361

    崧归，至十月而陈不至，崧如望岁。有《岁暮怀人》二绝云：
    不见耒阳陈十洲，霜花千点故人秋。当时几树萧疏柳，难绾江心离别舟。
    相逢不啻十年交，杯酒论文客意消。何事雁回湘水上，教人懒听五更潮。
    一日，陈生忽至，又偕一友来。崧喜，各道离思。又问友人姓氏，陈曰：“此山东许伯端也。吾于伯端之文，师之也；于诗歌，则友之而已矣。”许笑曰：“陈良，楚产也，奈何从许子之道哉？”陈曰：“兄北方之学，莫之先者。”相与狂喜。遂亦与崧通款曲。崧见许如澄波千顷，汪汪大度，愈加钦佩。于是三人跫然足音，鼎峙而立，有缺一不可之势。崧乃额其斋曰“岁寒知晚轩”。轩外旧有松一本，自取为号者；更植梅花竹树，复为图以纪之。所谓相遇不疏，以相与于有成也，名曰“三友图”。许有诗曰：

    Explanation

    361

    Sung returned home, and by October, Chen had still not arrived, and Sung was as if waiting for the harvest. He wrote two quatrains titled "Year-End Thoughts of People":

    Not seeing Chen Shizhou from Leiyang,
    A thousand points of frost flowers on an old friend's autumn.
    At that time, a few sparse willows,
    Hard to tie the boat of parting at the heart of the river.

    Meeting is no less than a ten-year friendship,
    Discussing literature over a cup of wine, the guest's intention fades.
    Why do geese return to the Xiang River,
    Making one lazy to listen to the early morning tide.

    One day, Chen suddenly arrived, accompanied by another friend. Sung was delighted and shared their thoughts. He then asked about the friend's name. Chen said, "This is Xu Boding from Shandong. In Xu's writing, I follow him; in poetry, we are just friends." Xu laughed and said, "Chen Liang, a native of Chu, why follow Xu Zi's way?" Chen replied, "Brother, your Northern learning is unparalleled." They were both overjoyed. Sung also became acquainted with Xu. Seeing Xu, Sung felt he was as vast and deep as a thousand-acre lake, with a broad and generous nature, and he admired him even more. Thus, the three of them stood together, like footsteps in the wilderness, forming a triad that could not be separated. Sung named his study "Age-Hard Knowing Late Pavilion." Outside the pavilion, there was an old pine tree, which he used as his name, and he planted plum blossoms and bamboo trees, creating a diagram to commemorate it. This is what is known as "Three Friends Diagram." Xu wrote a poem:

    (Note: The poem by Xu is not provided in the source text, so it is not included in the translation.)

    362

    尔我三人上画图，阿谁俊逸阿清癯。美人君子知何许，漫向山中忆大夫。
    陈有诗曰：
    冰霜非幸事，各抱岁寒情。身外无馀想，图中好共盟。
    忘年风更雨，要久死同生。漫赋嘤鸣句，区区求友声。
    崧有诗曰：
    千古论文结契真，每逢摇落倍精神。相与阅历寒中味，同是萧条雪里身。
    觌面未须伤晚节，素心端许说前因。莫愁吾道孤无侣，寂寞香魂更有邻。
    忽一日，陈、许托故出。夜归，二人若相私语。崧诘之，不肯言。固问，陈曰：“言之恐见异耳。我二人非人也，实鬼耳。生前淹滞名场，郁郁而没，无可告语。伯端与我有同情也，今冥中加科取士，名曰‘敦宿科’。盖以新进多无实学，凡有游魂幽彦、耗鬼闇修，其年分深远者，令造具历册以闻。此千载一遇，倘得一撷青紫，亦足偿我二人困顿之苦。”崧闻言，深为庆幸，更无疑异。至期二人遂杳，三日后始返。崧为慰劳，问以何题，许曰：“善为吏树德、不善为吏树怨论。”二人颇自负。

    Explanation

    362

    We three ascend to the painting, who is elegant and slender? The beautiful person and the gentleman, where do they come from? Aimlessly recalling a great man in the mountains.

    Chen has a poem to say:
    Encountering ice and frost is not a fortunate event, each holds the sentiment of enduring cold. Beyond oneself, there are no other thoughts, it is good to make a covenant together in the painting.
    Forgetting the years in the wind and rain, living together for a long time. Aimlessly composing a poem, seeking the voice of a friend.

    Song has a poem to say:
    A true covenant formed through writing over thousands of years, becoming even more spirited when encountering withering. Experiencing together the taste of cold, being the same in desolation in the snow.
    Not grieving over the late stage when facing each other, truly speaking of the past. Not worrying that my path is lonely without companions, the lonely soul has more neighbors.

    Suddenly one day, Chen and Xu leave under an excuse. At night, the two seem to whisper privately. Song interrogates them, but they do not speak. Firmly asking, Chen says, "Speaking of it is afraid of being seen differently. We two are not humans, but are ghosts. Before death, we were stuck in the field of fame, died with depression, and had nothing to tell. Bo Duan has sympathy with us. Now in the underworld, a new examination is added to recruit scholars, named 'Dun Su Ke.' Because new scholars often lack real learning, all wandering spirits, hidden talents, and wasted ghosts who have been secretly cultivating for a long time are ordered to prepare a list to report. This is a once-in-a-thousand-year opportunity, if we can even pick up the blue and purple (official titles), it would be enough to repay our two people's suffering of being stuck." Song hears this, and deeply rejoices, with no doubts. By the time, the two disappeared, and returned three days later. Song comforts them and asks what the topic is. Xu says, "The topic is 'The Good Official Establishes Virtue, the Bad Official Establishes Grievances.'" The two are quite proud.

    363

    至深更，许、陈出。半晌，陈独归，谓崧曰：“许伯端擢第二，我落榜矣。”言罢，泪落如雨。崧曰：“升沉变态，悉如梦幻。得何足喜，丧不足忧。固当为伯端幸，亦何必不自达观耶？”陈曰：“我挟此区区之志，五十年来，苦心孤诣，不获一售。至潦倒淹忽，终不灰颓。宁复计几经磨蝎，逢此机缘，竟成画饼。呜呼！悲哉，诚不可与命争也！今伯端扶摇直上，足为老儒吐气。我有褊心，不愿见之。”崧亦为之凄然。呜咽之顷，而十洲顿失所在。崧急相招，其人已杳，从此数日寂然。崧离索之感，大难为情。

    Explanation

    363

    At the deep hour of night, Xu and Chen left. After a while, Chen returned alone and said to Song, "Xu Borden has been ranked second, and I have failed." Upon finishing his words, tears fell from his eyes like rain. Song said, "The ups and downs of life are all like dreams. What is gained is not worth rejoicing over, and what is lost is not worth worrying about. One should naturally be happy for Borden's success, and why should one not maintain a broad perspective?" Chen replied, "I have held this humble aspiration for fifty years, laboring with great effort and dedication in isolation, but have not achieved any success. I have been destitute and obscure, yet never lost heart. How could I count the many difficult periods I have endured, and encounter this opportunity, only to end up as a mere illusion? Alas! How sad, it is truly impossible to contest with fate! Now that Borden rises swiftly like a whirlwind, it is enough to give a sigh of relief for the old scholars. I have a narrow heart and do not wish to see this." Song was also saddened by this. During Chen's weeping, the Ten Continents suddenly disappeared. Song urgently called out to him, but the person had vanished, and for several days afterward, all was silent. Song felt a deep sense of loneliness and isolation, finding it extremely difficult to bear emotionally.

    364

    一日，许峨冠至。见崧，深道喜幸。问陈，崧告之故。许曰：“相需殷者遇偏疏，十洲其老于无闻乎！”又告崧曰：“我已授泗州城隍，即日赴任。从此远别，相见无期。我当遣人觅十洲同我往泗也。君后当发，但场厄未满，犹须踬挫数科。”崧告以不愿求进。许曰：“此天定之数，不可逃也。”门外车马填溢，许遂别崧而行。
    后“岁寒轩”中，寂寥寡偶。每一念及，吊影伤情，未尝不嗟悼于室迩人遐也。崧五十岁中乡榜，截取五河令，升泗州牧。抵泗之日，崧宿庙见许，并询无十洲下落，相为凄惋。廨中崧独营一室，夜则许来谈宴。一切民隐舆情，无不预闻，故崧于泗有司马青天之目。凡民间鹅鸭之争，雀鼠之讼，诚有不敢渎我神君清听者。后许告崧曰：“兄可营后事，将不永于年。”十日前，清厘案牍无馀，遂卒于官。百姓哀之，如婴儿之失父母云。

    Explanation

    364

    One day, Xu, dressed in a tall hat, arrived. He saw Song and deeply expressed his joy and good fortune. When asked about Chen, Song told him the reason. Xu said, "Those who are greatly needed often face neglect; will Ten Isles grow old in obscurity?" He then told Song, "I have been appointed as the city god of Sizhou and will take office immediately. From now on, we will have a long separation with no more meetings in the future. I will send someone to find Ten Isles to accompany me to Sizhou. You will be promoted later, but your examination difficulties are not yet over; you will still need to face several stumbles and falls in the exams." Song told Xu that he did not wish to seek advancement. Xu said, "This is fate determined by heaven and cannot be avoided." Outside the door, carriages and horses were filled with people, and Xu then bid farewell to Song and left.

    Later, in the "Age of Cold Hall," it was lonely with few companions. Every time Song thought of it, he consoled his shadow and felt sorrow, never failing to lament the closeness in distance but the distance in relationship. At the age of fifty, Song passed the provincial examination, was appointed as the magistrate of Wuhé, and was promoted to the governor of Sizhou. On the day he arrived in Sizhou, Song stayed in the temple and met Xu, and also asked about the whereabouts of Ten Isles. They were together in sorrow. In the government office, Song alone built a room, and at night, Xu came to talk and feast. All the people's grievances and public opinion were known in advance, so Song in Sizhou had the reputation of "Blue Sky Governor." Among the people, disputes over geese and ducks, lawsuits over sparrows and mice, truly there are those who dare not disrespect my divine lord's clear hearing. Later, Xu told Song, "You can prepare afterlife affairs, as you will not live long." Ten days before, he cleared up all official documents and then died in office. The people mourned him like an infant lost its parents.

    365

    湘潭社神
    湖南湘潭镇有张姓者，走无常，恒数日卧不起。后以泄阴曹事，杖革之。然冥路悉熟，时或一游。会夜出，背后一人呼曰：“张大哥，有事奉恳。”张伫视，乃冥司肩夫石五也。石曰：“有鬻妇者夜觅舆，急无人，兄可与我舁之，得楮镪，当瓜分以佐酒资。”张曰：“冥中钱我固无用，我助一臂可也。”遂与舁一空舆，往至一处，门署“北郭福社”。张曰：“鬻妇者谁与？”石曰：“神也。”张异曰：“因贫乃仕，岂仕犹贫？今以一方保障，尚不能庇一浑家，何以官为？岂诚国而忘家耶？抑不足以养廉也？”顷内呼舆，一妇着蓝纻衣出，登舆；神敝衣破靴，惨沮送之，相与洒泪而别。张视神，故同镇滥赌秀才尹某也。

    365

    In the town of Tiantang, Hunan, there was a man surnamed Zhang who served as a messenger of death (wu chang). He often lay in bed for several days after performing his duties, likely due to the nature of his role. Later, he was punished for revealing secrets from the underworld (yin cao), which is considered taboo and punishable. Despite this, he was well-acquainted with the paths of the afterlife (ming lu) and sometimes visited it. One night, as he was out, someone called from behind him, saying, “Brother Zhang, I have something to ask of you.” Zhang turned around and saw Stone Five, a porter from the underworld (ming si jian fu). Stone Five said, “There is a woman being sold who needs a vehicle at night, but no one is available. Could you help me carry her, and if we get paper money (chu qiang), we can share it equally to supplement our drinking funds.” Zhang replied, “Paper money from the afterlife is useless to me, but I can help with one arm.” They then carried an empty vehicle to a place called “North Deity Temple” (Bei Guo Fu She). Zhang asked, “Who is the woman being sold?” Stone Five replied, “She is a deity.” Zhang was surprised and said, “He became an official due to poverty, but how can an official still be poor? Now, he is supposed to protect a region (yi fang bao zhang), yet he cannot even provide for his family (yi huan jia). Why does he serve as an official? Does he truly serve the country while forgetting his family? Or is his salary insufficient to maintain integrity (bu zu yi yang lian)?” Soon, a woman dressed in blue hemp clothing appeared and got into the vehicle. The deity, dressed in tattered clothes and worn-out boots, looked sorrowful as he sent her off, and they both shed tears before parting ways. Zhang looked at the deity and recognized him as a scholar or official from the same town who was known for gambling.

    In the town of Tiantang, Hunan, there was a man surnamed Zhang who served as a messenger of death (wu chang). He often lay in bed for several days after performing his duties, likely due to the nature of his role. Later, he was punished for revealing secrets from the underworld (yin cao), which is considered taboo and punishable. Despite this, he was well-acquainted with the paths of the afterlife (ming lu) and sometimes visited it. One night, as he was out, someone called from behind him, saying, “Brother Zhang, I have something to ask of you.” Zhang turned around and saw Stone Five, a porter from the underworld (ming si jian fu). Stone Five said, “There is a woman being sold who needs a vehicle at night, but no one is available. Could you help me carry her, and if we get paper money (chu qiang), we can share it equally to supplement our drinking funds.” Zhang replied, “Paper money from the afterlife is useless to me, but I can help with one arm.” They then carried an empty vehicle to a place called “North Deity Temple” (Bei Guo Fu She). Zhang asked, “Who is the woman being sold?” Stone Five replied, “She is a deity.” Zhang was surprised and said, “He became an official due to poverty, but how can an official still be poor? Now, he is supposed to protect a region (yi fang bao zhang), yet he cannot even provide for his family (yi huan jia). Why does he serve as an official? Does he truly serve the country while forgetting his family? Or is his salary insufficient to maintain integrity (bu zu yi yang lian)?” Soon, a woman dressed in blue hemp clothing appeared and got into the vehicle. The deity, dressed in tattered clothes and worn-out boots, looked sorrowful as he sent her off, and they both shed tears before parting ways. Zhang looked at the deity and recognized him as a scholar or official from the same town who was known for gambling.

    In the town of Tiantang, Hunan, there was a man surnamed Zhang who served as a messenger of death (wu chang). He often lay in bed for several days after performing his duties, likely due to the nature of his role. Later, he was punished for revealing secrets from the underworld (yin cao), which is considered taboo and punishable. Despite this, he was well-acquainted with the paths of the afterlife (ming lu) and sometimes visited it. One night, as he was out, someone called from behind him, saying, “Brother Zhang, I have something to ask of you.” Zhang turned around and saw Stone Five, a porter from the underworld (ming si jian fu). Stone Five said, “There

    366

    遂舁妇行，后一役随之。妇呜呜不辍，其役投杠慰之曰：“夫人勿过伤也。从来博之一道，无常负之理。倘主人一旦为雄，呼卢辄胜，则完璧归赵，犹反手也。”妇曰：“负心人殆以我为孤注耳，今何望矣！请从此辞。彼实负我，非忍相负也！”张知尹在生时嗜赌，产荡尽，后为博徒所困，陷以滚赌，冤死狱中。今死后犹不悛改，至割床头昵爱，甘心一掷，亦可哀也。行十馀里，至镇上社祠前，役入内，良久出，曰：“卢以金偿，不许以人代。盍舁之返？”张惫不欲行，石哀之，张不得已，复舁返北乡。妇入，闻内汹汹然，又欲呼舆。张苦其烦，躲隐处，逸而归。

    Explanation

    366

    The woman was then carried away, with a subordinate following behind. The woman sobbed continuously, and the subordinate, throwing down his pole, comforted her, saying, "Madam, do not grieve excessively. From ancient times, the way of gambling has no constant loss. If the master suddenly becomes powerful and calls for a win and wins every time, then returning something intact is as easy as turning one's hand." The woman replied, "The faithless person probably considers me as a last bet. What hope is there now! Please leave me now. He truly betrayed me, not out of cruelty but betrayal!" Zhang Zhiyin, in his lifetime, loved gambling and lost all his property. Later, he was trapped by gamblers, forced into a rolling bet, and died unjustly in prison. Now, even after death, he still does not repent, to the point of cutting off his closest affection and willingly betting everything, which is also pitiful. They walked more than ten miles to the front of the village temple, where the subordinate went inside and came out after a long time, saying, "The master pays with gold and does not allow a substitute. Why not carry her back?" Zhang, exhausted and unwilling to continue, was pitied by Shi, and Zhang, reluctantly, carried her back to the north village. The woman entered and heard commotion inside, and again wanted to call for a carriage. Zhang, bothered by it, hid in a secluded place and escaped and returned.

    367

    寤时天已曙，闻镇上人传社神增一夫人塑像。张至祠视之，果然。乃告曰：“此北郭之社夫人也。北社神与我社神博，北社负，穷不能偿，以夫人抵。”后北郭人来舁以归，至夜，其像仍返。屡舁屡返。今湘镇社主齐人也，而北郭之神犹鳏焉。
    (余于役彝陵，合郡守掾至丞尉，莫不从事于博。其胜者，虽属吏亦傲上台；负者，即长官且气沮于末僚，将不至北郭社神之去妻偿债也不止。呵呵！七如)

    Explanation

    367

    When I awoke, it was already dawn. I heard people in the town saying that the local god had added a statue of a lady. I went to the temple to see it, and indeed it was true. I then informed them, "This is the lady of the northern suburb's temple. The god of the northern temple played a game of dice with our god, lost, and was unable to pay his debt, so he offered this lady as compensation." Later, people from the northern suburb came to take her back, but at night, the statue returned. They took her back many times, but she always returned. Now the main deity of the town of Xiang is from Qi, while the god of the northern suburb remains unmarried.

    (When I was in service in Yiling, all the magistrates, officials, and subordinates were engaged in playing dice. Those who won, even low-ranking officials, became arrogant towards higher-ranking officials; those who lost, even high-ranking officials, felt discouraged by lower-ranking officials. They would not stop until the god of the northern suburb's wife was taken to pay the debt. Ha ha! Seven times!)

    368

    卷十一 鬼魅类
    刘碧环
    金阊怡亭王子、石顽刘子、鸳鸠、拳石李子、焦亭曹子，同下榻于平江抚署之“来鹤楼”。时在暮春，积润初滑，觉帘布绿云，燕衔红雨。昼长无事，相与共涉遐思。奚奴在旁曰：“此楼昔有仙人，请之则降，叩之则灵。”众闻之，遂盥手爇香，进盘盂，布米沙，悬榻竖颖，祷祀以求。俄而乩诩诩动，既而撇画成文，录诗一首曰：
    十年幽梦在墙阴，一夕无端碎玉琴。万种春愁人不见，彩毫飞处墨花淋。
    “奴碧环也。诸君子瓣香持至，于意云何？”时众以亚阑朱槛之间，木香正盛，乞一赐咏。清谈未已，佳阕早成。其词曰：
    不比杨花轻贱，不似梨花娟倩。棚底弄清芬，勾引蜂狂蝶恋。堪羡，堪羡，乱洒珠玑万点。调寄《如梦令》
    书毕，乩停，寂然。童子曰：“仙乎去也。”于是众惊其事，且爱其文。
    翌朝而又设坛焉。乃降曰：
    细雨花魂在，荒园草梦迷。不关蝴蝶舞，自有杜鹃啼。

    Explanation

    368

    Chapter Eleven: Ghosts and Spirits
    Liu Bihuan

    The Prince of Yiting in Jinmen, the scholar Shi Wan Liu, the couple Yujiu, the scholar Qianshi Li, and the scholar of Jiao Ting Cao, all stayed together at the "Laihe Tower" in the Pingjiang governor's office. It was late spring, and the accumulated moisture made the ground slippery. The curtains seemed like green clouds, and the swallows carried red petals like rain. The day was long and idle, so they indulged in deep, reflective thoughts together. A servant beside them said, "This tower once had a spirit. If you invoke it, it will descend; if you ask it, it will respond." Upon hearing this, they washed their hands, lit incense, placed offerings of food and water, spread rice and sand, set up a bed and writing materials, and prayed to summon the spirit. Soon, the divination tool moved gently, and then it drew characters that formed a poem:

    For ten years, a hidden dream lingered in the shadows of the wall; one night, a beautiful jade zither was shattered without reason. With countless sorrows of spring, no one is seen; where the colorful brush flies, ink flowers cascade.

    "I am Bihuan. The gentlemen have offered incense and come to me. What do you desire?" At that moment, the group was in the area between the red railings, where the fragrance of sandalwood was strong. They asked for a poem as a gift. Before their conversation ended, a beautiful poem was already composed. Its words were:

    Unlike willow flowers, which are too light, nor like pear blossoms, which are too delicate. Beneath the canopy, a clear fragrance is played, attracting the frenzy of bees and the love of butterflies. Admire, admire, the scattering of precious jewels in countless points. Set to the tune of "Rumengling."

    After writing, the divination tool stopped, and the room fell silent. The servant said, "The spirit has left." Thus, the group was both amazed by the event and fond of the poetry.

    The next day, they set up another altar. The spirit descended and said:

    In the fine rain, the spirit of the flower lingers; in the neglected garden, the dream of the grass is lost. It is not the dance of butterflies that matters; there are always the calls of cuckoos.

    369

    麦饭餐风耳，椒浆吸露兮。最怜愁绝处，日暮下鸡栖。
    众问其姓氏，又书曰：
    闻鸡起舞愧雄才，曾向元都观里来。玉格冰清谁与共，阮郎携我入天台。
    众又问：“是否姓刘？何缘羁此？是岂紫燕楼头，伤心关盼；黄鹤云外，返驾仙人乎？”又书一绝云：
    絮语如绵问欲连，前生误摘并头莲。深藏金屋空怜惜，翠绕珠围十七年。
    由是知其为金阁芳魂，玉楼幽艳，共叩示出处。又书一古云：
    我家原住隋堤曲，阿父相携戍鸭绿。十二十三学秦声，十四十五教弦索。
    裁得鸾笺写硬黄，吟成小句藏笥腹。亭亭二八入侯门，可怜匝岁尘埋玉。
    多情肠裂泪偷弹，床头湿透芙蓉褥。夜雨铃声泣马嵬，秋风草色悲金谷。
    词名檐铁付飘湮，瘦兰十卷谁来读。姻缘会合红莲客，何日妥予山之麓。
    众读毕，咸为嗟叹。复请埋玉之所。环曰：“诸君可谓怜香之至矣。”复作一绝云：
    高冢如山牧马牛，唐陵汉寝几曾留。不想千年石宝塔，但求一个土馒头。

    Explanation

    369

    Eating barley porridge and dining on the wind, drinking pepper juice and sucking dew. Most pitiful is the place of deepest sorrow, where at dusk the chickens roost.
    The crowd asked about her name and surname, and she wrote:
    Feeling ashamed of my heroic talent, I have heard the rooster crow and risen to dance. Who could share in my purity and clarity like jade and ice? The White Snake carried me into the fairyland of Tian Tai.
    The crowd asked again, "Are you surnamed Liu? Why are you trapped here? Are you the one who grieved at the head of the Purple Swallow Tower, or the one who returned from beyond the clouds on the back of a crane?" She wrote another verse:
    Soft, continuous speech, asking if we are connected, I mistakenly picked the twin-headed lotus in my past life. Deeply hidden in a golden house, I am pitied in vain, surrounded by beauty and luxury for seventeen years.
    From this, we knew she was the fragrant soul of the Golden Pavilion, the secluded beauty of the Jade Tower, and we jointly inquired about her origin. She wrote an ancient poem:
    Originally, my home was in the winding path of the Sui Dyke, and my father took me to guard the Yellow River. At twelve and thirteen, I learned Qin music, and at fourteen and fifteen, I was taught stringed instruments.
    I crafted a phoenix letter and wrote it on hard yellow paper, composing short verses and hiding them in my chest. At eighteen, I entered the noble gate, but for a whole year, I was buried in dust.
    With a loving heart, I secretly shed tears, wetting the bed with a lotus quilt. The sound of bells in the night rain wept at Ma嵬, and the color of grass in the autumn wind mourned at Jin Valley.
    The name of the poem was given to the iron eaves, but it was lost to the wind. Who would read the ten volumes of thin orchid? The union of fate and marriage, the red lotus guest, when will you rest at the foot of the mountain?
    The crowd finished reading and sighed in unison. They then asked where she was buried. Huan said, "You can truly be called lovers of fragrance." She wrote another verse:
    A high tomb like a mountain where horses and cows graze, the tombs of the Tang and Han dynasties have long since disappeared. Uninterested in the stone pagodas that stand for a thousand years, I only seek a simple clay mound.

    370

    书毕遂去。怡亭子曰：“前作有日暮鸡栖之句，旧闻东墙下有瘗骨，得毋即是？”众乃荷锸相从。深不三尺，果得遗蜕，玉质莹然。众急掩之，谋窀穸。
    环临乩致谢。拳石乃问：“夜台寂寞，何如尘世凄凉？”环即书曰：
    夜雨残灯太寂寥，风吹檐铁伴秋宵。断肠阕子无多句，只有零星旧数瓢。
    众乃请示《檐铁》、《瘦兰》二集。环曰：“《檐铁》词乃记来鹤楼隐事，未便浪示人间；《瘦兰》词，俟月夕录考。”众遂皆以诗吊，环一一步答。惟拳石构思不就，仅得二句云：
    往事追思血泪枯，音容回首已模糊。
    环即接云：
    感君吊我知君意，吟到伤心一字无。
    拳石遂搁笔，唯唯逊谢而已。自此诗坛笔阵无宁晷，更无虚夕，诗词不下千言，惜未尽录。
    会当深夜，酒阑人散，拳石一心皈礼，展拜而祝曰：“窃愿一睹仙容，得遂仰止。毋令人闻声感慕，空结遐思，致慨于霜露三秋之想，梦魂千里之情，诚为万幸。”环即画沙聚米，奋乩挥曰：

    Explanation

    370

    After finishing the writing, he left immediately. At the Yi Ting Pavilion, someone said, "The previous work had a line about chickens roosting at dusk, and I've heard there are buried bones under the eastern wall. Could it be that?" The crowd then carried spades to follow. Digging no more than three feet deep, they indeed found a discarded shell, which was made of jade and gleamed. The crowd quickly covered it up and planned the burial.

    Huan came to the spirit board to thank them. Quan Shi then asked, "Is it lonely in the underworld, or how does it compare to the desolation of the mortal world?" Huan immediately wrote:

    The night rain and dim lamp are too lonely,
    The wind blows the iron roof tiles, accompanying the autumn night.
    The heartbroken poet has few words left,
    Only a few scattered lines remain.

    The crowd then requested to see the Yan Tie and Shou Lan collections. Huan said, "The Yan Tie collection records hidden matters at the Lai He Tower, and it is not suitable to show it to the world casually; the Shou Lan collection, I will record it on a moonlit night for your perusal." The crowd then composed poems to mourn, and Huan answered step by step. Only Quan Shi could not complete his thoughts and managed to write only two lines:

    Thinking back on past events, tears dry up,
    The sound and appearance when looking back have become blurred.

    Huan immediately continued:

    Feeling your mourning for me, I understand your intention,
    Writing poetry until heartbroken, not a single word remains.

    Quan Shi then put down his pen, bowing and thanking humbly. From then on, there was no peace on the poetry battlefield, and there was no idle night; the amount of poetry and prose did not fall short of a thousand words, but it is regrettable that not everything was recorded.

    It happened on a deep night when the wine had run out and the people had dispersed. Quan Shi, with a single heart devoted to worship, bowed and prayed, "I secretly wish to see the divine face, to fulfill my admiration. Do not let others hear the sound and feel admiration, only to form distant thoughts, leading to lamentation over the longing for the distant past, the emotions of dreams and souls traveling thousands of miles, truly a great fortune." Huan then drew sand and gathered rice, vigorously writing on the spirit board:

    [The rest of the text is left blank as it is not provided in the source text.]

    371

    岂同仙女下阳台，蔓草扶苏莫浪猜。不必洪都寻羽客，一庭明月梦中来。
    旋作兰麝香数阵。拳石子心醉神移，于乩前折腰再拜。既乃就枕徘徊，情思缭乱，不觉身游于白杨荒草之间。顷见月色朦胧，风情料峭，又闻鸣珮丁珰，双勾蹀躞。恍惚之间，而碧环已姗姗至前，敛衽端肃曰：“蒙君雅爱，不避陋劣，忝颜冒渎，感与愧俱。”拳石惊喜过望，相与促坐。第见其非云非雨，欲合欲离，如来洛浦，俨在巫阳。于是爱而生敬，敬则邪念俱融。环曰：“妾乃维扬人。年十六，即为前抚军小星之选，来至节署。缘以宠故，遂遘妒根。百般凌辱，竟至魂销一缕，冤结千丝。幸遇太乙慈尊，以我屈死，拯出恶道，一切解脱。今已召赴骞林。惟是枯骨颓垣，尤望诸公怜而瘗之，则感佩厚德，永衔泉壤矣。”言罢，呜咽掩面而去。

    Explanation

    371

    A Fairy Descending from the Balcony

    Is it the same as a fairy descending from a balcony? Do not rashly guess, for creeping vines and the name Fusu. There is no need to seek immortals in Hongdu; a courtyard bathed in moonlight comes to me in a dream.

    The fragrant incense wafts several times. The person deeply moved by the incense, with heart intoxicated and spirit moved, bows twice before the divination tool. After lying down to bed, restless and wandering, emotions are tangled, and without realizing, I find myself lost in a desolate landscape of white poplars and withered grass. Suddenly, the moonlight is hazy, the atmosphere is chilly and crisp, and I hear the sound of jade pendants and the slow, deliberate footsteps. In a daze, the graceful and elegant Bihuan approaches, gathers her sleeves, and stands in a dignified and respectful posture, saying, "Granted your noble affection, I do not avoid being ugly and inferior, and feel ashamed for intruding. I am grateful and ashamed." The person, overjoyed and surprised, sits close together. Seeing that she is neither cloud nor rain, neither close nor distant, as if coming from the Luo River and standing in the Wu Yang, love transforms into respect, and respect melts away all evil thoughts. Bihuan says, "I am from Yangzhou. At the age of sixteen, I was chosen as a concubine for a former governor and came to the government office. Because of my favor, I encountered jealousy. In various ways, I was insulted and humiliated, eventually leading to my soul dissolving into a thread and my wrongs entangled in a thousand threads. Fortunately, I met the benevolent deity, who rescued me from an evil fate and liberated me completely. I have been called to the celestial garden. Only my dry bones and ruined walls remain, and I especially hope that gentlemen will pity me and bury me, so that I will forever hold gratitude for your great virtue, even after death." After speaking, she sobs, covers her face, and leaves.

    372

    拳石惊寤，遍告同人，并记其集唐十绝志别。其一云：
    漫道沧江吴楚分，独悲孤鹤在人群。春风一夜吹乡梦，回首姑苏是白云。
    众遂择虎丘贞娘墓傍葬之，颜其墓道碑云“刘碧环之墓”。

    胡曼
    胡曼一名断肠草，俗呼打破碗。闽广山林川泽之阻，虎狼虺蝮虽能害人，其毒尤亚于此。叶如茶，其花黄而小。一叶入口下咽，七窍流血，人无复生。惟急服山羊血可救，盖以羊食不死，故曰：“羊食大凉，人食断肠。”此物种类颇繁，枝叶多不能识别。奸徒取以毒人固多，而误中者亦复不少。又云被毒死者其魂尝附其根，迷惑来往之人而中伤之。每每无风能自招摇，诚妖物也。
    粤博罗某村，黎氏一女年十七。因随母探外家归，路傍见黄花的烁，碧叶如油，一茎暴长尺许，心异而摘之，插鬓间。归饭，花忽堕饭器中，女箸入口，一嚼旋吐。俄而肠绞痛，色变。家人问视，饭器皆黑裂，知中蔓毒，急觅羊血得之，灌女复苏。

    Explanation

    372

    Awakened by a stone, he informed all his companions and recorded a collection of ten Tang dynasty poems as a farewell gesture. One of the poems read:

    Though the river divides Wu and Chu,
    Only the lonely crane grieves among the crowd.
    The spring wind blows the dream of home in one night,
    Looking back, Suzhou is but a cloud.

    The companions then chose to bury him beside the tomb of Zhen娘 in Tiger Hill, and inscribed the tombstone with the words "Tomb of Liu Bihuan."

    The Hu Man
    The Hu Man is also known as Duan Chang Cao (Broken Heart Grass), and is commonly called "Broken Bowl." In the mountainous and marshy regions of Fujian and Guangdong, tigers, wolves, vipers, and other dangerous creatures may harm people, but the poison of this plant is especially severe. Its leaves are tea-like, and its flowers are small and yellow. If even a single leaf is ingested, blood will flow from all seven orifices, and the person will not survive. Only by quickly consuming sheep's blood can one be saved, as sheep eat it without dying. It is said that "sheep eat it and it is cooling, but humans eat it and it breaks the intestines." This plant has many varieties, and its branches and leaves are often hard to distinguish. Though many use it to poison people, there are also not a few who accidentally ingest it. It is also said that the spirits of those who died from the poison may attach to the plant's roots, confusing and harming those who pass by. It often sways on its own even without wind, truly a monstrous thing.

    In a village in Boluo, Guangdong, a seventeen-year-old girl from the Li family was returning home after visiting her relatives with her mother. Along the way, she saw bright yellow flowers and oil-like green leaves, and a stem that grew suddenly to about a foot long. Finding it strange, she picked it and inserted it into her hair. When she returned home to eat, the flower suddenly fell into her food dish. She picked it up and put it in her mouth, chewing it before spitting it out immediately. Soon her intestines twisted in pain, and her complexion changed. Her family investigated and found the utensils blackened and cracked, realizing she had ingested the poison. They quickly found sheep's blood and administered it, saving her.

    373

    同村麦秀者，尝求婚于黎女，黎父母嫌其贫，不许。是日，麦自塾中归。村外野篱边，见一女子衣服鲜洁，独立丛莽间，近谛，白皙而美。女招之，麦应声至前。女曰：“汝非麦门仔耶？”麦曰：“何知我？”女曰：“奴黎蚬妹。我父母虽拒婚，我固未尝一日去怀也。”麦喜，四顾无人，遂与投绿蕉密箐之中而野合焉。麦觉女口中芗泽宜人，乃抚其颐曰：“何物甘香乃尔？”女曰：“嚼槟榔耳。”乃舌舐出尖，如碧芽茶，麦吮咽之。忽闻有人呼蚬妹声，女曰：“吾家来觅我。”遂匆匆振衣，约以后会而去。
    麦至家毒发，家人问之，麦告以途中事。家人曰：“早晨黎女中毒，闻羊血救甦。当往乞其馀。”黎母曰：“无矣。”黎盖恐麦之不死，其女便不得生。坚请，黎氏终不与，家人空而返。麦捧腹泣曰：“我生以贫故，不能娶黎氏女。想我死后，冥中另换

    Explanation

    373

    A Villager Named Ma Xiu Once Proposed Marriage to Li's Daughter, but Her Parents Refused Due to His Poverty. On That Day, Ma Returned from School. At the Edge of a Wild Fence in the Village, He Saw a Girl with Fresh and Clean Clothes, Standing Alone in the Dense Grass. Approaching to See, He Found Her White and Beautiful. The Girl Called Him, and Ma Responded and Went Forward. She Asked, "Are You Not Ma's Son?" He Replied, "How Do You Know Me?" She Said, "I Am Li's Daughter. My Parents Rejected the Marriage, but I Have Never Forgotten It." Ma Was Happy, Looked Around and Found No One, and Then They Went into the Dense Green Banana Grove and Had a Tryst. Ma Felt the Girl's Mouth Had a Pleasant Fragrance, and He Then Touched Her Cheek and Said, "What Is This Sweet Fragrance?" She Replied, "I Am Chewing Betel Nut." She Then Licked It Out with Her Tongue, Like a Green Tea Bud, and Ma Sucked It In. Suddenly, Someone Called Li's Daughter, and She Said, "My Family Is Looking for Me." She Quickly Straightened Her Clothes, Promised to Meet Again, and Left.

    Ma Returned Home and the Poison Started to Affect Him. His Family Asked Him, and He Told Them About What Happened on the Way. They Said, "Li's Daughter Was Poisoned This Morning, and It Is Said That Sheep's Blood Can Revive Her. We Should Go and Ask for Some." Li's Mother Said, "No, There Is None Left." She Was Afraid That Ma Would Not Die, So Her Daughter Could Not Live. They Insisted, but Li Finally Did Not Give It, and the Family Returned Empty-Handed. Ma Cried While Holding His Belly and Said, "I Was Born Poor and Could Not Marry Li's Daughter. I Think After I Die, in the Afterlife, Something Else Will Happen."

    374

    一世界，或不至如此炎凉。黎氏女，我必妻之。”言讫遂死。而麦固不知其死，心忆篱落，乃趋而往。见黎女坐木棉树下，自靸其履。麦至，执手曰：“妹何来恁快？”女曰：“郎厚意相爱，妹已身许。今父欲夺我志，故来见郎，同适他所，以图永好。”麦曰：“奈未带得行李资。”女于袖中出一帕，皆黄白镪，示麦曰：“足用否？”麦曰：“何须太多。”遂与女行。
    数十日，而路行者觌面曾不问及。至闽，凡五六年，生二子。黎女常思父母，终日涕泣。言曰：“妹当时不忍负郎，违弃大义，竟蹈私奔之嫌。今经六载，恩慈间隔；即郎违乡井，亦裘葛屡易。覆载虽宽，何地不可容身，而抚心何处可容也？”麦然之，遂偕归。麦令女且在舟中，先自抵家。及门，门有戟髯者持铁蒺藜挝麦，麦奔。忆其家或他徙，欲往黎家，愤其岳，且羞见之，于是复返舟告女。女曰：“郎在舟，妹且当归告，来接汝。”

    Explanation

    374

    In another world, perhaps things would not be so cold and distant. "I will surely marry the daughter of the Li family," he said, and then he died. But Mai certainly did not know of his death. Remembering the fence, he hurriedly went to see her. He saw the Li girl sitting under a kapok tree, tying her own shoes. When Mai arrived, he took her hand and said, "Why did you come so quickly?" The girl replied, "My husband, you have shown me great love, and I have given myself to you. Now my father wants to take away my resolve, so I came to see you, to go somewhere else together, in hopes of a lasting happiness." Mai said, "But I didn't bring any luggage or money." The girl pulled out a handkerchief from her sleeve, filled with gold and silver coins, and showed it to Mai, saying, "Is this enough?" Mai said, "Why do you need so much?" And so they set off together.

    For several days, the travelers on the road did not even ask about them. They reached Fujian, and after five or six years, they had two sons. The Li girl often thought of her parents and cried all day. She said, "At the time, I could not bear to betray my husband, violating and abandoning righteousness, and ended up being suspected of eloping. Now after six years, the bond of love and kindness is distant. Even though my husband has left his hometown, the seasons have changed many times. The heavens and the earth are vast, there is no place that cannot accommodate one, but when one reflects on one's heart, where can one find a place to rest?" Mai agreed, and they returned together. Mai ordered the girl to stay in the boat and went home first. When he reached the gate, a man with a beard holding an iron weapon struck at Mai, who ran away. Remembering that his family might have moved elsewhere, he wanted to go to the Li family, angry with his father-in-law and ashamed to see him. So he returned to the boat and told the girl. The girl said, "My husband is in the boat. I will go back and inform, to come and pick you up."

    375

    女登岸，携一子，抱一子，至黎家。门人惊：“蚬妹病狂，奈何出行市上，拾人子女消遣耶？”女不答，笑而入。见黎父，拜于庭，诉其嫁麦至闽，养子来归之事。父曰：“汝患病数年，辗转床寝，何诡说为？速入自内。”忽黎母自内哗而出曰：“蚬妹出外来矣。”是时，女卧室中，忻忻起，趋而出，问之不答，至庭外，而庭中者甫下阶。是两蚬妹也，众皆见之。少则翕然而合。视其二子，呱呱于前，无所异。黎父母惑焉，使人验诸舟中，麦郎果在。女乃令人持女衫覆麦迎赘焉。

    Explanation

    375

    The woman went ashore, carrying one child and holding another, and arrived at the Li family's home. The people at the door were startled and exclaimed, "The oyster girl is sick and mad; why would she go out into the marketplace to pick up other people's children for amusement?" She did not answer, but smiled and entered. She went to the courtyard and bowed to the Li father, telling the story of her marriage to Mai in Fujian and the return of her child. The father said, "You have been suffering from illness for several years, moving restlessly in bed; why would you lie? Quickly enter from inside." Suddenly, the Li mother came out loudly from inside and said, "The oyster girl has come out from inside." At that time, the woman had joyfully gotten up from her bedroom and rushed out, but when asked, she did not answer. She reached the courtyard, while the one in the courtyard had just stepped down the stairs. These were the two oyster girls, and everyone saw them. In a moment, they merged together. Looking at her two children, they were crying in front, but there was nothing unusual about them. The Li parents were confused, so they sent someone to check in the boat, and indeed, Mai was there. The woman then had someone bring her dress to cover Mai and welcome him as a son-in-law.

    376

    麦至，人不能见，独与女同处。麦家人闻之来视，皆女为之传言，不得见麦之形声也。惟黎翁待婿若稍有芥蒂，则百般扰乱，甚至门窗瓶盎悉为灾，故奉之如神明焉。
    先君子署博罗令，欲尽草根以除民患，下令曰：“凡以事告理者，须拔数十本与词来，然后得进。”樵人不可得，黎女携其
    子往采，则盈捆载。邑人皆鬻之，于是麦氏富而妖草尽矣。
    (是《聊斋·水莽草》一段情景脱化出来。七如
    观此，则倩女离魂，合抱为一，当不虚也。傅声谷)

    泥鬼博

    Explanation

    376

    When Mc arrived, people could not see him, and he was only seen in the company of a woman. The Mc family heard about it and came to see, but all the women were the ones who relayed the message, and they could not see Mc's form or voice. Only Lao Li treated his son-in-law with any slight resentment, and then caused all sorts of disturbances, even destroying the doors, windows, bottles, and vases, so they treated him like a deity.

    The author's father, appointed as the magistrate of Boluo, wanted to eradicate the roots of the grass to eliminate the people's suffering. He issued an order: "Whenever someone comes to report an issue, they must bring dozens of roots and words with them, only then can they be admitted." The woodcutters could not be found, so the woman from the Li family took her son to gather them, and they were gathered in full bundles. The townspeople all sold them, thus the Mc family became wealthy, and the evil grass was completely eradicated.

    (This is a scene adapted from Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio: The Water Grass. By Chiu Ju.
    Observing this, one can see that the soul of the fair maiden has merged with Mc, and it is not false. Fu Shenggu)

    The Mud Ghost Bo

    377

    豫章灵官庙，为阛阓幽静之所。庙久残蚀，其肖像皆有神色，相传非当时人工所能。乞丐无赖常聚于此，夜则樗蒲幺掷之声连宵达旦。耽于博者，往往不计美恶。
    陈一士有赌癖，时或囊涩，便觅小局。每一往博。庙中皆破落子，见陈至，咸趋迎之，故陈亦乐就。既而陈赌资愈窘，而入庙频频。庙故无门钥，来者忽去，而去者亦复可来，更柝者不屑稽留巡于此。
    时有短须人来博，衣履如胥役状。凡掷皆红，亦不作呼卢势，入手固无不如意。场上皆不识为何许人，问其里居，皆不答。每夜深来入局，晓筹未唱，则兜肚垂垂满腰以去。陈姓及诸人连日颇为所窘。即易局设法，亦无不见负于彼，咸以为异。局散，尾之，至门而忽没。逾夕复来，众乃哗，短须者张皇而遁，后不复来。
    会春淫雨弥月，满城舍漏垣颓。庙门有塑泥马二，作两泥鬼羁之。其一鬼短髭，忽身旁马渗倒，腹中钱堆满地上。众争取，约十馀缗，举首见泥鬼，酷类前之博者，乃悟为此物作祟。

    Explanation

    377

    The Temple of the Spirit Official in Yuzhang is a place that is bustling yet quiet. The temple has been deteriorating for a long time, and its statues have a lifelike appearance, which is said to be beyond the skill of the artisans of that time. Beggars and ruffians often gather there, and at night, the sound of gambling with dice continues day and night. Gamblers, often careless of the consequences, frequently do not consider the good or bad outcomes.

    Chen Yishu, who has a gambling habit, sometimes finds himself broke and seeks out small gambling circles. Whenever he goes to gamble, the temple is filled with poor and disreputable people who welcome him upon his arrival, making him happy to join. However, Chen's gambling funds become increasingly scarce, and he visits the temple more frequently. The temple has no door lock, and people come and go as they please, with no one bothering to check or patrol the area.

    One night, a man with a short mustache came to gamble, dressed like a government clerk. Every time he rolled the dice, they landed on red, and he did not need to shout or make any gestures; he always won. No one knew who this man was or where he lived, and he did not answer when asked. He often came to the gambling circle late at night and left with a heavily loaded undergarment the next morning. Chen and the others were often at a disadvantage against him, and they all found this strange. After the game, they followed him, but he suddenly disappeared at the temple gate. The next night, he came again, and the crowd became noisy, causing the short-mustached man to panic and flee, and he did not return.

    It happened that during the spring, there was a prolonged period of heavy rain, causing widespread damage to houses and walls across the city. At the temple entrance, there were two clay horses, each tied to a clay statue of a ghost or demon. One of the clay statues had a short mustache, and suddenly, the horse beside it collapsed, revealing a pile of money inside. The crowd rushed to take the money, about ten strings of coins, and when they looked up, they saw the clay statue, which closely resembled the gambler, realizing that the statue was causing trouble.

    378

    噫！博何常之有？得之于人，终亦失之于人。至于泥鬼，且不甘心于一掷。然则博者皆鬼也，博亦奚独鬼也哉！

    鬼酌
    博山多佳山水。有市井人尚可法，夜欲登山玩月。至半崖，有人呼之，遂与共坐，倾酒共酌。其斟杯皆凸起寸许，隆然不溢涓沥。既而大醉，酩酊而归，于时惘然。夜半微醒，因忆其人是孙姓名起，死于酒已数年。
    翌旦，往视其坐处，空樽在焉，竟亦无恙。
    (按沈石田有《挽醉死黄道士》一律云：“汝师因醉死，汝死亦师如。坟以糟丘筑，碑当酒德书。足跏趺后折，面胀疽成虚。身化难留影，吾诗妙写渠。”最佳。)

    娟娟
    张如瞻，鲁人。幼孤，为诸生，游学晋梁间，以笔代耕，就壶关作书记。居署之东偏“古香书屋”后，草茨三间。琴书之外，了无长物。日与前庭谈饮，晚间营营作鱼雁使。斋外荒亭一区，有老楸树数株，风萧萧响。

    Explanation

    378

    Ah! What is gambling but a fleeting thing? What is gained from others will ultimately be lost to others. As for greedy ghosts, they are not willing to give up in one throw. Thus, all gamblers are like ghosts, and why should gambling itself be considered a ghost?

    Ghostly Drink

    Bo Mountain is renowned for its beautiful landscapes. There was a commoner named Sun Qi, who, despite his humble status, was still worthy of respect. One night, he wished to climb the mountain to enjoy the moonlight. As he reached halfway up the cliff, someone called out to him, and they sat together, sharing wine and toasting. The cups they used were convex, protruding by an inch or so, bulging without overflowing even a drop. Soon, he became drunk and returned in a state of drunken stupor, feeling bewildered. In the middle of the night, he woke up slightly and remembered that the person he had met was named Sun Qi, who had died from alcohol many years ago. The next morning, he went to look at the place where they had sat, and the empty cups were still there, yet there was no harm.

    (Shen Shitang wrote a poem titled "Elegy for the Taoist Priest Who Died from Drinking," which reads: "Your teacher died from drinking, and you, too, died like your teacher. The tomb was built on a hill of wine lees, and the stele should be inscribed with the virtues of wine. His feet were folded and then broken, his face swelled with sores and became hollow. His body transformed and left no trace, and my poem beautifully depicts him." This poem is considered the best.)

    Jian Jian

    Zhang Ruzhan was from the state of Lu. Orphaned at a young age, he became a student and traveled to study in the regions of Jin and Liang, earning a living with his pen. He served as a secretary in the office of the governor of Huguan, living in the eastern wing of the official residence, behind the "Ancient Fragrance Study." His simple dwelling consisted of three thatched rooms. Outside his books and instruments, he had nothing else. He spent his days chatting and drinking in the front courtyard, and at night, he was busy like a messenger bird. Outside his study, there was a desolate pavilion with several old catalpa trees, and the wind whistled through them.

    379

    更阑独坐，童子垂头。方凄恻间，忽闻斋外有人吟曰：
    一年容易送春风，打叠秋声月影空。捱到夜深传舍静，怕人还步画栏东。
    反覆吟咏，声楚楚，听之细婉如女子。明日起视亭前，杳无踪迹。逾夕二更后，吟如故。张潜步往，声顿辍。良久，隐约间有女坐树根，俯首低吟，张甫动，女遂杳然而没。
    张初以为署内官眷，今乃悟其为鬼，然心窃慕之。由是常徙倚亭际，朗吟而和之曰：
    荒原飒飒下西风，孤馆萧然花事空。料得芳魂与客梦，一般凄楚隔墙东。
    张归就榻，忽见一丽人来，敛衽谢曰：“君子风雅士也，妾多所畏避。”张惊喜，挽之坐。女秀俊宜人，大家举步。张问为谁，答曰：“妾前邑侯韩凤山女也，钱塘人，字娟娟。生前好食酸杏子，因误食双仁核中毒，十六岁殂。今停柩城外，魂固依署中。所吟己作，蒙君致和，光生泉壤。”张喜，与为欢会。自此靡夕不至。

    Explanation

    379

    Deep into the night, I sat alone, and the young servant hung his head. Just as I was feeling deeply sorrowful, I suddenly heard someone chanting outside my study:

    "A year passes easily, sending away the spring breeze. The sounds of autumn gather, and the moonlight is empty. Enduring until the inn becomes quiet at night, I fear people will step onto the eastern painted railing."

    The chanting was repeated, the voice clear and distinct, soft and gentle like that of a woman. The next morning, I looked in front of the pavilion and found no trace. After midnight, during the second watch, the chanting continued as before. I stealthily went to investigate, and the sound suddenly stopped. After a long while, in the distance, a woman was seen sitting at the base of a tree, bowing her head and softly chanting. As soon as I moved, the woman disappeared without a trace.

    Initially, I thought she was a member of the official's family, but now I realized she was a ghost. Nevertheless, I secretly admired her. Therefore, I often wandered near the pavilion, loudly chanting and responding with:

    "The desert rustles with the west wind, the solitary inn is desolate, and the flowers are gone. I suppose the fragrant soul is with the guest's dream, the same sorrowful feeling on the east side of the wall."

    Returning to my bed, I suddenly saw a beautiful woman come, folding her sleeves and saying, "You are a gentleman of elegance and culture. I have many things to fear and avoid." I was surprised and delighted, pulling her to sit. She was beautiful, graceful, and pleasing, her steps light and elegant. I asked who she was, and she replied, "I am the daughter of the former magistrate Han Fengshan, from Qiantang, named Juanyuan. Before my death, I liked to eat sour apricots. I mistakenly ate a double-seeded kernel and was poisoned, dying at the age of sixteen. Now my coffin is stopped outside the city, and my spirit remains in the office. The chants I made are now harmonized by you, bringing light to the realm of the dead." I was pleased and had a joyful meeting with her. From then on, she came every night without fail.

    380

    女固善书，所有案头启事，暑夜寒宵，尝为张捉管代劳。张爱秘之，二人绸缪如夫妇。一日女至，泪滴阑干，曰：“夙世缘尽今夕。受君恩爱，实不忍离。吾家父母将遣伻来迁柩，势不可留，当返省视。魂归千里，后会为难。君一岁辞馆归，烦一往浙。”遂于发间摘一翠钿与生：“可持见我二老。妾有隐愿，以图报君情于万一。然成否有数，不敢预期。”珍重涕零，张亦泣，侵晓而去。明日，果有浙人来迁女公子柩。自此亭舍寂然。
    岁聿云暮，孤馆愁思，簿书颠倒，时忆芳魂。偶翻遗墨，无不系人魂梦。乃辞居亭旋里。略为摒挡，家无遗孑，买舟作西湖之游。三月而抵杭。

    Explanation

    380

    The woman was indeed skilled in writing, and during hot nights and cold mornings, she often helped Zhang by picking up the brush and doing it for him. Zhang loved to keep their relationship secret, and the two were as intimate as a couple. One day, the woman came, tears streaming down her face, and said, "Our past-life缘分 is ending tonight. I have received your kindness and love, and I truly cannot bear to part. My parents will send someone to come and move my coffin, and there is no way to stay. I must return to visit them. My spirit will return thousands of miles away, and it will be difficult to meet again. You will resign from your position and return home next year, please go to Zhejiang." She then took a jade hairpin from her hair and gave it to Zhang, saying, "You can bring it to see my parents. I have a hidden wish, to try to repay your kindness in the slightest way. But success or failure is determined by fate, and I dare not expect it." With tears in her eyes, Zhang also cried, and they left just before dawn. The next day, indeed, someone from Zhejiang came to move the woman's coffin. From then on, the pavilion was silent.

    The year was nearly over, and in the lonely room, Zhang was filled with sorrow and confusion, often thinking of her beautiful spirit. Occasionally, he would flip through her belongings, and nothing was not connected to her spirit. He then left the pavilion and returned home. After making some preparations, with no one left at home, he bought a boat to travel to West Lake. After three months, he arrived in Hangzhou.

    381

    先是女有一妹名好好，无兄弟，年已十八，未字人。今其姊榇归，家中忙醮事。其妹好好忽扑地昏绝，逾刻醒，曰：“大女娟娟不孝，中途弃高堂，别几年矣！幸老人康健。”父母曰：“儿果来归乎？勿惊汝妹。”女曰：“幽明异域，觌面河山。今儿自晋数年归，儿冥冥中已定山东秀才张如瞻。儿已将所殉金翠钿与之，不日婿来拜岳完娶。但儿魂魄无依，旧舍不可居，曷借我二妹躯？”父母曰：“不可。儿固得所，如汝妹何？”女曰：“二妹与儿幼时最相爱。小时曾共誓：得嫁一个好书生，吾两人共事之，斯愿已足。今来特与妹妹合舍，使一其身而两其人。望爹姥许我。”父母曰：“儿病狂耶！”女生时好以手撩鬓发，言次辄作故态，神气声音，宛然似昔。复谆谆订其父，乃绐之曰：“俟婿来区处。”女喜谢。由是忽而娟娟，忽而好好。中夜帏帐唧唧作两人语，俨若姊妹联床，即趋视，孑然也。家人咸以为痫。

    381

    Before her death, the elder sister Jujuan had a younger sister named Hoho, who was 18 years old and not yet married. Now that the elder sister's body had returned, the family was busy preparing for her funeral. Suddenly, Hoho fainted and fell to the ground. After a moment, she woke up and said, "The eldest daughter Jujuan was ungrateful, abandoning her parents halfway through life and leaving them for several years! Fortunately, the old people are still healthy." Her parents asked, "Is the daughter really coming back? Don't frighten your sister." Hoho replied, "The realm of the living and the dead is different, separated by rivers and mountains. Now that I have returned from Jin after several years, I have already decided on the scholar Zhang Ruzhan from Shandong in the afterlife. I have already given my jewelry to him, and soon my husband will come to visit his in-laws and complete the marriage. But my soul has no place to rest, and the old house cannot be inhabited. Why can't I borrow my younger sister's body?" Her parents said, "No, it's not possible. You have found a good match, but what about your younger sister?" Hoho said, "My younger sister and I were closest as children. As children, we made a vow together: to marry a good scholar and serve him together, which would be sufficient. Now, I especially want to live together with my sister, making one body and two people. I hope my parents will allow me." Her parents said, "Are you crazy?" When she was alive, Hoho often played with her hair. As she spoke, she made her old gestures, and her spirit, voice, and mannerisms were exactly as before. She repeatedly urged her father and then deceived them by saying, "Wait until the groom comes to sort things out." Hoho happily thanked them. From then on, she sometimes was Jujuan and sometimes was Hoho. In the middle of the night, the curtains whispered as if two people were talking, seeming as if the sisters were lying together. When they rushed to look, there was only one person there. The family all thought she was having an epileptic seizure.

    382

    越日，张生果至，以刺及翠钿入谒。翁异之，延入客舍。女窥帘见之，骤出，捉袂与语。父恚甚，母诃之，始惭沮而返。生感泣，遂告以晋署之事，垂涕拜伏不起。翁扶生，不以为侮，乃许以字。生谢出。至日，生往赘。花烛灿列，新妇入青庐。搭面既揭，生不敢认。娟娟曰：“汝不识奴，何眈眈视？”卺饮后，欢洽纵谈，别绪缕缕，乃谓张曰：“明日我妹子来，妹子年幼稚，望君怜之。以爱我之情爱我妹，则妹感君，而我更为感之也。君其视我与妹勿贰焉。”张曰：“卿即卿妹，卿亦卿姊，况卿妹固不殊于卿姊，而视卿妹者，又安忍异视于卿姊耶！”翌旦，如婚礼，而女则娇羞婉转，俨然新妇，非复昨日之如旧昏媾也。后一日为姊，一日为妹，皆相笃爱。或家中有宴喜大事，则姊妹皆出，为一人而事可兼综而共理。彼二人者，既同气而连枝，张待二人，自不敢二心而膜视。张在南中十年，岳父母终，殡葬后仍携眷而东。

    Explanation

    382

    The next day, Zhang Sheng indeed arrived, entering and paying respects with a card and a jade ornament. The old man was surprised and invited him into the guesthouse. The girl peeked through the curtain and saw him, quickly coming out to take his sleeve and speak with him. The father was very angry, and the mother scolded her, causing her to return, ashamed and discouraged. Zhang Sheng was moved to tears and told him about the matters of the Jin office, weeping and prostrating himself, refusing to rise. The old man supported him, not considering it disrespectful, and agreed to marry his daughter. Zhang Sheng thanked him and left. On the appointed day, Zhang Sheng went to live with the bride's family. The wedding candles were brightly arranged, and the bride entered the green tent. When the veil was lifted, Zhang Sheng dared not recognize her. The bride, named Juanjuan, said, "You don't recognize me, why do you stare at me so intently?" After drinking from the two cups, they talked freely and happily, their parting thoughts lingering. She then said to Zhang, "Tomorrow my younger sister will come. She is young and innocent; I hope you will take care of her. Treat her with the same love you have for me, and she will be grateful to you, and I will be even more grateful. Please treat me and my sister without distinction." Zhang replied, "You are my sister, and you are also my elder sister. Your younger sister is not different from your elder sister. How could I treat her differently from you?" The next day, the wedding proceeded as usual, but the girl was shy and graceful, behaving like a proper bride, not the same as the previous night's old marriage. The following day, she was sometimes the elder sister and sometimes the younger sister, and they both deeply loved each other. When there were important family gatherings, both sisters would come together, handling affairs comprehensively and jointly. The two sisters, being of the same breath and branches, Zhang treated them with loyalty and respect, not having a divided heart or looking down on either. Zhang lived in the south for ten years, and after his father-in-law and mother-in-law died, he still took his family eastward.

    383

    时稍有囊资，遂下帷攻苦。壬子举于乡，五年复官于晋，即为壶关令。衙斋无事，夫人尝至“古香书屋”，抚此长楸，泫然流涕，曰：“此姊去妹，三年孤苦，离父母，会张郎，郁郁于此。今复何时？树犹如此，不禁令人悲喜交集耳！”各生一子，视同己出。张官至和州牧，卒于署。夫人命其子诣杭，扶榇来东，皆合葬焉。

    马二娘
    慨自南齐衰世，东昏骄淫，纵一日之侈靡，贻后人之沿袭。如金莲贴地，事属偶然。浸假而闺房士女，无不学步后尘。亡国之习，流毒一至于此。吁，始作俑者，其无后乎！然而风俗于人，贤者不免，又乌能力矫其众非，而一衷于独是？此马二娘之自爱其纤纤也，可述已。

    Explanation

    383

    When he had accumulated some savings, he devoted himself to diligent study. In the year of Ren Zi, he passed the provincial examination, and after five years, he was reinstated to office in the state of Jin, becoming the magistrate of Huguan. With nothing pressing to do at the office, his wife once came to the "Ancient Fragrance Study," where she touched a tall pine tree and shed tears of sorrow, saying, "This sister parted from her sister, enduring three years of loneliness, away from her parents, meeting Zhang Lang, suffering in this place. Now what has become of us? The tree has grown so much, it can't help but evoke a mix of joy and sorrow!" Each gave birth to a son, treating them as their own. Zhang became the governor of Hezhou and died in his office. The lady ordered her son to go to Hangzhou, carry the coffin eastward, and bury them together.

    Ma Erniang
    Reflecting on the declining era of the Southern Qi dynasty, with the emperor's arrogance and debauchery, allowing one day of extravagance to be passed down to future generations as a model. Like the practice of walking with the toes touching the ground, which was an occasional occurrence. Gradually, women in the inner chambers all followed suit. The customs of a fallen state have spread to this extent. Alas, the one who first started this practice, will he have no descendants? However, customs affect even the virtuous, and how can one correct the many wrongs and be alone in being right? This is the story of Ma Erniang's self-love for her delicate nature, which can be told.

    384

    马字桂樽，绍兴人。随父幕于晋之大同。初生，母梦流水上落花一片，遂拾入怀。父母以为不祥，因命桂樽以禳之。及笄，丰姿态度，澄然深秀，又善自妆饰。或增一分脂粉，不嫌其污；或减一分容华，愈觉其丽。至裙下双钩，尤所爱护。晋有缠足女师，朝夕缚结。桂复自为扎勒，裂缯刻玉，以求瘦小。又作金丝履，凤头尖，软香帮，并刻梅花粉底，种种增华。通诗书。后其父随张学山太守入粤，旋被逮。桂乃流寓羊城。年二十，无问蹇修者。父死益困。有鹤令雷姓，闽人也，以三百金鬻为妾。
    令本粗俗，夫人更悍妒。初见日，即厉声加凌，桂俯首受命。夫人固闽产，两凫如藕船。及睹桂之纤么，愈形己之壮趾，益恨桂。常锢禁之不与令通，又使终日侍立，稍不如意，即梃击其足，否则以彼足蹑桂足，一痛入骨，如刀刖胫。无人处，桂常蹲地，手抚双翘，凄然泪下如雨。日则刻眉灼目，夜则长漏寒灯，了无生趣。遂绝食七日而逝，葬城南圆通寺侧。

    Explanation

    384

    Ma Ziguizun, a native of Shaoxing, followed her father into service in Datong, Shanxi. At birth, her mother dreamed of a piece of fallen flower on the water, which she picked up. Her parents considered this an inauspicious omen and ordered Ziguizun to ward off evil. Upon reaching the age of fifteen, she was known for her clear and profound beauty, and she was skilled in dressing herself. Adding a touch of makeup enhanced her appearance, and even a slight reduction in her beauty made her seem even more graceful. She was particularly protective of her bound feet. In Shanxi, there was a woman who specialized in teaching foot binding, and Ziguizun practiced this daily. She also bound her own feet with bandages, meticulously shaping them to be slender. She crafted exquisite shoes with gold thread, pointed toes, soft leather, and carved plum blossom patterns, which added to her elegance. She was well-versed in poetry and literature. Later, her father followed Governor Zhang Xueshan to Guangdong, but was soon arrested. Ziguizun then settled in Guangzhou. At the age of twenty, no one inquired about her as a potential match. Her father's death left her even more destitute. A local official named Lei, from Fujian, bought her as a concubine for 300 gold pieces.

    The official was coarse and vulgar, and his wife was even more fierce and jealous. On the day they first met, she shouted harshly and bullied Ziguizun, who submitted to her commands. The wife, originally from Fujian, had large feet, like lotus roots. When she saw Ziguizun's slender feet, she felt even more ashamed of her own and grew to hate Ziguizun. She often kept Ziguizun locked up, preventing her from seeing the official, and made her stand all day. If Ziguizun did not please her, she would beat her feet with a stick; otherwise, she would step on Ziguizun's feet with her own, causing excruciating pain as if her legs were being cut off with a knife. When no one was around, Ziguizun would sit on the ground, her hands on her bound feet, weeping bitterly. By day, she would pierce her eyebrows and burn her eyes; by night, she would sit under the cold lamp, finding no joy in life. Eventually, she starved herself to death after seven days and was buried near the Yuan Tong Temple in the southern part of the city.

    385

    寺有李子玉寓居焉。一日，见一老妪持一对串珠履欲售，李爱而买之。持归，灯前把玩，见其针工缜密，仅二寸，以汉古尺度之，盖三寸也。正凝想间，忽一美丽在前，蝉袖云鬟，若近若远。生曰：“卿岂遗舄仙人乎？卿固解不当阶，我亦非廋之自牖。”女曰：“一线之遗，漫劳三顾。感君雅爱，特来踵谢。”李挑灯撩裙，下照红莲，见其弓弯一捻。喜曰：“此诚卿物，否则无此巧，亦无此小也。”生抱女于怀，女殊羞赧，惟首自顾，漫蹴其裙边金线。生与之狎，颇极欢昵。由是夕至不虚。生尝弄其两足，赞曰：“柔腻甘香，端正瘦小。”啧啧不己。女亦自深心赏。生复引其一弯，引入唇边。女曰：“狂生太啰唣矣，盍为妾咏焉？”生遂成二律云：

    Explanation

    385

    There is a temple where Li Ziyu resides. One day, he saw an old woman holding a pair of beaded shoes to sell. Li loved them and bought them. He took them home and played with them by the lamp, seeing that the needlework was fine. Only two inches, but according to the ancient Han measurement, it is about three inches. Just as he was deep in thought, suddenly a beautiful woman appeared in front of him, with sleeves like cicadas and hair like clouds, as if near and far. Li said, "Are you a fairy who left behind her shoes? You are indeed not meant to be on the steps, nor am I a thief who enters through the window." The woman replied, "To trouble you three times with a single thread. Thank you for your kind love, I specially came to thank you." Li picked up the lamp and lifted her skirt, looking down at the red lotus, seeing her slender foot. He said, "These are truly yours. Otherwise, there would be no such craftsmanship or such smallness." Li hugged the woman in his arms, and she was very shy, only looking at herself, casually kicking the gold thread at the edge of her skirt. Li played with her, and they were very intimate. From then on, every night was not in vain. Li often played with her two feet, praising, "Soft, smooth, sweet, and fragrant, elegant, slender, and small." He murmured endlessly. She also deeply appreciated it. Li again took one of her feet and brought it to his lips. The woman said, "You are too noisy. Why don't you write a poem for me?" Li then wrote two poems.

    386

    一双么凤巧如锥，小立翘然恐不支。春暖瓣开花绰约，夜深钩上月参差。
    脱来似剥新菱角，裹后如缠嫩笋皮。漫拟凌波仙浦外，轻盈好向掌中持。
    曾向潘妃步后尘，弯来寸寸可堪珍。印成软玉留香径，舞罢轻莲落绣茵。
    怕是蹴醒春梦客，几回勾动早朝人。深闺夜静双遗舄，还当金钱问卜频。
    女敛衽曰：“得此佳韵，死有馀荣矣。”乃缅述其为鬼，并生前遭际坎坷，声泪俱迸。生复曰：“卿何不效唐张云容故事乎？”女曰：“游魂所变，半涉荒幻。即令复起，无能益我，适足祸人，不屑为也。”言罢，杳然成烟，氤氲于衽席间，经时而散。

    Explanation

    386

    A pair of delicate phoenixes, as sharp as needles, stand upright yet seem fragile, as if they might not support themselves. In the warmth of spring, their petals bloom gracefully, and at night, the moon's reflection on the water appears uneven and irregular. When removed, they seem like freshly peeled water chestnuts, and when wrapped, they resemble the tender skin of a young bamboo shoot. They are likened to a fairy standing on water, light and delicate, perfect to be held in the palm of one's hand. They once followed the graceful steps of Pan's wife, each inch of them precious and worthy of admiration. They leave behind a soft, fragrant trail, like a path made of soft jade, and after dancing, they gently fall onto a embroidered carpet, like a lotus flower. They might wake someone from a spring dream, and have stirred the early risers. In the quiet of the night, in a secluded room, two abandoned shoes remain, and one might frequently consult a fortune-teller with money. The woman bowed her sleeves and said, "To receive such a fine verse, even in death, there would be enough honor." She then recounted her story as a ghost, including the hardships and difficulties she faced in life, her voice and tears bursting forth. The man replied, "Why don't you follow the example of Zhang Yunrong from the Tang dynasty?" The woman said, "A wandering soul transformed, partly involving the fantastical. Even if it were to return, it would not benefit me, and might instead bring harm. I do not wish to do so." After speaking, she vanished into thin air, fading away in the space between the bedcovers, and eventually dispersed.

    387

    (荆州沙市，有蜀妓徐金，足趾小瘦端好，尝自爱其纤纤，客有誉之者则喜。余见而握，如珍瑜不释手，徐感恩而相知，许以身事。我值被议，后不及。徐金今年廿五。夏间，有蜀武孝廉某，以三百金欲买为媵。徐不许，曰：“世间人谁是知己？惟知己不可负耳。”其鸨家亦知之，不相强云。徐面有微麻，身修长，步不轻佻，无妓女习气。不喜歌笑，烟酒若无能者，心最灵警，又大方。)

    沈耀先
    沈耀先，嘉兴人。居乡，为人诚实。尝出入大户作保佃，人咸爱之。有年病卒。忽一日清晨叩其友门，童子应出，讶其为沈。俄顷，其友出见之，声音笑貌不类死者，因执手慰劳曰：“人言汝已死矣。”沈曰：“病诚有之，何至于死？皆谬传也。”遂留共饭。沈固好饮，尝恋杯不起，且醉，无所不说。既而沈半醺，友探之曰：“阴阳相反，其世界亦自不同？”沈曰：“无大差别。大约好人得逍遥自在，恶人定受苦报。

    Explanation

    387

    In Shashi, Jingzhou, there was a courtesan from Sichuan named Xu Jin. Her feet were slender and elegantly proportioned, and she often admired her delicate hands. She was pleased when guests praised her. I saw her and held her hand as if I would not let go of a precious gem. Xu Jin was grateful and became my confidante, offering herself in service. However, I was later caught in a controversy and could not follow through with my commitment. Xu Jin is now twenty-five years old. During the summer, a scholar-official from Sichuan offered to buy her as a concubine for three hundred gold coins. Xu Jin refused, saying, "Who in the world is a true friend? Only a true friend can be betrayed." Her madam also knew of her decision and did not force her to change her mind. Xu Jin has a few small blemishes on her face, a tall and graceful figure, and a dignified demeanor. She does not enjoy singing or laughing, and she does not indulge in smoking or drinking. She is intelligent and generous in spirit.

    Shen Yaoxian
    Shen Yaoxian was from Jiaxing. In his hometown, he was known for his honesty. He often acted as a guarantor for large landowners and tenants, and everyone loved him. One year, he fell ill and passed away. One morning, he unexpectedly knocked on his friend's door. The servant boy opened the door and was surprised to see Shen. Shortly after, his friend came out and saw Shen. His voice and appearance were unlike those of a deceased person, so he took Shen's hand and comforted him, saying, "People said you had already died." Shen replied, "I did have an illness, but why did it lead to death? It was all a false rumor." He then stayed and had a meal together. Shen was a heavy drinker and often lingered over his cups, getting slightly intoxicated and speaking freely. Later, when Shen was half-drunk, his friend asked him, "Is the world of the dead different from this one?" Shen said, "There is no great difference. Generally, good people enjoy freedom, while evil people will surely suffer."

    388

    ”友曰：“但不知阴曹着何样衣履？”沈曰：“有红顶花翎者位最尊，至县城隍，则金顶耳，然有钱又掌权。”友曰：“世间所焚之楮钱果有用乎？”沈曰：“亦好。”友曰：“僧道诵经有益乎？”沈曰：“若真修行僧，诵之甚佳；若凡庸辈，则是徒费饶舌耳。”友曰：“究竟此际甚么用得着？”沈曰：“看来还是读书的用得着。冥司最重读书人，且读书者门路多。尝见有小过犯，辄见朱衣人来关白人情。此时冥官多系阳世读书者，往往以曲为直而徇蔽之。”友曰：“汝何知之真而见之凿耶？”沈不能答。视其色，若惨沮，言有嗫嚅，张皇四顾，倏殁于地，杳无所见。其友亟往其家吊唁之，而沈已死十日矣。

    Explanation

    388

    The friend said, "But I don't know what kind of clothes and shoes the officials in the underworld wear." Shen replied, "Those with red hats and plumes are the most respected, and the county magistrate has a golden hat. However, having money and power is also important." The friend asked, "Are the paper money burned in the world actually useful?" Shen said, "It's good." The friend asked, "Is it beneficial for monks and Taoists to recite scriptures?" Shen replied, "If it's a truly practicing monk, reciting scriptures is very good; if it's an ordinary person, it's just a waste of words." The friend asked, "What is actually useful in this situation?" Shen said, "It seems that scholars are the most useful. The underworld highly values scholars, and they have many ways to succeed. I have often seen that for minor offenses, a person in red clothes would come to explain the situation. At this time, most of the underworld officials are scholars from the mortal world, and they often twist the truth to cover it up." The friend asked, "How do you know so much and see so clearly?" Shen could not answer. Seeing his expression, it was pale and dejected, his speech was hesitant, he looked around nervously, and suddenly he died on the spot, and nothing was seen. The friend rushed to his home to pay condolences, but Shen had already been dead for ten days.

    389

    (济宁有刘姓者，为吾友王惺斋砚席交。性悭吝，有半伊尹之风，负人债多不还，又有富岁子弟之行。一日，为冥司勾去。见冥官，官怒曰：“负人债务累累，是设心不偿还耶？”笞二十。而醒告人曰：笞之官戴亮蓝顶，见其举手掣签时，亦尖口袍袖云。)

    孟氏家鬼
    邹邑孟氏，贤裔家。长房多绝，又相继夭殂，皆支嗣，家多孀妇。前厅厝三世柩，未就窀穸。
    余从兄雨亭系其内戚。尝仆马往探，晚则宿焉。夜谈，止其家，遣婢灯送书舍宿。舍则前厅西厢。婢入内，阖宅门。前厅去大门守宿处甚远。呼其仆，不知所往。初料其事刍秣，备戒旦行。雨亭素有胆气，亦不介意。出院中，徘徊阶除间。明月横空，寒云四起，颇有苍凉之况。第见一庭如水，壮志顿消，有不禁惕惕然为之心恻者也。

    Explanation

    389

    In Jining, there was a man of the Liu surname who was a close friend of my friend Wang Xingzai, a fellow scholar and friend at the writing desk. He was stingy, with half the virtue of Yi Yin, owed many debts to others but did not repay them, and also behaved like a spoiled rich kid. One day, he was summoned by the underworld officials. He met the underworld officials, and the official angrily said, "Owing so many debts, does this mean you have no intention of repaying them?" He was flogged twenty times. And when he woke up, he told people, "The flogging official wore a bright blue hat, and when he raised his hand to draw a lot, his mouth and sleeves were sharp and pointed."

    Shades of the Meng Family
    The Meng family in Zou County was a family of virtuous descendants. The eldest branch of the family had many deaths, and they died one after another.
