All were collateral descendants, and the family had many widows. Three generations of coffins were placed in the front hall, not yet buried in a proper tomb. My cousin Yu Ting is related to them by marriage. I once went to visit them on horseback, and stayed there at night. At night, I talked with them, stayed at their home, and a maid lit the way to the study where I slept. The study was in the west wing of the front hall. The maid went inside, and closed the main gate. The front hall was far from the main gate where the guards were stationed. I called for his servant, but I didn't know where he went. I initially thought he was taking care of the horses' feed, preparing to leave early the next day. Yu Ting has always had courage, and he didn't mind it. He walked out of the courtyard, pacing back and forth in the hall. The bright moon hung in the sky, and cold clouds gathered all around, creating a rather desolate atmosphere. Only seeing the courtyard like water, his heroic aspirations were suddenly extinguished, and he couldn't help but feel a sense of unease and sorrow.

    390

    入舍闭管，就东北榻。榻临窗，皆疏棂。几上灯膏殆尽，吹就卧。月色照屋梁，反侧不寐。俄闻厅格扇开，雨亭起舐窗，见朝冠老少者三，簪凤衣帔妇女亦如之，蹀躞庭院，如有隐忧。其老者以手指西舍，馀皆西望，作点首状，怅怅良久，互相嗟叹而返。少间，又一妇人白衣缞绖，行至西舍，若欲启扉。雨亭方无所计，忽厅格响，妇乃逡巡退归。既而厅格顿合，雨亭心甫定，气稍舒，思出寻仆归。欻西北甬道中，一鬼突如其来，凶恶无伦。衣黑缕，咻咻而前，举首对月，则须发交而血模糊也。低首望西舍，似知有人，喜而跃，抵门，洞开。入，左右视，目瞪瞪，见雨亭。雨亭不敢视，以手掩面，拳曲榻头。鬼初作扑攫状、招手状，继乃作嘻笑状、哭泣状，终复大嗥，满屋跃跳，更无状不作。雨亭浑身立冰雪，心怔忡出顶际，两太阳凭空乱钟磬声。

    Explanation

    390

    Entering the room, he closed the windows and went to the bed in the northeast corner. The bed was near the window, with latticed shutters. The oil in the lamp on the table was almost gone, so he blew it out and lay down. The moonlight shone on the roof beams, causing him to toss and turn, unable to sleep. Soon, he heard the hall's sliding doors open. Rain Pavilion stood up and looked out the window, seeing three elderly and young men wearing official hats, and women with phoenix-adorned headdresses and robes, walking back and forth in the courtyard as if they had hidden worries. The elderly one pointed towards the west room, and the others all looked westward, nodding their heads, and sighed for a long time, then returned together. Shortly after, another woman in white mourning clothes walked to the west room, as if she wanted to open the door. Rain Pavilion had no idea what to do, but suddenly heard the hall's sliding doors open, and the woman hesitated and returned. Soon after, the hall's sliding doors closed, and Rain Pavilion's heart calmed down, and his breath eased. He thought of going out to find his servant and return. Suddenly, in the northwest corridor, a ghost appeared, extremely fierce and evil. Dressed in black rags, he walked forward with a hissing sound, raising his head to the moon, his hair and beard tangled and bloody. Lowering his head to look at the west room, he seemed to know there was someone, and happily jumped, reaching the door and opening it wide. Entering, he looked around, his eyes wide open, seeing Rain Pavilion. Rain Pavilion dares not look, covering his face with his hand, curling up at the head of the bed. The ghost initially made grabbing and beckoning gestures, then laughing and crying, and finally roared loudly, jumping around the whole room, doing nothing else. Rain Pavilion felt as if standing in ice and snow, his heart palpitates to the top of his head, and the sun seems to be ringing bells and gongs randomly.

    391

    良久鸡唱，鬼乃张皇遁去。于是万籁甫寂，而雨亭一灵方返舍。遂蹶然起，振衣蹑履，开户奔。忽觉耳后踯躅，又如鬼追。急行，扑面又一撞，跄踉满怀，雨亭竟于是乎仆。不知乃其仆方饮罢，自外归也。仆朦胧视扶其主人，犹喃喃作醉中语。雨亭狼狈起，气转若游丝，亦不暇咎仆。侵晓入内，细述所见。询之历历不爽，甬道鬼是其悍奴受笞自戕者。

    僵鬼
    唐县张姓，家贫无行，耽于博。有妻韩氏，纺绩之资以及衣饰等物，皆供张一赌而罄之。于是家徒壁立，犹卜夜不归也。
    一日，张聚赌于某所，深更囊匮，群挤之出局，张犹恋不起。有张表弟萧某，鼠窃也，亦在列。张私语曰：“吾内室败簏中有青蚨三百，是汝嫂卖棉钱。愿假我表弟妙手窃来，济我一时之急。”萧曰：“嫂溺也，不可以手。吾何敢盗嫂之金也？”张曰：“有兄在，即嫂觉，彼如季子何？”力促其往。萧不得已遂行。

    Explanation

    391

    After a long while, the rooster crowed, and the ghost panicked and fled. At that moment, all sounds fell silent, and the spirit of Rain Pavilion returned to the house. It suddenly rose, shook off its clothes, put on its shoes, opened the door, and ran out. Suddenly, it felt something stumbling behind its ear, as if a ghost was chasing it. It ran quickly, and was hit in the face, staggering and falling down, only to find that it was the servant who had just finished drinking and returned. The servant, drowsy and half-asleep, saw and supported his master, who was mumbling as if drunk. The master rose in a disheveled state, his breath becoming weak, and did not have time to blame the servant. Just before dawn, he entered the house and described in detail what he had seen. When asked, he recounted it clearly and without error, and it turned out that the ghost in the passageway was the servant who had committed suicide after receiving a beating.

    A Stiff Ghost
    In Tang County, there was a man named Zhang, who was poor and had no moral conduct, indulging in gambling. He had a wife named Han, who earned money from spinning and weaving, as well as from clothing and other items, all of which he gambled away. As a result, his house was as empty as bare walls, and he still stayed out until late at night and did not return home.
    One day, Zhang gathered to gamble at a certain place, and by deep night, his purse was empty. The crowd pushed him out of the game, but Zhang still clung to the table and did not want to leave. His cousin Xiao, who was a petty thief, was also present. Zhang whispered to him, "In my inner room, there is a broken basket with three hundred copper coins, which is the money my wife earned from selling cotton. I wish to borrow my cousin's skill to steal it and help me in my urgent need." Xiao said, "If my sister-in-law is drowning, I cannot use my hands. How dare I steal her money?" Zhang said, "If my brother is there, even if my sister-in-law finds out, what can she do to him?" He urged Xiao to go. Xiao, having no choice, set off.

    392

    抵张舍，而韩氏适在户外。萧喜，入室，启笥得钱。忽氏返，萧即缘格板椽上，欲俟嫂转动时，乘隙乃去。其嫂阖外户，执灯檠，持缫车庋门际，坐地轧轧不停。萧不能出，正凝睇间，忽见门缝中进一人，着油绿袍，青马褂，小秋帽，微鬓缩腮，立其嫂身后。萧曰：“嫂之私也。吾今为兄盗而得嫂奸，幸甚！”俟之约多时，视其人遥立不作一语，而嫂又若未之见者。“噫！何人斯？岂鬼也耶？”继而其人以手断其嫂手中线，嫂又不见，断而复续，如是者三，嫂乃停手，遂潸潸泪落。其人在，久若有喜者。“噫！是鬼也，非人也。”审视之。既而韩氏起，持灯返，几觅绳一缕，系窗棂间。鬼喜且跃，复为之挽结作套，移凳扶韩氏，将入套。萧急，大呼曰：“吊杀

    Explanation

    392

    Arriving at Zhang's house, Han happened to be outside. Xiao was pleased and entered the room, where he found money in a chest. Suddenly, Han returned, and Xiao immediately climbed onto the rafters, intending to wait for an opportunity to leave when his sister-in-law moved. His sister-in-law closed the outer door, held a lamp stand, and placed a spinning wheel by the door, sitting on the ground and making a continuous grinding sound. Xiao could not get out, and as he stared intently, he suddenly saw a person enter through the crack in the door, wearing an olive-green robe, a blue mandarin jacket, and a small autumn hat, with a thin mustache and a sunken cheek, standing behind his sister-in-law. Xiao said, "This is a private matter. I have now stolen for my brother and caught my sister-in-law's infidelity, which is fortunate!" He waited for a long time, watching the person stand far away without saying a word, while his sister-in-law seemed not to notice him. "Ah! Who is this person? Could he be a ghost?" Then the person cut the thread in his sister-in-law's hand, but she did not see it, and he cut and continued three times. She then stopped, and tears fell continuously. The person remained there, seemingly happy for a long time. "Ah! It is a ghost, not a human." Xiao examined him closely. Soon, Han got up, held a lamp, and searched for a piece of rope, tying it to the window frame. The ghost was happy and jumped, then wove a noose, moved a stool, and helped Han, about to put her into the noose. Xiao became very anxious and shouted, "Hanged!"

    393

    人也！”从梁格间跳落，后败格一扇亦随之而倒，其声砰塌。
    邻人皆闻。哄然入视，第见韩氏坠地昏然，萧伏地悚然，鬼则立地挺然。众皆掖韩氏，问萧指鬼为何人。萧神定，述其来由，告以氏之缢，即是鬼之祟。众始惊为鬼，噪之。鬼犹僵，众击以木，则空空然，过而复合，如烟凝，如气结，如泡如幻，有形有影。俨然秋帽绿衣，悄乎其容，终夜达旦，不消不灭。于是一村之人，咸以为怪异之甚，遂鸣于宰。乃命二尹来视。时日已晡，尚觉形影可吊。后闻越三日而渐循墙，五日而身面壁，七日之后如淡描一人影于环堵之上。
    吁！人见鬼而神能丧，鬼亦遇人而气不伸。故游魂所变，不能不屈于精气之充。萧之一呼，精气之充也，宜乎鬼遇之而馁已，鬼岂又有死乎？

    Explanation

    393

    "A person!" From the beam and lattice, he jumped down, and the next door also fell down with him, making a loud crashing sound.

    The neighbors all heard it. They rushed in chaotically to see, and only saw Han Shi fall to the ground unconscious, while Xiao crouched on the ground in fear, and the ghost stood upright and rigid. Everyone supported Han Shi, and asked Xiao who the ghost was. Xiao regained his composure, explained the cause, and told about her hanging, which was the ghost's haunting. Everyone was shocked to realize it was a ghost and made noise. The ghost remained motionless, and the crowd hit it with wood, and it appeared hollow, dispersing and then re-forming, like smoke condensing, like gas gathering, like bubbles and illusions, with a visible form and shadow. It wore a conical hat and green robe, with a solemn expression, persisting throughout the night until dawn, neither dissipating nor disappearing. Thus, the villagers all considered it extremely strange and reported it to the magistrate. The magistrate then ordered two officials to come and inspect. By late afternoon, the ghost's form was still somewhat visible. Later, it was heard that over three days it gradually moved along the wall, after five days it faced the wall, and after seven days it appeared as a faint outline on the wall.

    Alas! When people see ghosts, their spirits can be lost, and ghosts also encounter people and their energy cannot be extended. Therefore, wandering spirits cannot help but be subdued by the fullness of vital energy. Xiao's shout is the fullness of vital energy, and it is appropriate that the ghost meets it and is already weakened. Can ghosts truly die?

    394

    (事甚诡谲，而笔能达之，故佳。)

    杨椒水
    钱塘杨大本，号椒水，邑庠生，性孤介，颠于诗，复狂于酒。其自署私居一联云：“蠹腹食残经典，马蹄踏尽烟花。”其自负如此。醉后尝入学师署痛哭，教官恶之，杨亦复诃谯之。有诗云：
    采薇非耻周人粟，颁胙能争孔子豚。三月可怜忘肉味，萧萧苜蓿掩黉门。
    月课“有教无类”题，文中有“不堪教谕，不足训导”之句，遂行请劣，褫其巾。杨益狂放不羁。游岭外，当道诸公怜其才，多悬榻焉。会七夕，宴于陶观察署，成一诗云：
    一拳打破支机石，两手拆坍乌鹊桥。四十鳏夫犹未返，双星不许度今宵。
    满座为之击节。尝病酒，上元不起，有句云：
    傲我乾坤醉复顽，惊他岁月去难还。人生安得元宵死，一路灯光到冥关。
    性爱砚，至端州购石十馀方，置行箧。舟人以为金也，将磨刃而甘心焉。杨觉之，启钥出石，濡墨磨研，故令舟人见之，始解。杨诗曰：

    Explanation

    394

    (Indeed, the matter is very strange and elusive, but the writing can express it well, hence it is good.)

    Yang Jiao Shui
    Yang Daben of Qiantang, styled Jiao Shui, was a student of the local school. He was solitary and aloof, mad about poetry, and also crazy about wine. He inscribed a couplet at his private residence: "Devouring classical texts with a worm's belly, horse hooves have trampled all the flowers." Such was his self-confidence. After drinking, he once wept bitterly at the schoolmaster's office, which angered the teacher. Yang also rebuked the teacher harshly. He wrote a poem:

    "Refusing to eat grain from the Zhou dynasty was not a shame, but competing for a piglet with Confucius was a challenge. Three months of pity, forgetting the taste of meat, the sound of alfalfa blocking the school gate."

    For a monthly assignment on the topic "Education without Distinction," he wrote: "Cannot bear teaching, not worthy of instruction," which led to his being deemed inferior and having his hat removed. Yang became even more unrestrained and unbound. Traveling to the south, officials admired his talent and often invited him to stay. On the seventh day of the seventh month, he attended a banquet at the residence of Tao Guancha and composed a poem:

    "With one punch, break the writing stone; with two hands, dismantle the magpie bridge. Forty bachelor men still have not returned, the twin stars do not allow them to cross the night."

    Everyone in the hall clapped in admiration. Once, he fell ill from drinking and did not recover until the Lantern Festival, writing:

    "Defiant of the universe, drunk and stubborn, startled by the passing years, difficult to return. How could one in life die on the Lantern Festival, a path of lights to the gate of the nether world."

    He loved stones and purchased over ten pieces of stone in Duanzhou, placing them in his travel chest. The boatmen thought they were gold and intended to kill him. Yang sensed this and unlocked the chest, taking out the stones, moistening the ink, and grinding them, thus showing the boatmen and calming them. Yang's poem said:

    395

    凤凰山下苦书生，行李萧萧一担轻。酒债诗逋多未了，榜人何用太相惊。
    年近五十，醉于胥江，扣舷对月，忽忆李白骑鲸故事，一跃入水，杳不可得。后十年，其友曾子一卿入粤。夜泊江干，闻沙际吟曰：
    枯骨葬江边，浪打形骸朽。知音人忽来，奠我一杯酒。
    曾子凄然曰：“此钱塘杨椒水也。”于是唏嘘凭眺，酹酒江心而诔之曰：“呜呼悲哉！杨子椒水，生为才人，死为才鬼。”
    其人为吾师袁南庄先生所契重，唱和诗甚夥，惜忘之不复记忆也。闻其遗稿转在张太守孝泉处，复经袁师评选。未知曾付刊否？

    鬼妻
    任城东仲家浅，贤裔仲氏居焉。有为仲氏佣者，母子二人，诚朴谨笃，任劳力，寡言笑，其子年二十未娶。仲氏故家鲜有礼，子弟豪肆，多狭斜群妓淫娃，聚于临水一楼，丝竹笑语之声，朝夕不绝。楼临远河，过客望之，未尝不逆而送焉。独佣子仆役其间，终若勿顾。

    Explanation

    395

    At Phoenix Mountain, there lived a poor scholar whose meager possessions were carried in a single, light bundle. His debts for wine and poetry remained unpaid, and the boatman seemed overly startled by his presence.

    At nearly fifty years old, he became drunk on the Xiang River, striking the oars and gazing at the moon, suddenly recalling the legend of Li Bai riding a whale. He leapt into the water, disappearing forever. Ten years later, his friend Zeng Ziyi, who had gone to Guangdong, stopped overnight by the riverbank. That night, he heard someone reciting by the shore:

    "Buried bones along the riverbank, waves beat against my decaying body. A true friend suddenly arrives, offering me a cup of wine."

    Zeng Ziyi, moved by grief, said, "This is Yang Jiao Shui of Qiantang." He then sighed and looked out over the river, pouring wine into the water and lamenting, "Alas, how sad! Yang Jiao Shui, you were a talented man in life and now a spirit of talent in death."

    This man was highly esteemed by my teacher, Yuan Nan Zhuang, with whom he exchanged many poems. Sadly, I have forgotten most of them. I have heard that his unpublished works are now in the possession of Zhang Taishou Xiaoquan, who has also reviewed them. I do not know if they have ever been published.

    In Dongcheng, there was a place called Zhongjiaqian, where a virtuous descendant of the Zhong family lived. There was a mother and son who worked for the Zhong family; they were honest, simple, and diligent, rarely speaking or smiling. The son, at twenty years old, had not yet married. The Zhong family, though once noble, had become lacking in manners, with their children living extravagantly and frequenting a brothel near the river, where music and laughter could be heard day and night. The brothel overlooked a distant river, and passersby could not help but glance at it. The servant, however, worked there without ever seeming to notice.

    396

    一日，主人役往卞泗寄物。归，至班村凹中，夕阳在山，暮烟将垂，疲息柳阴路傍，击石镰吸淡巴菇。往来无人，遥见一女子飘逸而来，年约十八九，蒙髻网，衣服朴洁，面白皙，着秋白裤，小红布两翘，疑近村女。佣不敢视。至近，女即趺地坐。佣他顾焉。女曰：“尔吸者济宁烟草耶？乞假一管。”佣欲易而与之。女曰：“不劳更换。我不胜此力，但令唇尖一嗅香味足矣。尔居何庄？”佣曰：“仲家浅，为人佣。”女曰：“有父母否？”曰：“母在堂。”女曰：“有家室否？”佣曰：“未有也。”女曰：“我作尔妇何如？”佣颊頳，曰：“还我烟具，日暮当遄归。”女笑曰：“呆块！年若许，尚腼腆作羞态。野合本非礼，今夜尔俟我于尔寝所。”佣漫应之，取具而去。亦意料为谁家荡妇耳。晚抵舍，返面主人毕，与老佣同草炕，阖户就寝。

    Explanation

    396

    One day, the master's servant was sent to Biansi to deliver items. On his return, he reached a low-lying part of Ban Village. The sun was setting behind the mountains, and the evening mist was beginning to rise. Tired, he rested under the shade of willow trees by the roadside, using a stone pipe to smoke tobacco. There was no one else around. Far off, he saw a woman walking gracefully toward him. She was about eighteen or nineteen years old, with her hair tied up in a net-like fashion, wearing simple and clean clothes. Her face was fair, and she wore white trousers with red cloth flaps at the bottom. He suspected she was a girl from a nearby village. The servant dared not look at her directly. When she came closer, she sat down on the ground. The servant turned his gaze away. The woman said, "Are you smoking tobacco from Jinan? May I borrow your pipe?" The servant wanted to give her a new one. The woman said, "There's no need to change it. I can't handle the strength of this, but just letting my lips touch the tip to smell the fragrance is enough. Where do you live?" The servant replied, "I live in Zhongjiashan and work as a servant." The woman asked, "Do you have parents?" He answered, "My mother is still alive." She then asked, "Do you have a wife?" The servant replied, "I don't have one yet." The woman said, "How about if I become your wife?" The servant blushed and said, "Give me back my smoking tools, it's getting late and I should return home quickly." The woman laughed and said, "Fool! At your age, you're still shy and embarrassed. A union outside of proper marriage rituals is not right. Tonight, wait for me at your sleeping place." The servant replied vaguely and took his tools to leave. He thought it was just some loose woman. In the evening, he returned to the house, reported to his master, and went to bed on a straw mat with the old servant, closing the door behind him.

    397

    残月明灭窗棂，目未交睫，忽见门枨下露妇人足，心忆其来，佯睡。女已入室，且倚其床云：“路远弓窄，尔先我多时至？”佣不答。女曰：“尔无怖。我固非人，然不为尔害，实与尔有夙缘。我亦善经理。垂白母，我事之，环堵室，负郭田，我当为尔办。何必向玉川先生家作裹头奴一世哉！”佣曰：“此事当告我母，许则遂，不许则已，我不敢擅专。请俟异日。”俄而老佣起溲，赤身出户。女怒曰：“老奴太无礼！女流在，何亵？”以手指之，老佣遂以手自批其颊十馀下。佣为告免。女不得已，订之而贻以一物，嘱勿令他人见，言讫而灭。

    Explanation

    397

    The waning moon cast a flickering light through the window lattice, and the servant, unable to sleep, suddenly saw a woman's foot exposed at the threshold of the door. Remembering her arrival, he pretended to be asleep. The woman had already entered the room and leaned on the bed, saying, "The road is long and narrow, and you arrived much earlier than I did." The servant did not answer. She said, "You have nothing to fear. I am not a human, but I will not harm you; I have a predestined connection with you. I am also good at managing. I will take care of your elderly mother, provide you with a modest home, and arrange land near the city wall. Why should you spend your life as a servant in the household of Master Yuchuan?" The servant replied, "This matter should be reported to my mother. If she agrees, then it will be done; if she does not, then it will not. I dare not act on my own. Please wait for another day." Soon after, the old servant got up to urinate and went out naked. The woman angrily said, "The old servant is too rude! A woman is present, how disrespectful!" She pointed at him, and the old servant then slapped himself across the face more than ten times. The servant pleaded for forgiveness. The woman, reluctantly, agreed and gave him an item, instructing him not to let others see it. After speaking, she vanished.

    398

    及晨，老奴起操田事，自云半面皆肿，不知何故。佣寻枕畔，有纸裹，启视则绣鞋一只，折花囊一枚，持以入，告其母。母戒勿与通。易其处。而女又来，佣坚不与合。其少主人索鞋藏之，而病呓，乃还佣。后女子每夜必至，求媒合，母颇厌患之，无能治。
    适济上落拓生乡进士刘天骥者，过仲太史家，言其事而异，继而疑。终乃呼其母子而告之曰：“夫鬼，人为之也。人能为鬼，鬼即可以为人。使人即与人合，而以鬼道处其人，则人亦与鬼近矣。苟人而与鬼合而以人道交，其鬼则鬼特即为人用，即人也，何鬼之有？”乃指架上通书云：“我当与尔诹吉。今夜天德合，河魁不房，无再诿。今不取，恐反受殃矣。”遂与之合。

    Explanation

    398

    By morning, the old servant got up to tend to the fields, claiming that half his face was swollen, but he didn't know why. The hired man found a paper bundle by the pillow, and when he opened it, there was a embroidered shoe and a folded flower bag. He took them inside and told his mother. The mother warned him not to have relations with her. They changed the place. But the girl came again, and the hired man firmly refused to have relations with her. The young master took the shoe and hid it, but he fell ill and spoke incoherently, then returned to the hired man. Later, the girl would come every night, asking for a matchmaker. The mother was very annoyed by this and could not find a solution.

    Just then, a wandering scholar named Liu Tianji from the village of Jidi passed by the home of Zhong Taishi and spoke about the matter, finding it strange and then doubtful. Finally, he called the mother and son and told them, "Ghosts are created by people. If people can become ghosts, then ghosts can also become people. If a person becomes one with another person, but treats them as if they were a ghost, then the person will also become close to being a ghost. If a person becomes one with a ghost and treats them as a person, then the ghost will only be used by the person, and will be a person, so what ghost is there?" He then pointed to a book on the shelf and said, "I will consult the book for you. Tonight, the heavenly virtue is harmonious, and the river chief is not in a position to obstruct, so do not delay. If you do not take it now, you may suffer harm." So he agreed to the arrangement.

    399

    后年，春夏多雨，将漫莲堤，佣母子夫妇先其灾而去之西乡。果置产力田，今称小裕。而佣之谨悫，见之者以为不异其初。
    (七如氏曰：佣以愿守。维今之人，意其遭际穷约，殆不可以庇一身，又乌料其拥妻子享庸庸之福，而鬼神且阴护之？是故佻达儇薄，巧终见拙，又何异于所适之多不偶也。)

    盐亭旧屋
    盐亭旧屋一区，多怪异，人无敢居者。有吴伶数十人，过其地欲僦寓焉。主人告以故，伶曰：“能无惧，魅纵厉，乌能困我数十伶哉！”晚，众皆寝。其三人夜饮醉，涂面着优孟衣冠，妆关帝像暨周将军、关平侯侍焉，秉烛以观其变。俄而风格磔响，欻见一人血糊满身，号而入跪于前。三人惧。鬼复起，大号灭烛而去。众闻之起，独三人仆而死，冠冕皆毁裂。

    Explanation

    399

    The following year, the spring and summer were marked by heavy rains, threatening to flood the lotus embankment. The couple, fearing the disaster, moved to the west. They eventually acquired land and farmed it, achieving a modest prosperity. The couple remained diligent and honest, and those who saw them thought they were no different from when they started.

    (Chu Ruzi said: The couple remained steadfast in their virtue. In today's world, people often believe that they are destined for poverty and hardship, unable to protect themselves, let alone provide for their families. How could they have anticipated that they would enjoy a simple and ordinary life with their family, while being secretly protected by the spirits and gods? Therefore, those who are frivolous and shallow, who rely on superficial cleverness, will ultimately reveal their clumsiness. How is this different from the frequent misfortune that befalls them?)

    The Old House in Yanting
    The old house in Yanting was full of strange occurrences, and no one dared to live there. Several dozen Wu actors passed by and decided to rent a place to live. The owner warned them about the strange events, but the actors remained fearless. They said, "We can remain fearless. Even if the spirits are fierce, how could they trap us dozens of actors?" In the evening, everyone went to sleep. Three of the actors drank and became drunk, painted their faces, and dressed in the costumes of the actors from the play "优孟衣冠." They set up an altar with statues of Guan Yu and his generals and held candles to observe the changes. Suddenly, they heard a strange and eerie sound and saw a person covered in blood enter and kneel before them. The three actors were frightened, and the spirit rose, making a loud noise and extinguishing the candles before leaving. The other actors woke up and found that the three actors had died, and their hats and crowns were all torn.

    400

    甚矣，人之不可以伪为也。畸人正士，尚不容以袭取一时，况冒天帝、假圣神，自取厉也？固然无足怪。又见豪门大族，每于“晓风残月”之中，翻演“大江东去”，不亦亵之已甚也乎？
    (昔余在都门，见梨园扮演关圣，必先焚楮镪，告诫诚敬，然后敢施朱绘面。终阕后，犹跪拜默祝，其尸敬何如耶！)

    床前影
    余前在单父，居署西偏矮屋中。时值夏秋，淫雨连绵。一夜更深忽醒，窗纸透亮，视床前有人影。余披衣起，遂不见。复睡，且闻履声。又起，寂然。晨兴，余促济南之装。阍曰：“雨载途，不可以车。”余曰：“盍易以马？”及中道，马陷泥中，乃舍而徒，几惫。余有句云“西风东向南城客，卧病骑驴苦雨时”，即此时也。

    Explanation

    400

    How great is the fact that one cannot be false! Even eccentric and upright people cannot gain temporary success through deceit; how much more so those who claim to be gods or saints, bringing disaster upon themselves! It is not surprising, therefore. I have also seen wealthy families, who often perform grand plays in the midst of a serene, melancholic scene ("晓风残月"), is it not too disrespectful?

    (Once I was in the capital, and I saw the theater troupe performing Guan Yu. They would first burn paper money, warn, and show sincere respect, then dare to apply makeup. After the performance ended, they still knelt and silently wished, how great was their reverence!)

    In my previous residence in Shanfu, I lived in a small house to the west of the government office. It was summer and autumn, with continuous heavy rain. One night, deeper into the night, I woke up, and the window paper was bright. I looked at the shadow in front of my bed. I got up in my clothes, and the shadow disappeared. I went back to sleep, and I heard footsteps. I got up again, and it was silent. In the morning, I hurried to pack my things in Jinan. The gatekeeper said, "The roads are filled with rain, it cannot be done by carriage." I said, "Why not change to a horse?" But halfway, the horse got stuck in the mud, so I had to walk, almost exhausted. I have a line of poetry: "A traveler facing west while heading east in the southern city, lying ill and riding a donkey during a rainy time," which describes this moment.

    401

    后闻余去之日，至夜而西墙颓。吁，使余一日不去，余将在岩墙之下矣！彼所谓榻间蹀躞而谘咀者，果伊谁耶？说者曰：“鬼神实阴相之。”呜呼，生死祸福，有数存焉否耶？世之巧为趋避，卒蹈陷阱而不自知者，抑独何哉？抑独何哉？

    鬼头王
    金陵指挥王敏，无子，以运粮把总，过济宁。买一妾，极美，未几生一子。夫与正室相继死，妾治家抚子。既而子袭官，复为把总。部运北上，恳请其外家所在。但言嫁时年幼，已忘之矣。归王氏三十年，早起梳沐，必于榻上帷幙中，至老愈严肃。子妇晨省，立于户外，伺其自出，然后敢前。近侍有二婢，亦未尝见其梳沐也。
    一日晨兴，甫晏，二婢立榻前。忽风动帐开，乃见一无头人持髑髅置膝上，妆饰未完。见二婢，仓皇举头加颈不及，身首俱仆。婢惊呼子妇入视，则一枯骨也。人因呼其子为鬼头王。

    Explanation

    401

    After hearing that I was leaving, at night the western wall collapsed. Alas, if I had stayed one more day, I would have been beneath a cliff! Those who pace back and forth in the bedroom, whispering and muttering, who are they? Someone said, "It was the spirits secretly assisting." Ah, do life and death, fortune and misfortune, have a predetermined fate? The world is full of people who skillfully avoid danger but eventually fall into traps without realizing it. Why is this so? Why is this so?

    Ghost Head Wang
    Wáng Mǐn, a military officer from Jinling, had no children. While transporting grain, he passed through Jining and bought a concubine who was extremely beautiful. Soon after, she gave birth to a son. Her husband and the main wife died one after another, and she took charge of managing the household and raising the child. Later, the son inherited his father's rank and became a military officer. When he was ordered to transport supplies northward, he requested to visit his mother's family. But he only said that she was young when she was married, and she had forgotten the location. After returning to the Wáng family for thirty years, she always combed and bathed in the bedroom behind the curtains. Even until old age, she remained very strict. The son's wife would wait outside the door in the morning, and only when she saw her come out would she dare to approach. There were two maids who also never saw her combing or bathing.

    One morning, just as she was getting up, the two maids stood in front of the bed. Suddenly, the wind blew and the curtain opened, revealing a headless figure holding a skull on its lap, still in the process of dressing. Seeing the two maids, the figure hurriedly raised its head to its neck but failed, and both body and head fell to the ground. The maids screamed and rushed in to see, and it was just a skeleton. People began to call her son "Ghost Head Wang."

    402

    (此条见盛百二补入《济宁志》。)

    金酒缸
    登州属某邑令奉调入帘，有金姓候补者往摄篆务，车从简少，惟厮仆二三人而已。金好饮，尝理民词，登堂以大瓢置案头，当其喜则以糊涂了事，其怒则捶楚交下。邑人恶之，名曰“金酒缸”。一日，为司铎邀请，大醉不能升舆，遂就其坐椅，群舁之归。招摇于市，司铎某公送之，谑曰：“堂上翁今满载归，真可谓名教之罪人也。”后金偶得热症，暴卒。尸未殓，仆役四人逻守。
    夜深，相与席地共饮。其三人背尸坐，一人坐东而西向者则对尸。尸忽起，西向者见之惊仆。三人回首，见尸下，众哗起。尸跃于席，众急以挂锞哀杖乱击之，乃倒。比晓，视尸右手捧握黑磁椎壶，牢不可破。其西向之仆亦死。探其喉间，有一小粗磁酒杯呃于嗓，为之抉出，而气亦不复续。闻是仆尝贪主人之馀沥者。

    Explanation

    402

    This entry was added to the Jining Gazetteer by Sheng Bai'er.

    Jin Wine Vat

    A magistrate from a certain county under the jurisdiction of Dengzhou was appointed to oversee the imperial examinations. A man surnamed Jin, who was on standby for an official position, went to temporarily handle administrative affairs. His entourage was small, consisting only of a few servants. Jin was fond of drinking, and when he would hear cases, he would place a large gourd on his desk. When pleased, he would settle matters carelessly; when angry, he would beat people mercilessly. The townspeople despised him and called him "Jin Wine Vat." One day, he was invited by the local official and became so drunk that he could not mount his carriage. They carried him back on a chair. He paraded through the streets, and a certain official from the local church accompanied him, joking, "The old man in the hall is now returning fully loaded, truly a great sinner against proper conduct." Later, Jin suddenly fell ill with a fever and died. His body had not yet been buried, and four servants were guarding it.

    At night, they sat on the ground and drank together. Three of them sat with their backs to the body, while one sat facing west, opposite the body. Suddenly, the body rose, and the one facing west saw it and fell in shock. The three others turned around and saw the body rising, and they all shouted in alarm. The body leaped onto the ground, and the group quickly beat it with ceremonial sticks until it fell. By morning, they found that the body's right hand was holding a black ceramic wine jug, firmly fixed and unbreakable. The servant who had been sitting facing west also died. When they examined his throat, they found a small, rough ceramic wine cup stuck in it. They removed it, but he also did not continue to breathe. It was said that this servant had once coveted the owner's leftover liquor.

    403

    朱广
    济南朱广，邑庠生。妻张氏，魂游于野，孑然独行，衣无下体，徘徊于石桥危磴之间。俄见一女郎环珮璆然，翩跹而来，如贵家娃，侍婢一人前导。张氏自惭形秽，避之桥下。女至，指婢曰：“此朱相公家娘子也，可掖之来，我与语。”婢引之出，女敛衽曰：“嫂何至此？久将神不返舍矣。我与朱兄久别，常相忆，欲一觌面，恒难。我送嫂归，盍假舍以见吾朱兄？诚为两得。”张氏许之，遂与同归。
    入门户，张氏上床隅，女即襆被而起曰：“两世隔绝，与兄固途人也。兄固不识弟，且弟又隔世为女，况今又借嫂舍以探兄耶？然弟深爱兄数首诗，惓惓不能去诸怀，犹记四首诗。如《忆梅》一首云：
    盎盎春生到兔园，此花消息费评论。遥知南国佳人信，远嫁西湖处士村。
    三尺雪深还偃蹇，一声笛慢又黄昏。茂陵诗本今犹在，曾否冲寒下荜门。

    Explanation

    403

    Zhu Guang
    Zhu Guang of Jinan, a student at the local school. His wife, Zhang, had her soul wandering in the wild, walking alone, with no lower garment, wandering between the stone bridge and dangerous steps. Soon, she saw a young lady with jade ornaments, coming gracefully, like a young woman from a wealthy family, with a maid servant leading the way. Zhang felt ashamed of her appearance and hid under the bridge. The young lady arrived and pointed to her maid, saying, "This is the lady of Master Zhu's household. You can bring her here, and I will speak with her." The maid led her out, and the young lady gathered her skirt and said, "Sister, what brings you here? You have not returned to your body for a long time. I have been apart from Brother Zhu for a long time, often thinking of him, and it has always been difficult to meet. I will send you back, and why not let me stay here to see my Brother Zhu? It would be a win-win situation." Zhang agreed, and they went back together.
    Entering the door, Zhang climbed onto the corner of the bed, and the young lady immediately covered herself with a blanket and said, "Separated by two lifetimes, we are strangers to each other. You certainly don't know me, and I am a woman from a different era, and now I am borrowing your home to see my brother. However, I deeply love your several poems, and I cannot forget them from my heart. I still remember four poems. For example, the poem 'Recalling the Plum Blossom' says:
    The abundant spring comes to the rabbit garden, and the news of this flower is hard to comment on. Far away, I know the message of a beautiful woman in the south, who has married to a scholar in the West Lake village.
    Three feet deep in the snow, still struggling, and the slow sound of a flute at dusk. The poetry of Maoling is still here today, have you ever braved the cold to come down the bamboo gate?

    404

    《探梅》一首云：
    野色围桥古驿遮，琼瑶碎踏兴偏奢。几回路暗初无迹，不断香蒸何处花。
    人到岭头纤月落，神传竹外一枝斜。未知持赠伊谁好，欲寄遥情天水涯。
    《赏梅》一首云：
    孤山选胜白云乡，何逊幽怀此寄将。淡处还如僧入定，夜深浑觉月生香。
    亭亭瘦影思无邪，寂寂寒芳味正长。今日断桥春尚早，正披风帽过雷塘。
    《惜梅》一首云：
    冰胎结到此时成，褪尽繁华却有卿。入画丰神曾作态，坠楼时节总无声。
    书传陇上相思恨，梦绕江南逆旅情。惆怅岁寒踪迹少，漫劳仙客竟呼兄。”
    乃作曼声吟咏，或以手承颐，或搔首而踟蹰。张氏素不知文。朱异之，以笔记焉。女复谓朱曰：“今世为山阴王幕之女，夭殂，厝于正觉寺中。幕无子，最爱我，明日扶我榇归。后会无期。然兄诗固常吟诵于屋梁落月间。”俯首仆枕，半晌而苏，则妻张氏也。共述其异。明晨访诸城隅，果有南来扶柩归者，询之，为节署幕友王雨亭之女也。

    Explanation

    404

    Translation:

    A poem titled "Exploring Plum Blossoms" says:
    The wild scenery surrounds the ancient bridge and the old post station; walking through the snow or ice, the luxury of the experience is heightened. Several times, the path is dark and initially indistinct; the continuous fragrance rises, but from where the flowers? When people reach the mountain peak, the thin moon sets; a divine transmission occurs, with a single branch leaning outside the bamboo. It is unknown who would be the best recipient for this gift; the desire to send far-reaching emotions to the farthest reaches of the horizon.

    A poem titled "Appreciating Plum Blossoms" says:
    On Solitary Mountain, the most beautiful place is selected in the land of white clouds; the deep feelings of He Xun are conveyed here. In the subtle places, it is still like a monk entering meditation; in the deep night, one completely feels the moon emitting fragrance. The upright and graceful shadow, pure thoughts, and the quiet and still atmosphere, with the cold fragrance that is long-lasting. Today, the broken bridge is still early in spring; just putting on a wind hat, passing by Lei塘.

    A poem titled "Cherishing Plum Blossoms" says:
    The ice embryo has formed by this time; shedding all luxury and splendor, yet there is you. Entering the painting, the graceful spirit once posed; falling from the tower, always silent. Letters convey longing and resentment on the hill; dreams wander, feelings of a traveler in the south of the river. Melancholy, few traces in the cold season; in vain, the immortal guest finally calls brother.

    She then recited the poem in a long, drawn-out voice, sometimes holding her chin with her hand, sometimes scratching her head and pacing back and forth. Zhang, who was not familiar with literature, found this strange and noted it down. The woman then told Zhu, "In this world, I was the daughter of the magistrate of Shanyin, who died young and was laid to rest at Zhengjue Temple. The magistrate had no children and loved me dearly. Tomorrow, they will bring my coffin back. We will not meet again. However, my brother's poems are often recited under the moonlight on the eaves." She bowed her head and fell asleep, half an hour later waking up as her wife Zhang. They recounted the strange events. The next morning, they visited the outskirts of the city and found a group from the south bringing a coffin back. Upon inquiry, they learned that it was the daughter of Wang Yuting, a magistrate's assistant in the provincial office.

    405

    (七如曰：作幕者多无后，何也？盖刑名法术，稍不慎重，即能杀人。是不必有心草菅人命、倒置是非也。余友雨亭，谨悫士也，胡令其一掌珠尚沉网底耶？岂果天罚其嗣哉！尝见一“幕字本草”，云：“幕，性寒，有毒，味微酸，无种，产江浙，皆晚生。”)

    罗浮心
    岱宗之高四十里，衡山四千一十丈，华山五千仞，恒山三千九百丈，嵩山少室八百六十丈，天台一万八千丈，罗浮三千丈，青城三千六百丈，天目七千五百丈，武夷五百仞，昆仑一万一千里。此盖天地盘礴之势，孕结而成。好奇者不知经几千百人之游历，几千百年之考志。微特高人逸士蜡屐支筇，探幽而寻胜，即深闺名媛，未尝不开卷卧游，时怦怦动于中，而不能恝然置也。

    Explanation

    405

    (Seven said: Those who serve as secretaries often have no descendants, why is that? It is because legal and administrative techniques, if not handled with caution, can lead to death. This is not necessarily due to a deliberate disregard for human life or a reversal of right and wrong. My friend Yu Ting, a cautious and honest man, why has his beloved daughter remained unmarried? Is it truly a punishment from heaven for not having descendants? I once saw a "Mùzì Běncǎo" (Dictionary of Secretarial Terms), which states: "Mù, its nature is cold and toxic, with a slightly sour taste, no seed, produced in Jiangsu and Zhejiang, all late-born.")

    Luofu Heart
    Mount Tai is forty li high, Mount Heng is 4,110 zhang, Mount Hua is 5,000 ren, Mount Heng is 3,900 zhang, Mount Song's Shaoshi Peak is 860 zhang, Mount Tiantai is 18,000 zhang, Mount Luofu is 3,000 zhang, Mount Qingcheng is 3,600 zhang, Mount Tianmu is 7,500 zhang, Mount Wuyi is 500 ren, and Mount Kunlun is 11,000 li. This is the result of the vastness and grandeur of heaven and earth, formed through gestation and accumulation. Those who seek novelty do not know that it has been visited by thousands of people over thousands of years. Not only do reclusive scholars and gentlemen with waxed shoes and walking sticks explore the depths and seek the sublime, but even women of refined taste, when they open the book, often find themselves transported to these places, their hearts stirred, and unable to let go easily.

    406

    湘陵熊孝泉，少负奇气，读书略识大意。家素封，不求名达，恣情山水。出则搜罗岩谷，入则参订方舆，因镌印章曰“有名山美女癣”。
    一年游西湖灵隐寺，僧寮几上，一庋笔物，非金非石，五彩相宣。熊见而爱之，问所自来。僧谓得之山中古冢旁，土剥蚀满，刷而新之，宝莫能名。熊愿以金易，僧喜。熊得之，置斋头，日夕抚玩。高不二寸，周不完规，重不逾两，而洞壑崇峦，层见叠出，不可胜数。谛观三月，难穷其奥境。雕以檀坐，贮之锦囊，若匹夫怀盈尺之壁，鲛人获径寸之珠，竟不令他人见。

    Explanation

    406

    Xiangling's Xuanyuan Xiang, young and full of extraordinary spirit, read books and grasped their general meanings. His family was wealthy, and he did not seek fame or recognition, instead indulging in the enjoyment of mountains and rivers. When he ventured out, he searched and gathered treasures from the mountains and valleys; when he returned home, he studied and verified geographical records, and thus engraved the seal "Famous Mountains, Beautiful Women, and a Skin Condition."

    One year, he traveled to Lingyin Temple on West Lake. On the desk in a monk's room, there was a pen holder, neither made of gold nor stone, but adorned with five colors. Xuanyuan Xiang saw it and loved it, asking where it came from. The monk said it was found near an ancient tomb in the mountains, covered in soil and eroded, but cleaned and made new. It was a treasure beyond naming. Xuanyuan Xiang wished to exchange gold for it, and the monk was pleased. Xuanyuan Xiang obtained it, placing it at the head of his study, and played with it day and night. It was no more than two inches high, not perfectly round, and weighed less than two taels, yet it depicted deep valleys and towering peaks, appearing in layers and succession, countless in number. He observed it for three months, yet still found it hard to fathom its profound mysteries. He carved a sandalwood seat for it and stored it in a brocade bag, like a commoner holding a jade plate the size of a foot, a mermaid obtaining a pearl the size of an inch, yet never allowing others to see it.

    407

    会当月夕，有款户声。熊启视，则嫣然一女子入，华妆妙丽，婉而多风，笑谓熊曰：“劫坟贼今得之矣。”熊悦其美，戏曰：“从未见夤夜入室，反诬良人为盗者。”女曰：“汝怀中者，是吾旧家物。”熊白其无。女乃取诸袖，曰：“此一品非耶？”熊错愕，捉襟已失，遂与女争辩为己物。女曰：“诚如君言，此物何名？”熊不能名。女曰：“吾固知之也。此名‘小罗浮’，中有四百名峰，历历可指，请以验之。”女于灯前按迹而稽，若者为“铁桥”，为“老人”，为“大、小旗”诸峰，“通天”、“朱明”各洞，皆毫厘可认，直如问道素经。熊狂喜，以为得遇真赏，挽女入坐。女曰：“失而得之，不幸之幸。”囊裳欲去。熊曳女裙，不令出。

    Explanation

    407

    At the moonlit night, there was a knocking sound at the door. Xiong opened it, and a charming and graceful woman entered, dressed in elaborate and beautiful attire. She smiled and said to Xiong, "The grave robber has been caught." Xiong was delighted by her beauty and playfully replied, "I have never seen someone enter my room at night and accuse me of being a thief." The woman said, "The thing in your possession is from my former home." Xiong denied it. The woman then took it from her sleeve and said, "Isn't this one of them?" Xiong was startled and checked his clothes, but the item was already gone. He then argued with the woman that it was his own possession. The woman said, "If what you say is true, what is the name of this item?" Xiong could not name it. The woman said, "I know it well. It is called 'Xiaoluofu,' and it contains four hundred famous peaks, each clearly identifiable. Please verify it." The woman examined it by the lamp, pointing out features such as "Tieqiao," "Laoren," "Daxiaoqi" peaks, and "Tongtian," "Zhuming" caves, all of which were clearly recognizable, as if she had studied it thoroughly. Xiong was overjoyed, thinking he had found a true connoisseur, and pulled the woman to sit down. The woman said, "Losing and finding it is both unfortunate and fortunate." She was about to leave with her clothes. Xiong pulled her skirt to prevent her from leaving.

    408

    女曰：“君欲我投璧而返，我则欲君完璧而归。君既不忍舍此，我又安能割爱耶？”熊曰：“卿留此，与不穀同好，何如？”女曰：“我心匪石，不可转也。将必不得已而去，于斯二者何先？”熊曰：“石不能言，花如解语，皆我所欲也，无一可去。”遂抱入帏，相与狎。问女名，曰：“朝霞。”自此每夕必至，宛如夫妇。有时谈论诗文，间及游览。凡熊昔日所历之境界，尽为霞今日所言之陈迹。两人无事，指点其风雨合离之状。熊曰：“人心之不同，如其面然，物何独异？倘抱此区区，遂谓与勾漏遗迹若合符契，正恐此山真面目，又未必如斯耳。”女曰：“君言诚是也。所谓徒有胜情，恨无情具。”熊曰：“是不难。海上游蓄心已久，卿如有志，当作仙侣同舟。”女应之。

    Explanation

    408

    The woman said, "You wish for me to throw the jade back and return, but I wish for you to return the jade intact. Since you cannot bear to part with it, how could I possibly part with it?" The man said, "Why don't you stay here and share my interests? What do you think?" She replied, "My heart is not stone and cannot be turned. If it is absolutely necessary to part, which of these two should come first?" He said, "Stones cannot speak, but flowers can understand, and both are what I desire. There is none that I can let go of." He then embraced her into the bedchamber, and they became intimate. When he asked her name, she said, "Chaoxia." From then on, she came every evening, as if they were a married couple. Sometimes they discussed poetry and literature, and occasionally mentioned travels. All the places the man had visited in the past were now described by Chaoxia as old memories. When there was nothing else to do, they would point out the changes in the weather and the merging and separation of clouds. The man said, "People's hearts differ just as their faces do; why should objects be any different? If one holds such a small thing, they might say it matches perfectly with the traces of Goulou, but I fear that the true face of this mountain may not be as such." She said, "Your words are indeed true. What is meant is that there is only a superficial understanding, but no true emotional depth." He said, "That is not difficult. I have long harbored the desire to travel to the sea. If you have the will, we will be like immortal companions on the same boat." She agreed.

    409

    买舟入粤，十日抵广州境，去罗浮尚三百里。南望一抹黛痕，弯如新月。女曰：“此增城飞云顶也。”熊不之信，询舟师，诚然。抵增，篮舆入山。日暮至梅花村，宿卖酒田。是夜月明，熊与女凭栏远眺，遥见两山蜿蜒，青翠插天。晨起迤逦前进，观夫星坛天成，石鉴圆洁，湖韫冰玉，竹产茏葱。奏清音于乐地，耀寒光于丹灶，而文禽异卉，交错如锦绣，诚可谓此外无奇。群峰壁立，石楼倚汉，铁桥横空，势凭天倪，影侵溟渤。郭之美之图传，良非虚语，而神在阿堵之间，更无间然矣。女喟然曰：“自有宇宙，便有此山；自有吾生，便忆此山。游踪客迹，登此山、坐此石者，何可胜道！百年之中，谁复能料此身之登此山、坐此石。即百年之后，又乌能料有知之魂魄，犹登此山、坐此石哉。”言已泣下，谓熊曰：“妾有罗浮癖。生前以未到此山，成恨而死。迄今百五十年，始得与君竟了夙缘。我将别矣。”

    Explanation

    409

    I bought a boat to enter Guangdong, arriving at the border of Guangzhou in ten days, still three hundred li away from Luofu Mountain. Looking south, I saw a faint blue line curving like a new moon. The woman said, "This is Feiyun Peak in Zengcheng." Bear did not believe her and asked the boatman, who confirmed it was true. Upon reaching Zengcheng, we entered the mountain by sedan chair. At dusk, we arrived at Plum Village and stayed in a field where wine was sold. That night, the moon was bright, and Bear and the woman leaned on the railing to look far into the distance, seeing two mountains winding in the distance, green and lush reaching up to the sky. In the morning, we proceeded slowly, observing the naturally formed star altars, the round and clean stone mirrors, the lakes hidden with ice and jade, and the dense bamboo groves. Clear sounds were played in a place of music, and cold light shone on the alchemy furnace, while exotic birds and flowers intertwined like brocade. Truly, there is nothing else remarkable. Cliffs stand like walls, stone buildings lean against the sky, and an iron bridge spans the heavens, its momentum relying on the edge of the sky, its shadow extending into the sea. The beauty of Guo's maps and paintings is not an empty claim, and the spirit is in this place, with no doubt about it. The woman sighed and said, "Since the beginning of the universe, this mountain has existed; since I was born, I have remembered this mountain. The traces of travelers, those who have climbed this mountain and sat on this stone, how can they be counted! In a hundred years, who could have predicted that I would climb this mountain and sit on this stone. Even after a hundred years, who could predict that the knowing soul would still climb this mountain and sit on this stone?" After speaking, tears fell, and she said to Bear, "I have a passion for Luofu Mountain. Before death, I regretted not having visited this mountain. After one hundred and fifty years, I finally fulfill my destiny with you. I will depart."

    410

    熊方欲语，女忽颓然，发秃肌黄，身缩如茧，杳杳而灭。熊惊，急探袖中，已化数点杜鹃红泪，斑斑如渍而已。
    噫！熊之好，女之病也。癖之于人甚矣，独熊也乎哉！熊有六言三绝云：
    蝴蝶飞来栩栩，梅花开后沉沉。香阁无缘览胜，芳魂何幸登临。
    丹灶仙翁葛令，西湖贤守坡公。心在桃源洞里，人归飞瀑岩中。
    危石深林鸟道，小桥流水人家。梵宇声沉暮霭，天风吹散朝霞。
    (又与《情史·化石人》同一窠臼，而胸罗青翠，离合风雨，更有奇致出尘。)

    泥娃娃
    颜神镇国氏女，嫁人，即有病，未久而死。其夫哭之痛。数日后，夫独宿。忽见妻牵帏入，华妆盛服，艳逾生时。夫挽诸怀，见其言笑，皆极燕婉情意，多喜悦事。每夜必至，凌晨揽衣而去，衣作纸摺声。其夫往往诉伊生前死后，备极凄楚，妇对之愈为展笑。

    Explanation

    410

    The Bear was about to speak when the woman suddenly collapsed, her hair thinning, skin yellowing, and body shrinking like a cocoon, vanishing into the distance. The Bear was shocked and quickly reached into his sleeve, only to find a few spots of red tears, like those of a cuckoo, now stained on his clothes.

    Ah! The Bear's affection was the woman's illness. Obsession is a grave malady for people; is it only the Bear who suffers from it? The Bear had six lines and three verses to say:

    Butterflies flutter by, vibrant and lively; plum blossoms bloom, heavy and serene. The fragrant chamber lacks the chance to see its wonders, yet the soul of the flower is fortunate to ascend. The alchemist Ge Hong, the virtuous governor Su Shi. The heart resides in the Peach Blossom Spring, the person returns to the waterfall's cliff.

    Steep cliffs and deep forests, paths for birds; small bridges over flowing streams, homes of people. The sound of the temple fades into the evening mist, the wind disperses the morning glow.

    (And it shares the same essence as "The Stone Person" in "History of Love," yet it is filled with verdant imagery, intertwined with wind and rain, and possesses a unique, ethereal quality.)

    The Clay Doll

    A woman from Yan Shen Town, married, fell ill shortly after and died. Her husband mourned her deeply. Several days later, while he was alone in bed, he suddenly saw his wife pull the curtain aside and enter, dressed in splendid attire, more beautiful than ever before. He held her in his arms and saw that she spoke and laughed, expressing the utmost tenderness and affection, filled with joyous moments. Every night she would come, and in the early morning, she would take her clothes and leave, the fabric making a crinkling sound like paper. Her husband often recounted the events before and after her death, filled with sorrow, and she would smile more brightly in response.

    411

    月馀家人觉之，以为祟，驱之不能。将及一载，抚其妇腹膨膨，然至房事亦不戒。一日，妇忽告其夫曰：“今当赴泰山，不复能时来。但身有孕，分娩后，当送来交汝育之。”遂去，自此寂然。次年，其夫夜眠醒，摸被中得一泥娃，亦无他异。
    (是鬼之冒妻送儿，皆成游戏，亦鬼道中之趣鬼也。)

    卷十二 怪异类
    画版
    洋画以京师为最。一切古鼎彝器，无不确似。为山树楼阁，远近深邃，尺幅千里。一邱一壑、一枝一叶、一棂一庋，皆能突起于阴阳向背之间。闻其初来自西域，京师易之，所谓界尺活也。至人物，则以广南玻璃画为独步，面目须发，有跃跃欲飞之势。余有一律云：
    一幅亚洋画得成，千盘万曲讶深閎。定神玩去疑身入，着手摸来似掌平。
    幻出楼台蜃气结，描将人物黛眉生。壁间高挂终惶惑，错认邻家院落横。

    Explanation

    411

    Translation

    After a month, the family noticed something unusual and thought it was a ghostly disturbance, but they could not drive it away. Almost a year passed, and he felt his wife's swollen belly, yet he did not abstain from sexual relations. One day, the wife suddenly told her husband, "I am now going to Mount Tai and will no longer be able to come often. But I am pregnant, and after giving birth, I will send the child to you to raise." She then left, and from then on, there was silence. The following year, the husband woke up during the night and found a clay doll in the bed; there was no other anomaly. (This ghost pretending to be the wife delivering a child is all part of the game, a kind of playful ghost.)

    Chapter Twelve: Strange and Unusual

    Painting

    Foreign paintings are best found in the capital. All ancient bronze vessels and ritual objects are depicted with great accuracy. Mountains, trees, pavilions, and towers are portrayed with depth and perspective, creating a vast landscape in a small space. Hills, valleys, branches, leaves, windows, and cabinets all emerge vividly in the interplay of light and shadow. It is said that these paintings originally came from the Western Regions and were adapted in the capital, known as "boundary ruler" techniques. As for figures, Guangnan glass paintings are unparalleled, with faces and hair rendered in a lifelike manner, as if they are about to leap off the canvas. I have a poem that goes:
    A foreign painting is complete,
    With winding paths and deep realms that astonish.
    Focusing intently, one feels as if entering,
    Touching it, it seems flat as a palm.
    Miraculously, palaces rise like mists,
    Portraying figures with delicate eyebrows.
    Hanging high on the wall, one remains in awe,
    Mistakenly thinking it is a neighbor's courtyard.

    412

    辛丑游粤，在新会袁春舫业师署，闻库中有西洋美人画一对，甚异。师令胥吏持入廨观之。已昏，设炬置桌。俄而持二版至，各长四五尺，盖随人画形而刓之者，皆系以械。其一衣绯，色剥落，约二十许，丰颐隆准，高钿云髻，一手持物如烛台形，一手自理衣带，如大家娃；其一衣黄，修容，堕马，半面惊顾之状，两手捧物不能辨，丰神凛然，面上有爪痕，年较稚。灯光寻丈之外，望之若生，流波凝睇，若接若离，可惊可怖。
    先有黎姓少年癖于画本，凡有山水人物，极力求取，而纸上丽人，尤所珍爱。一日，有僧至其家，募修大士像，生不为容。僧云：“闻居士好丹青，盈箱箧，想无佳者。贫衲能为笔墨。苟不为叶公好，当结一翰墨缘。”生喜，问所欲纸椠，僧曰：“无须。君卧室双扉后，愿为君图所好。”生延入内寝。僧探囊取物，色色俱备。笑谈之间，二美已具。生大喜，赠以金缗而去。

    Explanation

    412

    In the year of Xin Chou, I traveled to Guangdong and stayed at the residence of my former teacher Yuan Chunfang in Xinwei. I heard that there were two Western beauty paintings in the treasury, which seemed very strange. My teacher ordered the clerks to bring them into the office for viewing. It was already dusk, so we set up torches and placed them on the table. Soon, two panels were brought, each about four or five feet long. These were carvings made according to the shapes of the people, all bound with restraints. One of them wore red, with the color faded, and was about twenty years old. She had full cheeks and a prominent nose, a highly adorned cloud-like hairdo, holding an object shaped like a candlestick in one hand and adjusting her belt with the other, like a noble lady's daughter. The other one wore yellow, with an elegant appearance, falling off a horse, with a half-turned, startled look, holding something that could not be identified, her radiant and awe-inspiring presence, with claw marks on her face, and she appeared younger. The light was several feet away, and it looked as if they were alive, with flowing gazes and fixed looks, as if connected and disconnected, both shocking and terrifying.

    There was a young man surnamed Li who was passionate about paintings. He sought out all kinds of landscapes and figures, especially cherishing the beautiful women depicted on paper. One day, a monk came to his home, soliciting donations to repair a statue of a bodhisattva. The young man did not receive him kindly. The monk said, "I have heard that you enjoy painting, with a full collection of works, but I think there are none of high quality. I am a poor monk who can provide you with ink and brushes. If you are not like the man who loved dragons but only wanted the picture, we can form a bond through calligraphy and painting." The young man was pleased and asked what kind of paper and blocks the monk needed. The monk replied, "There is no need. In your bedroom, behind the double doors, I will create what you desire." The young man invited him into his inner chamber. The monk took out various objects from his bag, all prepared. In the midst of their conversation, the two beauties were completed. The young man was greatly pleased and gave him a sum of money before he left.

    413

    生夜爇火阖户，相对双隗，心摇目眩。将从前所好置之高阁，惟注意在人，静掩双扉，更阑欲上床矣。偶于醉后假寐灯几，有人倚隅捏肩云：“君子醉休。曷太不自珍千金躯，欲向醉乡老耶？”生惊起，见一丽姝在侧，嫣然可爱，遂不为诧。问曰：“卿仙乎？人乎？胡多露而不畏耶？”姝曰：“我画中人耳。君朝夕相对，何觌面转相忘？”生觑扉间脱空其一，望见阶前月影，俨如窗开。心荡不自持，相抱而狎，衽席颇致情款。女云：“奴号左青，怜感君德而奔君。二兰女子熟睡不知。奴去也，恐为所觉，不耐伊啰唣。且伊性悍不驯，君勿与接也。”转盼，人与扉合。生不知是梦是幻，怅惘久之，酒气全消。

    Explanation

    413

    At night, the man lit a fire and closed the door, sitting opposite a woman, his heart agitated and his eyes dazzled. He set aside his previous interests and focused solely on her, quietly closing the double doors, deep into the night, about to get into bed. After a brief nap by the lamp on the table, he was startled awake by someone leaning against the corner and gently touching his shoulder, saying, "Sir, you are drunk. Why do you not value your precious body, wanting to grow old in drunkenness?" The man jumped up, seeing a beautiful woman beside him, charming and lovely, and was not surprised. He asked, "Are you a goddess? A mortal? Why are you so exposed and not afraid?" The woman replied, "I am a woman from a painting. You see me every day, yet you forget me face-to-face?" The man looked at the gap in the door, and saw the moonlight on the steps, as if a window had opened. His heart was overwhelmed with emotion, and they embraced, indulging in each other's affection. The woman said, "I am called Left Green. I was moved by your virtue and came to you. The two women are asleep and unaware. I must go now, fearing they will discover me, and I cannot bear their nagging. Besides, she is fierce and unyielding; do not associate with her." As she turned to leave, the person merged with the door. The man did not know if it was a dream or an illusion, feeling longingly lost for a while, the wine fumes completely dissipated.

    414

    正凝思间，双影齐下，若闻诟谇，生不敢置喙。二兰云：“好女子，好女子，丑事羞人！”左青云：“人家事，何预尔？”二兰云：“同门合楣，岂容尔私？”生云：“二女既可同居，三人不更同心乎？”遂两袭其裾，同登卧榻，共相偎倚。生欲与二兰致情，左青隔，不使通。既而事齐不可，事楚又不可，悉索交敝，终夜不宁。欲树静而风转摇之，调停向背，位致大小，各不相亚。口角之间，未尝不絮絮然当以旗鼓。

    Explanation

    414

    As he was lost in thought, two shadows descended together, and he seemed to hear gossip and criticism, but he dared not speak up. The two Lanes said, "Good women, good women, how shameful this scandalous affair is!" Left Qing remarked, "It's someone else's business, what concern is it of yours?" The two Lanes replied, "We share the same family, how can you act so privately?" The man said, "If two women can live together, wouldn't three be even more united?" He then caught hold of both their sleeves, and they climbed onto the bed together, leaning against each other. The man wanted to express his feelings to the two Lanes, but Left Qing blocked them, preventing communication. Soon, favoring one would not do, nor would favoring the other; they exhausted all efforts, leading to mutual exhaustion, and the whole night was restless. Trying to make the tree still, but the wind kept shaking it; trying to balance conflicting interests, in terms of importance or status, none were inferior to the others. In the midst of a dispute, they never ceased talking, as if they were competing like drums and flags.

    415

    从此日夜奔命，摄乎两大之间，不旬日而形同枯槁矣。家人不知所以，乃移入母室。至夜，两女悉至，更相交谪。家人不见其形，但闻其声。医来不瘳，巫至不压，一家鼎沸，四邻皆为不安。后其父夜起，隐忧不寐，步庭前，见其子所居之二扉，如刻人形而中离，燃以膏，疑是怪，遂破其空扉。至晨，而二版画在焉。父衔之，付诸丙，弗戢；投诸渊，不沉。床笫厨室大肆杂谑，不堪其扰，犹治丝而棼。生已奄息，阖邑哄传。
    邑令鲁人司马氏，秉正不阿。访闻之，不信，呼其父而问，无异词。乃拘系其版，函以印而封于库。其画至今存，然非其人有终任，不敢启视者。而吏备述其颠未。春舫师曰：“是不可以不纪其事。”时徐闻尹梅公云官、同门蔡都谏秦均、二世弟堂，各有记。予因次日束装北旋不暇，舟次清远峡中，为补书其略如此。

    Explanation

    415

    From this point on, he was constantly on the move, caught between two great powers, and within a few days, his appearance was as withered and gaunt as a corpse. His family did not know why, so they moved him into his mother's room. At night, two women came, taking turns blaming each other. The family could not see their forms, but only hear their voices. The doctor came but failed to cure him, and the witch arrived but could not suppress the phenomenon. The whole family was in turmoil, and the neighbors were all uneasy. Later, his father got up at night, troubled by hidden worries and unable to sleep, walking in the courtyard. He saw two doors in his son's room, carved with human figures that were split apart, burning with oil, and suspected it was a supernatural phenomenon. He then broke the empty door. By morning, the two wooden carvings were there. The father took them, burned them, but they did not burn; he threw them into the river, but they did not sink. Bedrooms, kitchens, and living rooms were filled with vulgar jokes, unbearable to endure, like untangling a ball of thread. The man had already died, and the whole town was abuzz with talk.

    The magistrate, a man from Lu named Sima, upheld justice without compromise. He heard of it but did not believe it, so he called the father and asked, and there was no different account. He then seized the carvings, sealed them with a stamp, and stored them in the treasury. The carvings still exist today, but unless the right person has the final responsibility, no one dares to open and look at them. The officials have fully recorded the events. The teacher, Chunfang, said, "This cannot be left unrecorded." At the time, the magistrate of Xunwen, Mei Gong Yun Guan, and his fellow censor, Cai Du Jian Qin Jun, as well as the second-generation brother, Tang, each had records. I, due to the urgency of packing and heading north the next day, was on the boat at the Qianyuan Gorge, and supplemented the account in this way.

    416

    曲居士
    曲居士，掖县人。居城西草庵，貌甚古，言多颠狂，人未之识也。雍正十二年春，草庵夜火，其光烛天。比熄，则居士端坐其中，俨然如生，惟顶上露一孔，体如铜铸。当时余从叔次南在莱，曾经亲见，持烟具击之，铛铛有声。
    (望夫石实有其人乎？)

    耿姓
    历城东北乡耿某，逸其名。贩枣为业，往来乐陵诸处。一日，推小车，置省界，休大树下，击镰吸烟。欻有少年来，批其颊曰：“孩子一二言语，便使木杯性，数年不归家门！”耿见其意不恶，料是郎舅相狎者，曰：“无作剧，我非尔家娇客。”少年曰：“尊舅前妆懵懂耶？”诮让间，有二三人至，曰：“王姐夫归来乎？”耿不认识。少年以足踢其臀曰：“打你个当场不认父。”

    Explanation

    416

    Mr. Qu
    Mr. Qu, a reclusive scholar from Yexian, lived in a thatched cottage on the west side of the city. He had an ancient appearance and often spoke in a crazy and erratic manner, which made people unfamiliar with him. In the spring of the twelfth year of the Yongzheng Emperor's reign, a fire broke out at night in the thatched cottage, and its light illuminated the sky. By the time the fire was extinguished, Mr. Qu was found sitting upright in the center, as if he were still alive, except for a small hole on top of his head, and his body was as hard as if it were cast in bronze. At that time, my second uncle, Zinan, who was in Lai, had personally witnessed this. He struck Mr. Qu with a smoking utensil, and it made a clanging sound.
    (Does the Weifushi stone really refer to such a person?)

    Mr. Geng
    Mr. Geng from the northeastern part of Licheng County, whose name is unknown. He made a living by selling jujubes and traveled to various places in Lecheng. One day, he pushed a small cart, stopped at the provincial boundary, rested under a large tree, and smoked while cutting grass. Suddenly, a young man came and slapped his cheek, saying, "With just a few words, you've turned into a wooden cup, and you've been away from home for years!" Mr. Geng saw that the young man's intentions were not bad and guessed that he might be a relative who was teasing him. He said, "Don't play around, I'm not your family's favorite guest." The young man replied, "Were you previously ignorant, your uncle?" While scolding him, two or three more people arrived and asked, "Has Uncle Wang returned?" Mr. Geng did not recognize them. The young man kicked him in the buttocks and said, "I'll teach you a lesson for not recognizing your father in front of everyone."

    417

    众拥而行，及其车，哄然入村，曰：“王家姐夫归。”抵一草门，老妪出视，曰：“好儿子，真令我望眼俱穿矣！”入室，一少妇娉婷，二十许，泪涔涔，以袖拭面曰：“是那向风吹了来也！”耿两手频摇，力辩其非。众皆排挤嘈杂，或笑或诽，不容置喙。俄妪及妇入厨下，邻党渐散，惟前少年数人在坐。耿方缓颊，陈词，备道乡贯姓名居址：“并非无根蒂人，奈何诬以桃僵，竟用张冠错戴哉？”少年曰：“声音面貌，酷肖无两。世间岂真出鲤鱼精变化，要包丞相断无头案？姐夫莫诳我也。夫妻无隔宿仇，何必乃尔。”顷间，妪与妇具馔，耿局促不敢举箸。妪及诸人若或贰焉。妇呼其弟至窗外语曰：“尔姐夫左胯有黑痣，隆起生毛。”耿闻言大窘，手护腼瞅，罔知所措。群乃争褫之，布裤穷而痣毛见焉。耿虽百舌不能辩，佥曰：“尚何抵赖！”耿无奈。

    Explanation

    417

    The crowd moved together, reaching his carriage, and entered the village in a noisy and chaotic manner, shouting, "The brother-in-law of the Wang family has returned." They arrived at a humble gate, and an old woman came out to see, saying, "Good son, you've really made me wait anxiously!" Inside the room, a graceful young woman, about twenty years old, with tears streaming down her face, wiped her face with her sleeve and said, "He was blown in by the wind!" The protagonist shook his hands frequently, vigorously defending himself against the accusation. The crowd pushed and shoved, creating a noisy and chaotic environment, some laughing, others criticizing, and not allowing him to speak. Soon, the old woman and the young woman entered the kitchen, and the neighbors gradually dispersed, leaving only a few young men sitting. The protagonist spoke softly, explaining his background, detailing his hometown, occupation, name, and address: "I am not someone without roots. Why falsely accuse me of being a substitute for a peach tree, and why have I been given the wrong hat?" One of the young men said, "Your voice and appearance are extremely similar. Is it really true that there are fish spirits transforming into people? Even if the Chancellor of the Han dynasty (Bo Yi) were to judge, there would be no unresolved case. Brother-in-law, don't deceive me. Husband and wife should not have overnight grudges. Why be so extreme?" Shortly after, the old woman and the young woman prepared food, and the protagonist felt awkward and unable to pick up the chopsticks. The old woman and the others seemed to be doubting. The young woman called her brother to the window and whispered, "Your brother-in-law has a black mole on his left hip that protrudes and has hair." The protagonist, upon hearing this, was greatly embarrassed, covering his face and looking shyly, not knowing what to do. The crowd then struggled to remove his clothes, and when the cloth pants were removed, the mole and hair were revealed. Despite his many words, he could not defend himself, and everyone said, "Still trying to deny it!" The protagonist was helpless.

    418

    饭毕，日向暮，妇持檠至，诸人散，妪去。妇掩户喜，近耿曰：“真丈夫何以假为？”耿曰：“武陵源今虽误入，实非前度渔郎。第问津有自，殊惭唐突西子耳。”妇曰：“何其形神之似我夫也？”夜半，妇谛审熟玩，颇觉其异；然两人情好甚欢。妇曰：“今若此，所谓非真即真，只好将错就错耳。”耿曰：“固然，但恐真者至，而乱真者无容身之地矣！”妇曰：“世道聩盲，皆认假而不认真者，故真者假之，假者真之，率相诈伪，比比皆是。尔又何必私心过计为哉！”妇于枕畔告以家人姓名，及其前夫入赘始末，并邻里亲故。诘旦，捱门遍谢。一村之中，无假之者，咸以王某归，得健忘病。遂为夫妇如初，而两人恒惴惴恐其前夫返。五六年迄无音耗。

    Explanation

    418

    After dinner, as the day was drawing to a close, the woman brought a lamp to light the room. The others dispersed, and the old woman left. The woman closed the door with joy and approached Geng, saying, "Why pretend to be a real man when you are not one?" Geng replied, "Though I have mistakenly entered the Wuling Source, I am not the previous fisherman. Though I have my own reasons for asking, I am truly embarrassed to have intruded on you." The woman said, "How similar you look and act to my husband!" At midnight, the woman examined him carefully and thoroughly, feeling somewhat strange about it; however, the two of them were very affectionate. The woman said, "Now that we are in this situation, it is said that whether it is true or not, one must just make the best of a bad situation." Geng said, "Indeed, but I fear that if the real one comes, the one pretending will have no place to hide!" The woman said, "The world is deaf and blind, all recognizing the fake and not the real, so the real ones pretend to be fake, and the fake ones pretend to be real. Deception and pretense are everywhere. Why should you worry excessively about this?" The woman told him about the names of her family members, the beginning and end of her previous husband's marriage, and their neighbors and relatives. At dawn, they went door-to-door to thank everyone. In the entire village, no one doubted them, and they were all told that Wang had returned, suffering from amnesia. They became husband and wife again, but the two of them were always anxious, fearing that her previous husband would return. For five or six years, there was no news of him.

    419

    耿仍以贩枣，时一至济南，家中俱悉其事。后其妪死，耿执婿礼，克尽孝道，一切衣衾丧葬，皆耿经理，诸内弟咸感之。
    耿一日绐其众曰：“向年返里，忍为此态者，诚以愤愤出门，过而不入。我在山东历城贸迁颇富，业经娶妻生子，薄置田产。乃诸弟遮道挽回，我又念岳母垂暮，未能心恝。今幸大事已完，诸弟克自成立。‘倒札门’终非了局，几见有啜丈人家碗，算好男子耶？此间乡僻，无以为计，我将移家济南，亦免心恋两地耳。”当时诸弟俱完娶，方愁食指，初闻其说，留之；继亦允可。妇乃整装。邻串饯食者数日。妇跨一驴，耿膏其车，载行李，轧轧得得，出村以去。送之者挥涕成行。抵家，其妻邵氏相安。妇与邵叙年齿，遂姊邵。又十馀年，其前夫渺无闻矣，诸弟时来相探云。

    Explanation

    419

    Geng continued to sell dates and occasionally visited Jinan. Everyone in the family knew about this. After his mother-in-law died, Geng observed the etiquette of a son-in-law and fully practiced filial piety. All matters concerning clothing, burial, and funeral arrangements were handled by Geng, and all his brothers-in-law were moved by him.

    One day, Geng told his family, "In the past year when I returned home, I endured this situation, truly because I left in anger, passing by without entering. I have been quite wealthy from trading in Licheng, Shandong. I have already married and had children, and I have a small amount of land and property. Then my brothers blocked the road to try to persuade me to stay, but I also thought of my mother-in-law who was old and frail, unable to leave her. Now that important matters are settled, my brothers have become established. 'Reversing the lock' is not a final solution. How often do you see a man who eats from his father-in-law's bowl and is considered a good man? This place is remote, and there is nothing to be done. I will move my family to Jinan, which will also prevent me from being torn between two places." At that time, all his brothers were married and had families, and they were worried about supporting them. At first, they tried to persuade him to stay; later, they also agreed. The woman then prepared to leave. Neighbors and relatives held farewell feasts for several days. The woman rode a donkey, while Geng greased the cart, loaded the luggage, and they left the village with a creaking sound. Those who saw them off shed tears, forming a line. Upon arriving home, his wife, Shao, was comfortable. The woman and Shao discussed their ages, and the woman became Shao's elder sister. After more than ten years, his former husband had no news, and his brothers occasionally came to visit.

    420

    (耿郎狐耶？王郎鬼耶？世有此巧事耶？)

    地市
    余少时返里，随先君子晨兴出城，上故阡。时当秋初晴晓，白露晞阳。平野之间，忽现山林城郭，彷佛有人物车马往来驰骤之状，周遭皆水，相映诸影，悉倒其下，历历可指。水中又起一小陂陀，上有数人环坐，举杯共酌。余洞视，无毫发间。先君子不之见，但以为晓雾迷漫耳。顷之日出，幻灭不见。人谓近海有海市，近山有山市，南方有鬼市，兹则地市也。

    海风
    登州滨海多风，冬最寒，又时多雪。盖海气随风而易作，人往往多中海风，得痿疾。有李姓者，一日晨起出门外，为海风所刮，耳目口鼻皆尚左，百药罔效。年馀，又立门外与人谈及前此被风得疾状。忽又为风所刮，耳目口鼻皆尚右。噫！昔也所恶于左，毋以交于右；今则所恶于右，毋以交于左。而为之风者，则左之右之，无不宜之。

    Explanation

    420

    (Is Geng Lang a fox? Is Wang Lang a ghost? Is there such a clever or strange event in the world?)

    Land Mirage
    When I was young, I returned to my hometown and, accompanied by my father, rose early in the morning to leave the city and ascend the old path. At the beginning of autumn, on a clear dawn, the white dew was drying under the sun. In the vast plain, a sudden appearance of forests, cities, and streets emerged, as if people, carriages, and horses were passing by in a hurried manner. All around was water, and the reflections of the surroundings were all inverted beneath it, clearly visible. In the water, a small hillock rose, on which several people were sitting in a circle, raising their cups to drink together. I could see everything clearly, not missing even the tiniest detail. My father did not see this, but thought it was just the morning mist obscuring the view. Soon the sun rose, and the illusion vanished. People say that near the sea there is the sea mirage, near the mountains there is the mountain mirage, and in the south there is the ghost market. This, however, is the land mirage.

    Sea Wind
    Dengzhou, which borders the sea, is often windy, especially in winter when it is coldest, and there is often snow. The sea mist is easily stirred by the wind, and people often suffer from sea wind, resulting in paralysis. There was a man surnamed Li, who one morning, upon stepping outside, was struck by the sea wind, causing his ears, eyes, mouth, and nose to all tilt to the left. Despite taking countless medicines, none were effective. After a year, he stood outside again and talked to someone about his previous illness caused by the wind. Suddenly, he was struck by the wind again, and this time his ears, eyes, mouth, and nose all tilted to the right. Alas! What he once disliked about the left, he no longer dislikes on the right; what he now dislikes about the right, he no longer dislikes on the left. And the wind that caused it, whether it tilts to the left or the right, finds no fault in it.

    421

    猪妖
    镇海县西门外，有何姓民家女，年十七。病疳瘵，瘦黄不支，行路皆倩人扶掖，爇蒸不得眠，医药杂投，百无一效。而匤儴之态，正似残花遇雨，弱絮随风。其父母深以为忧。
    一日，有书生款户求见。何翁延入，视其状，睛圆耳大、面广身赘，揖而请曰：“某朱姓。闻掌珠有恙，特来奉一刀圭，以疗痼疾。”翁遑遽，未及答，朱起立曰：“请诣绣闼，一诊视之。”翁挽其袖曰：“素昧平生，即使妙国手，奈何仓猝入人闺阃耶？”朱拂衣飘然而入。翁蹀躞尾之，扬于内曰：“不知何许人，突如来如！”其女方起坐榻上，以衾围下体，闻父哗喧，急曳衾面里。朱骤至，据床揭被而赞曰：“足似红莲，臂如白藕，真令我魂消矣！”翁踵接，见女剥肤，缩而出，大诟詈。其母及婢咸来，室中无所见。翁告以故，皆惊。女覆衾，复起坐，但觉面颊敷红，鬓丝抖乱，惘然若有所注，问之亦不答。

    Explanation

    421

    In the western suburbs of Zhenhai County, there was a girl from a common household named He, aged seventeen. She was suffering from a wasting illness called ganzhai (likely tuberculosis or a similar condition), and she was extremely thin and weak, unable to walk without being carried by others. She was restless and could not sleep, and despite trying various medicines, none of them had any effect. Her pitiful appearance was like a withered flower in the rain and weak cotton seeds in the wind. Her parents were deeply worried about her condition.

    One day, a scholar knocked on the door and asked to see the girl. Mr. He invited him in and saw that the man had large eyes, large ears, a broad face, and a protruding body. The man bowed and said, "I am surnamed Zhu. I heard that your daughter is ill, and I have come to offer a remedy to cure her chronic illness." Mr. He was startled and did not have time to respond. The man stood up and said, "Please allow me to visit her in her room to take a look." Mr. He grabbed the man's sleeve and said, "We have never met before. Even if you are a skilled doctor, how can you suddenly enter a woman's private quarters?" The man brushed off his sleeve and entered the room. Mr. He followed him and shouted inside, "Who is this person who suddenly comes like this!"

    The girl was just sitting up on the bed, covering her lower body with a quilt. When she heard her father's shouting, she quickly pulled the quilt over her face. The man suddenly arrived, sat on the bed, lifted the blanket, and praised, "Your feet are like red lotus blossoms, and your arms are like white lotus roots. Truly, you make me lose my heart!" Mr. He followed him and saw that the girl's skin was peeling off. He was shocked and shouted. Her mother and maidservants all came running, but there was no one in the room. Mr. He told them what had happened, and they were all shocked. The girl covered herself with the quilt again and sat up, but she felt her cheeks were flushed, her hair was tousled, and she was in a daze, as if something were affecting her. When asked, she did not respond.

    422

    至夜，闻帷中若絮絮作两人语。其母启帏来视，女瞪目怒。母曰：“儿终夜何所事？”女曰：“儿事不干预老人。”逾夕，则笑语盈盈，如莺雏学啭，在花柳深处。咸以为妖，无计可去，而女常有喜容。一月，女之色渥丹，颜舜华，渐至腰围时解，钮扣频松；三阅月，而颐丰颊腻，非复当时之瘦影堪怜、鸡骨大都一把矣。翁终不怿，多方延访有能制者。
    后闻有天台僧某，善驱邪，正欲往诣。忽中堂朱语曰：“泰山何见嫌？我与令千金原有夙因，半载以来，未尝不利于翁家。我固非人，然我尝以人道自处。故我之于人也，不惟不忍残其生，抑且必欲救其死。令爱于尸居馀气之下，顿起沉疴，精完本返，伊谁之力？今犹不以我为倩，而以祟目我。我岂能郁郁坦腹于兹耶？我去矣！”其女急出，泪荧荧，呼曰：“朱郎！朱郎！曷归乎来？”亦无所应。自此杳然。

    Explanation

    422

    By night, the sound of soft murmuring as if two people were talking could be heard from behind the curtain. Her mother opened the curtain to look, and the girl glared angrily. The mother asked, "What have you been doing all night?" The girl replied, "My affairs do not interfere with the old lady." The next day, she was laughing and talking, like a young bird learning to chirp in the depths of flowers and willows. Everyone thought it was an omen, but there was no way to get rid of her, and the girl always had a joyful expression. After a month, her complexion was rosy red, her face was as beautiful as the flower of the mulberry tree, and her waist became looser, with her buttons frequently loosening. After three months, her cheeks were plump and smooth, no longer the thin shadow that was pitiable. The old man was still unhappy and sought out many people who could control the situation.

    Later, it was heard that there was a monk from TianTai who was skilled in exorcism, and the old man was about to go to consult him. Suddenly, in the middle of the hall, Zhu spoke, "Why do you dislike me, Mount Tai? I have a predestined relationship with your daughter. For half a year, I have never been harmful to your family. I am not human, but I always act according to human principles. Therefore, I do not only refrain from harming her life, but also wish to save her from death. Your daughter was in a state of near-death, suddenly recovered from a serious illness, her spirit returned to its original state, whose effort was it? Now you still don't consider me a favor, but you treat me as an evil spirit. How can I stay here feeling depressed? I am leaving!" The girl rushed out, tears streaming down her face, calling out, "Zhu Lang! Zhu Lang! Where have you gone? Come back!" But there was no response. From then on, she disappeared completely.

    423

    女尝言其脊有黑毛如棕，直达尾闾。疑是猪妖。未及一年，女之丰姿辄减，羸瘦倍于前。翁为之择婿出嫁，后痨瘵日甚，又不生育云。

    杨汝虔
    滇南杨汝虔，为银商，开生矿，家暴富，得银之磄也。族无缙绅，时见凌于官长。杨奋然携多资，直上长安。回首五华峰顶，饶有司马题桥之志。抵都，假寓于珠宝市。初犹雏也，一切冠履器具，少合时宜。杨固多金，一月而衣裳楚，二月而仆马都，三阅月而候门者多王公卿矣。于是夤缘当道，求托他途。会边戍需储，开纳粟例。杨输貲巨万，遂得官，议叙湖州太守。

    Explanation

    423

    A woman once claimed that she had black hair like a palm tree on her back, extending to her tailbone. It was suspected that she might be a pig demon. Within a year, her beauty diminished significantly, and she became much thinner than before. Her father arranged for her to marry, and after marriage, she developed tuberculosis, which worsened over time, and she did not bear children.

    Yang Ruqian
    Yang Ruqian from Yunnan was a silver merchant who became extremely wealthy by mining silver. His family lacked influential officials and was often mistreated by local authorities. Yang, determined and carrying a lot of wealth, traveled directly to Chang'an. Looking back at the top of Wuhua Peak, he had the same ambition as Sima Xiangru who inscribed a bridge. Upon arriving in the capital, he rented a place to live in the jewelry market. Initially, he was inexperienced, and his attire and possessions did not quite fit the standards of the time. Yang, however, quickly became well-dressed, had fine horses and servants, and soon attracted many high-ranking officials to visit him. He then sought connections with influential people to find other ways to advance. At the time, there was a need for supplies for border defense, and a system was established for people to contribute grain in exchange for official positions. Yang contributed a large amount of money and thus obtained an official position, being appointed as the governor of Huzhou.

    424

    杨去家远，不能假归，领凭后，买舟赴任。都门祖饯，行色甚壮。又置一燕姬，长途消遣，珍珠船真十倍于书画舫也。渡扬子江，榜人谓司厨者：“今日幸勿烹饪，恐熏香引猪婆龙等怪。”杨舟中乃肉林酒池，庖人固不为怪。杨正凭栏望金、焦，倏起巨浪，一鼋扬首欲吞杨。姬忽张皇，而杨固守舟中，乃顾姬曰：“一波起落，真怖畏人。”姬扶杨入帏，数日不起。问前日事，皆不记忆，家人以为惊迷。
    病小愈，姬侍侧，便能喋沓作京中人语。初杨娶姬，姬笑其滇语之咻咻也，欲其京语之滑滑，而杨之聱牙诘屈，喉不转而口卒瘏。一病之后，何以顿改前腔？讵福星至者机心灵耶？
    抵湖署，蒞任之初颇精明，阶下吏不敢视为初任官。惟贪婪甚于寻常，又好饮酒，渐至是非颠倒。独能迎合上官，卑躬折节，几于吮舐，为鄙夫笑。好聚属吏作十日醉。时大雪，杨有赏雪诗一首云：

    Explanation

    424

    Yang, being far from home, could not take leave to return. After receiving his credentials, he bought a boat to go to his new post. At the capital gate, there was a grand farewell feast. He also took a concubine from Yan to entertain him on the long journey; the pearl-laden ship was truly ten times more luxurious than a boat for paintings and calligraphy. Crossing the Yangtze River, the boatman told the cook, "Today, please do not cook, as the aroma might attract monsters like the 'pig婆 dragon.'" In Yang's boat, there was a forest of meat and a pool of wine; the cook naturally did not find it strange. Yang was leaning on the railing, looking at Jin and Jiao, when suddenly a huge wave arose, and a giant turtle raised its head, wanting to swallow Yang. The concubine suddenly became frightened, but Yang remained in the boat, and he looked at her and said, "One wave rising and falling, truly terrifying." The concubine helped Yang into the bedchamber, and he did not get up for several days. When asked about the events of the previous day, he could not remember them. The family thought he was in a state of shock and confusion.

    After a slight recovery, the concubine stayed by his side, and he could then chatter and speak like a person from the capital. At first, when Yang married the concubine, she laughed at his Dianese accent, wanting him to speak the smooth language of the capital. But Yang's speech was harsh and difficult to understand, with his throat not turning and his mouth becoming hoarse. After one illness, how did he suddenly change his previous manner of speaking? Is it that the arrival of a lucky star has made his mind sharp?

    Upon arriving at the prefectural office, in the early days of taking office, he was very shrewd, and the officials below him dared not treat him as a newly appointed official. But he was much more greedy than ordinary people, and he also liked drinking, gradually leading to a reversal of right and wrong. But he was able to flatter his superiors, bowing and lowering himself, almost to the point of licking their boots, and this was laughed at by the common people. He liked to gather his subordinates for a ten-day drinking spree. At the time of heavy snow, Yang composed a poem to enjoy the snow, which goes as follows:

    [Insert the poem here if available]

    425

    掩尽地皮不见土，白占田园千万亩。到处砖瓦变成银，面糊糊满湖州府。
    即此一诗，而其居官率属，大概可想。居常不御姬妾。姬固燕产，多淫荡，始以杨为病惫，继则疑以公冗，终竟杳然。徒使桃花春涨，不见渔篙；野渡无人，扁舟泛泛，岂能安稳也哉！恒私奔与仆隶眠。先犹惧杨闻，后即有风声，而杨若聋聩者然，于是姬乃大快。郡人曾有一联粘署门云：日昃尚衔杯，惟酒政太守醉也；夜长不闭户，此淫风夫人启之。咸相传以为笑谈。
    明年，其弟自滇来探兄，相见虽欢聚，而家中事皆茫然。杨曰：“兄一病后，如隔世人，今更善忘。”弟口是之，而心颇异焉。平昔常贮百瓮水于后园中，当沐浴，秘不使窥。忽一日杨浴，其弟潜窥，见一大鼋累然，喷吐瓮水。大惊，不敢泄。逾期告归，杨挽而厚赠之。

    Explanation

    425

    The land was stripped bare, leaving no soil visible, and vast tracts of land were occupied without payment. Everywhere, bricks and tiles turned into silver, and the entire prefecture of Huzhou was covered in a thick layer of mud. This poem alone suggests that Yang's governance and the behavior of his subordinates were likely corrupt. He usually did not keep concubines. The concubine was from Yan and was often licentious. Initially, she thought Yang was ill and weak, then she suspected he was too busy with official duties, and finally, she disappeared altogether. This only made the spring flood of peach blossoms unseen, the ferry empty, and the small boat drifting aimlessly—how could it be stable? She often secretly ran away to sleep with servants. At first, he feared that Yang would hear, then the rumors spread, but Yang acted as if deaf and dumb, and so the concubine was greatly relieved. A couplet was posted at the prefectural office door: "Even at noon, still holding the cup, only the wine-loving prefect is drunk; the night is long and the door is not closed, this licentious wind is started by the lady." Everyone passed it around as a joke.

    The following year, his younger brother came from Yunnan to visit him. Although they met happily, he was completely unaware of what had happened at home. Yang said, "After my illness, it feels like I am separated from the world, and now I have become even forgetful." His brother agreed, but he felt quite strange about it. He usually kept a hundred jars of water in the back garden, and when he bathed, he kept it secret so no one could see.
