A few days later, women in the inner chambers said, "Zhu is very good or very bad, depending on one's perspective." "Lalì" is a colloquial term for "bald" or "shaven," which might be a play on words.

    At the time, Hangzhou experienced a prolonged drought, with wells and springs drying up. Everyone agreed, "The great person should go to Tianzhu to invite the Bodhisattva into the city, then it will rain." Zhu replied, "The Bodhisattva is an unknown figure and an unknown deity. If she is a Bodhisattva, she should universally save all beings. Why should one consider a single invitation as an honor?" Zhu refused. No one else knew what to do. A Daoist named Xu, who could perform talismanic and exorcism techniques, had connections and moved freely in and out of the palace. He was then called a true person. Xu just arrived in Hangzhou, and people said, "The drought demon dare not cause disaster." When invited, Zhu respected him. Zhu said, "To ask for rain on behalf of the people, if it is beneficial to life and death, I will do it, even if it involves a minor ritual. But I fear it may not work." Reluctantly, he prepared banners and canopies and personally led the way. The Daoist was arrogant and haughty. After arriving at the altar, they set up elaborate offerings, and Zhu stood at the altar from morning till evening.

    537

    道士谓公曰：“为汝飞符于上帝，请雨三日，雨当足否？”公以手加额曰：“幸甚。”第见百姓云屯，观者堵墙。三辰雨不降，道士曰：“此地灾沴，由抚军获罪于神所致。为汝再请七日，当有雨泽。”公唯唯曰：“罪在轼一人，百姓何辜？”如期又不雨。公曰：“真人将奈何？”道士曰：“天悭未破，非人力所能回。”且请去。公勃然大怒，曰：“左道之流，妖惑实甚，须当立毙！”命左右曳下坛，杖四十。血流臀股，并置俎上，曝烈日中。人皆咋舌而言曰：“我公不请大士，虽不得雨，无后灾。打杀真人，祸乃不可言矣。”群掩面不敢仰视。

    Explanation

    537

    The Taoist priest said to the official, "I will fly a talisman to the Supreme Deity on your behalf and request rain for three days. Will the rain be sufficient?" The official placed his hand on his forehead and said, "That would be wonderful." The crowd gathered like clouds, and the spectators formed a wall. After three days, the rain did not fall. The Taoist priest said, "This land is suffering from calamities, caused by the military official's sin against the gods." The official replied hesitantly, "The sin lies solely with me. What have the common people done wrong?" As expected, the rain still did not come. The official said, "What can the Taoist sage do now?" The Taoist priest replied, "The heavens are stingy and have not yet broken open; it is beyond human power to change this." He then asked to leave. The official suddenly became very angry and said, "Practitioners of heretical ways, the deception and confusion are indeed great. They must be killed immediately!" He ordered his attendants to drag the Taoist priest off the altar and flog him forty times. Blood flowed from his buttocks, and he was placed on a chopping block, exposed to the intense sunlight. Everyone covered their faces and dared not look up. They whispered, "Our lord did not invite the Bodhisattva, so even if we did not get rain, there would be no further calamities. Killing the Taoist sage, the disaster is beyond words." The crowd covered their faces and dared not look up.

    538

    公乃焚香设席，虔祷其词曰：窃惟官以治明，神以理幽，官不职而殃民则罚随，神不灵而灾民则祀绝。兹届夏秋，十旬弗雨，土焦禾槁，神岂不见？四野老幼，盈庭哀号，神岂不闻？不见不闻，何贵尔神？汝竟忝然庙貌哉！今抚某与汝神约：一日之内，速赐霖雨，苏百物而救万姓，神之灵也，某之幸也，浙民之福也。不然，则块然土木，抚某将率众而绝汝神之血食。
    祝毕，忽而云涡四旋，雷电交作，甘霖大霈，平地数尺。士民皆长跪泥涂，欢声腾沸，与雷声互应，拥朱公下坛，仪卫前导以归。后羁一囚，躄而随者，则俎中真人也。乃知朱公精忱格天，甚于剪爪焚躯万万矣。

    Explanation

    538

    Governor Zhu then burned incense and set up a seat, and in a sincere prayer, he said: "I humbly reflect that officials govern the world, while deities manage the spiritual realm. If officials fail in their duties and harm the people, they will face punishment; if deities are ineffective and bring disasters to the people, their worship will be cut off. Now, during the summer and autumn seasons, it has been ten days without rain, the land is parched, and the crops are withered. Deity, can you not see this? The elderly and children in the fields, as well as those in the courtyard, are filled with sorrow and crying out in distress. Deity, can you not hear this? If you cannot see or hear, what value is there in your divinity? Are you disgracing your temple? I, as the governor, make a pact with you, the deity: within one day, quickly bestow a heavy rain. Revive all things and save the people. This is the deity's power, my good fortune, and the blessing of the Zhejiang people. If not, you will be nothing but a block of wood and stone, and I will lead the people to stop your worship."

    After the prayer, clouds suddenly began to swirl, and thunder and lightning struck together. Heavy rain poured down, flooding the ground by several feet. The officials and people all knelt in the mud, and cheers erupted, echoing with the thunder. They surrounded Governor Zhu and brought him down from the altar, with ceremonial guards leading the way. Later, a prisoner was bound and followed, limping. He was the real person from the sacrificial altar. It was then known that Governor Zhu's sincere heart reached the heavens, far more powerful than cutting off his nails and burning his body.

    539

    后公抚晋，晋方灾，公至一祈即雨，晋民歌之。

    袁硕夫
    袁猷壮，赣之七鲤镇人。字硕夫，改夫曰肤，又曰“石桴图”，号行川，又号榕楣———其村濒江，多大榕。吾春舫业师长子也，少我一岁，垂髫受业时，共笔砚一寒暑。硕夫庸于才，又懒且邋遢，不修边幅，师督之严。
    乾隆庚寅，吾师设教庾岭道南书院。每课，硕夫终日不完卷，又潦草任意。师曾握其发辫撞石碣上，头肿起若胡桃，憨受之。壬辰，师出宰粤东，多大邑，有能声。吾师磊落负奇，不务纤啬，好挥霍。硕夫以庠生，不获随任。自太师母及师母眷属俱往，硕夫独留，一妾伴处。尝曰：“不举火，甘藜藿。”岁至粤省视一次，布衣破袜，终无贵介气。将告归，必多索银及布。其余玩好及广之羽毛、茧绸、珠玉、沉檀，一切无所取携，大非吾师意。阖署人咸笑大郎君太傻角，穷措大气。

    Explanation

    539

    After the official governed Jin, the region was suffering from a disaster. Upon his arrival and a single prayer, it rained, and the people of Jin sang about it.

    Yuan Shoufu
    Yuan Youzhuang, a native of Qili Town in Gan Prefecture, was known by several names: Shoufu, Fu, and "Shifu Tu." He also had the pseudonyms Xingchuan and Rongmei. His village was near a river, with many large banyan trees. I am the eldest son of my master, Master Chunfang, and he is one year younger than me. We studied together from childhood to the next year. Shoufu was mediocre in talent, lazy, and untidy, not caring about his appearance, and was disciplined strictly by the teacher.

    In the Gengyin year of the Qianlong era, my teacher set up a school at Yuling Dao Nan Academy. During every class, Shoufu would not finish his work all day, and his work was sloppy and careless. The teacher once grabbed his hair and hit him against a stone tablet, causing his head to swell like a walnut, and he endured it foolishly. In the Renchen year, the teacher went to govern Guangdong, a large and prosperous area, and had a good reputation. My teacher was upright and had extraordinary qualities, not being stingy, and liked to spend freely. Shoufu, as a student, was not allowed to accompany him. From the teacher's mother to the teacher's wife and family, they all went, leaving Shoufu alone, accompanied by a concubine. He once said, "I will not light a fire, and I am content with wild vegetables." He visited Guangdong once a year, wearing a plain robe and torn socks, never showing any airs of nobility. When he was about to return home, he would always ask for a lot of silver and cloth. He did not take anything else, such as toys, feathers, silk, pearls, jade, sandalwood, and agarwood. Everyone in the office laughed at the eldest son for being too naive and poor.

    540

    及其归，以银计息，布称是，贷诸贫乏，日会而月计之。又籴贱粜贵，权子母，如是者十年。硕夫本素封，得此以益，家愈饶。惟其财之裕也，故其心之悭。余辛丑过赣，访硕夫于家，喜甚，留两日，作竟夜抵足之谈。餐用饲狸小鱼、马齿苋菜，若只鸡、豚蹄，固未之前闻也。壬寅，师疾卒于官。家口甚繁指，初不知所为计。太师母与师母及眷属扶榇归里百十人。是日，内外数十席，碗箸匙不计数，即晚百人需百床，皆取诸宫中，不缺一。既观其仓有余谷，箧有余布，园圃多蔬菜，池塘鱼鳖不可胜食。若吾师宦囊中携归之端溪砚田，不可耕而耨也；英德美石，不可煮而食也；书册画卷，不可寒而衣也，相与束之高阁。夫然后一家之人，皆食大郎之食，衣大郎之衣。迄于今又十年，恒取给焉，无所匮。

    Explanation

    540

    When he returned, he calculated interest in silver, measured clothes accordingly, and lent to the poor and needy. He met daily and counted monthly. He bought at low prices and sold at high prices, balancing the principal and interest. For ten years, Shuo Fu, who originally came from a wealthy family, gained from this to increase his wealth, and his family became even more prosperous. Only because of his abundant wealth, thus his heart was stingy. In the year Xin Chou, I passed through Ganzhou and visited Shuo Fu at his home. He was very happy, and I stayed for two days, engaging in a conversation that lasted until the next morning. The meals consisted of small fish for the cats and mallow vegetables, like a single chicken or pig trotter, which I had never heard of before. In the year Ren Yin, the teacher fell ill and died in office. The family was very large, and initially, they did not know what to do. The mother of the Grand Tutor and the tutor's wife, along with their relatives, brought the coffin back to their hometown with over a hundred people. On that day, there were dozens of tables inside and outside, with bowls, chopsticks, and spoons not counted, and at night, a hundred people needed a hundred beds, all taken from the palace, without any shortage. After observing that the granary had surplus grain, the chests had surplus cloth, the garden had many vegetables, and the ponds had so many fish and turtles that they could not be eaten up, it became clear that the inkstones from Duanxi, brought back from his official duties, could not be tilled or weeded; the beautiful stones from Yingde could not be cooked and eaten; and the books and paintings could not be worn for warmth. They all put them away in a high place. Thus, all the family members ate the food of the eldest son and wore his clothes. Until now, it has been another ten years, and they always took what they needed, without any shortage.

    541

    戊申，硕夫举于乡；其二弟堂，博学倜傥，例为州丞；三弟域，幼入邑庠。硕夫三子，献禧亦诸生，献祜业儒，黑狗稚，皆其善持筹之妾所出也。乙卯公车，与予同落第，留长安。昕夕往还，尝备述其家事。
    次年春二月，病于京邸。无亲故，余视其汤药二十五日，遂捐馆焉。呜呼，吾师犹父也，师之视余犹子也，硕夫弟也，今其死焉，能不恸伤！其棺厝诸南城义园，咸我殡，并书致其家来搬柩。迹其生平，了不异人，然矫情励俗，甘淡泊以成家，有足多者。余特书之，以代挽章。

    Explanation

    541

    In the year of the Earth Rooster, Shuo passed the provincial examination; his second brother Tang, who was learned and free-spirited, followed the example of becoming a state deputy; his third brother Yu, who entered the county school at a young age. Shuo's three sons, Xianxi and Xianhu, who were students, and Heigou, who was young, were all born to his skillful concubine. In the year of the Wood Rabbit, he took the imperial examination with me and we both failed, so we stayed in Chang'an. We visited each other daily, and he often described his family matters in detail.

    The following spring in the second month, he fell ill in the capital. Without any relatives or friends, I cared for his medicine for twenty-five days, and then he passed away. Alas, my teacher was like a father to me, and he treated me like a son. Shuo was like a younger brother, and now that he has died, how can I not be deeply grieved? His coffin was placed in the South City Public Cemetery, and I arranged for the funeral. I also wrote to his family to come and move the coffin. Looking at his life, he was no different from others, but he modified his behavior to encourage the customs, enjoyed a simple life, and managed to build a family, which is commendable. I specially wrote this to serve as a eulogy.

    542

    简翁
    粤之甘泉先生，讲学天关。有简翁者年百有二岁，就而问学，将执弟子之礼。先生不受，延翁忠义堂上，东西坐以宾之，倾谈。甘泉谓：“是翁容貌凝然，所养纯一，赤子之心已复。吾当北面事之。”遂转而受业于翁。
    甘泉时年八十有五，观者谓其有三达之尊，而谦让不遑，致礼于布衣之一老，诚为有道之风。时有黎养真者，年八十三；黄慎斋者，年八十一；吴藤川者，年八十。皆游甘泉门下，称为“三皓”。有歌云：“养真慎斋与藤川，三皓同时及吾门。”而袁教授邮，亦年七十，与慎斋同驻甘泉心性图书一堂之上，师弟子皓首庞眉，太古衣冠。好事者因与简翁合绘一图曰：“师弟六老人。”后甘泉至九十五，复开学龙潭书院。又有钟景星七十二，张春岗七十三，开讲时皆雍雍侍侧也。

    Explanation

    542

    Mr. Ganquan from Yue was teaching at Tian Guan. There was an old man named Jian who was one hundred and two years old. He went to ask for knowledge and was about to perform the formalities of being a disciple. Mr. Ganquan did not accept, but invited Jian to the Loyalty and Righteousness Hall, seating him east and west to treat him as a guest, and they engaged in deep conversation. Mr. Ganquan said, "This old man has a composed and dignified appearance, cultivating purity and unity, and has restored the innocence of a newborn baby. I should serve him facing north." Thus, he switched and learned from the old man.

    Mr. Ganquan was eighty-five years old at the time. Observers said he had three ways of reaching respect, but he was humble and yielding, offering respect to an old man in plain clothes. This truly reflected the style of having virtue. At the time, there was Li Yangzhen, who was eighty-three years old; Huang Shenzhai, who was eighty-one years old; and Wu Tengchuan, who was eighty years old. All of them studied under Mr. Ganquan and were called the "Three Whites." There was a song that said, "Yangzhen, Shenzhai, and Tengchuan, the Three Whites studied together under my master." Yuan Jiaoshi You, who was also seventy years old, stayed in the same hall as Huang Shenzhai, where teachers and disciples with white hair and thick eyebrows, dressed in ancient attire, gathered. People who liked to do good deeds combined this into one picture and called it "The Six Elder Teachers and Disciples." Later, Mr. Ganquan reached ninety-five years old and reopened Longtan Academy. There were also Zhong Jingxing, who was seventy-two years old, and Zhang Chunge, who was seventy-three years old. When they started lecturing, all were respectfully present on the side.

    543

    柳孝廉
    青州府诸生柳鸿图，夫妻完娶。值岁歉不能谋生，携妻就食于外。继且结衣行乞，而乞者又多如蚁。一日，夫妻饥甚，相抱而哭。妇曰：“盍鬻我，汝得生。留我则并死无益也。”柳感动，莫知所言，但摇手而已。俄见有小车载男女数人，盖贩人者。妇曰：“推车大哥，我夫妇饥惫，愿鬻身以就食。”车者见妇美，乃曰：“问尔男子几何值？”柳泣不能答。妇曰：“得十缗则随汝往。”贩者曰：“不值，五缗则可。”路傍人见而怂之，得八缗。车者随脱贯出。妇负镪置柳前，曰：“我生时幼少，父母爱我，呼我‘一捻金’，孰知竟成今日之谶。柳郎，柳郎，有此则生，无此则亡。但无虚生，为前人光。鬻妻活命，过时莫忘。”柳号曰：“以妻之貌，何所不可，我今与妻遂永诀于斯耶？抑尚有重逢之日耶？”车者促之，两人相持不舍。车者拥妇上车，推柳仆地，辗軨而奔。柳望影失声，孑然挟资，呜呜以北。

    Explanation

    543

    Liu the Xiao Lian

    Liu Hongtu, a student from Qingzhou Prefecture, was married with a complete family. During a bad harvest year, unable to make a living, he took his wife to seek food elsewhere. Soon, they wore rags and began begging, and beggars were as numerous as ants. One day, the couple was extremely hungry and embraced each other, crying. The wife said, "Why not sell me, so you can live? If you keep me, we will both die in vain." Liu was deeply moved but could not speak, only shaking his head. Suddenly, he saw a small cart carrying several men and women, likely people traffickers. The wife said, "Driver, my husband and I are starving. I am willing to sell myself for food." The driver saw that the woman was beautiful and asked, "How much is your husband worth?" Liu cried and could not answer. The wife said, "If you give me ten strings of cash, I will go with you." The trafficker said, "Not worth it, but five strings of cash would do." A passerby saw this and encouraged him, and they agreed on eight strings of cash. The driver then took out the money. The wife carried the money and placed it in front of Liu, saying, "I was born young, my parents loved me, and called me 'A Nian Jin.' Who knew it would become a prophecy for today. Liu, Liu, with this money, you can live, without it, you will die. But do not live in vain, bring glory to our ancestors. Selling my wife to save your life, don't forget this after the time." Liu cried, "With my wife's beauty, what can't she do? Am I now saying goodbye to her forever? Or will there still be a day when we meet again?" The driver urged them, and the two held onto each other, unwilling to part. The driver pushed the woman onto the cart, pushed Liu to the ground, and drove away, running over him. Liu lost his voice upon seeing the shadow, alone with the money, crying and heading north.

    544

    妇车行数日，问价者颇多，贩者又奇其货，遂不得售。一日，抵新城一村。村有王凤山，武生也，家殷实，而性慷慨，事母最孝，乡里畏敬之。年虽灾，而是村赖王得安。于村口开一旅店。值贩者来投宿，王见妇举止非贱流，且凄惋欲动人怜。王知其为贩，而恐其流于娼也。王问贩者曰：“若女有姿。”贩者曰：“相公如爱好，何不留之？但得如本偿，不敢望倍利。”王归告母，母不许。王曰：“儿非爱其貌，实怜其人。母盍女之以为保？”母点首。王至店见之，告以为妹，故妇感谢。王以二十缗得之。王母遂视如女焉。后欲为女婚，女不从，愿以老女终事母。王母亦乐得膝前煦妪云。

    Explanation

    544

    A woman traveled by carriage for several days, and many people asked about the price of her goods, which were considered valuable by the traders, so she could not sell them. One day, she arrived at a new village. In this village, there lived Wang Fengshan, a martial scholar with a wealthy family and a generous nature. He was the most filial to his mother and was respected by the villagers. Despite the famine, the village relied on Wang for safety. At the village entrance, Wang opened an inn. One evening, a trader came to stay, and Wang saw the woman's dignified demeanor and her sorrowful expression, which moved him to pity. Wang knew she was a trader but was concerned that she might end up in a brothel. He asked the trader, "Your daughter is beautiful." The trader replied, "If you like her, why not keep her? I will accept the original price, but I dare not hope for double profit." Wang returned home to tell his mother, but she did not agree. Wang said, "I do not love her for her appearance; I truly pity her. Why not take her as a daughter-in-law?" His mother nodded in agreement. Wang went to the inn and saw the woman, telling her she was his sister, so she thanked him. Wang bought her for twenty strings of cash. Wang's mother then treated her like a daughter. Later, Wang's mother wanted to marry her off, but the woman refused, wishing to remain unmarried to serve her mother. Wang's mother was also happy to have her as a daughter-in-law.

    545

    当柳生之北也，欲往关东，值关禁，不许出，复还东。是夏麦大收，遗穗于道，乃为人佣。逾年还乡，迤逦东归。至新城，亦宿于是店。柳固穷，一身外了无长物。夜雨达旦，积水满院，不能行，柳拥彗为之粪除。值王生至，见阶前如洗，喜曰：“那个人扫得院中无一点泥？”柳曰：“雨后早起无事，故洒扫耳。”王生曰：“汝何处人？”柳曰：“我姓柳，青州人，自早岁离家，今欲作归计。”王曰：“想富贵还乡矣。”柳曰：“如此蓝缕，何相谑耶？但谋得一枝栖，亦随处可安身也。”王曰：“汝归计既未决，盍为我店中料理冗事？”柳曰：“固所愿也。”王喜，即令其居柜前屋，日则洁尔舍宇，暮则安彼行旅。又识字能算，王倚赖之，乃不以佣视柳，而柳竟以兄视王，称莫逆焉。

    Explanation

    545

    When Liu traveled north, he wished to go east but encountered border restrictions that did not allow him to leave, so he returned east. That summer, the wheat harvest was abundant, and leftover ears of grain were scattered on the road. He then became a hired laborer. After a year, he returned to his hometown, gradually making his way back east. Upon reaching Xincheng, he also stayed at this inn. Liu was poor but resolute, possessing nothing of value beyond himself. The night rain continued until dawn, filling the courtyard with water, making it impossible to move around. Liu held a broom to clean and remove the dirt. When Wang arrived, he saw the steps as if they had been washed, and said happily, "That person has swept the courtyard so thoroughly that there is not a speck of mud!" Liu replied, "After the rain, I got up early with nothing to do, so I cleaned up." Wang asked, "Where are you from?" Liu said, "I am surnamed Liu, from Qingzhou. Since my early years, I have been away from home, and now I want to make plans to return." Wang said, "You must be returning home in wealth and prosperity!" Liu responded, "In such a tattered state, why are you teasing me? As long as I can find a place to rest, I can settle down anywhere." Wang said, "Since you haven't decided on your return plans, why not help me with the store's busy work?" Liu said, "It is what I have always wanted." Wang was pleased and immediately assigned him to live in the room in front of the counter, where he cleaned the house during the day and accommodated travelers at night. Liu also knew how to read and calculate, and Wang relied on him, no longer seeing him as a hired laborer, but Liu treated Wang as a brother, calling themselves bosom friends.

    546

    如是二年有余。无事时柳犹咿唔章句于梦魂鸡火间也。岁次戊申乡比，柳诡言于王曰：“弟欲还乡一省家门，往返约可月余耳。”王即为之治装，衣履悉更，复厚赠之。柳别王就道，则易东辙而南辕。至省录遗，场事终返，王以其自青州来也。时将重九，东省揭晓多在三、四两日。柳屈指，心怦怦动。是年新城落科，故无耗。越日，闻传榜首出寿光，柳不怿。出村口蹀躞于大槐荫间，遥见两人喘而来，坐树根。柳视之，似传报者。柳心痒，问曰：“二位何往？”甲曰：“自青州来。”乙曰：“休题起，时晦至此，言之恐人讪。费尽手眼，谋得一新举人报。

    Explanation

    546

    For two years or more, when there were no events, Liu would recite verses in his dreams and between dawn and dusk. In the year Wu Shen, the local examination took place, and Liu deceitfully told Wang, "I want to return home to visit my family. The round trip will take about a month." Wang immediately prepared his luggage, changed his clothes and shoes, and gave him generous gifts. Liu bade farewell to Wang and set off on the road, changing from traveling east to traveling south. After reaching the provincial examination and returning, Wang thought he had come from Qingzhou. It was close to the Double Ninth Festival, and the results of the eastern provincial examination were usually announced on the third or fourth day. Liu counted the days, and his heart beat rapidly. That year, Xincheng failed the examination, so there was no news. The next day, he heard that the top scorer came from Shouguang, and Liu was unhappy. He walked back and forth at the village entrance under the shade of a large locust tree, seeing two people panting as they approached and sitting at the base of the tree. Liu looked at them and thought they were messengers. His heart itched, and he asked, "Where are you going?" Person A said, "We came from Qingzhou." Person B said, "Let's not mention it. The time is now dark and obscure, and talking about it might make people laugh at us. We used all our efforts to get a report of a new juren."

    547

    星驰往青，四觅并无其人，佥曰‘荒歉携家不知所往’，岂鬼也耶？”柳忖逾时，曰：“日之夕矣，盍入此室？我逆旅主人也。”二人从之，入村店宿。晚时灯上，柳携壶酌来，曰：“二友遄行惫，盍饮我一瓯秫{米窄}，以消烦闷。”两人起谢，遂同饮闲谈。柳复煨一壶来，皆酣。柳曰：“适所访青州举人，其姓伊何？”甲曰：“柳姓。”柳曰：“汝报人将何为据？”甲曰：“有草榜剪出蓝条者。”柳曰：“乞借一观。”甲若吝，乙曰：“至好，相示何碍？”甲解缠开摺以示，柳拭目曰：“第四十名柳鸿图，青州府廪膳生。”柳观罢，凄然泪落如雨。甲曰：“兄何悲切为，岂族兄弟耶？”柳曰：“非也。”曰：“岂堂子侄耶？”柳曰：“亦非也。盖族兄弟之弟兄，堂子侄之叔父耳。”两人起曰：“然则新举人乎？”柳曰：“惭愧！”众人皆哗。

    Explanation

    547

    The narrator rushed to Qingzhou, searching all around but found no one. The villagers all said, "Due to famine, they have taken their families and gone somewhere unknown." Could they be ghosts? After some time, the narrator thought and said, "It is getting late, why don't we enter this room? I am the owner of this inn." The two men agreed and entered the village inn to stay. In the evening, the narrator brought a pot of millet wine and said, "My two friends, you must be tired from a long journey. Why don't you drink a cup of millet wine with me to relieve your anxiety?" The two men stood up and thanked him, and they drank together, chatting leisurely. The narrator then warmed another pot of wine, and they all became deeply intoxicated. The narrator asked, "The person I just visited, what is his surname?" One of the men replied, "Liu." The narrator asked, "What evidence do you have for reporting him?" The man replied, "There is a blue strip cut from a draft list." The narrator asked, "May I see it?" The man seemed reluctant, but the other man said, "It's fine, show it to us." The man unwrapped it and showed it to the narrator, who wiped his eyes and said, "Fortieth name, Liu Hongtu, a廪膳生 from Qingzhou Prefecture." After seeing this, the narrator wept bitterly, his tears falling like rain. One of the men asked, "Why are you so sad, are you his clan brother?" The narrator replied, "No." The man asked, "Are you his cousin?" The narrator replied, "No, I am the brother of his clan brothers and the uncle of his cousins." The two men stood up and asked, "So he is the new scholar-official?" The narrator said, "I am ashamed!" Everyone was in a commotion.

    548

    王生至，问柳，柳乃细述赴省伪作归计事。王大喜，安置两捷人，奔告母，母亦喜。乃为罗酒浆，村之中皆贺客也。一日，母与女在厨下置馔馔柳，捧盆者入厨曰：“柳伙在此二年，竟不闻名，今贵矣，皆知其为柳鸿图。”女闻之失箸。母忖曰：“此女誓不嫁，今闻柳名而若惊，岂以显者动心耶？”晚王生归，母问曰：“柳伙有妻否？”王生曰：“家尚无，焉得有室？”女曰：“是青州人否？”王曰：“然。”至夜，母谓女曰：“自儿随侍我二年有余，颇称孝顺，即亲生女无以过此。但筵席百年，终有散期。趁我暮年尚在，眼看汝寻一佳婿，我亦瞑目。

    548

    Wang arrived and asked Liu, who then detailed his plan to pretend to return home. Wang was greatly pleased, arranged for the two successful candidates, and hurried to inform his mother, who was also pleased. They then prepared wine and drink, and everyone in the village came to congratulate. One day, the mother and daughter were preparing food for Liu in the kitchen. A person carrying a bowl entered the kitchen and said, "Liu has been here for two years, but no one knew his name, now he is famous, and everyone knows he is Liu Hongtu." The daughter dropped her chopsticks upon hearing this. The mother thought to herself, "This girl has sworn never to marry, but now she is surprised by Liu's name, could it be that a famous person has moved her heart?" In the evening, Wang returned, and the mother asked, "Does Liu have a wife?" Wang replied, "He still doesn't have a family, how could he have a wife?" The daughter asked, "Is he from Qingzhou?" Wang said, "Yes." By night, the mother told the daughter, "Since you have been with me for more than two years, you have been quite filial, even a biological daughter couldn't surpass this. But feasts last a hundred years, but there will eventually be a time of separation. While I am still alive, I want to see you find a good husband, and I can then rest in peace."

    549

    无执前见。若个人家女儿在闺中老者？”女固深沉，已审其为柳，又不欲直言之，但曰：“惟母命是从耳。”母告王，王告柳，且重以母命。柳曰：“生离甚于死别。凶荒捐弃，临别数言依依在耳。我今得续佳偶，恐人在天涯，不胜白头之叹。则男儿薄倖，莫我为甚！”王曰：“鸾胶再续，为无后计。兄必欲胶柱鼓瑟，作抱桥之守，倘果琴碎人亡，则终身留无涯之憾，又孰重而孰轻耶？”柳曰：“恩兄之言，加以老母之命，敢不谨从。犹有言者：万一珠还璧合，尚望不栉公稍屈一坐耳。”王反命。母颔之而视女，女曰：“俟到其间，再作商量未晚也。”

    Explanation

    549

    Do not cling to preconceived notions. "If a daughter of a family is old enough to be in the inner chambers?" The daughter, being deep and thoughtful, had already discerned that it was Liu, but she did not wish to speak directly, so she said, "I will only follow my mother's command." The mother told Wang, who then told Liu, and emphasized the mother's command. Liu said, "Living apart is worse than death. In times of famine and hardship, we were separated, and the words we exchanged at parting still echo in my ears. Now that I have found another good match, I fear that if he is far away, I will not be able to bear the sorrow of old age. Then, a man who is unfaithful, could be no worse than me!" Wang replied, "If you can reunite like the phoenix's tears forming a glue, it would be without considering the consequences. If you insist on being inflexible like gluing the column of a zither, and remain steadfast like someone who guards a bridge, then if indeed the zither breaks and the person dies, you will have a lifelong regret, and who is more important and who is less important?" Liu said, "Your words, my esteemed brother, combined with my mother's command, I dare not disobey. I still have something to say: if there is a chance of reunion, I still hope that the respected elder will slightly bend his seat." Wang returned the message. The mother nodded and looked at the daughter, who said, "It is not too late to discuss further when we are in that situation."

    550

    王即店中设青庐焉。至日彩舆鼓吹，女著锦帔。至撤帐换盏，诸嫂姨俱来。柳簪花冠带，为亲揭红盖。妇见柳喜动颜色，不觉嗤然有声，既而止。诸嫂见之，以姑不识羞归告其母。柳固未近觑，亦私以为：何其貌之似我妻也？及晚，客散入室，柳执烛前，妇掩面悲恸。柳执其手，惊曰：“卿真我前妻一捻金耶？”妇曰：“郎固无恙乎？”柳大恸。继复挑灯话旧，细数离悰，悲喜交集，真若再世。及晨，侍妪扑被，第见鸳枕波纹，渍渍盈尺，将不知其湿从何处来也。柳乃衣冠见王，长跪谢曰：“吾兄恩义，令我刻骨镂心。此并非当时杨裴诸公所可比拟。”王惊问，柳夫妻始告以破镜重圆之故。王母知之，亦怡然曰：“吾故料女之不苟笑也。”

    Explanation

    550

    King immediately set up a green tent in the shop. On the day, colorful sedan chairs and music were used, and the bride wore a brocade veil. After the tent was removed and replaced with a table, all the sisters-in-law and aunts came. Willow branches were inserted into flower crowns and ribbons, and one of the sisters-in-law personally lifted the red veil. The woman saw Liu and her face lit up with joy, and she couldn't help but laugh, but then stopped. The sisters-in-law saw this and told her mother that the mother-in-law did not know shame. Liu had not yet gotten close to look, but he privately thought: how much her appearance resembles my wife! By evening, the guests had dispersed, and Liu entered the room with a candle in hand. The woman covered her face and wept bitterly. Liu took her hand and said in surprise, "Are you truly my former wife, One Nian Jin?" The woman replied, "Are you still well, my lord?" Liu was deeply moved. They then lit the lamp and talked about old times, recounting their partings and reunions, and their emotions were a mix of sorrow and joy, as if they were in another world. By morning, the servant woman shook the quilt, and they only saw the wave-like pattern on the mandarin ducks' pillow, with wetness spreading for a full foot, and one would not know where the wetness came from. Liu then dressed in his official attire and knelt before the king, saying, "My brother's kindness and righteousness have deeply touched me. This is not something that can be compared to the kindness of Yang and Pei at that time." The king was surprised and asked, and Liu and his wife then told him the story of the broken mirror being reunited. The king's mother heard this and said with a smile, "I knew that your daughter would not laugh lightly."

    551

    后柳居新城，王为之揽生徒，设教于乡。忆自五十、五十一两年，东省各府旱荒，苗枯棉槁，杼轴为空，民皆束手待毙。国家蠲免之令、赈济之事、备御之策，靡不周详，较之前古，实所未有。而野中饿莩为狗鸢食者，仍相望不绝。呜呼，救荒无善策，诚哉是言也！又复鬻妻卖女，比比皆是，官府知之而不禁。盖鬻之则妻女去，而父母与其夫获生，否则终为沟壑鬼耳。是时草根芰蔓，每斤十钱。市中有货食者，辄抢而奔，比追及，已入口矣。又有数十为群，沿村夺食，夜则放火。故日未晡即锢户，通宵不得安静。如柳生之幸，诚千万中之一耳。

    Explanation

    551

    After Liu moved to the new city, Wang gathered students and taught in the village. Recalling the years 50 and 51, the eastern provinces experienced severe drought and famine, with crops failing and textile production grinding to a halt. The people were helpless and facing death. The government issued decrees for tax exemptions, provided relief, and implemented preventive measures, all of which were comprehensive and unprecedented in ancient times. Despite these efforts, the fields were still filled with the dead, who were devoured by dogs and crows. Alas, there is no good strategy for famine relief, and this is indeed true. People resorted to selling their wives and daughters, and such cases were numerous. The government knew about this but did not prohibit it. The reason was that selling them would allow the parents and husbands to survive, otherwise, they would die as ghosts in the ditches. At that time, even weeds and water plants were sold at ten coins per pound. In the markets, those who had food would rush to buy it, and by the time someone could catch up, the food had already been consumed. There were also groups of dozens who would gather and steal food from villages, and at night, they would set fires. Therefore, villagers would lock their doors before sunset and could not rest peacefully all night. Liu's survival was indeed a rare and fortunate occurrence.

    552

    (读之凄怆动人。
    世有恩谊如王生母子，当铸金事之。傅声谷)

    小黄粱
    晋人蒋仲翔，年二十登进士。入翰苑，转黄门给谏，以廉直著。不避权贵，辄加弹劾，满朝侧目。出使豫章廉访，使远之也。
    蒋行至严州新安江上，水清舟逆，潭不掩鳞。李白诗“青溪清我心，水色异诸水。借问新安江，见底何如此。人行明镜中，鸟度屏风里。向晚猩猩啼，空悲远游子”，即此地也。乃命奚奴出佳茗，以石铫汲江水烹之。坐观兰阴，富春山色，掩映篷窗。一时神与俱往，遂隐几而卧。

    Explanation

    552

    Movingly sorrowful and touching, it is indeed heartrending to read. In this world, there are bonds of affection and loyalty like those between Wang Sheng and his mother, and such virtues should be honored with great respect.

    The Yellow Millet Dream

    Jiang Zhongxiang, a man from the Jin dynasty, passed the imperial examination at the age of twenty. He entered the Hanlin Academy and was later promoted to the position of Yellow Gate Advisor, known for his integrity and uprightness. He did not shy away from powerful and influential people, always adding accusations against corrupt officials, earning the disapproval and fear of the entire court. He was sent on a mission to Yuzhang for inspection, which may have been a way to exile him.

    When Jiang reached the New An River in Yanzhou, the water was clear, and the boat moved upstream. The depths of the river did not hide the fish's scales. Li Bai's poem, "The green stream clears my heart, the water color is different from other waters. Asking about the New An River, how clear is its bottom? People walk in a mirror, birds fly across a screen. In the evening, the macaques cry, evoking sadness for distant travelers," perfectly describes this place. He then ordered his servant to bring out fine tea, boiled with water drawn from the river in a stone pot. Sitting under the shade of the lilies, he watched the scenery of Fuchun Mountain reflected in the window. For a moment, his spirit seemed to transcend the physical world, and he leaned on the table and fell asleep.

    553

    忽觉一身已在苍苍翠翠中，烟波江上，日暮低徊。正值问津无自，而一叶扁舟欸乃随水云荡出。蒋急呼之，舟抵岸，蒋登舟，则十六七一女子，姣好无比，载之而去。至一深潭峭壁下，女维缆入舱，问蒋何之。蒋悦女美，以无所归栖告。女治馔烹鲈鲙，相与劝飧。无何，月上斗牛间，照彻波光，皎皎如镜。
    蒋问女子名，女曰：“奴名翡翠，生长新安江上，打鱼为业。今得郎来，相与垂纶把钓，当不让鹿门双隐也。”蒋喜。更深，女下篷，相与就寝。布衾竹枕，共效结褵，真如鱼游暖水，欢若平生。寝晓女起，蒋卧睨之，见其凌波作镜，理发如云，撩水盥靥，天然百媚。继复晨炊，黄鱼白饭，香可鼓腹。镇日无事萦怀，或于水际一竿，静消清昼，时则得鱼，鼓鬣扬鳞，满筐金碧。女以河水虀盐煮之，味无上品。余者蒋携入市，换盐米而返，从无匮日。

    Explanation

    553

    Sudden, I found myself amidst a lush, verdant landscape, on a misty river at dusk, the sun lingering low in the sky. At that moment, I was lost and unsure of my way, but a small boat, its oars making a soft aene sound, emerged from the water and clouds. I called out to it, and the boat reached the shore. I boarded, and there was a girl, about sixteen or seventeen years old, of unparalleled beauty, who took me aboard and we set off.

    We arrived at a deep pool beneath steep cliffs. The girl tied the boat and entered the cabin, asking me where I was going. I admired her beauty and told her I had no place to stay. She prepared a meal, cooking fish, and we shared it together. Soon, the moon rose between the constellations of Dou and Niu, its light illuminating the water, clear and bright like a mirror.

    I asked the girl her name, and she replied, "My name is Feicui. I was born and raised on the Xin'an River, where I make a living fishing. Now that you are here, we can fish together and live a life not unlike that of the hermits of Lu Men." I was delighted. As night deepened, the girl lowered the sail, and we went to sleep together. We shared a simple bed with a bamboo pillow, our companionship as comfortable as fish swimming in warm water, and the joy of our time together felt like we had known each other for a lifetime.

    At dawn, I woke up and saw her hair flowing like water, her face washed with water, her natural beauty captivating. She prepared breakfast, a simple meal of yellow fish and white rice, the aroma filling the air and satisfying my hunger. Throughout the day, we had no worries, sometimes fishing by the water's edge, quietly enjoying the peaceful hours. Occasionally, we would catch fish, their scales shimmering as they filled our basket. The girl cooked them with river water and salt, the flavor unmatched. Any surplus, I would take to market, exchanging it for salt and rice, and we never lacked for anything.

    554

    逾年，翡翠临蓐，生一女，呱呱在怀，又生一子，女曰秀娥，子曰云上。虽浮家泛宅，而往来只在岩濑间。蒋固渔，止知渔，并不知渔之为蒋也。会春暮，翡翠携子女入山劚笋。蒋独坐船头，掀须自得，乃歌曰：“富春山中苦笋生，子陵滩下鲈鱼多。风掀笠，雨披蓑，月明归去笑呵呵。”翡翠归，野笋盈筐，佐以鱼酒，酕醄放适，正复不知人世事。又有庐西老渔翁，亦有一子一女，遂各为婚姻焉。自此两篷齐挂，双浆同摇，芦塘月港，于以不孤。又一年，而含饴弄孙矣。

    Explanation

    554

    The following year, the jade green bird gave birth, first to a daughter who cried in her arms, and then to a son. The daughter was named Xiu'e, and the son was named Yunshang. Although they led a nomadic life, their travels were confined to the rocky streams. Jiang, the fisherman, was unaware that he was human, knowing only his life as a fisherman. One evening in late spring, the bird took her children into the mountains to gather bamboo shoots. Jiang sat alone at the bow of his boat, lifting his beard in self-satisfaction, and sang: "Bitter bamboo shoots grow in the mountains of Fuchun, and grouper fish abound in the waters of Ziling. The wind lifts my hat, the rain soaks my cape, and under the bright moon, I return home laughing heartily." When the bird returned, her basket was full of wild bamboo shoots, which she served with fish and wine. They drank and feasted, completely unaware of the world's affairs. There was also an old fisherman from the west of the village, who had a son and a daughter. The two families eventually married their children to each other. From then on, two sails hung together, two oars rowed in unison, and in the reed pond and moonlit harbor, they were no longer alone. Another year passed, and they enjoyed the bliss of holding candy and playing with their grandchildren.

    555

    忽闻水沸之声，豁眸惊寤，正奚奴火扇初红，蟹眼翻花际也。蒋怅怅若失，而两腋风已胜于七碗后矣。尝语人曰：“四十年如炊黍，固知贵不如贱，富不如贫。一切向平婚嫁，利欲萦怀，尽是危机祸水。何若逍遥苕、霅，武陵源可指迷津。古人濠濮间，想其会心不在远也。”蒋从此顿忘世情，绝意功名。一年风宪，与人无忤，告归，作林下翁。曾以是梦记曰“小黄粱”。
    (此条，在任城和希斋巡漕行馆作记室时稿。)

    吕公子
    武进吕公子，父为宫保，家财盈溪壑。父死，公子享其丰，不能安。谓人曰：“人之所少，我何为而多？彼之所无，我何为而有？是以高明之家，鬼瞰其室。我时凛厚亡之惧，而惕焚身之戒。”于是轻财好施，求无不与。时人呼之为小春申。而挥霍任意处，虽曰豪举，皆出奇想。盖以速贫为愈也。当时食客颇多，方丈宴饮，动费万钱。

    Explanation

    555

    A sudden sound of boiling water startled him awake, his eyes wide open in shock. The fire fan was just beginning to glow red, and the water was starting to bubble like a crab's eyes. Jiang felt a sense of loss and emptiness, but the cool breeze in his armpits was more refreshing than the feeling after drinking seven cups of tea. He once told others, "Forty years have passed like the time it takes to cook millet. I now understand that being poor is better than being wealthy, and being lowly is better than being prominent. All the social obligations and desires for profit and pleasure are nothing but sources of danger and disaster. How much better it would be to live a carefree life in Taoshan and Zhazhi, where the source of the Wuling River can guide one to the right path. The ancient people who lived near the Hao and Pu rivers understood that true understanding does not depend on physical proximity. From then on, Jiang suddenly forgot about worldly affairs and gave up on his ambitions for fame and fortune. After serving as an official for a year without offending anyone, he resigned and became a hermit in the woods. He once recorded this dream, calling it "The Small Yellow Millet Dream."

    (These words were written in the draft of a letter while serving as a secretary in the inspection office in Rengcheng, accompanying He Xizhai on his tour.)

    Lü Gongzi
    The son of Lü Gongzi from Wujin, whose father was a high-ranking official, inherited a vast fortune. After his father's death, Lü Gongzi enjoyed his wealth but could not find peace. He said to others, "What others lack, why do I have so much? What others do not have, why do I possess? Therefore, a prominent and wealthy family is watched by ghosts. I am always afraid of losing my wealth and am constantly on guard against the dangers it brings." Thus, he became generous and willing to give away his wealth, giving freely to those who asked. People called him "Little Chunshen." Though he spent lavishly and acted carelessly, these were all acts of generosity and imagination. He believed that embracing poverty quickly was better. At that time, he had many guests and followers, and his grand banquets cost a great deal of money.

    556

    有客善吹，席间忽坠其羊脂玉笛，客窘。吕曰：“久不闻此碎玉声。”遂相与纵谈如故。一日，园中海棠将开，吕颦眉独倚亚栏。诸客曰：“公子何为？”吕曰：“春愁没可奈何。”时诸客皆述所好以进，吕悉见惯不鲜。一客忽附耳笑而云云。吕鼓掌曰：“可以作一日消遣矣。”即致书各大官，便降西园，一赏海棠之盛。当道诸公，非出其门下，即是吕门客，故莫不承召而至。吕固屋宇宏深，迓客入三进，诸从者皆不得入。惟主宾数人，东西升降而已。至园中，但见海棠十树，红如车盖；树下群女百辈，皆短衣团绣，执刀雁行立。客曰：“何用女戎？”吕曰：“非此不可以为欢。

    Explanation

    556

    A guest skilled at playing a musical instrument accidentally dropped his jade flute during the banquet, causing him to feel embarrassed. Lu remarked, "I haven't heard this sound of broken jade for a long time." They then continued their conversation as before. One day, in the garden, the crabapple flowers were about to bloom, and Lu frowned, standing alone leaning against a railing. The guests asked, "What is troubling you,公子?" Lu replied, "Spring sorrow, there's nothing to be done." At that time, all the guests described their likes and presented them to Lu, but he found them all too familiar and uninteresting. One guest suddenly whispered something to Lu and laughed. Lu clapped his hands and said, "This can be a way to pass the day." He immediately wrote letters to all the officials, inviting them to descend to the Western Garden to enjoy the beauty of the crabapple flowers. The officials in power were either his disciples or his guests, so none failed to come when summoned. Lu's house was vast and deep, and he welcomed the guests into the third courtyard, while the attendants were not allowed to enter. Only the host and a few guests moved back and forth. When they reached the garden, they saw ten crabapple trees, red as chariot canopies. Under the trees, a hundred women stood in formation, dressed in short clothes with embroidered patterns, holding knives in a line like geese. The guest asked, "Why use female warriors?" Lu replied, "Without them, there would be no enjoyment."

    557

    ”登堂设皋比，吕踞坐，曰：“命免冠。”客愕然。众刀簇拥，环向如猬，诸人皆免冠。吕曰：“更巾帼衣。”诸人遂皆更巾帼衣。吕曰：“歌以侑觞。”诸人以为未尝肄业及之也。吕怒曰：“杀无赦！”众女以刀扼其吭。诸人惧，有为之歌《鹿鸣》者。后一人作靡靡之音，如效侏儒舞，则司马白公也。吕大笑而起，乃亲为加冠于首，曰：“贤者而后乐此。老夫亦聊借为娱耳，幸勿罪！”诸人不敢言。遂大设醴酺。诸人出，群切齿共谋吕氏，若甚于季文子台上之羞，将不移晷也。及归，其事皆寝。询以故，盖吕当宴客时，已载厚币往，候其出而赂之。及宴归，而金已在笥矣。

    Explanation

    557

    Upon ascending the hall and setting up the tiger skin seat, Lu sat in a relaxed manner and said, "Remove your hats." The guests were startled. Surrounded by many swords, they formed a hedgehog-like circle, and all removed their hats. Lu then said, "Change into women's attire." All the guests changed into women's clothing. Lu said, "Sing to accompany the drinking." The guests thought they had not practiced enough for such a task. Lu became angry and said, "No mercy for those who fail!" The women used knives to threaten the guests. The guests were terrified, and one began to sing the "Lu Ming" (a classical poem). Later, one person sang a soft, gentle tune, mimicking the dance of a dwarf, identified as Si Ma Bai Gong. Lu laughed and stood up, personally placing a hat on the person's head and said, "Only the wise can enjoy this. I am just using it for entertainment; please do not blame me!" The guests dared not speak. A grand feast with sweet wine was then set up. After the feast, the guests left and plotted against the Lu family, feeling more humiliated than Ji Wenzi on his platform, and they would do so in a very short time. Upon returning, the matters were abandoned. When asked why, it was revealed that Lu had already carried rich gifts to the place, waited for them to leave, and bribed them. After the banquet, the gold was already in the box.

    558

    吕尝游瞰江山，令多人撒放金箔于峰头。吕坐松风台，置酒临江，玩其迷漫炫烂之景，号为金雪。自辰及申，犹霏霏不止。嘻，如吕氏之所为，岂吕氏之所能自为？盖诚有大力者驱而为之，以深明夫聚敛附益之为。作牛马于儿孙者，徒为多事！是吕氏之散金游戏，其智不在中人下。说者多愚之。孰愚孰智，必有能辨之者。

    邵嗣尧
    国朝邵嗣尧，山西人，庚戌进士。励志好修，尔室不愧，真君子儒也。初为北直清苑令，刚正不阿。妻子来任所，公不许入城，赋诗以却之曰：
    看罢家书意惘然，纷纷相劝置庄田。狼山不卷千年画，鸡水新栽五亩莲。
    击鼓登堂真说法，燃灯独坐类参禅。囊空犹是当年我，未许妻儿索俸钱。

    Explanation

    558

    Lord Lü once roamed and gazed at the rivers and mountains, ordering many people to scatter gold foil on the mountain peaks. Sitting on the Pine Wind Pavilion, he placed wine by the river and enjoyed the bewilderingly splendid scene, calling it "Golden Snow." From the hour of Chen to the hour of Shen, the gold foil continued to fall gently. Ah, as for what Lord Lü did, could it be something he could do by himself? It is indeed a powerful force that drives it, deeply revealing the act of accumulating and increasing. Those who make their descendants work like oxen and horses just cause trouble! This game of scattering gold by Lord Lü is no less intelligent than that of an average person. Many commentators consider him foolish. Who is foolish and who is wise? There must be someone who can distinguish this.

    Sin Shih-yao
    Sin Shih-yao, an official of the current dynasty, was from Shanxi and passed the imperial examination in the year Geng Xu. Determined to cultivate virtue, his room was not ashamed, truly a gentleman-scholar. Initially serving as the magistrate of Qingyuan in Beizhili, he was upright and unbending. When his wife and children came to his post, he did not allow them to enter the city, writing a poem to refuse them:

    After reading the family letters, feeling bewildered, they urge him to buy land and property. Wolf Mountain does not roll up its thousand-year-old painting, Chicken Water newly planted with five acres of lotus. Striking the drum and ascending the hall to truly speak the law, lighting a lamp and sitting alone, like meditating. My purse is still empty, just like it was in the past, I do not allow my wife and children to ask for salary money.

    559

    妻子阅诗，仍归故里。后公擢御史，督学江南。今崇祀北直名宦祠。

    邵士梅
    邵士梅，字峄晖，济宁人。初生时能言，邵父母以为怪，灌以辰砂，邵遂不言。及长而慧，读书能记。娶妻岳氏。合卺之夕，其嫂夜潜听之，小夫妇絮絮叨叨，如远年久别，枕边话旧云。两人最相昵爱，余视之皆客寄也。顺治辛卯举于乡，癸巳进士，谒选得登州教授。
    一日，檄署栖霞教谕。甫入署，有二老秀才来谒，便问斋夫二生居某村否，又言其丰范吻合，相与握手道故。问曰：“贵庄之高东海犹在乎？”二生愕然曰：“瘐死二十余年，止有一子。先生何以知之？”邵曰：“故人也。”先是东海为里正，素无赖，然性豪爽，好义轻财。有负租而卖女者，高即倾囊代赎。又尝私一娼。娼坐隐盗，官捕甚急，逃匿高家。官知之，收高备极拷掠，高不服，寻狱归。高死之日，即邵生之年。邵夫妇在登尝恤其子，为之置田宅焉。

    559

    Shao Shimei's wife would read poetry, and he returned to his hometown. Later, Shao was promoted to the position of censor and oversaw education in Jiangnan. Today, he is honored at the Temple of Distinguished Officials in Beizhili.

    Shao Shimei, courtesy name Yihui, was from Jining. He could speak at birth, which his parents found strange, so they gave him cinnabar to drink, and he subsequently became mute. As he grew older, he became wise and was able to remember what he read. He married a woman surnamed Yue. On the night of their wedding, his sister-in-law secretly listened in and heard the young couple chattering away, as if they had been separated for many years, talking about old times. The two were most affectionate and loving, and I see them all as guests in this world. In the year Ximao of the Shunzhi era, he passed the provincial examination, and in the year Gui Si, he passed the imperial examination, after which he was appointed as a professor in Dengzhou.
    One day, he was ordered to serve as the magistrate of Qixia. Just as he entered the office, two elderly scholars came to visit, and he immediately asked if the two students lived in a certain village. He also noted that their appearance and demeanor matched, and they shook hands and spoke of old times. He asked, "Is your village's Gao Donghai still alive?" The two scholars were startled and said, "He starved to death over twenty years ago, and there is only one son left. How did you know this?" Shao replied, "He is an old friend of mine." Previously, Gao Donghai was the village head, always a rascal, but he was generous and open-hearted, valuing righteousness and not attached to money. There was someone who sold his daughter because of unpaid rent, and Gao immediately spent all his money to redeem the daughter. He also secretly kept a courtesan. The courtesan was accused of hiding stolen goods, and the officials were very eager to catch her. She fled and hid in Gao's house. The officials found out and arrested Gao, subjecting him to severe torture. Gao did not confess and was eventually released from prison. The day Gao died was the same year as when Shao was born. Shao and his wife in Dengzhou once helped his son, providing him with land and a house.

    560

    后邵妻病笃，告邵曰：“又将别矣。死当生馆陶董家。所居滨河河曲第三家。君异时官罢后，萧寺繙经，尚当重结丝罗也。”已而迁吴江知县，谢病归。家居无聊，有同年某为馆陶令，因访之。出游郊外，至宝相寺，寺中有藏经，邵忆妻语，繙阅良久。忽闻人曰：“寺后河水清泚可玩。”邵即至寺后门，见隔水盈盈，河滨篱落可指数。视第三门，顿启，一垂髫女约十五六，对邵若有低徊之意。问之，果董姓。邵归告宰，且自述其异，遂访之。

    Explanation

    560

    后邵的妻子病重，她告诉后邵说：“我们又要分别了。我死后会转世到馆陶的董家。他们的家住在河边弯曲处的第三户。你将来卸任后，在萧寺读经时，我们还会再结丝罗。”不久后，后邵被调任为吴江县令，因病辞职回家。在家闲居无聊时，有一位同年好友担任馆陶县令，后邵便去拜访他。一次出游郊外，来到宝相寺，寺中有藏经阁，后邵想起妻子的话，便在那里翻阅了很久。忽然听到有人说道：“寺后的河水清澈，可以玩赏。”后邵随即走到寺后门，看到隔河相望的美景，河岸上的篱笆清晰可见。他看了看第三户人家的门，突然打开，里面走出一位约十五六岁的垂髫少女，对后邵似乎有依依不舍之意。后邵上前询问，得知她确实是董家的女儿。后邵回家后告诉了这位好友，并讲述了自己遇到的奇异之事，于是便去拜访了那位少女。

    561

    董姓云：其女知前生事，年十五不字人，惟待济宁邵进士来。遂娶焉。觌面时，邵犹不敢一见如故，而董氏视邵之斑苍更欢，若忘年交。岳氏未育，今董氏生二子。又十余年，董病又欲死，复与邵诀曰：“襄阳城王氏门前有两柳树者，君来访我于此，当再作夫妇。”邵抚之恸曰：“一再至三，从古罕有。今我年逾半百，人寿几何？行将就朽，纵使余喘尚存，齿豁发落，何以为情？且月老红丝，岂真尔我如意珠耶？”妻不谓然，固盟而逝。邵后自都中返，六十五岁，无疾终。

    Explanation

    561

    The Dong family said: Their daughter knew about her past life, and at fifteen, she had not yet married, only waiting for the Jinan scholar Shao to come. She eventually married him. When they first met, Shao still dared not feel as if they had known each other for a long time, but the Dong woman found Shao's aged appearance even more pleasing, as if they were a friendship between different ages. The wife (Yue Shi) had not given birth, but now the Dong woman has given birth to two sons. After more than ten years, the Dong woman fell ill again and was on the brink of death. She then swore an oath with Shao, saying, "At the gate of the Wang family in Xiangyang, there are two willow trees. If you come to visit me there, we shall again become husband and wife." Shao comforted her, weeping, "From one to two, and then to three, such a thing is rare from ancient times. Now I am over fifty, how long is human life? I am about to wither away, even if I still have a few breaths left, my teeth are falling out and my hair is thinning, how can I continue our relationship? And the red thread of Yue Lao, is it truly our perfect match?" His wife did not agree, and she passed away after swearing the oath. Shao returned from the capital at the age of sixty-five and died peacefully.

    562

    后襄阳王氏有女及笄，求婚者日盈于门，父母欲许，而女严拒之。城中亦有邵姓，楚世家。其小公子随父母游岘山归，妪仆群从过王氏门。见二柳树，公子伫立，攀条泫然，且欲入其家。妪携之入，王姓见之，啖以果馅。咸因公子幼稚，呼女出见，公子曰：“卿怎不似馆陶重会时乎？”女惊泣曰：“不料郎君已再世矣！”相与痛哭，家人异焉。由是公子日夜号泣，思念王氏。父母以王氏长七岁，不愿婚，公子欲之，父母不得已从焉。公子十五而娶，女已年二十有二。

    Explanation

    562

    After the Wang family of Xiangyang had a daughter who came of age, suitors filled their door every day, eager to propose marriage. Her parents wished to accept one, but the daughter sternly refused all of them. In the city, there was also a family surnamed Shao, a prominent lineage from the state of Chu. The youngest son of this family returned from a trip to Xian Mountain with his parents, and as they passed by the Wang family's gate, he saw two willow trees. He stood still, touched the branches, and shed tears, seemingly wanting to enter their home. The servant led him inside, and the Wang family welcomed him, feeding him with fruit and pastry. They, considering the young man's youth, called out to the daughter to come and meet him. The young man said, "Why don't you look like the time we met again in Guantao?" The daughter was surprised and wept, saying, "I didn't expect you to have passed away!" They wept bitterly together, and the family was amazed by this. Since then, the young man wept day and night, thinking of the Wang family. His parents, considering the Wang girl was seven years older, did not wish to marry them, but the young man desired it, and his parents had no choice but to agree. He married at fifteen, while the girl was already twenty-two.

    563

    王氏言邵三世性情微有不同，今生独贪曲糵。酒后人尝问邵前世事，邵每言至夫妇重聚之故，其言即止。至一日大醉，告人曰：“冥曹姻缘簿载我夫妇一节，因装砌时钉入夹缝，曹椽翻忙迫，往往遗漏，故由我两人自为之也。”王氏于屏后窃闻。及邵归，大咎之，邵亦悔之不及。邵夫妇自此常相厮守，唯恐他生不卜，再聚良难。遂绝意功名，蓬蒿终老。王氏享寿八十二岁，邵享年七十四岁，二子六孙。计此生完聚以来，六十年中，未尝一日相离。即济宁之故地，亦不若栖霞之再到矣。
    余在郧阳守恒德侄署，客有襄人徐子为余言，因取留仙、渔洋、竹垞所记，总而成之，更增补其说。

    贾秀才
    鲁南歉岁之余，疫流氓户，济宁、鱼台尤甚。宁之西乡，贾氏聚族而居，曰“贾家海”。有贾文学者，饩于庠。会疫行，其族靡有孑遗，而贾生亦染疫死。

    Explanation

    563

    Wang stated that Xiao's temperament varied slightly across three generations, and in this life, he was particularly fond of alcohol. After drinking, people often asked Xiao about his past lives, and Xiao would always stop speaking when he reached the point of his reunion with his wife. One day, while heavily intoxicated, he told someone, "The register of marital fate in the underworld records my marriage, but during the assembly, it was nailed into a narrow gap. The officials were busy and rushed, often overlooking or forgetting such details, so it was left to us two to handle." Wang secretly heard this from behind the screen. When Xiao returned, Wang scolded him severely, and Xiao regretted it too late. From then on, Xiao and his wife stayed together constantly, afraid that in another life, they might not meet again easily. They then gave up on pursuing official careers and chose to live a simple, secluded life. Wang lived to be eighty-two years old, and Xiao lived to be seventy-four years old. They had two sons and six grandchildren. Since their reunion in this life, they never parted for even a single day over sixty years. Even though they returned to their old hometown in Jinan, it was not as good as revisiting Qixia again.

    I was in charge of the office of my nephew Chengde in Yunyang, and a guest from Xiang named Xu told me. I compiled the records of Liu Xian, Yu Yang, and Zhu Cha, and added more details.

    Jia Scholar
    In the aftermath of a poor harvest in southern Shandong, there was an outbreak of plague among the common people, particularly severe in Jining and Yutai. In the western suburbs of Ning, the Jia clan lived together in a place called "Jia Family Sea." There was a scholar named Jia Wenxue, who was supported by the school. When the plague struck, none of his clan members survived, and Jia Wenxue also died from the plague.

    564

    当贾生之死也，单、曹亦无传染。有曹邑之青堌集耿姓庄户，夫妻半百，一女名改[玫]姑，字同里岳家作媳，家皆殷富。时改[玫]姑忽遘疫，举家张惶，旬日之间，百医罔效，至夜奄息已绝。夫妇痛切娇生，岳姓亦来吊唁。其母抚其胸，有微热，守之而哭。至更阑，忽闻女腹作格格声。其母惊视，则目睫已若转动，四肢皆温。父母喜出望外，阖家环视。
    母掖之坐，女左右顾，作呻吟声。忽跃起曰：“我贾相公也，何绐至此？诸男女恶混遝为？”其母曰：“儿勿劳，初甦，语谜谵，尚不认父母。”女曰：“谓他人父，谓他人母耶？我将返。”其母灌以汤，女泼而不食。强而起，行动俨如男子。而自顾足缠发挽，不觉诧异，因复坐，默默思想。终夜之间，母娣姊妹交床叠枕，不胜厌烦。继欲溺，起亦不似初，因大悟其前身借壳也。晨兴，奁事皆不能办。诸娣姊为之，习以为常。

    Explanation

    564

    When Jia Sheng died, Dan and Cao were not infected. There was a family of the surname Geng in Qingguji Village, Cao County, with a husband and wife in their fifties and a daughter named Gai [Mei] Gu, who was married to a man from the same neighborhood. The family was prosperous. At that time, Gai [Mei] Gu suddenly contracted an illness, causing the entire household to panic. Within ten days, no doctor could cure her, and by nightfall, she had breathed her last. The couple mourned deeply for their beloved daughter, and the husband's family also came to pay their respects. Her mother felt her chest and found it slightly warm, and she wept over her. As the night deepened, she suddenly heard a faint sound coming from her daughter's abdomen. Her mother was startled and looked at her, seeing that her eyelids were fluttering and her limbs were warm. The parents were overjoyed and the whole family gathered around.

    Her mother helped her sit up, and the girl looked around, making moaning sounds. She suddenly jumped up and said, "I am Jia Xiangong, why have I been deceived here? What are these men and women doing together?" Her mother replied, "Don't strain yourself, you just woke up, you're speaking nonsense and don't recognize your parents yet." The girl said, "Are they calling others father and mother? I will return." Her mother gave her some soup, but she spat it out and refused to eat. She forced herself to stand up, moving like a man. She looked at her own feet bound and hair tied up, not realizing it was strange, so she sat down again and thought silently. Throughout the night, her mother and sisters shared beds and pillows, unable to bear the annoyance. She then wanted to urinate, but her movements were different from before, and she suddenly realized that she had taken over another person's body. In the morning, she could not perform her daily tasks. Her sisters took over, and she became accustomed to it.

    565

    女一日告父母曰：“母若父非我父母也。今我实借女身以为身，敢不以女之父母为父母乎？”言讫呜呜。父母异之，曰：“然乎，信乎？始吾女也，今更有子道焉，不庸愈乎？第尔已委禽于人矣。此曹邑也，去汝家三百里耳。予家耿姓。”女曰：“前身以疫死，而家之病疫者殆尽。天命至此，复何可言？”事父母颇醇谨达礼，无前女娇养之习，渐经家务，耿赖之。岳姓知其事，尤喜，催就瓜期，而女转多难色。既而缔姻合卺，虽女其形，实男其心，床笫之间，并不解裙带味，无一点脂粉态。往往搦管呫哔，酸措大气却有时流露。夫婿青年，女代塾师教之，而变化之权更自易易，盖自善诱者深矣。三年，其婿游于泮。

    Explanation

    565

    One day, the girl told her parents, "If my mother and father are not my true parents, then now I truly borrow this female body as my own. How could I not regard your parents as my own?" After saying this, she wept. Her parents were surprised and said, "Is it true? Is it certain? At first, I was just a daughter, but now I have the ways of a son. Is it not better? But you have already been betrothed to someone. This is the town of Cao, only three hundred miles from your home. My family is surnamed Geng." The girl said, "My previous life was taken by an epidemic, and almost all of my family fell ill. Fate has brought me here; what more can be said?" She served her parents with great sincerity, diligence, and propriety, without the spoiled habits of a pampered daughter. Gradually, she became adept at household affairs, and the Geng family relied on her. The family of her fiancé, the Yues, knew about this and were especially pleased, urging her to fulfill the marriage contract. But the girl turned to a more reluctant expression. Later, they were married and consummated their union. Though she had the form of a woman, her heart was that of a man. In the bedroom, she did not understand the taste of skirts and ribbons, and she had no trace of feminine demeanor. She often held a brush and wrote, though she had a scholarly and reserved demeanor, she sometimes let her true feelings flow out. Her young husband, the girl taught him as a private tutor, and the ability to transform things became even easier for her, for those who are good at guiding others are deep. Three years later, her husband went to study at the local school.

    566

    后为婿纳妾，生一子。二十年，婿贡满，秉铎莱属，携眷往。道经泲上，而贾生已半老佳人。入其乡，寻式里居，遍问故人，街衢井巷悉所旧识，曰：“我故庠生贾文学后身也。”里之中黄发台背，是当时征逐聚首者，尚一二在。言及己事，一毫不爽，因竞传其事。
    (七如曰：两世之事，古亦志之，独异乎贾生以巾帼师儒，能成儒子之名耳。岂偶然哉！)

    卖菜李老
    苏有卖菜李老者，一夫一妇，僦楼而居。邻巷多富贵，独李老一佣介乎其间。三十年倡随如比翼，从不闻有诟谇声。巷之中以病废、以贫去、以富且贵死，不知凡几，而佣之况如常。

    Explanation

    566

    Afterward, the son-in-law took a concubine and gave birth to a son. Twenty years later, the son-in-law completed his term of service, held the bell, and belonged to the state of Lai, taking his family with him. Passing through the area around the Ji River, the woman (Jia) was already a middle-aged beauty. Entering the village, he searched for the old residence, asked everyone about the old acquaintances, and found that the streets, alleys, and wells were all familiar. He said, "I am the reincarnation of the former scholar Jia Wenhua." Among the elderly people in the village, those who were together at that time, still a few remain. When talking about her own affairs, not a single detail was wrong, thus, the story was widely spread.

    (Commentator: Two lifetimes' worth of events, ancient texts have recorded them, but what is unique about Jia is that she, as a woman, became a teacher and could achieve the name of a scholar. Is this by chance?)

    As for the old man Li who sold vegetables, he and his wife rented a house to live in. The neighborhood was filled with the wealthy, but Li was just a simple tenant in between. For thirty years, they lived together like birds of a feather, and there was never a single quarrel. In the alley, many people fell ill and became disabled, others left due to poverty, and still others died of wealth and honor, countless in number, yet Li's situation remained unchanged.

    567

    四十余得一女，绕膝下。晨，妻女酣酣醺睡，李起，笠而跣，持一空挑子出城外易菜数捆。如春韭、秋瓜之属，盈筐簏，一周于市，而青蚨入囊橐，尽一日度支。归，日未晡，妻女方起盥，李亦盥焉。饭后，则蹀躞山塘间，或啜苦茗，或饮薄醪。晚归，则小楼月上，李乃说荒唐杂剧，欢笑一时，真如生公坐石上演大法乘，又如马鸣大士化毘婆罗，眷属皆皈依也。有富室某，谂李甚详，遂重其人。乃曰：“李老一日不作，则一日不食。我愿假多金权倍蓰，则一劳可以永逸。”李曰：“我福薄，恐不能消受。”其妻闻之喜，怂焉。李为之动，领其资。于是持筹握算，碌碌不得安帖，鸡鸣而起，日昃尚不归。女见其惫，曰：“父何以不若前日之贫而乐也？非娱老计，请辞富而就贫。”李老不能纳其言而卸肩焉，竟以劳病死，又无儿可悯也。
    吁，利之一途，其转移之权抑何甚？以李老三十年之雅操，尤且不能不改节于末路，遑问其他！

    Explanation

    567

    In his forties, Li had a daughter who was always at his side. In the morning, his wife and daughter were still sleeping soundly. Li, wearing a straw hat and barefoot, carried an empty basket to exchange for several bundles of vegetables outside the city. He filled his basket with spring onions, autumn melons, and other vegetables, went around the market, and the money entered his bag, covering his daily expenses. He returned before noon, and his wife and daughter just woke up and washed up. After meals, he strolled along the mountain stream, sometimes sipping bitter tea, sometimes drinking light wine. Returning late, the moon rose over the small pavilion. Li then told absurd plays, and they laughed together for a while, truly like Sheng Gong performing the Great Dharma on the stone, and like Ma Ming the Great Bodhisattva transforming Vipashyana, all relatives and subjects taking refuge. A wealthy person, who knew Li very well, thus respected him greatly. He said, "If Old Li doesn't work one day, he doesn't eat that day. I wish to lend you a lot of money so you can work less and live more comfortably." Li said, "My good fortune is thin, I fear I cannot bear it." His wife heard it and was pleased, urging him. Li was moved and accepted the money. Thus he held the abacus and calculated, busy and unable to settle down, getting up at cockcrow and not returning until sunset. The daughter saw his exhaustion and said, "Why is Father no longer as happy as before when poor? This is not a plan for entertainment; please give up wealth and return to poverty." Old Li could not accept her words and thus shouldered the burden, eventually dying of overwork, and there was no son to mourn him. Alas, the power of profit is so great. Even with Old Li's thirty years of noble conduct, he could not maintain it in the end, what more can be said about others?

    568

    (七如曰：余作秀才时，不肯教书，尝以笔墨遨游齐鲁间。久之，为当道诸公内记室，岁得束脯百余金，腊底言归，一家八口从无卒岁之虞。乡荐后，心羡仕途，遂尔一行作吏，簿书鞅堂，仆仆尘埃。回忆曩昔襟期，不啻霄壤，正与李菜佣同一失足，良可恨叹！
    晚节极难，韩魏公真可自信矣。)

    李福
    乾隆二十五年，潍人李福，年四旬，止一子方五岁，家贫。诣京师，积银二十两。回家。
    夜行，路旁有一舍，灯光微露。因天寒借火，吃烟，见一老妪守一病儿在炕，意境惨然。询之，乃云：“孤孙两世所系，今病危，医者欲用参，计值二两，苦无力。”福遂赠镪如数。
    及归，见其子羸尫，如病新愈者。妇曰：“是儿病，将不治，于某夜梦祖母至，予参一碗，饮之顿愈。”征其期，正予金时也。视囊金亦无少缺。嘻！生我之爱，庇及两世，无间幽明，洵深恩罔极矣。

    Explanation

    568

    (Seven R's said: When I was a scholar, I refused to teach, and instead traveled extensively in the regions of Qi and Lu with my pen and ink. After a long time, I became an inner secretary for the officials in power, receiving a yearly stipend of over a hundred gold pieces. At the end of the year, I returned home, and with an eight-member family, there was never any worry about making ends meet. After being recommended by the local authorities, I envied the official career and thus embarked on a career as an official, busying myself with official documents and records, tired from dust and travel. Recalling past aspirations, I found a vast difference between them and my current situation, just like Li Caiyong, it was a misstep. Truly regrettable!

    The latter part of one's life is extremely difficult, Grand Duke Han truly can be confident in himself.)

    Li Fu
    In the 25th year of the Qianlong era, Li Fu from Weifang, in his forties, with only one son who was five years old, had a poor family. He went to the capital, accumulated twenty taels of silver, and returned home.
    Traveling at night, he saw a cottage by the side of the road with faintly visible light. Because it was cold, he borrowed fire and smoked, seeing an old woman guarding a sick child on the kang (a heated brick bed). The scene was pitiful. He asked her, and she said, "My grandson, who is the only hope for two generations, is now critically ill. The doctor wants to use ginseng, worth two taels, but I have no money." Li Fu then gave her the amount of money as requested.
    Upon returning home, he saw his son emaciated, as if he had just recovered from an illness. The wife said, "This child was sick and would not recover. On a certain night, he dreamed that his grandmother came, gave him a bowl of ginseng soup, and drank it and immediately recovered." He asked about the date, and it was the time when he gave the money. He checked his money and found it was still intact. Ah! The love of those who gave birth to me, protecting two generations, without distinction between the living and the dead, truly an immense and boundless grace.

    569

    张兆富
    即墨之蓝村张兆富，幼委禽于同村之李氏女。女长于张，女过笄而张甫成童，故结褵尚迟迟。然与岳家门楣相望，常往来也。张母孀，无兄弟，有薄田数十亩，可以度日。
    当夏初雨后，母呼张曰：“尔可向丈人家借一斗豆种来耩地。”张至李家，其家人皆下田耩豆，独其女在炕上弄针黹。见张笑问曰：“郎来甚么？”张曰：“借一斗豆。”女曰：“做种耶？”张曰：“良然。”女起曰：“我家这地，雨后滋润，也待耩。无豆种借与你。”张曰：“你家地我自有种下。”女起，以手拍张曰：“小郎谑我哉。”张顾无人，遂与女狎。乡女儿以其为夫也，故亦不拒。曰：“今日之事，终身之计。”乃褫张衣为信。张不与，女强取而藏诸箧。张恐人来，惶惧奔。

    Explanation

    569

    Zhang Zhaofu
    Zhang Zhaofu, from Lan Village in Jimo, was betrothed to a Li family daughter from the same village when he was young. The woman was older than Zhang, having reached the age of marriage (笄) while he was still a child, so their wedding was delayed. However, their families lived close to each other, and they often visited one another. Zhang's mother was a widow, and he had no brothers. They had a modest amount of farmland, enough to support their daily needs.

    One summer after the rain, Zhang's mother called him and said, "You can go to your future father-in-law's house and borrow a measure of soybean seeds to sow the fields." Zhang went to the Li family's house, where everyone was already out in the fields sowing beans, except for the woman, who was sitting on the kang (a traditional raised bed) doing needlework. She saw Zhang and asked with a smile, "What brings you here, sir?" Zhang replied, "I came to borrow a measure of soybeans." The woman asked, "For planting?" Zhang confirmed, "Yes, exactly." She stood up and said, "Our land is moist and fertile after the rain, and we are also ready to sow. I can't lend you any seeds." Zhang said, "I have my own seeds for my land." The woman laughed and patted Zhang, saying, "You're teasing me, young master." Zhang looked around to see if anyone else was there, and then he began to flirt with her. The village woman thought of him as her husband, so she did not resist. She said, "Today's events will determine our lifelong fate." She then took off Zhang's clothes as a token of their commitment. Zhang refused, but the woman forcefully took them and hid them in a chest. Zhang, fearing that someone might come, ran away in fear and panic.

    570

    时方夏，止穿一袷，乃抱肩而走，不遑问豆，亦不敢回家见母。行十余里，茫无止所。蓝村官道也，往来官商，络绎不绝。有西客乘骡丁丁数头过，张随之走，客顾曰：“娃娃那里去？赤膊炎天，可不炙焦皮肤吗？”张曰：“我失路人。父母行乞，弃我去矣。”客怜之。抵逆旅，客又细询，张绐而黠。客喜，收为义子，解衣衣之，推食食之，载之同客而西，学贸易。张能，客以千金倚之，不数年利倍蓰。客固无家，赖张代其劳。客欲为张娶室，张曰：“关山行旅，何以家为？盍归乎休，未为晚也。”隐念老母，又恐终堂定妻，难保守室。一朝羞去，便不能归。时一念及，如针毡之坐不宁。

    Explanation

    570

    It was summer, and Zhang wore only a single layer of clothing, hunching his shoulders as he hurried along, too preoccupied to worry about food and too ashamed to return home to see his mother. After walking more than ten miles, he had no idea where to stop. He was on the official road in Lan Village, where government officials and merchants passed by continuously. A group of Western travelers riding several donkeys passed by, and Zhang followed them. One of the travelers looked back and asked, "Where are you going, child? Bare-chested in the scorching heat, aren't you burning your skin?" Zhang replied, "I'm a lost person. My parents were beggars, and they abandoned me." The travelers pitied him. They arrived at an inn, and the travelers asked Zhang more questions. Zhang answered deceitfully but cleverly. The travelers were pleased and took him as a son, taking off their clothes to dress him and offering him food. They took him with them to the west to learn trade. Zhang proved capable, and the travelers entrusted him with a thousand gold pieces. Within a few years, his profits multiplied several times. The travelers had no home, relying on Zhang to do the work. They wanted to marry Zhang, but he said, "Traveling through mountains and passes, why have a home? Why not return and rest, it's not too late." He secretly thought of his mother, but he feared that finalizing a marriage would make it difficult to maintain a household. Once ashamed to leave, he could not return. Sometimes thinking of it, he felt like sitting on a needle mat, unable to be at peace.

    571

    荏苒间已十八寒暑。客老死，一切殡事，张亦尽礼尽哀。于是乃怀厚资，决计归家门，垂垂囊橐，庶几一洗前日之羞。而李女自张逸后，遂得一子。父母恶之，女乃持张衣裹其子，奔张氏之母，哭诉其由。张母认子衣，抱孙曰：“汝诚吾媳也，是诚吾孙也。抚孤而侍孀，何如？”女曰：“固所愿也。”女之父母遂无词。张孙长，定婚于王姓家，亦饶裕。王以张母与李氏皆孀，邀其婿读书于家。数年，张孙俊慧，大有父风，亦先与王女通焉。瓜期择日完娶。

    Explanation

    571

    In the course of time, eighteen years have passed. The guest died of old age, and all funeral arrangements were made; Zhang also performed all the rituals and showed deep sorrow. Carrying a substantial sum of money, Zhang decided to return home, with a heavy purse, hoping to wipe away the shame of the past. After Zhang left, Li's daughter eventually gave birth to a son. Her parents disapproved of her, and she wrapped her child in Zhang's clothes and ran to Zhang's mother, crying and recounting the reason. Zhang's mother recognized the child's clothes and embraced the grandson, saying, "You are truly my daughter-in-law, and this is truly my grandson. To raise an orphan and care for a widow, how about that?" The daughter said, "It is indeed what I wish." The daughter's parents had no argument. Zhang's grandson grew up, and he was arranged to marry a family surnamed Wang, who were also wealthy. Wang invited Zhang's mother and Li to live in his household because they were both widows, and he invited his son-in-law to study at home. After several years, Zhang's grandson was intelligent and wise, greatly resembling his father, and also secretly had a relationship with Wang's daughter. They chose a date for their marriage during the auspicious period.

    572

    北俗，亲迎鼓吹而来，王氏忽产儿于彩舆中。送亲者皆赧颜欲回车，张孙邀而自陈其罪。入门，张母曰：“喜得重孙。”李氏曰：“其不改父之行，是难能也。”正攘攘哄笑之间，忽一人轩昂而入，门外骡驮累累。见母在堂，趋前抱膝，跪而哭曰：“儿不孝，十八年出亡在外。今返家门，幸老母无恙。”哽咽不能成言。母手摩其面，审谛再三，曰：“是儿来耶？是我梦耶？”向内呼曰：“媳妇，尔男子归家，怎不出视？”李女不肯出，母乃破涕为笑曰：“此事我知之，然我难料理也。

    Explanation

    572

    In the north, during the traditional wedding ceremony where the groom arrives with a musical procession, Lady Wang suddenly gave birth to a child in the decorated carriage. The attendants, all blushing and wanting to turn back the carriage, were taken aback by the unexpected event. Zhang's grandson invited them and confessed his own fault. Upon entering the house, Zhang's mother said, "I am happy to have a great-grandson." Lady Li remarked, "That he has not changed his father's behavior, which is difficult to achieve." Just as they were in a state of chaotic laughter, a man entered with a dignified and proud manner, and outside, there were many donkeys carrying loads. Seeing his mother in the hall, he rushed forward, embraced her knees, and knelt to cry, "I have been ungrateful and have been away for eighteen years. Now I have returned home, and I am glad that my old mother is well." He choked with sobs and could not speak. His mother touched his face and examined him carefully, saying, "Is this my son? Is this a dream?" She called out to the daughter-in-law, "Daughter-in-law, your husband has returned home, why don't you come out to see him?" Lady Li refused to come out, and the mother then laughed through her tears, saying, "I know about this, but I find it difficult to handle."

    573

    ”乃告诸亲串，又令其孙来拜父，张恧形于颊。众亲哗曰：“今日张母得子，李氏有夫，张孙获妇，王氏诞儿，三善备，四事集，宜计日而行贺。”旁有鼓人执乐而前曰：“请设两青庐，重筵加酒，尽一日欢。我为一一吹笙击鼓，以并力奏技，主人家当四倍其金钱，则此事办矣。”一乡之中，是亲非亲，无富贵贫贱，男男女女，杂沓咸来致庆。筵席排至门外皆满，比秋成之赛社，尤有加等。是张兆富，有斗伯比之行，张孙又读父书，本无足道。然论之乡里，毋太绳拘。《诗》有之“中冓之言，不可道也，所可道也，言之丑也”，轩渠之而已矣。

    Explanation

    573

    Then, he informed all the relatives and friends, and also ordered his grandson to come and pay respects to his father. Zhang's face showed a look of embarrassment. The relatives and friends shouted in excitement, saying, "Today, Zhang's mother has a son, Li has a husband, Zhang's grandson has a wife, and Wang has a son. Three good things are complete, and four events have come together. It is appropriate to plan the celebration day by day." A drummer with musical instruments approached and said, "Please set up two green tents with double seating and add wine. Enjoy the whole day. I will play the sheng and beat the drum, using all my strength to perform. The host should quadruple the amount of money, and then this matter will be settled." In the entire village, whether related or not, regardless of wealth or poverty, men and women all came to celebrate. The banquet tables were so numerous that they filled up to the door, and it was even more splendid than the autumn harvest festival. Zhang Zhaofu had the conduct of Dou Berbi, and Zhang's grandson also read his father's books, which originally were not worth mentioning. However, when discussing it in the village, do not be too strict. The Book of Songs says, "What is said in private, should not be spoken; what is spoken, is shameful." They laughed it off and that's all.

    574

    (只是张子拥厚资归，遂成佳话。利之时义大矣哉！太冲)

    野寺宿
    胡某夜行，至野寺，敲门求宿。一老僧出曰：“大殿不可以假寐。东厨惟老衲独眠一床。西廊中尚得容膝，但无床榻。有人寄一空材，客能淹夕于棺盖否？权当悬榻，未审客意如何？”胡曰：“我椒山自有胆，此上最安乐。即使柩有牛鸣，我何妨作楼上人，况空空一槥者。”僧喜，遂令阖户，持灯而去。

    Explanation

    574

    The Prosperity of Zhang Zai

    Zhang Zai returned home with a great fortune, thus becoming a fine story. The righteousness of profit is indeed great! (Tài Chōng)

    An Overnight Stay at a Wild Temple
    One night, a man named Hu was traveling and arrived at a temple in the countryside. He knocked on the door to ask for lodging. An elderly monk came out and said, "The main hall is not suitable for light sleep. The kitchen is where I sleep alone on a bed. The western corridor still has enough space for knees, but there is no bed. Someone has left an empty coffin; can you spend the night on the coffin lid? Let's consider it a hanging bed, but I don't know what you think." Hu replied, "I, from Jiaoshan, have my own courage, and this place is the most peaceful. Even if the coffin makes a cow's sound, why can't I be a person on the upper floor, especially since it is just an empty coffin?" The monk was pleased and then ordered the door to be closed, taking the lamp with him and leaving.

    575

    胡奋身登木，其兴致几欲歌“女手卷然”之句。无何，而目交睫合，神将离而魂欲杳，且以为明日与老僧盖棺论定，必谓我胆周于身矣。忽棺内作一响，胡惊觉，四顾昏黑；又响，胡战栗不自持，顿缩板上，浑浑肉皆颤颤动，觉棺内亦颤动，与己身之觳觫相互答。胡在上愈惊，而其下愈响。胡魄丧胆落，为之滚落板下。又闻棺盖若揭，胡奔命夺门出院，趋东厨呼僧出视，而胡已匍匐奄息，两胫骨间犹作辘轳转。半晌心定，始告僧。
    僧不之信，相与火视，排闼入，见盖已起，凛然一人立其中。僧亦惊，喝而问，答曰：“我前村之宁五也。”僧曰：“胡为乎柩中？”曰：“我病虐，避鬼卧于此。顷闻棺上响，我固不知假寐者。我以彼为鬼自外至，彼则又以我为鬼从中来。于是乎我疑彼鬼，彼疑我鬼，各具鬼胎，遂皆鬼相，乃无往而非鬼矣。我闻客与我师捶门惊告，我始知其非鬼。客今当知我之非鬼也。我睡醒虐退，将归我前村，客请安稳眠棺上。”遂去。

    Explanation

    575

    Tru Feng climbed onto the wooden structure, his excitement nearly prompting him to sing a line from a poem. Soon, his eyes closed, and his spirit and soul felt detached and lost. He believed that the next day, the old monk would judge him, and surely he would be filled with courage. Suddenly, a sound came from inside the coffin. Tru Feng was startled awake and looked around in darkness. Another sound made him tremble uncontrollably, and he shrank back onto the board. His whole body trembled, and he felt the coffin also trembling, the trembling of the coffin and his own body seeming to echo each other. Tru Feng on top became more frightened, while the sounds below became louder. His spirit was lost and his courage fell, and he rolled off the board. He heard the coffin lid seemed to be lifted, and he rushed to the door and fled the courtyard, going to the kitchen and calling out to the monk to come and see. By then, Tru Feng was crawling and barely breathing, his leg bones still making a turning motion like a wheel. After a while, his heart calmed down, and he finally told the monk.

    The monk did not believe him, and they lit a fire to investigate, breaking down the door to see the lid already lifted, and a stern-looking man standing inside. The monk was also startled, shouting and asking, the man replied, "I am Ning Wu from the village ahead." The monk said, "Why are you in the coffin?" He replied, "I was ill with malaria and slept here to avoid the ghost. Just now, I heard a sound on the coffin, and I didn't know I was sleeping. I thought he was a ghost coming from outside, and he thought I was a ghost coming from inside. So, I suspected he was a ghost, and he suspected I was a ghost, each with a ghostly nature, and we became ghostly to each other, so there was no place that was not ghostly. I heard the guest and my master banging on the door and shouting in alarm, and I realized that they were not ghosts. Now, you should know that I am not a ghost. I have woken up and my malaria has subsided, and I will return to my village ahead. Please rest assured and sleep peacefully on the coffin." And he left.

    576

    胡视夜半，竟不能眠，与老僧话，东方既白，仓皇而行。

    生员
    有余杭生员某，偶于乙未夏月出城，见一青衣云：“我乃冥府差人，有票拘汝。但汝不应路死，可速归，待我摄完四十余人，方来唤汝。”某急还家，而二青衣已候门外。即辞妻子，摒挡家务，痛哭而逝。至冥司，青衣羁之阶下。闻唱名声，某应曰：“生员有。”冥司云：“生员不是拿者，毋亦误甚！”遂鞭勾使。一庭错愕，乃遣某还苏。后有年，以官事诬，牵入衙鞫讯，亦唱名，某亦应曰：“生员有。”堂上官拍案大怒，曰：“汝以生员作护身符耶？”不容辨析，手戒二十。两掌隆肿，负痛还。某尝愤恨世道愦愦，致令斯文扫地。

    Explanation

    576

    At midnight, staring at the night sky, he could not sleep, and talked with an old monk. When the east turned white, he hastily departed.

    There was a scholar from Yuhang who, in the summer of the year of the horse, happened to go out of the city. Suddenly, a blue-clad attendant appeared and said, "I am a messenger from the underworld, and there is a warrant to arrest you. But you should not die on the road; you can quickly return home. Wait for me to capture more than forty people before I come for you." The scholar hurriedly returned home, but two blue-clad attendants were already waiting outside the door. He bid farewell to his wife and children, settled his household affairs, and departed in tears. Upon arriving at the underworld, a blue-clad attendant detained him at the steps. When the names were called, the scholar responded, "I am a scholar." The underworld official said, "A scholar is not the one to be taken; it is also a great mistake!" Then he was whipped and summoned. The entire court was startled, and he was sent back to Suzhou. Years later, due to false accusations in official matters, he was dragged into the office for interrogation. When the names were called again, the scholar responded, "I am a scholar." The official on the dais slammed the table in anger and said, "Do you use your status as a scholar as a talisman?" There was no room for explanation, and he received twenty strokes of the cane. Both his palms were swollen, and he returned in pain. Once, he felt angry at the world's confusion, leading to the degradation of culture.

    577

    噫！等一生员也，岂独能宽于冥法，而不获宥于官刑哉？是其幸与不幸，初不在生员之有无，而在生员之自为也。然则生员可有乎，可不有乎？
    (近日之刁生劣监，哄堂打鼓，长官畏避之不暇，手戒之说绝响矣。
    有生员可手戒，有生员不可手戒，在长官自行剖别耳，岂得以击鼓遂概目为刁劣耶？傅声谷)

    伤膂夫
    华亭钱鹤滩学士，归营私第，工役烦苦，土木垩丹，经岁不休。有一夫蹩，且不任役，将责焉。蹩者告曰：“我不敢舍镘以嬉。我操镘而入富贵之家有年矣。往时黄提刑营第，我受役而伤膂。今其屋已瓦败而垣颓，过者以为墟，而我之膂犹伤而未可。呜呼，岂今之屋有同于黄之屋耶？何我之罪适符乎往日之罪耶？”学士感之，且罢工役。
    (七如曰：夫夫也，一言而罪释于己，役罢于工，两得之矣。又非“圬者王承福”之论之徒，以独善其身而已也。)

    Explanation

    577

    Aha! As a scholar-official, one might be lenient in the afterlife, but not escape punishment from earthly laws! Whether fortunate or unfortunate, it does not lie in whether one is a scholar-official, but in one's own conduct. So, can one be a scholar-official? Or not?

    (Recently, unruly scholar-officials and students have been causing a ruckus, making officials too afraid to handle them, and the idea of physical punishment has fallen out of favor. There are scholar-officials who can be physically punished, and there are those who cannot; it is up to the officials to discern this for themselves. Can one simply label all who beat drums as unruly and wicked?)

    A Man with a Wounded Limb

    The Scholar-official from Huating, Mr. Qian Hetan, returned to his private residence, where the labor was arduous, and the construction with earth, wood, and paint lasted for years without respite. One worker, limping and unable to perform his duties, was about to be punished. The limping worker said, "I dare not abandon my trowel for leisure. I have been working in wealthy households for years. In the past, when the magistrate Huang was building his residence, I was injured while working. Now, his house is in ruins, and passersby think it a wasteland, yet my injury remains unhealed. Alas, is today's house the same as Huang's? Why does my fault match that of my past?"

    The scholar-official was moved by this and halted the construction.
