    (Chu Ruxi said: The worker, with one word, was absolved of his guilt, and the labor ceased. He achieved both. He was not like the mason Wang Chengfu, who spoke only of self-improvement.)

    578

    南中行旅记
    五月十三日。早晴，饭后，暴雨，点大如粟，俗呼为“磨刀雨”。逾时霁，出归德门，同许姓能通使者，看十三行。
    屋临水，粉垣翠栏，八角六角，或为方，或为圆，或为螺形，不可思议。前则平地如坡，门仿闉式，开于旁侧。白饰雕镂，金碧焜煌，多幔缋。门有番奴，目深碧闪闪，卷曲毛发，类脊鼻騧。持佛郎机，为逻守，衣多罗辟支，悬霜刃，烛人毫芒。非问途已经者，不敢入。其户重以绣帘，窗棂悉用滨铁为之，既壮观，且可守御，内嵌琉璃大瓦，当屦满时，皆铿锵作应山谷响。地铺洋氍毹，腥红如滟滪波，几不能履，恐袜生尘也。座设漆雕为之，两旁庋手，中以革垫，其一角前向，出入两股中。几为月形，或半圭，层层凿菱蓉攒花。其白面碧瞳者为大贾。冠以黑绒三叉，望类毘卢笠。衣青尼，束身大金钮，累累贯珠。绦用杂色纬，通体皆缚扎无懈处。革履，操赤藤，人谓其藤中藏芒刃云。通使言，赤藤者最贵。

    Explanation

    578

    South China Travelogue
    May 13th. Early in the morning, the sky was clear; after eating, heavy rain fell, with droplets as large as millet grains, locally called "grinding knife rain." After some time, the rain cleared up. I left through the Gui De Gate, accompanied by a messenger surnamed Xu who could communicate, to visit the Thirteen Rows.

    The houses stood by the water, with white walls and green railings. Their shapes were octagonal, hexagonal, square, round, or spiral, all inconceivable. In front, the ground was flat like a slope, with gates resembling ancient defensive structures, opening on the side. The houses were adorned with white carvings, gold and jade, shining brightly, and many curtains and paintings. The gates were guarded by foreign slaves, with deep, shiny green eyes, curly hair, resembling a type of horse with a hump on its back. They held firearms (佛郎机), serving as guards, dressed in multi-colored clothing, with sharp swords hanging, bright enough to illuminate the finest details of people. Only those who had been asked for the way could enter. The doors were heavily adorned with embroidered curtains, and the window frames were made of iron, both magnificent and capable of defense. Inside, large glass tiles were embedded, making a resonant sound like the echo of a valley when stepped on. The floor was covered with foreign carpets, with a blood-red color like the waves of the Yanyu River, so one could hardly walk on it, fearing that the socks would get dirty. The seats were made of lacquered wood, with handrests on both sides and leather cushions in the middle. One corner faced forward, allowing entry and exit between the legs. The tables were crescent-shaped or half-circular, with layers of carved lotus flowers. The white-faced, green-eyed individuals were wealthy merchants. They wore hats made of black velvet with three points, resembling the hat of the Buddha Vairocana. Their clothing was blue robes, tied with large gold buttons, with beads strung together. Their belts were multicolored, with the entire body tightly bound without any slack. They wore leather shoes and carried red rattan walking sticks, with the belief that the rattan contained hidden blades. The envoy said that the red rattan was the most valuable.

    579

    导以意，作免胄礼，叙宾主欢，余答以揖。进金盒烟，嗅之辛香不可耐，渠则盈掬充两突间，噏噏不作一嚏。顷设馔。器质亦豫章窑，但金碧满绘，五彩相煊，与时用者异。每器可容十升，盛鸡匹，悉刲其头爪，囫囵以具，不脔切，用铁牙叉为箸。食用麦，杂以茴胡蔴熯块肉。酒具用白玻璃，晶莹彻内外，口盎而中直。酒芳冽，余尽三器，渠渍渍喜，作指环抵唇者三，通使告余：“羡君能豪。”

    Explanation

    579

    Guided by intention, we performed the ritual of removing helmets, narrated the joy of host and guest, and I responded with a bow. A precious box of incense was presented; its spicy fragrance was almost overwhelming, and he filled the space between two points with it, inhaling deeply without sneezing. Soon after, a meal was prepared. The utensils were of high quality, made in the豫章窑, fully painted with luxurious and colorful designs, different from those commonly used at the time. Each utensil could hold ten liters, serving a whole chicken with all its head and claws removed, presented whole without being cut into pieces, and using a metal fork as chopsticks. The food was a mixture of wheat, fennel, sesame, and roasted meat. The wine utensils were made of white glass, clear and transparent throughout, wide-mouthed and straight in the middle. The wine was fragrant and strong, and I finished three utensils. He was pleased and satisfied, making the gesture of placing a finger ring to his lips three times. The envoy told me, "I admire your ability to drink heavily."

    580

    继乃散步槛廊，穷观奇异。有乐钟，至时则诸音并奏，声节无讹，刻时不爽。有千里镜，可以登高望远，二三里能鉴人眉目。又有显微。多宝，小自鸣表，持之耳畔，如橐虫之啄木。又有海洋全图、贝多罗花、丁香藤、相思鸟、五色鹦鹉、倒挂禽、獴兽、短狗之类。檐间悬水晶灯，璎珞露垂，风来则珠霰摇空，铮铮相击撞，贮火可五十盏。
    余往来珠江，夜深则遥见之。辛卯，都中亦见此。门有悬旗，色用朱红布地，作叉股者，是贺兰贾也。余处未观，日将匿，遂返。续游竟不果。

    旱魃辨
    《诗·大雅》“旱魃为虐”，朱传云：“旱神也。”未闻有人死为魃者。《山海经》载黄帝征蚩尤，尤请风伯、雨师作大风雨，帝乃召女魃止之，遂诛蚩尤。《神异经》言，南方有人长二三尺，袒身，目在顶上，行如风，名曰魃，所见之国大旱，又名旱母。遇者得之，没溷中乃死，旱灾即消。此亦诞语不经。然要未有以死人称魃之理。

    Explanation

    580

    He then strolled along the corridors and railings, thoroughly observing the strange and unusual. There was a musical bell, at the appointed time all the sounds were played together, with the rhythm and pitch without error, not a moment late or early. There was also a telescope, which could be used to climb high and see far, and from two or three miles away, one could see someone's eyebrows and eyes. There were also many small treasures, such as a self-winding watch, held to the ear, like a beetle eating wood. There were also complete maps of the ocean, the Bodhi tree flower, the fragrant vine, the bird known for its call, the parrot with five colors, the bird that hangs upside down, the mongoose, and the short-legged dog. Crystal lamps were hung on the eaves, with jeweled ornaments hanging down. When the wind came, the beads and snowflakes swayed in the air, clashing together with a ringing sound, capable of holding fire for fifty lamps.

    I traveled along the Pearl River, and at night, I could see them from afar. In the year Xinmao, I also saw them in the capital. There were flags hung on the doors, with a red cloth background and a forked pattern, these were flags of the Helan merchant. I did not observe them in my place, and as the sun was about to set, I returned. The continued tour was not completed.

    The Debate on the Drought Demon
    The Book of Songs in Da Ya says, "The drought demon wreaks havoc." Zhu Xi's commentary states, "It is the drought deity." I have never heard of a dead person becoming a drought demon. The Classic of Mountains and Seas records that Huangdi, the Yellow Emperor, went to war against Chiyou. Chiyou requested the Wind Lord and Rain Master to create a great storm, and Huangdi then summoned the drought demon to stop it, and thus Chiyou was defeated. The Book of Strange Things states that in the south, there are people who are two or three feet tall, naked, with their eyes on their heads, and they move like the wind. They are called the drought demon, and the countries they visit suffer from great droughts, and they are also known as the mother of drought. If one encounters them and captures them, they must be buried in a latrine to die, and then the drought disaster will dissipate. This is also an absurd and unbelievable story. However, it is important to note that there is no reason to call a dead person a drought demon.

    581

    山左乡愚，每逢岁旱，辄以新冢上微湿者即以为魃。乘夜聚众掘墓开棺，磔其尸，碎其首。值天雨，尸主固无辞；不雨，群议息之。此等异传，正不知倡自何人，其流毒一至是！夫开棺见尸者拟绞，残毁加等。煌煌律令，罪难稍逭。乃毫不为怪，相沿成习，其间蚩蚩之氓不晓法律，犹有可原；又有黠猾者或诳诱乡民、阴泄私愤，更不可言。
    乾隆辛亥秋，旱，有平原张姓妻死，甫葬，村人某诡以为魃。一村哄起，掘墓出尸，以绳结之，犁地而行。其夫惨恨，鸣于官。官捕至，首倡者逃未获，从者论戍。吁！安得著明罪条，遍告乡邑？余故为是辨，使览者广为布闻，亦有无量功德也。

    Explanation

    581

    The ignorant people of Shandong, whenever there is a drought, always take a newly built tomb that appears slightly moist as the cause of the drought, believing it to be a bá. They gather at night to dig up graves and open coffins, dismembering the corpses and crushing their heads. If it rains, the owners of the corpses have no excuse; if it does not rain, the crowd stops discussing it. Such strange tales, it is truly unknown who initiated them, and the harm they cause reaches this extent! Those who open a coffin and see a corpse are sentenced to hanging, and the punishment is increased for causing damage. The clear and strict legal codes make the crime hard to escape. Yet, they show no concern and continue this practice, with simple-minded people not knowing the law, which can still be excused; but there are also shrewd individuals who may deceive and entice the villagers or vent private grievances secretly, which is even more reprehensible.

    In the autumn of 1751, during a drought, a woman from Pingyuan, surnamed Zhang, died and was just buried. A villager named someone falsely claimed it was a bá. The entire village was roused, digging up the grave and pulling out the corpse, tying it with ropes and dragging it through the fields. The husband, filled with grief and anger, reported it to the authorities. The officials apprehended the followers, but the leader escaped and was not caught. The followers were sentenced to military service. Alas! How could clear legal statutes be established and spread throughout all the villages? I write this to clarify the matter, so that readers may widely disseminate the information, and it will bring immeasurable merit and virtue.

    582

    (原系正论。然事有不可解者，旱魃往往为祟。吾乡亦曾遭亢，焚其尸即雨，甚奇。)

    述意
    场上设豆棚一架，满开豆花。陈几案，笔砚瓶麈。中悬“雨丝草堂、桂馥书屋”匾额，两旁挂“白昼饶人听说鬼，青天扯淡坐浓阴”对联。
    [生三髯，巾服上]黄叶飞来怕打头，闭门家里一书囚。只今学会安排法，秃管消磨豆雨秋。老夫七如居士，山东人也。幼识之无，长贪呫哔，年逾见恶，学不知非。虽是四壁萧然，却不离花、酒、琴、棋、诗、字、画，取个七如道号；还求那柴、米、油、盐，酱、醋、茶，弄得来一件俱无，倒也觉空诸所有。怎奈囊中无钞之时，便要作脚下生风之想。所以出外的日多，在家的日少。谁知这一些儿清福，老天竟是不轻与人的。昨日海上回来，天气炎热，暂作杜门之计。且喜妻贤妾淑，儿大女娇，八口清贫，一家欢聚。正是：大鹏息以六月，鹪鹩止于一枝。这也不表。近作《小豆棚》数卷，不免携到豆棚之下，校阅一番便了。

    Explanation

    582

    Translation

    Original Argument

    Originally, this was a main point. However, there are things that are hard to explain, such as the drought demon often causing mischief. My hometown has also experienced droughts, where burning its body brings rain, which is very strange.

    Preface

    In the courtyard, a bean trellis is set up, fully blooming with beans. A table and writing desk are arranged, with inkstones, brushes, and other stationery. In the center hangs a plaque inscribed with "Rainy Grass Pavilion, Fragrant Book Room," and on either side are couplets that read, "During the day, people enjoy hearing ghost stories, under the clear sky, they sit in deep shade and chat nonsense."

    [The character appears with a three-beard, wearing a hat and a robe] Falling yellow leaves fear hitting the head, so I shut myself in the house as a prisoner of books. Now I have learned how to arrange things, using a worn-out brush to pass the time during the autumn rain. I am the recluse known as Qiru, from Shandong. From a young age, I knew nothing, and as I grew older, I indulged in trivial matters. By the time I was old, I realized my mistakes and did not know how to correct them. Despite having bare four walls, I cannot be separated from flowers, wine, the lute, chess, poetry, calligraphy, and painting. I took the name Qiru for myself; I also seek those basic necessities like firewood, rice, oil, salt, soy sauce, vinegar, and tea. If I can manage to get even one of them, I feel content with all that I have. How can I help but think of making my feet fly when my purse is empty? Therefore, I spend more days outside than at home. Who would have thought that such a simple pleasure is not easily given by heaven. Returning from the sea yesterday, the weather was hot, so I temporarily decided to stay indoors. Fortunately, my wife is virtuous, my concubine is gentle, my children are grown, and my daughters are charming. We are eight in the family, living in poverty but united. It is true that the great Peng rests for six months, and the jiao bird finds contentment on a single branch. This is not worth mentioning. Recently, I have written several volumes of Xiao Dou Peng, and I will carry them to the bean trellis to review them.

    583

    〖北醉太平〗[生取书行唱]寂寞山家，有甚喧哗，门前几树鸟儿喳，一棚儿豆花。荒园镇日无人呀，我一个著书黄叶深林下，你看这长天那个来闲话。只恁般顽耍。
    [坐介]你看这豆花、豆叶，紫的紫，绿的绿，开的来满院浓阴，那太阳一点也是透不进来的，真好看也。
    〖北黄钟醉花阴〗[生唱]几曾见锦幔花棚，消得受套和袍，卧甚瓜藤架。吃惯的淡酒儿慢品咂，捧一盏火柴的苦熬茶。戴甚么乌纱，怎似俺破方巾任歪任斜。一支笔一本书，胡诌乱扯。

    Explanation

    583

    Translation:

    〖北醉太平〗[The actor takes a book and sings] In this lonely mountain home, what noise is there? Birds chirp in front of the house, and a trellis is covered with climbing beans. The desolate garden is empty all day, and I am the only one writing books under the deep forest with yellow leaves. Who comes to chat under such a vast sky? Just play like this.
    [Sitting down] Look at these bean flowers and leaves, purple ones are purple, green ones are green. They spread over the entire courtyard with dense shade, and the sun cannot even penetrate a bit. It is truly beautiful.

    〖北黄钟醉花阴〗[The actor sings] Have you ever seen a silk canopy and flower trellis that can withstand robes and coats? Lie on what kind of melon vine trellis? Accustomed to sipping light wine slowly, holding a cup of bitter tea like firewood. Why wear black gauze caps, how can it compare to my tattered square cap that can be worn crookedly? A pen and a book, writing nonsense.

    584

    [旦上引]浪游客子攻学懒，中馈娘儿做活勤。官人。[生]娘子请坐。[旦坐]自从官人还家之后，闭户清闲，十分自在，把我娘儿两个倒忙坏了。[生]娘子忙着何来？[旦喏]
    〖南画眉序〗[旦唱]头梳粉未搽，洗手清晨入厨下。煮一锅麦饭，赛仙洞胡麻；烧一束湿柴枝，是秋水蒹葭；采半篮野菜根，比西山薇蕨。这忙忙难速刺。小奁花绣鞋，帮跑来多大。
    [生笑介]其实难为娘子。[旦]我们得一刻闲，大家都来听你闲话。[生]这个甚好。[旦向内介]二娘，你且暂停针线，抱你孩儿来豆棚底下坐坐。[小旦]来了。[小旦抱小女儿，贴扮大女捧茶上，小旦引]乳花香透娇儿哺。[贴引]岕片茶浓爱女擎，爷爷请茶。[生]你们都坐下了，待我说几段故事与你们听听。[小旦坐]晓得。[贴摘豆花一枝与小儿戏介]

    Explanation

    584

    Translation:

    [旦上引] The wandering traveler studies lazily, while the wife of the middle kitchen works diligently. Husband. [生] Please sit down, wife. [旦坐] Since the husband returned home, he has been idle and at ease, but he has made my wife and I very busy. [生] Why is the wife so busy? [旦喏]

    [旦唱] She combs her hair and applies powder, washes her hands in the morning and goes into the kitchen. She boils a pot of wheat porridge, which rivals the sesame seeds of the fairy cave; she burns a bundle of wet wood branches, which are like the reeds and rushes of the autumn water; she gathers half a basket of wild vegetable roots, which are comparable to the wild vegetables and ferns of the western mountains. This is a busy and difficult task. The small box contains embroidered shoes, which are very large.

    [生笑介] Indeed, it is difficult for the wife. [旦] If we have a moment of leisure, let us all listen to your idle talk. [生] That is very good. [旦向内介] Second wife, please stop your needlework for a moment and bring your child to sit under the bean trellis. [小旦] Coming. [小旦抱小女儿，贴扮大女捧茶上，小旦引] The milk of the nursing mother is fragrant and nourishes the delicate child. [贴引] The tea is strong and the daughter loves it. Grandfather, please have some tea. [生] Everyone, please sit down, and I will tell you some stories. [小旦坐] I understand. [贴摘豆花一枝与小儿戏介]

    This translation aims to capture the essence of the original text while maintaining the cultural and historical context, and ensuring that the idiomatic expressions and syntactic structures are faithfully rendered into English.

    585

    〖北喜迁莺〗[生唱]说几个儿孙牛马，说一回欢喜冤家。窦娥儿惹下飞霜禾尽打，新息侯薏苡明珠真乱假。台空铜雀犹留瓦，到不如汉淮阴求一饭，甘心胯下。陶彭泽五斗腰叉。
    [小旦]虽是这等讲，那世态纵有炎凉，人心自留公道。[旦]还是守分安贫，知足常乐，我和你今日呵。
    〖南归朝欢〗[旦、小旦同唱]壶觞市不赊，吃不起蹠鸡臛鸭。春衣典没些，穿不上绣裙罗袜。一任他江山锦片前程大，争似俺风雪单寒处士家。何须论金谷繁华，玉堂声价。
    [生]你们谈到这里，真个一家眷属，尽已皈依。我把那愤世嫉俗的心肠，也就冰消瓦解了。
    〖北搅筝琶〗[生唱]把闷弓儿且按下，莫管他风月在谁家。且放开笑和尚的布袋，丢了那莽八戒的钉钯。不平事莫问咱，一谜价妆聋做哑。写一部天花，学一尊菩萨。但愿他没一个冤家，好人满天下，只就我吊古扳今，斩鬼封神平妖怪，都是逢人劝化。

    Explanation

    585

    Final Translation

    North: Huanxiang

    [Male Singer] Talking about children and grandchildren as oxen and horses, talking about loving enemies. Dou E caused a snowstorm and destroyed all the crops, Xinxihou was falsely accused of bringing back pearls made of rice grains. The Copper Bird Terrace is in ruins, but still leaves tiles behind. It's better to be Han Xin, who sought a meal and endured humiliation. Tao Yuanming resigned because he could not tolerate being paid with only five dou of rice.

    [Young Female Singer] Even though we talk like this, the world may have its hot and cold moments, but people's hearts will always uphold justice. [Female Singer] It's better to remain content and poor, to be satisfied and happy. This is what we have today.

    South: Returning to Joy

    [Female Singers] Wine and cups are not for sale, we cannot afford a dish made from chicken and duck. Spring clothes have been pawned away, we cannot wear embroidered skirts and silk socks. Let others enjoy their grand prospects, how much better is our humble life in the cold. Why worry about the opulence of Jin Valley or the prestige of the jade hall?

    [Male Singer] You have talked about this, truly, a family has found peace. My heart, which was once filled with anger and hatred of the world, has melted away.

    North: Stirring the Zither and Plectrum

    [Male Singer] Put down the troubled heart, don't worry about who owns the moon and flowers. Let go of the laughing monk's bag, drop the clumsy Bajie's pickaxe. Don't ask about unfair things, pretend to be deaf and mute. Write a book of heavenly flowers, learn from a Buddha. May there be no enemies, may good people be everywhere. I will reflect on the past and present, slay demons, seal gods, and pacify monsters, all while guiding others.

    586

    〖南学画眉〗[旦合唱]听到这雨飞花，胜生公点石夸。普陀常在空山寺，鲁门何日闲车马。打叠起忠良孝义，大家齐向抬头看，照着样儿描画。
    [丑草笠持竿，提鱼一尾上]雅无酒肉曾元养，却有鳞鱼杜孝干。我适在溪边钓得一尾金色鲤鱼。你看天色尚早，着回家里供我父亲晚膳来。此也是自家门首。[打门介][贴]哥哥回来了。[丑]居来哉。[贴看鱼介]母亲，你看这鱼呀！[丑]启禀爹爹，孩儿在溪上钓了一只金色鲤鱼来家，你看，鲜鳞活跳个来。[生]妙呀，得此一物，尽勾老夫下酒了。
    〖北四门子〗[生唱]稚儿学得敲针法，小竹竿得个鱼偏大。这便是、子陵台上桐江下，这便是、钓叟烟波也不差。管甚么滋味佳，器皿华，瓦盆中水煮清华。添取一壶村醪酣余话，直到月上山头更鼓打，方才去高眠下榻。

    Explanation

    586

    Translation:

    South Learning and Painting Eyebrows
    [Chorus of the Maiden] Hearing the rain and falling flowers, surpassing the marvel of Sheng Gong turning stones to gold. Potalaka often resides in the secluded temple on the mountain, when will the gate of Lu be quiet from carriages and horses? Gather the virtues of loyalty, filial piety, and righteousness, let everyone look up and follow the model, and draw according to the example.
    [The Clown, wearing a straw hat and holding a fishing rod, carrying a single fish] There is no fine wine or meat for sustenance, yet there is a fish with scales, caught by Du Xiaogan. I just caught a golden carp by the stream. Look, the sky is still early, so I will return home to serve it as my father's evening meal. This is my own doorstep. [Knocking on the door] [The Maid] Brother has returned. [The Clown] Come in. [The Maid, looking at the fish] Mother, look at this fish! [The Clown] Reporting to Father, I caught a golden carp by the stream and brought it home. Look, it is fresh and lively. [The Elder] Splendid, with this, I can have a drink.

    North Four Gates
    [The Elder sings] A young child has learned the art of fishing, a small bamboo pole caught a big fish. This is it, on the terrace of Zi Ling by the Tong River, this is it, the fisherman in the mist and waves is not bad. Who cares about the taste or the fancy dishes, in a clay pot, the water boils clear. Add a pot of village wine to enjoy the conversation, until the moon rises over the mountain and the night watchman's drum beats, then finally go to sleep.

    587

    〖南鲍老催〗[丑递鱼与旦唱介]母亲你忙回厨下。调腥味，须甘滑；刮鲜鳞，防刺扎；寸葱花，研桂擂姜都不彻。小刀儿须漫杀，破锅儿当先刷，饶得个压西湖五柳居无亚。
    [旦]这个自然。[生起介]我想几年出外，旅况萧条，今日故园风味，乐不可言。那些波涛尘鞅，真令人黯然销魂也。
    〖北水仙子〗[生]自从俺久别家，把楚水吴山入梦遐。这支儿秃毛锥成话靶，写张儿破潇湘且嗑牙。那里是、雨丝风片打秋瓜，只弄得、山空夜静飞檐瓦，却少个、东坡听咱说鬼碧萝凹。
    [旦]天色已晚，我们整备夜饭。待到月上，再至棚中玩赏。官人请。
    〖南双声子〗[合唱]多清暇，多清暇，一家人真快活。休当假，休当假，一出戏皆实话。当根钗，卖幅画，且消受落照欹斜，花容妖冶。

    Explanation

    587

    Translation:

    [Southern Old Man Baocui] [The clown hands the fish to the maiden and sings] Mother, hurry back to the kitchen. Adjust the fish's flavor to be sweet and smooth; scrape the fresh scales and be careful of the sharp bones; finely chop the scallions, grind the cinnamon, and pound the ginger thoroughly. Use the knife carefully, clean the pot first, and prepare a dish that rivals the famous West Lake Wu Liu Restaurant.
    [The maiden] Naturally, I will do so. [The male character stands up] I have been away for several years, and my travels have been bleak. Today, tasting the flavors of my hometown is indescribably joyful. Those tumultuous waves and dusty paths truly move one to tears.
    [Northern Water Nymph] [The male character] Since I have been away from home for a long time, the waters of Chu and Wu have entered my dreams. This piece of writing, like a bald pen, becomes a target for words, and my scribbles on broken Xiang paper are just chatter. There, the rain threads and wind patches beat the autumn melons, only to make the mountains empty and the night quiet, with tiles flying off the eaves, but there is no Su Shi to listen to us tell ghost stories in the green, winding paths.
    [The maiden] It is getting late, and we should prepare for dinner. Wait until the moon rises, and then we will go to the pavilion to enjoy ourselves. Sir, please.
    [Southwestern Double Sound] [Chorus] So much leisure, so much leisure, one family is truly happy. Do not pretend, do not pretend, this play is all true words. Wear a hairpin, sell a painting, and enjoy the slanting evening light, the beautiful and alluring flowers.

    588

    〖尾声〗七如行乐谁能写，把自家心事，直作宫商打。他年演唱豆棚图，须认咱。
    [生旦下][贴]哥哥，明日你到溪边钓一个小小鱼儿，养在缸里好耍子。[丑]是哉。[混下]

    附录
    南屏赠蕉白砚记
    端州有斧柯之山，在大江南，为羚羊峡对山。下际潮水，上立峻壁。沿而溯焉，即为砚岩，有泉出焉。唐宋悉采砚于兹。
    岩口为穴，匍匐入，五六丈，为正坑，从左转为西坑，从旁入为中坑，从右转为东坑。坑外大江也。坑中渊渟，以罂瓮传水注槽，乃可下凿。东坡云：“千夫挽绠，百夫运斤，篝火下锤，乃得斯珍。”
    坑之为言洞也，洞石无眼。又入为“康子洞”，此岩最寒，能伤人。又入为“东洞”，多蕉叶白，纯白成大片。其后为正洞，又名“北洞”，石弥纯粹，水弥深，盖泉出其中，故润自性成。外近江水，弥漫崩摧，岁久滋虞。宋治平中，凿留数砫，今也则无，以木代之。石工难采，往往穿漏压陷。风雨晨夜，时闻鬼哭。

    Explanation

    588

    Final Translation

    Tailpiece

    Who can write about the joy of traveling seven times? Expressing one's own thoughts and feelings through music. In the future, when performances are given, the audience will recognize us.

    [The male and female leads exit][The maiden] Brother, tomorrow you should go to the stream and catch a small fish, keep it in a jar for fun. [The clown] Right. [They all exit]

    Appendix

    Record of the Gift of the Banana Leaf White Inkstone from Nanping
    In Duanzhou, there is a mountain called Fuxi Mountain, located on the south bank of the Yangtze River, opposite the Lingyang Gorge. Below, tidal waters flow, and above, steep cliffs stand. Following the river upstream, one reaches the inkstone rock, where springs emerge. During the Tang and Song dynasties, inkstones were collected here.

    At the entrance of the rock is a cave. Crawling in, one reaches a depth of about fifteen to eighteen meters, known as the main pit. Turning left, one enters the western pit; entering from the side, one reaches the middle pit; turning right, one enters the eastern pit. Outside the pits lies the great river. Inside the pits, the water is deeply still. Large jars are used to transfer water into a trough, and only then can one begin to dig downward. Su Dongpo once said, "A thousand men pull ropes, a hundred men wield axes, campfires are lit, and hammers are struck downward, and thus this treasure is obtained."

    The word "pit" refers to a cave. The cave stone has no pores. Further inside is the "Kangzi Cave," which is the coldest and can harm people. Entering further is the "Eastern Cave," which contains many pieces of pure white stone, forming large sheets. Later, one reaches the main cave, also known as the "Northern Cave." The stone becomes increasingly pure, and the water increasingly deep, because springs emerge from it, thus naturally becoming moist. Near the outside, the river water spreads out and collapses, becoming more dangerous over time. During the Zhiping period of the Song dynasty, several pillars were carved and left, but now they are gone, replaced with wood. Stone workers find it difficult to extract the stone, and it often penetrates and leaks, collapsing and sinking. Rain and wind day and night, one often hears ghostly cries.

    589

    僧一行曰：“天地两戒，山河与天之云汉始末，为百川下流，束三江之水。”羚羊峡产石为“瑊硊”，盖东西两粤扶舆之脉，蕴结而成。欧阳文忠亦以“精石”目之也。
    余辛丑游粤，值中丞李文介开采旧坑，时在阳春袁春舫业师处。见其董率工事，因得其概。南屏沈子贻砚，所为旧蕉白，信是“康子洞”前之产，非时代物。欣喜过望，遂忆往事，爰笔为记。时在嘉庆二年春，客汶上馆中。

    段子崄
    栖霞有二石工，兄弟也，居段子崄。尝登崄开石为业。遇大雪飘飘如掌，峰峦玉琢，野甸银铺，粉本模糊，鹅毛飞舞，兄曰：“曷归，将谋晚炊。”遂去，弟检点锤凿入皮囊，负之下山。

    589

    Final Translation:

    Monk Yi Xing said, "The boundaries of heaven and earth, the mountains and rivers, and the celestial river from beginning to end, are the ultimate destination for all rivers, gathering the waters of the three great rivers." The Lingyang Gorge produces a stone called "jianwei," which is formed by the accumulation of the geological features of the eastern and western regions of Guangdong and Guangxi. Ouyang Xiu also referred to this stone as "jingshi."

    In the year Xinchou, I traveled to Guangdong and happened to be at the residence of my former teacher, Yuan Chunfang in Yangchun, where Governor-General Li Wenjie was mining an old quarry. I saw him overseeing the work and thus understood its essence. Nanping's Shen Ziyi gave me a writing brush, and the old banana leaf paper he provided was indeed produced before the "Kangzi Cave," not a product of its time. Exceedingly pleased, I recalled past events and thus wrote this down. It was in the spring of the second year of the Jiaqing era, while I was a guest at the Wen Shang Academy.

    In the Segziyan area of Qixia, there were two stonemasons who were brothers and lived in Segziyan. They used to climb the cliffs to quarry stone for a living. One day, they encountered a heavy snowfall, with snowflakes falling like palm leaves, the peaks and ridges looking like jade carvings, and the fields covered in silver. The landscape was blurred, and the feathers of the snowflakes danced in the air. The elder brother said, "Let's go back, we should plan for the evening meal." So they left, while the younger brother checked his hammer and chisel, put them in his bag, and carried them down the mountain.

    590

    至路口，见一女以长帕蒙首垂肩际，着翠色布袄，靸镶花小靴，立琼瑶中。工望之嫣然，女曰：“迷漫遍野，不辨途径，畴导我以先路耶？”工曰：“娘子将何之？”女手指曰：“我住山南村也。”工导之行。至村口，工伫立，女曰：“盍送我于家？”工复行。女至门款户，有老媪出曰：“儿冒雪归耶？”女曰：“中途有送儿来者。”媪即招工曰：“看天公絮絮不止，又劳小郎远来作向导。请入草舍，拥炉一避寒气，俟稍霁再行未迟也。”工听媪言喜，入释其负。见地下小靴印泥，如白莲数瓣落水面。女方翘其足，庋小凳，曰：“皑皑直没到绣花帮。幸冻冷不就消融，否则渗透裹缠矣！”媪见工浑身冰絮，四顾无所为计。女自袖中出一帕与媪，转递工。工接巾自拂其衣。女复取柳柴架折足铛，俄而火隆隆起，燠满一室。

    Explanation

    590

    At the crossroads, the craftsman saw a woman covering her head with a long scarf that hung down to her shoulders, wearing a greenish cloth jacket and small embroidered boots, standing in a pristine setting. The craftsman looked at her with a gentle smile. The woman said, "The snow is everywhere, and I can't tell the way. Could you guide me on the right path?" The craftsman asked, "Where are you going, Lady?" The woman pointed and said, "I live in a village to the south of the mountain." The craftsman led her on her way. When they reached the village entrance, the craftsman stopped and the woman said, "Why don't you come with me to my home?" The craftsman continued to follow her. When they arrived at the door, the woman gently knocked. An old lady came out and said, "Did you come back in the snow, my child?" The woman replied, "There was someone who came along to escort me." The old lady then invited the craftsman, saying, "Look at the continuous snowfall. It's also a bother for you to come all this way as a guide. Please come into our humble cottage, warm yourself by the fire, and wait until the weather clears up a bit before continuing your journey." The craftsman, hearing the old lady's words, was pleased and entered, putting down his load. He saw the footprint of the woman's boot in the mud, resembling white lotus petals floating on water. The woman lifted her foot and placed it on a small stool, saying, "The snow is so deep, it covers my embroidered boot. Fortunately, the cold prevents it from melting, otherwise it would soak through and stick to my clothes!" The old lady saw the craftsman covered in ice and snow, looking around for a solution. The woman took a handkerchief from her sleeve and passed it to the old lady, who then gave it to the craftsman. The craftsman took the handkerchief and wiped his clothes. The woman then took willow twigs to light a three-legged stove, and soon the room was filled with warmth.

    591

    工向暖，以两手虚探其上。媪取一小壶，热秫浆斟工曰：“饮一杯荡风雪。”工接杯自酌，女坐媪后。媪问工姓氏里居并其家事，工一一告之。媪曰：“小郎尚未娶耶？”工曰：“然。”女起，目曼视工，遂入里室。媪曰：“我某姓老孀也，止一女，未有婿家。小郎若肯赘我，尽半子职，你终身薄粥，可不劳咄嗟也。”工曰：“蒙姥姥不嫌，实所厚感。但我无一钱，归告兄嫂，为我一番打算。”媪笑曰：“吾为尔室家，以攸宁尔，岂于尔顶上加愁帽耶！”

    Explanation

    591

    The worker warmed himself, tentatively reaching out with both hands. The old woman took a small pot and poured a cup of warm millet porridge, saying to him, "Drink one cup to dispel the cold." The worker took the cup and drank alone. The daughter sat behind the old woman. The old woman asked the worker about his name, place of origin, and family matters, to which the worker replied in detail. The old woman said, "Has the young man not yet married?" The worker replied, "Yes." The daughter stood up, her gaze lingering on the worker, and then entered the inner room. The old woman said, "I am an elderly widow of the surname X, with only one daughter who has no husband. If the young man is willing to marry into my family, he can fulfill the duties of a son, and you will not have to worry about daily sustenance." The worker said, "I am deeply grateful that you do not reject me. However, I have no money. I will inform my elder brother and sister-in-law and make some plans for me." The old woman smiled and said, "I will provide you with a home, to ensure your peace and stability. How could I add more worry to your life!"

    592

    媪看檐前雪狂正盛，天又向暮，曰：“小郎休矣！就今夜完成好事。况大雪漫漫，尔归途亦不为近；且吾家更无悬榻，此诚天作之合。”遂起入内间。半晌，闻母女私语，又哂笑声。
    媪持双炬，高烧而出。女随之，被一新纳水红衣。媪令工并立展拜。工捉襟则偌唱不圆，决踵而跽容不俯，草草成礼，媪受两拜。女入室，即持酒果罗案上，虽无胹熊炮鳖之精，而一蔬一饭，皆非工在家时所常得而属餍者。饭毕，与女入内，相得最欢。
    先是，工兄归待弟，晚不至，霙霰愈重，出村遥盼。初以为遇相识邀饮，及深更不见还，其嫂曰：“小叔最诚愿，非东家吃饭，西家便宿者。不归，令人悬悬！”早起，兄寻径登崄，四访无踪。惟有乱石坎坷，与寒光掩映于深岩溜磴之间。其兄手足颇笃，痛哭而返。累日访觅，不得音耗。觅帖招字，几遍城乡。

    Explanation

    592

    The old woman looked at the fierce snowflakes flying outside the eaves and saw that it was already dusk. She said, "Young master, please rest! We will complete this good deed tonight. Besides, the heavy snow makes your journey long, and our house has no spare room. This is truly a match made in heaven." She then got up and went inside. After a while, she heard the mother and daughter whispering and laughing.

    The old woman came out holding two torches, burning brightly. The woman followed, wearing a newly received water-red dress. The old woman made the craftsman stand up and bow. The craftsman, when he held his sleeves, sang not quite in tune, and with a resolute step and a kneeling posture, his head not bowed, the ceremony was conducted hastily. The old woman received two bows. The woman went into the room and served wine and fruit on the table. Though lacking the delicacy of stewed bear and roasted turtle, every vegetable and meal were not what the craftsman usually got to satisfy his appetite at home. After the meal, the craftsman and the woman entered the inner chamber, and they got along very well.

    Previously, the craftsman's brother returned to wait for his younger brother. In the evening, the younger brother did not arrive, and the snow and sleet became heavier. The brother went out of the village to look far and wide. Initially, he thought he met someone he knew who invited him to drink, but by deep night, he did not return. His sister-in-law said, "My younger brother is the most sincere and willing. He is not the type who eats at one house and sleeps at another. Not returning makes one anxious." In the morning, the brother searched the path and climbed the dangerous terrain, visiting everywhere without finding a trace. Only there were scattered rocks and uneven paths, and the cold light shone through the deep rock crevices and slippery steps. His brother was very fond of him and cried bitterly and returned. For many days, he searched for him without any news. He posted notices and posters, almost covering the entire town and countryside.

    593

    逾年夏，兄又至崄开石。见一洞，洞外石上一人，枕皮囊卧。逼视之，即其弟，呼而起。见兄仓皇，复欲入洞，兄曳归家。问其故，遂告前事，云已娶室数月。兄嫂以为怪。后其兄入山，不令弟同往，恐再为妖物摄去故也。
    一日，工立门外，遥见一妇戴纱罩，著新衣，骑一驴，丁丁入村。至工门首，勒辔揭罩。工视之，其妻也，遂掖妇下。入内拜见嫂，即呼以嫂；兄归，拜见兄，即呼以兄。兄嫂见其面庞端正，言语安详，衣服整洁，心性柔和，大喜曰：“是好妯娌，断不至离间我兄弟者。借使其妖，不犹愈于人乎！”遂安之。除屋一间，令与弟居。数日后，即与嫂同操井舂，辛苦不辞。三年，一切起居饮食，以及燕私动静，无一毫与人异。

    Explanation

    593

    The following summer, the elder brother again went to the dangerous place to open the stone. He saw a cave, and on the stone outside the cave, a person was sleeping with a leather bag as a pillow. He looked closely, and it was his younger brother. He called out and woke him up. Seeing his elder brother in a hurry, the younger brother wanted to go back into the cave. The elder brother pulled him back home. He asked the reason, and the younger brother then told him about what had happened before, saying he had married a wife several months ago. The elder brother and elder sister-in-law thought it was strange. Afterward, when the elder brother went into the mountains, he did not allow his younger brother to go with him, fearing that the younger brother might be taken again by some supernatural being.

    One day, while standing at the door, he saw a woman wearing a veil, dressed in new clothes, riding a donkey, and slowly entering the village. When she reached the entrance of the house, she pulled the reins and lifted the veil. He looked at her, and it was his wife, so he helped her down. He went inside to pay respects to his elder sister-in-law, and immediately called her "sister-in-law"; when the elder brother returned, he paid respects to him, and immediately called him "brother." The elder brother and elder sister-in-law saw that she had a pleasant face, spoke calmly, was neatly dressed, and had a gentle disposition, and they were very pleased and said, "She is a good sister-in-law, and she will not interfere between our brothers. Even if she were a supernatural being, wouldn't she be better than a human?" So they settled her down. They set aside a room for her to live with her younger brother. A few days later, she began working alongside her elder sister-in-law to draw water and pound grain, and she did not complain about the hard work. For three years, in all aspects of daily life, diet, and even private moments, she was indistinguishable from any other person.

    594

    是年秋，忽有老佣持一信，牵一驴来。妇拆阅，捶胸大痛，几不欲生。工与兄嫂检视其书，皆目不识一丁字。问之，始知其母讣音。妇匆匆裹头，脱其花鞋，呼夫偕往。兄恐弟去而不返，乃支吾曰：“弟妇奔丧，宜先去。弟随后从容备冥资来也。”妇不及致辞，出门跨蹇，犹含泪低语其夫曰：“起身仓卒，床头脱舄当收之，毋令人拾去也。”于是老佣挥鞭，如飞而杳，自此寂然。
    工后思妇綦切，每置祭榼入山，迷津难问，洞口常封。临风高呼，迄无应声而出者。工至今翻其零膏剩粉，未尝不泫然流涕，伤其忍去之若是恝也！
    (此是一幅李营邱白描雪景图画。)

    Explanation

    594

    In that autumn, suddenly an old servant arrived with a letter and a donkey. The woman read the letter, beat her chest in great pain, and almost could not bear living. The husband and his siblings checked the letter but were illiterate. When they asked, they learned that it was news of the mother's death. The woman hurriedly wrapped her head, removed her embroidered shoes, and called her husband to go with her. The brother feared that his younger brother would not return, so he hesitated and said, "Your wife should go first to mourn. You can come later to prepare the necessary items for the afterlife." The woman did not have time to say anything and went out, mounting the donkey, still crying softly and whispering to her husband, "I am leaving in a hurry, so the shoes left on the bed should be collected, and don't let anyone pick them up." Then the old servant whipped the donkey, and they disappeared like the wind, and from then on, there was silence.

    Later, the husband missed his wife deeply. Whenever he placed offerings and went into the mountains, he was lost and could not find his way, and the cave entrance was always sealed. He stood by the wind and shouted, but no one responded. To this day, whenever he turns over the leftover makeup and powder, he cannot help but shed tears, grieving that she left so indifferently.

    This is a white outline snow scene painting by Li Yingqiu.
